Genesis1:1 In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
Genesis1:2 Now the earth was formless and void
Genesis1:3 And God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw that the light was good
Genesis1:5 God called the light “day
Genesis1:6 And God said
Genesis1:7 So God made the expanse and separated the waters beneath it from the waters above. And it was so.
Genesis1:8 God called the expanse “sky.” And there was evening
Genesis1:9 And God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land “earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said
Genesis1:12 The earth produced vegetation: seed-bearing plants according to their kinds and trees bearing fruit with seed according to their kinds. And God saw that it was good.
Genesis1:13 And there was evening
Genesis1:14 And God said
Genesis1:15 And let them serve as lights in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth.” And it was so.
Genesis1:16 God made two great lights: the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night. And He made the stars as well.
Genesis1:17 God set these lights in the expanse of the sky to shine upon the earth
Genesis1:18 to preside over the day and the night
Genesis1:19 And there was evening
Genesis1:20 And God said
Genesis1:21 So God created the great sea creatures and every living thing that moves
Genesis1:22 Then God blessed them and said
Genesis1:23 And there was evening
Genesis1:24 And God said
Genesis1:25 God made the beasts of the earth according to their kinds
Genesis1:26 Then God said
Genesis1:27 So God created man in His own image; in the image of God He created him; male and female He created them.
Genesis1:28 God blessed them and said to them
Genesis1:29 Then God said
Genesis1:30 And to every beast of the earth and every bird of the air and every creature that crawls upon the earth—everything that has the breath of life in it—I have given every green plant for food.” And it was so.
Genesis1:31 And God looked upon all that He had made
Genesis2:1 Thus the heavens and the earth were completed in all their vast array.
Genesis2:2 And by the seventh day God had finished the work He had been doing; so on that day He rested from all His work.
Genesis2:3 Then God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it
Genesis2:4 This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created
Genesis2:5 Now no shrub of the field had yet appeared on the earth
Genesis2:6 But springs welled up from the earth and watered the whole surface of the ground.
Genesis2:7 Then the Lord God formed man from the dust of the ground and breathed the breath of life into his nostrils
Genesis2:8 And the Lord God planted a garden in Eden
Genesis2:9 Out of the ground the Lord God gave growth to every tree that is pleasing to the eye and good for food. And in the middle of the garden were the tree of life and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
Genesis2:10 Now a river flowed out of Eden to water the garden
Genesis2:11 The name of the first river is Pishon; it winds through the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 And the gold of that land is pure
Genesis2:13 The name of the second river is Gihon; it winds through the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The name of the third river is Hiddekel; it runs along the east side of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 Then the Lord God took the man and placed him in the Garden of Eden to cultivate and keep it.
Genesis2:16 And the Lord God commanded him
Genesis2:17 but you must not eat from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil; for in the day that you eat of it
Genesis2:18 The Lord God also said
Genesis2:19 And out of the ground the Lord God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air
Genesis2:20 The man gave names to all the livestock
Genesis2:21 So the Lord God caused the man to fall into a deep sleep
Genesis2:22 And from the rib that the Lord God had taken from the man
Genesis2:23 And the man said: “This is now bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called ‘woman
Genesis2:24 For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife
Genesis2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked
Genesis3:1 Now the serpent was more crafty than any beast of the field that the Lord God had made. And he said to the woman
Genesis3:2 The woman answered the serpent
Genesis3:3 but about the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden
Genesis3:4 “You will not surely die
Genesis3:5 “For God knows that in the day you eat of it
Genesis3:6 When the woman saw that the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eyes
Genesis3:7 And the eyes of both of them were opened
Genesis3:8 Then the man and his wife heard the voice of the Lord God walking in the garden in the breeze of the day
Genesis3:9 But the Lord God called out to the man
Genesis3:10 “I heard Your voice in the garden

Genesis3:12 And the man answered
Genesis3:13 Then the Lord God said to the woman

Genesis3:18 Both thorns and thistles it will yield for you

Genesis6:17 And behold

Genesis6:20 Two of every kind of bird and animal and crawling creature will come to you to be kept alive.
Genesis6:21 You are also to take for yourself every kind of food that is eaten and gather it as food for yourselves and for the animals.”
Genesis6:22 So Noah did everything precisely as God had commanded him.

Genesis7:23 And every living thing on the face of the earth was destroyed—man and livestock

Genesis8:2 The springs of the deep and the floodgates of the heavens were closed

Genesis8:4 On the seventeenth day of the seventh month
Genesis8:5 And the waters continued to recede until the tenth month

Genesis8:7 and sent out a raven. It kept flying back and forth until the waters had dried up from the earth.

Genesis8:9 But the dove found no place to rest her foot

Genesis8:11 And behold

Exodus21:6 then his master is to bring him before the judges. And he shall take him to the door or doorpost and pierce his ear with an awl. Then he shall serve his master for life.
Exodus21:7 And if a man sells his daughter as a servant

Exodus21:11 If
Exodus21:12 Whoever strikes and kills a man must surely be put to death.
Exodus21:13 If

Exodus21:15 Whoever strikes his father or mother must surely be put to death.
Exodus21:16 Whoever kidnaps another man must be put to death
Exodus21:17 Anyone who curses his father or mother must surely be put to death.
Exodus21:18 If men are quarreling and one strikes the other with a stone or a fist
Exodus21:19 then the one who struck him shall go unpunished
Exodus21:20 If a man strikes his manservant or maidservant with a rod
Exodus21:21 However

Exodus21:23 But if a serious injury results

Exodus21:25 burn for burn
Exodus21:26 If a man strikes and blinds the eye of his manservant or maidservant
Exodus21:27 And if he knocks out the tooth of his manservant or maidservant

Exodus21:29 But if the ox has a habit of goring
Exodus21:30 If payment is demanded of him instead
Exodus21:31 If the ox gores a son or a daughter
Exodus21:32 If the ox gores a manservant or maidservant
Exodus21:33 If a man opens or digs a pit and fails to cover it
Exodus21:34 the owner of the pit shall make restitution; he must pay its owner
Exodus21:35 If a man’s ox injures his neighbor’s ox and it dies
Exodus21:36 But if it was known that the ox had a habit of goring
Exodus22:1 “If a man steals an ox or a sheep and slaughters or sells it
Exodus22:2 If a thief is caught breaking in and is beaten to death
Exodus22:3 But if it happens after sunrise
Exodus22:4 If what was stolen is actually found alive in his possession—whether ox or donkey or sheep—he must pay back double.
Exodus22:5 If a man grazes his livestock in a field or vineyard and allows them to stray so that they graze in someone else’s field
Exodus22:6 If a fire breaks out and spreads to thornbushes so that it consumes stacked or standing grain
Exodus22:7 If a man gives his neighbor money or goods for safekeeping and they are stolen from the neighbor’s house
Exodus22:8 If the thief is not found
Exodus22:9 In all cases of illegal possession of an ox
Exodus22:10 If a man gives a donkey

Exodus22:12 But if the animal was actually stolen from the neighbor
Exodus22:13 If the animal was torn to pieces
Exodus22:14 If a man borrows an animal from his neighbor and it is injured or dies while its owner is not present
Exodus22:15 If the owner was present
Exodus22:16 If a man seduces a virgin who is not pledged in marriage and sleeps with her
Exodus22:17 If her father absolutely refuses to give her to him
Exodus22:18 You must not allow a sorceress to live.
Exodus22:19 Whoever lies with an animal must surely be put to death.
Exodus22:20 If anyone sacrifices to any god other than the Lord alone
Exodus22:21 You must not exploit or oppress a foreign resident
Exodus22:22 You must not mistreat any widow or orphan.
Exodus22:23 If you do mistreat them
Exodus22:24 My anger will be kindled
Exodus22:25 If you lend money to one of My people among you who is poor
Exodus22:26 If you take your neighbor’s cloak as collateral
Exodus22:27 because his cloak is the only covering he has for his body. What else will he sleep in? And if he cries out to Me
Exodus22:28 You must not blaspheme God or curse the ruler of your people.
Exodus22:29 You must not hold back offerings from your granaries or vats. You are to give Me the firstborn of your sons.
Exodus22:30 You shall do likewise with your cattle and your sheep. Let them stay with their mothers for seven days
Exodus22:31 You are to be My holy people. You must not eat the meat of a mauled animal found in the field; you are to throw it to the dogs.
Exodus23:1 “You shall not spread a false report. Do not join the wicked by being a malicious witness.
Exodus23:2 You shall not follow the crowd in wrongdoing. When you testify in a lawsuit
Exodus23:3 And do not show favoritism to a poor man in his lawsuit.
Exodus23:4 If you encounter your enemy’s stray ox or donkey
Exodus23:5 If you see the donkey of one who hates you fallen under its load
Exodus23:6 You shall not deny justice to the poor in their lawsuits.
Exodus23:7 Stay far away from a false accusation. Do not kill the innocent or the just

Exodus23:13 Pay close attention to everything I have said to you. You must not invoke the names of other gods; they must not be heard on your lips.
Exodus23:14 Three times a year you are to celebrate a feast to Me.
Exodus23:15 You are to keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread as I commanded you: At the appointed time in the month of Abib you are to eat unleavened bread for seven days
Exodus23:16 You are also to keep the Feast of Harvest with the firstfruits of the produce from what you sow in the field. And keep the Feast of Ingathering at the end of the year
Exodus23:17 Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord GOD.
Exodus23:18 You must not offer the blood of My sacrifices with anything leavened
Exodus23:19 Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the Lord your God. You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.
Exodus23:20 Behold
Exodus23:21 Pay attention to him and listen to his voice; do not defy him
Exodus23:22 But if you will listen carefully to his voice and do everything I say
Exodus23:23 For My angel will go before you and bring you into the land of the Amorites
Exodus23:24 You must not bow down to their gods or serve them or follow their practices. Instead
Exodus23:25 So you shall serve the Lord your God
Exodus23:26 No woman in your land will miscarry or be barren; I will fulfill the number of your days.
Exodus23:27 I will send My terror ahead of you and throw into confusion every nation you encounter. I will make all your enemies turn and run.
Exodus23:28 I will send the hornet before you to drive the Hivites and Canaanites and Hittites out of your way.
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out before you in a single year; otherwise the land would become desolate and wild animals would multiply against you.
Exodus23:30 Little by little I will drive them out ahead of you
Exodus23:31 And I will establish your borders from the Red Sea to the Sea of the Philistines
Exodus23:32 You shall make no covenant with them or with their gods.
Exodus23:33 They must not remain in your land

Exodus25:12 Cast four gold rings for it and fasten them to its four feet
Exodus25:13 And make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus25:14 Insert the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark
Exodus25:15 The poles are to remain in the rings of the ark; they must not be removed.
Exodus25:16 And place inside the ark the Testimony
Exodus25:17 And you are to construct a mercy seat of pure gold
Exodus25:18 Make two cherubim of hammered gold at the ends of the mercy seat
Exodus25:19 one cherub on one end and one on the other
Exodus25:20 And the cherubim are to have wings that spread upward
Exodus25:21 Set the mercy seat atop the ark
Exodus25:22 And I will meet with you there above the mercy seat
Exodus25:23 You are also to make a table of acacia wood two cubits long
Exodus25:24 Overlay it with pure gold and make a gold molding around it.
Exodus25:25 And make a rim around it a handbreadth wide and put a gold molding on the rim.
Exodus25:26 Make four gold rings for the table and fasten them to the four corners at its four legs.
Exodus25:27 The rings are to be close to the rim
Exodus25:28 Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold
Exodus25:29 You are also to make the plates and dishes
Exodus25:30 And place the Bread of the Presence on the table before Me at all times.
Exodus25:31 Then you are to make a lampstand of pure
Exodus25:32 Six branches are to extend from the sides of the lampstand—three on one side and three on the other.
Exodus25:33 There are to be three cups shaped like almond blossoms on the first branch
Exodus25:34 And on the lampstand there shall be four cups shaped like almond blossoms with buds and petals.
Exodus25:35 For the six branches that extend from the lampstand
Exodus25:36 The buds and branches are to be all of one piece with the lampstand
Exodus25:37 Make seven lamps and set them up on the lampstand so that they illuminate the area in front of it.
Exodus25:38 The wick trimmers and their trays must be of pure gold.
Exodus25:39 The lampstand and all these utensils shall be made from a talent of pure gold.
Exodus25:40 See to it that you make everything according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.
Exodus26:1 “You are to construct the tabernacle itself with ten curtains of finely spun linen
Exodus26:2 Each curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits long and four cubits wide —all curtains the same size.
Exodus26:3 Five of the curtains are to be joined together
Exodus26:4 Make loops of blue material on the edge of the end curtain in the first set
Exodus26:5 Make fifty loops on one curtain and fifty loops on the end curtain of the second set
Exodus26:6 Make fifty gold clasps as well
Exodus26:7 You are to make curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle—eleven curtains in all.
Exodus26:8 Each of the eleven curtains is to be the same size—thirty cubits long and four cubits wide.
Exodus26:9 Join five of the curtains into one set and the other six into another. Then fold the sixth curtain over double at the front of the tent.
Exodus26:10 Make fifty loops along the edge of the end curtain in the first set
Exodus26:11 Make fifty bronze clasps and put them through the loops to join the tent together as a unit.
Exodus26:12 As for the overlap that remains of the tent curtains
Exodus26:13 And the tent curtains will be a cubit longer on either side
Exodus26:14 Also make a covering for the tent out of ram skins dyed red
Exodus26:15 You are to construct upright frames of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
Exodus26:16 Each frame is to be ten cubits long and a cubit and a half wide.
Exodus26:17 Two tenons must be connected to each other for each frame. Make all the frames of the tabernacle in this way.
Exodus26:18 Construct twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:19 with forty silver bases under the twenty frames—two bases for each frame
Exodus26:20 For the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:21 and forty silver bases—two bases under each frame.
Exodus26:22 Make six frames for the rear of the tabernacle
Exodus26:23 and two frames for the two back corners of the tabernacle
Exodus26:24 coupled together from bottom to top and fitted into a single ring. These will serve as the two corners.
Exodus26:25 So there are to be eight frames and sixteen silver bases—two under each frame.
Exodus26:26 You are also to make five crossbars of acacia wood for the frames on one side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:27 five for those on the other side
Exodus26:28 The central crossbar in the middle of the frames shall extend from one end to the other.
Exodus26:29 Overlay the frames with gold and make gold rings to hold the crossbars. Also overlay the crossbars with gold.
Exodus26:30 So you are to set up the tabernacle according to the pattern shown you on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 Make a veil of blue
Exodus26:32 Hang it with gold hooks on four posts of acacia wood
Exodus26:33 And hang the veil from the clasps and place the ark of the Testimony behind the veil. So the veil will separate the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:34 Put the mercy seat on the ark of the Testimony in the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:35 And place the table outside the veil on the north side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:36 For the entrance to the tent
Exodus26:37 Make five posts of acacia wood for the curtain
Exodus27:1 “You are to build an altar of acacia wood. The altar must be square
Exodus27:2 Make a horn on each of its four corners
Exodus27:3 Make all its utensils of bronze—its pots for removing ashes
Exodus27:4 Construct for it a grate of bronze mesh
Exodus27:5 Set the grate beneath the ledge of the altar
Exodus27:6 Additionally
Exodus27:7 The poles are to be inserted into the rings so that the poles are on two sides of the altar when it is carried.
Exodus27:8 Construct the altar with boards so that it is hollow. It is to be made just as you were shown on the mountain.
Exodus27:9 You are also to make a courtyard for the tabernacle. On the south side of the courtyard make curtains of finely spun linen
Exodus27:10 with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases
Exodus27:11 Likewise there are to be curtains on the north side
Exodus27:12 The curtains on the west side of the courtyard shall be fifty cubits wide
Exodus27:13 The east side of the courtyard
Exodus27:14 Make the curtains on one side fifteen cubits long
Exodus27:15 and the curtains on the other side fifteen cubits long
Exodus27:16 The gate of the courtyard shall be twenty cubits long
Exodus27:17 All the posts around the courtyard shall have silver bands
Exodus27:18 The entire courtyard shall be a hundred cubits long and fifty cubits wide
Exodus27:19 All the utensils of the tabernacle for every use
Exodus27:20 And you are to command the Israelites to bring you pure oil of pressed olives for the light
Exodus27:21 In the Tent of Meeting
Exodus28:1 “Next
Exodus28:2 Make holy garments for your brother Aaron
Exodus28:3 You are to instruct all the skilled craftsmen
Exodus28:4 These are the garments that they shall make: a breastpiece
Exodus28:5 They shall use gold
Exodus28:6 They are to make the ephod of finely spun linen embroidered with gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have two shoulder pieces attached at two of its corners
Exodus28:8 And the skillfully woven waistband of the ephod must be of one piece
Exodus28:9 Take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the sons of Israel:
Exodus28:10 six of their names on one stone and the remaining six on the other
Exodus28:11 Engrave the names of the sons of Israel on the two stones the way a gem cutter engraves a seal. Then mount the stones in gold filigree settings.
Exodus28:12 Fasten both stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the sons of Israel. Aaron is to bear their names on his two shoulders as a memorial before the Lord.
Exodus28:13 Fashion gold filigree settings
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold
Exodus28:15 You are also to make a breastpiece of judgment with the same workmanship as the ephod. Construct it with gold
Exodus28:16 It must be square when folded over double
Exodus28:17 And mount on it a setting of gemstones
Exodus28:18 in the second row a turquoise
Exodus28:19 in the third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 and in the fourth row a beryl
Exodus28:21 The twelve stones are to correspond to the names of the sons of Israel
Exodus28:22 For the breastpiece
Exodus28:23 You are also to make two gold rings and fasten them to the two corners of the breastpiece.
Exodus28:24 Then fasten the two gold chains to the two gold rings at the corners of the breastpiece
Exodus28:25 and fasten the other ends of the two chains to the two filigree settings
Exodus28:26 Make two more gold rings and attach them to the other two corners of the breastpiece
Exodus28:27 Make two additional gold rings and attach them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus28:28 The rings of the breastpiece shall be tied to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue yarn
Exodus28:29 Whenever Aaron enters the Holy Place
Exodus28:30 And place the Urim and Thummim in the breastpiece of judgment
Exodus28:31 You are to make the robe of the ephod entirely of blue cloth
Exodus28:32 with an opening at its top in the center. Around the opening shall be a woven collar with an opening like that of a garment
Exodus28:33 Make pomegranates of blue
Exodus28:34 alternating the gold bells and pomegranates around the lower hem of the robe.
Exodus28:35 Aaron must wear the robe whenever he ministers
Exodus28:36 You are to make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it as on a seal: HOLY TO THE LORD.
Exodus28:37 Fasten to it a blue cord to mount it on the turban; it shall be on the front of the turban.
Exodus28:38 And it will be worn on Aaron’s forehead
Exodus28:39 You are to weave the tunic with fine linen
Exodus28:40 Make tunics
Exodus28:41 After you put these garments on your brother Aaron and his sons
Exodus28:42 Make linen undergarments to cover their bare flesh
Exodus28:43 Aaron and his sons must wear them whenever they enter the Tent of Meeting or approach the altar to minister in the Holy Place
Exodus29:1 “Now this is what you are to do to consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve Me as priests: Take a young bull and two rams without blemish
Exodus29:2 along with unleavened bread
Exodus29:3 put them in a basket
Exodus29:4 Then present Aaron and his sons at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 Take the garments and clothe Aaron with the tunic
Exodus29:6 Put the turban on his head and attach the holy diadem to the turban.
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil and anoint him by pouring it on his head.
Exodus29:8 Present his sons as well and clothe them with tunics.
Exodus29:9 Wrap the sashes around Aaron and his sons and tie headbands on them. The priesthood shall be theirs by a permanent statute. In this way you are to ordain Aaron and his sons.
Exodus29:10 You are to present the bull at the front of the Tent of Meeting
Exodus29:11 And you shall slaughter the bull before the Lord at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
Exodus29:12 Take some of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger; then pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar.
Exodus29:13 Take all the fat that covers the entrails and the lobe of the liver
Exodus29:14 But burn the flesh of the bull and its hide and dung outside the camp; it is a sin offering.
Exodus29:15 Take one of the rams
Exodus29:16 You are to slaughter the ram
Exodus29:17 Cut the ram into pieces
Exodus29:18 Then burn the entire ram on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the Lord
Exodus29:19 Take the second ram
Exodus29:20 Slaughter the ram
Exodus29:21 And take some of the blood on the altar and some of the anointing oil and sprinkle it on Aaron and his garments
Exodus29:22 Take the fat from the ram
Exodus29:23 along with one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 Put all these in the hands of Aaron and his sons and wave them before the Lord as a wave offering.
Exodus29:25 Then take them from their hands and burn them on the altar atop the burnt offering as a pleasing aroma before the Lord; it is an offering made by fire to the Lord.
Exodus29:26 Take the breast of the ram of Aaron’s ordination and wave it before the Lord as a wave offering
Exodus29:27 Consecrate for Aaron and his sons the breast of the wave offering that is waved and the thigh of the heave offering that is lifted up from the ram of ordination.
Exodus29:28 This will belong to Aaron and his sons as a regular portion from the Israelites
Exodus29:29 The holy garments that belong to Aaron will belong to his sons after him
Exodus29:30 The son who succeeds him as priest and enters the Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy Place must wear them for seven days.
Exodus29:31 You are to take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 At the entrance to the Tent of Meeting
Exodus29:33 They must eat those things by which atonement was made for their ordination and consecration. But no outsider may eat them
Exodus29:34 And if any of the meat of ordination or any bread is left until the morning
Exodus29:35 This is what you are to do for Aaron and his sons based on all that I have commanded you
Exodus29:36 Sacrifice a bull as a sin offering each day for atonement. Purify the altar by making atonement for it
Exodus29:37 For seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and consecrate it. Then the altar will become most holy; whatever touches the altar will be holy.
Exodus29:38 This is what you are to offer regularly on the altar
Exodus29:39 Offer one lamb in the morning and the other at twilight.
Exodus29:40 With the first lamb offer a tenth of an ephah of fine flour
Exodus29:41 And offer the second lamb at twilight with the same grain offering and drink offering as in the morning
Exodus29:42 For the generations to come
Exodus29:43 I will also meet with the Israelites there
Exodus29:44 So I will consecrate the Tent of Meeting and the altar
Exodus29:45 Then I will dwell among the Israelites and be their God.
Exodus29:46 And they will know that I am the Lord their God
Exodus30:1 “You are also to make an altar of acacia wood for the burning of incense.
Exodus30:2 It is to be square
Exodus30:3 Overlay with pure gold the top and all the sides and horns
Exodus30:4 And make two gold rings below the molding on opposite sides to hold the poles used to carry it.
Exodus30:5 Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus30:6 Place the altar in front of the veil that is before the ark of the Testimony —before the mercy seat that is over the Testimony—where I will meet with you.
Exodus30:7 And Aaron is to burn fragrant incense on it every morning when he tends the lamps.
Exodus30:8 When Aaron sets up the lamps at twilight
Exodus30:9 On this altar you must not offer unauthorized incense or a burnt offering or grain offering; nor are you to pour a drink offering on it.
Exodus30:10 Once a year Aaron shall make atonement on the horns of the altar. Throughout your generations he shall make atonement on it annually with the blood of the sin offering of atonement. The altar is most holy to the Lord.”
Exodus30:11 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:12 “When you take a census of the Israelites to number them
Exodus30:13 Everyone who crosses over to those counted must pay a half shekel
Exodus30:14 Everyone twenty years of age or older who crosses over must give this offering to the Lord.
Exodus30:15 In making the offering to the Lord to atone for your lives
Exodus30:16 Take the atonement money from the Israelites and use it for the service of the Tent of Meeting. It will serve as a memorial for the Israelites before the Lord to make atonement for your lives.”
Exodus30:17 And the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:18 “You are to make a bronze basin with a bronze stand for washing. Set it between the Tent of Meeting and the altar
Exodus30:19 with which Aaron and his sons are to wash their hands and feet.
Exodus30:20 Whenever they enter the Tent of Meeting or approach the altar to minister by presenting an offering made by fire to the Lord
Exodus30:21 Thus they are to wash their hands and feet so that they will not die; this shall be a permanent statute for Aaron and his descendants for the generations to come.”
Exodus30:22 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:23 “Take the finest spices: 500 shekels of liquid myrrh
Exodus30:24 500 shekels of cassia —all according to the sanctuary shekel—and a hin of olive oil.
Exodus30:25 Prepare from these a sacred anointing oil
Exodus30:26 Use this oil to anoint the Tent of Meeting
Exodus30:27 the table and all its utensils
Exodus30:28 the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils
Exodus30:29 You are to consecrate them so that they will be most holy. Whatever touches them shall be holy.
Exodus30:30 Anoint Aaron and his sons and consecrate them to serve Me as priests.
Exodus30:31 And you are to tell the Israelites
Exodus30:32 It must not be used to anoint an ordinary man
Exodus30:33 Anyone who mixes perfume like it or puts it on an outsider shall be cut off from his people.’”
Exodus30:34 The Lord also said to Moses
Exodus30:35 and make a fragrant blend of incense
Exodus30:36 Grind some of it into fine powder and place it in front of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting
Exodus30:37 You are never to use this formula to make incense for yourselves; you shall regard it as holy to the Lord.
Exodus30:38 Anyone who makes something like it to enjoy its fragrance shall be cut off from his people.”
Exodus31:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus31:2 “See
Exodus31:3 And I have filled him with the Spirit of God
Exodus31:4 to design artistic works in gold
Exodus31:5 to cut gemstones for settings
Exodus31:6 Moreover
Exodus31:7 the Tent of Meeting
Exodus31:8 the table with its utensils
Exodus31:9 the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils
Exodus31:10 as well as the woven garments
Exodus31:11 in addition to the anointing oil and fragrant incense for the Holy Place. They are to make them according to all that I have commanded you.”
Exodus31:12 And the Lord said to Moses
Exodus31:13 “Tell the Israelites
Exodus31:14 Keep the Sabbath
Exodus31:15 For six days work may be done
Exodus31:16 The Israelites must keep the Sabbath
Exodus31:17 It is a sign between Me and the Israelites forever; for in six days the Lord made the heavens and the earth
Exodus31:18 When the Lord had finished speaking with Moses on Mount Sinai
Exodus32:1 Now when the people saw that Moses was delayed in coming down from the mountain
Exodus32:2 So Aaron told them
Exodus32:3 Then all the people took off their gold earrings and brought them to Aaron.
Exodus32:4 He took the gold from their hands
Exodus32:5 When Aaron saw this
Exodus32:6 So the next day they arose
Exodus32:7 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus32:8 How quickly they have turned aside from the way that I commanded them! They have made for themselves a molten calf and have bowed down to it. They have sacrificed to it and said
Exodus32:9 The Lord also said to Moses
Exodus32:10 Now leave Me alone
Exodus32:11 But Moses sought the favor of the Lord his God
Exodus32:12 Why should the Egyptians declare
Exodus32:13 Remember Your servants Abraham
Exodus32:14 So the Lord relented from the calamity He had threatened to bring on His people.
Exodus32:15 Then Moses turned and went down the mountain with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hands. They were inscribed on both sides
Exodus32:16 The tablets were the work of God
Exodus32:17 When Joshua heard the sound of the people shouting
Exodus32:18 But Moses replied: “It is neither the cry of victory nor the cry of defeat; I hear the sound of singing!”
Exodus32:19 As Moses approached the camp and saw the calf and the dancing
Exodus32:20 Then he took the calf they had made
Exodus32:21 “What did this people do to you
Exodus32:22 “Do not be enraged
Exodus32:23 They told me
Exodus32:24 So I said to them
Exodus32:25 Moses saw that the people were out of control
Exodus32:26 So Moses stood at the entrance to the camp and said
Exodus32:27 He told them
Exodus32:28 The Levites did as Moses commanded
Exodus32:29 Afterward
Exodus32:30 The next day Moses said to the people
Exodus32:31 So Moses returned to the Lord and said
Exodus32:32 Yet now
Exodus32:33 The Lord replied to Moses
Exodus32:34 Now go
Exodus32:35 And the Lord sent a plague on the people because of what they had done with the calf that Aaron had made.
Exodus33:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus33:2 And I will send an angel before you
Exodus33:3 Go up to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I will not go with you
Exodus33:4 When the people heard these bad tidings
Exodus33:5 For the Lord had said to Moses
Exodus33:6 So the Israelites stripped themselves of their jewelry from Mount Horeb onward.
Exodus33:7 Now Moses used to take the tent and pitch it at a distance outside the camp. He called it the Tent of Meeting
Exodus33:8 Then
Exodus33:9 As Moses entered the tent
Exodus33:10 When all the people saw the pillar of cloud standing at the entrance to the tent
Exodus33:11 Thus the Lord would speak to Moses face to face
Exodus33:12 Then Moses said to the Lord
Exodus33:13 Now if indeed I have found favor in Your sight
Exodus33:14 And the Lord answered
Exodus33:15 “If Your Presence does not go with us
Exodus33:16 For how then can it be known that Your people and I have found favor in Your sight
Exodus33:17 So the Lord said to Moses
Exodus33:18 Then Moses said
Exodus33:19 “I will cause all My goodness to pass before you
Exodus33:20 But He added
Exodus33:21 The Lord continued

Exodus33:23 Then I will take My hand away
Exodus34:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus34:2 Be ready in the morning
Exodus34:3 No one may go up with you; in fact
Exodus34:4 So Moses chiseled out two stone tablets like the originals. He rose early in the morning
Exodus34:5 And the Lord descended in a cloud
Exodus34:6 Then the Lord passed in front of Moses and called out: “The Lord
Exodus34:7 maintaining loving devotion to a thousand generations
Exodus34:8 Moses immediately bowed down to the ground and worshiped.
Exodus34:9 “O Lord
Exodus34:10 And the Lord said
Exodus34:11 Observe what I command you this day. I will drive out before you the Amorites
Exodus34:12 Be careful not to make a treaty with the inhabitants of the land you are entering
Exodus34:13 Rather
Exodus34:14 For you must not worship any other god
Exodus34:15 Do not make a covenant with the inhabitants of the land
Exodus34:16 And when you take some of their daughters as brides for your sons
Exodus34:17 You shall make no molten gods for yourselves.
Exodus34:18 You are to keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib
Exodus34:19 The first offspring of every womb belongs to Me
Exodus34:20 You must redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a lamb; but if you do not redeem it
Exodus34:21 Six days you shall labor
Exodus34:22 And you are to celebrate the Feast of Weeks with the firstfruits of the wheat harvest
Exodus34:23 Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord GOD
Exodus34:24 For I will drive out the nations before you and enlarge your borders
Exodus34:25 Do not offer the blood of a sacrifice to Me along with anything leavened
Exodus34:26 Bring the best of the firstfruits of your soil to the house of the Lord your God. You must not cook a young goat in its mother’s milk.”
Exodus34:27 The Lord also said to Moses
Exodus34:28 So Moses was there with the Lord forty days and forty nights without eating bread or drinking water. He wrote on the tablets the words of the covenant—the Ten Commandments.
Exodus34:29 And when Moses came down from Mount Sinai with the two tablets of the Testimony in his hands
Exodus34:30 Aaron and all the Israelites looked at Moses
Exodus34:31 But Moses called out to them; so Aaron and all the leaders of the congregation returned to him
Exodus34:32 And after this all the Israelites came near
Exodus34:33 When Moses had finished speaking with them
Exodus34:34 But whenever Moses went in before the Lord to speak with Him
Exodus34:35 and the Israelites would see that the face of Moses was radiant. So Moses would put the veil back over his face until he went in to speak with the Lord.
Exodus35:1 Then Moses assembled the whole congregation of Israel and said to them
Exodus35:2 For six days work may be done
Exodus35:3 Do not light a fire in any of your dwellings on the Sabbath day.”
Exodus35:4 Moses also told the whole congregation of Israel
Exodus35:5 Take from among you an offering to the Lord. Let everyone whose heart is willing bring an offering to the Lord: gold
Exodus35:6 blue
Exodus35:7 ram skins dyed red and fine leather; acacia wood;
Exodus35:8 olive oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;
Exodus35:9 and onyx stones and gemstones to be mounted on the ephod and breastpiece.
Exodus35:10 Let every skilled craftsman among you come and make everything that the Lord has commanded:
Exodus35:11 the tabernacle with its tent and covering
Exodus35:12 the ark with its poles and mercy seat
Exodus35:13 the table with its poles
Exodus35:14 the lampstand for light with its accessories and lamps and oil for the light;
Exodus35:15 the altar of incense with its poles; the anointing oil and fragrant incense; the curtain for the doorway at the entrance to the tabernacle;
Exodus35:16 the altar of burnt offering with its bronze grate
Exodus35:17 the curtains of the courtyard with its posts and bases
Exodus35:18 the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the courtyard
Exodus35:19 and the woven garments for ministering in the holy place—both the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments for his sons to serve as priests.”
Exodus35:20 Then the whole congregation of Israel withdrew from the presence of Moses.
Exodus35:21 And everyone whose heart stirred him and whose spirit prompted him came and brought an offering to the Lord for the work on the Tent of Meeting
Exodus35:22 So all who had willing hearts
Exodus35:23 Everyone who had blue
Exodus35:24 And all who could present an offering of silver or bronze brought it as a contribution to the Lord. Also
Exodus35:25 Every skilled woman spun with her hands and brought what she had spun: blue
Exodus35:26 And all the skilled women whose hearts were stirred spun the goat hair.
Exodus35:27 The leaders brought onyx stones and gemstones to mount on the ephod and breastpiece
Exodus35:28 as well as spices and olive oil for the light
Exodus35:29 So all the men and women of the Israelites whose hearts prompted them brought a freewill offering to the Lord for all the work that the Lord through Moses had commanded them to do.
Exodus35:30 Then Moses said to the Israelites
Exodus35:31 And He has filled him with the Spirit of God
Exodus35:32 to design artistic works in gold
Exodus35:33 to cut gemstones for settings
Exodus35:34 And the Lord has given both him and Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus35:35 He has filled them with skill to do all kinds of work as engravers
Exodus36:1 “So Bezalel
Exodus36:2 Then Moses summoned Bezalel
Exodus36:3 They received from Moses all the contributions that the Israelites had brought to carry out the service of constructing the sanctuary. Meanwhile
Exodus36:4 so that all the skilled craftsmen who were doing all the work on the sanctuary left their work
Exodus36:5 and said to Moses
Exodus36:6 After Moses had given an order
Exodus36:7 since what they already had was more than enough to perform all the work.
Exodus36:8 All the skilled craftsmen among the workmen made the ten curtains for the tabernacle. They were made of finely spun linen
Exodus36:9 Each curtain was twenty-eight cubits long and four cubits wide; all the curtains were the same size.
Exodus36:10 And he joined five of the curtains together
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue material on the edge of the end curtain in the first set

Exodus36:14 He then made curtains of goat hair for the tent over the tabernacle—eleven curtains in all.

Exodus36:18 He also made fifty bronze clasps to join the tent together as a unit.

Exodus36:23 He constructed twenty frames for the south side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:24 with forty silver bases to put under the twenty frames—two bases for each frame
Exodus36:25 For the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:26 and forty silver bases—two bases under each frame.
Exodus36:27 He made six frames for the rear of the tabernacle
Exodus36:28 and two frames for the two back corners of the tabernacle
Exodus36:29 coupled together from bottom to top and fitted into a single ring. He made both corners in this way.
Exodus36:30 So there were eight frames and sixteen silver bases—two under each frame.
Exodus36:31 He also made five crossbars of acacia wood for the frames on one side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:32 five for those on the other side
Exodus36:33 He made the central crossbar to run through the center of the frames
Exodus36:34 And he overlaid the frames with gold and made gold rings to hold the crossbars. He also overlaid the crossbars with gold.
Exodus36:35 Next
Exodus36:36 He also made four posts of acacia wood for it and overlaid them with gold
Exodus36:37 For the entrance to the tent
Exodus36:38 together with five posts and their hooks. He overlaid the tops of the posts and their bands with gold
Exodus37:1 Bezalel went on to construct the ark of acacia wood
Exodus37:2 He overlaid it with pure gold
Exodus37:3 And he cast four gold rings for its four feet
Exodus37:4 Then he made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:5 He inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark in order to carry it.
Exodus37:6 He constructed a mercy seat of pure gold
Exodus37:7 He made two cherubim of hammered gold at the ends of the mercy seat
Exodus37:8 one cherub on one end and one on the other
Exodus37:9 And the cherubim had wings that spread upward
Exodus37:10 He also made the table of acacia wood two cubits long
Exodus37:11 He overlaid it with pure gold and made a gold molding around it.
Exodus37:12 And he made a rim around it a handbreadth wide and put a gold molding on the rim.
Exodus37:13 He cast four gold rings for the table and fastened them to the four corners at its four legs.
Exodus37:14 The rings were placed close to the rim
Exodus37:15 He made the poles of acacia wood for carrying the table and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:16 He also made the utensils for the table out of pure gold: its plates and dishes
Exodus37:17 Then he made the lampstand out of pure hammered gold
Exodus37:18 Six branches extended from the sides
Exodus37:19 There were three cups shaped like almond blossoms on the first branch
Exodus37:20 And on the lampstand were four cups shaped like almond blossoms with buds and petals.
Exodus37:21 A bud was under the first pair of branches that extended from the lampstand
Exodus37:22 The buds and branches were all of one piece with the lampstand
Exodus37:23 He also made its seven lamps
Exodus37:24 He made the lampstand and all its utensils from a talent of pure gold.
Exodus37:25 He made the altar of incense out of acacia wood. It was square
Exodus37:26 And he overlaid with pure gold the top and all the sides and horns. Then he made a molding of gold around it.
Exodus37:27 He made two gold rings below the molding on opposite sides to hold the poles used to carry it.
Exodus37:28 And he made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:29 He also made the sacred anointing oil and the pure
Exodus38:1 Bezalel constructed the altar of burnt offering from acacia wood. It was square
Exodus38:2 He made a horn at each of its four corners
Exodus38:3 He made all the altar’s utensils of bronze—its pots
Exodus38:4 He made a grate of bronze mesh for the altar under its ledge
Exodus38:5 At the four corners of the bronze grate he cast four rings as holders for the poles.
Exodus38:6 And he made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
Exodus38:7 Then he inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar for carrying it. He made the altar with boards so that it was hollow.
Exodus38:8 Next he made the bronze basin and its stand from the mirrors of the women who served at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
Exodus38:9 Then he constructed the courtyard. The south side of the courtyard was a hundred cubits long and had curtains of finely spun linen
Exodus38:10 with twenty posts and twenty bronze bases
Exodus38:11 The north side was also a hundred cubits long
Exodus38:12 The west side was fifty cubits long and had curtains
Exodus38:13 And the east side
Exodus38:14 The curtains on one side of the entrance were fifteen cubits long
Exodus38:15 And the curtains on the other side were also fifteen cubits long
Exodus38:16 All the curtains around the courtyard were made of finely spun linen.
Exodus38:17 The bases for the posts were bronze
Exodus38:18 The curtain for the entrance to the courtyard was embroidered with blue
Exodus38:19 with four posts and four bronze bases. Their hooks were silver
Exodus38:20 All the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the surrounding courtyard were bronze.
Exodus38:21 This is the inventory for the tabernacle
Exodus38:22 Bezalel son of Uri
Exodus38:23 With him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus38:24 All the gold from the wave offering used for the work on the sanctuary totaled 29 talents and 730 shekels
Exodus38:25 The silver from those numbered among the congregation totaled 100 talents and 1
Exodus38:26 a beka per person
Exodus38:27 The hundred talents of silver were used to cast the bases of the sanctuary and the bases of the veil—100 bases from the 100 talents
Exodus38:28 With the 1
Exodus38:29 The bronze from the wave offering totaled 70 talents and 2
Exodus38:30 He used it to make the bases for the entrance to the Tent of Meeting
Exodus38:31 the bases for the surrounding courtyard and its gate
Exodus39:1 From the blue
Exodus39:2 Bezalel made the ephod of finely spun linen embroidered with gold
Exodus39:3 They hammered out thin sheets of gold and cut threads from them to interweave with the blue
Exodus39:4 They made shoulder pieces for the ephod
Exodus39:5 And the skillfully woven waistband of the ephod was of one piece with the ephod
Exodus39:6 They mounted the onyx stones in gold filigree settings
Exodus39:7 Then they fastened them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the sons of Israel
Exodus39:8 He made the breastpiece with the same workmanship as the ephod
Exodus39:9 It was square when folded over double
Exodus39:10 And they mounted on it four rows of gemstones: The first row had a ruby
Exodus39:11 the second row had a turquoise
Exodus39:12 the third row had a jacinth
Exodus39:13 and the fourth row had a beryl
Exodus39:14 The twelve stones corresponded to the names of the sons of Israel. Each stone was engraved like a seal with the name of one of the twelve tribes.
Exodus39:15 For the breastpiece they made braided chains like cords of pure gold.
Exodus39:16 They also made two gold filigree settings and two gold rings
Exodus39:17 Then they fastened the two gold chains to the two gold rings at the corners of the breastpiece
Exodus39:18 and they fastened the other ends of the two chains to the two filigree settings
Exodus39:19 They made two more gold rings and attached them to the other two corners of the breastpiece
Exodus39:20 They made two additional gold rings and attached them to the bottom of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod
Exodus39:21 Then they tied the rings of the breastpiece to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue yarn
Exodus39:22 They made the robe of the ephod entirely of blue cloth
Exodus39:23 with an opening in the center of the robe like that of a garment
Exodus39:24 They made pomegranates of blue
Exodus39:25 They also made bells of pure gold and attached them around the hem between the pomegranates
Exodus39:26 alternating the bells and pomegranates around the lower hem of the robe to be worn for ministry
Exodus39:27 For Aaron and his sons they made tunics of fine linen
Exodus39:28 as well as the turban of fine linen
Exodus39:29 and the sash of finely spun linen
Exodus39:30 They also made the plate of the holy crown of pure gold
Exodus39:31 Then they fastened to it a blue cord to mount it on the turban
Exodus39:32 So all the work for the tabernacle
Exodus39:33 Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses: the tent with all its furnishings
Exodus39:34 the covering of ram skins dyed red
Exodus39:35 the ark of the Testimony with its poles and the mercy seat;
Exodus39:36 the table with all its utensils and the Bread of the Presence;
Exodus39:37 the pure gold lampstand with its row of lamps and all its utensils
Exodus39:38 the gold altar
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar with its bronze grating
Exodus39:40 the curtains of the courtyard with its posts and bases; the curtain for the gate of the courtyard
Exodus39:41 and the woven garments for ministering in the sanctuary
Exodus39:42 The Israelites had done all the work just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:43 And Moses inspected all the work and saw that they had accomplished it just as the Lord had commanded. So Moses blessed them.
Exodus40:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus40:2 “On the first day of the first month you are to set up the tabernacle
Exodus40:3 Put the ark of the Testimony in it and screen off the ark with the veil.
Exodus40:4 Then bring in the table and set out its arrangement; bring in the lampstand as well
Exodus40:5 Place the gold altar of incense in front of the ark of the Testimony
Exodus40:6 Place the altar of burnt offering in front of the entrance to the tabernacle
Exodus40:7 And place the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 Set up the surrounding courtyard and hang the curtain for the entrance to the courtyard.
Exodus40:9 Take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and everything in it; consecrate it along with all its furnishings
Exodus40:10 Anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils; consecrate the altar
Exodus40:11 Anoint the basin and its stand and consecrate them.
Exodus40:12 Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 And you are to clothe Aaron with the holy garments
Exodus40:14 Bring his sons forward and clothe them with tunics.
Exodus40:15 Anoint them just as you anointed their father
Exodus40:16 Moses did everything just as the Lord had commanded him.
Exodus40:17 So the tabernacle was set up on the first day of the first month of the second year.
Exodus40:18 When Moses set up the tabernacle
Exodus40:19 Then he spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering over the tent
Exodus40:20 Moses took the Testimony and placed it in the ark
Exodus40:21 Then he brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 Moses placed the table in the Tent of Meeting on the north side of the tabernacle
Exodus40:23 He arranged the bread on it before the Lord
Exodus40:24 He also placed the lampstand in the Tent of Meeting opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle
Exodus40:25 and set up the lamps before the Lord
Exodus40:26 Moses placed the gold altar in the Tent of Meeting
Exodus40:27 and he burned fragrant incense on it
Exodus40:28 Then he put up the curtain at the entrance to the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 He placed the altar of burnt offering near the entrance to the tabernacle
Exodus40:30 He placed the basin between the Tent of Meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing;
Exodus40:31 and from it Moses
Exodus40:32 They washed whenever they entered the Tent of Meeting or approached the altar
Exodus40:33 And Moses set up the courtyard around the tabernacle and the altar
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the Tent of Meeting
Exodus40:35 Moses was unable to enter the Tent of Meeting because the cloud had settled on it
Exodus40:36 Whenever the cloud was lifted from above the tabernacle
Exodus40:37 If the cloud was not lifted
Exodus40:38 For the cloud of the Lord was over the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 Then the Lord called to Moses and spoke to him from the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus1:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When any of you brings an offering to the Lord
Leviticus1:3 If one’s offering is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 He is to lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering
Leviticus1:5 And he shall slaughter the young bull before the Lord
Leviticus1:6 Next
Leviticus1:7 The sons of Aaron the priest shall put a fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 Then Aaron’s sons the priests are to arrange the pieces
Leviticus1:9 The entrails and legs must be washed with water
Leviticus1:10 If
Leviticus1:11 He shall slaughter it on the north side of the altar before the Lord
Leviticus1:12 He is to cut the animal into pieces
Leviticus1:13 The entrails and legs must be washed with water
Leviticus1:14 If
Leviticus1:15 Then the priest shall bring it to the altar
Leviticus1:16 And he is to remove the crop with its contents and throw it to the east side of the altar
Leviticus1:17 He shall tear it open by its wings
Leviticus2:1 “When anyone brings a grain offering to the Lord
Leviticus2:2 and bring it to Aaron’s sons the priests. The priest shall take a handful of the flour and oil
Leviticus2:3 The remainder of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the offerings made by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus2:4 Now if you bring an offering of grain baked in an oven
Leviticus2:5 If your offering is a grain offering prepared on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 Crumble it and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If your offering is a grain offering cooked in a pan
Leviticus2:8 When you bring to the Lord the grain offering made in any of these ways
Leviticus2:9 The priest is to remove the memorial portion from the grain offering and burn it on the altar as an offering made by fire
Leviticus2:10 But the remainder of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the offerings made by fire to the Lord.
Leviticus2:11 No grain offering that you present to the Lord may be made with leaven
Leviticus2:12 You may bring them to the Lord as an offering of firstfruits
Leviticus2:13 And you shall season each of your grain offerings with salt. You must not leave the salt of the covenant of your God out of your grain offering; you are to add salt to each of your offerings.
Leviticus2:14 If you bring a grain offering of firstfruits to the Lord
Leviticus2:15 And you are to put oil and frankincense on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 The priest shall then burn the memorial portion of the crushed grain and the oil
Leviticus3:1 “If one’s offering is a peace offering and he offers an animal from the herd
Leviticus3:2 He is to lay his hand on the head of the offering and slaughter it at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron’s sons the priests shall sprinkle the blood on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:3 From the peace offering he is to bring an offering made by fire to the Lord: the fat that covers the entrails
Leviticus3:4 both kidneys with the fat on them near the loins
Leviticus3:5 Then Aaron’s sons are to burn it on the altar atop the burnt offering that is on the burning wood
Leviticus3:6 If
Leviticus3:7 If he is presenting a lamb for his offering
Leviticus3:8 He is to lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it in front of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron’s sons shall sprinkle its blood on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:9 And from the peace offering he shall bring an offering made by fire to the Lord consisting of its fat: the entire fat tail cut off close to the backbone
Leviticus3:10 both kidneys with the fat on them near the loins
Leviticus3:11 Then the priest is to burn them on the altar as food
Leviticus3:12 If one’s offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 He must lay his hand on its head and slaughter it in front of the Tent of Meeting. Then Aaron’s sons shall sprinkle its blood on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:14 And from his offering he shall present an offering made by fire to the Lord: the fat that covers the entrails
Leviticus3:15 both kidneys with the fat on them near the loins
Leviticus3:16 Then the priest is to burn the food on the altar as an offering made by fire
Leviticus3:17 This is a permanent statute for the generations to come
Leviticus4:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus4:2 “Tell the Israelites to do as follows with one who sins unintentionally against any of the Lord’s commandments and does what is forbidden by them:
Leviticus4:3 If the anointed priest sins
Leviticus4:4 He must bring the bull to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting before the Lord
Leviticus4:5 Then the anointed priest shall take some of the bull’s blood and bring it into the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus4:6 The priest is to dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of it seven times before the Lord
Leviticus4:7 The priest must then put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense that is before the Lord in the Tent of Meeting. And he is to pour out the rest of the bull’s blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus4:8 Then he shall remove all the fat from the bull of the sin offering—the fat that covers the entrails
Leviticus4:9 both kidneys with the fat on them near the loins
Leviticus4:10 just as the fat is removed from the ox of the peace offering. Then the priest shall burn them on the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:11 But the hide of the bull and all its flesh
Leviticus4:12 all the rest of the bull—he must take outside the camp to a ceremonially clean place where the ashes are poured out
Leviticus4:13 Now if the whole congregation of Israel strays unintentionally and the matter escapes the notice of the assembly so that they violate any of the Lord’s commandments and incur guilt by doing what is forbidden
Leviticus4:14 when they become aware of the sin they have committed
Leviticus4:15 The elders of the congregation are to lay their hands on the bull’s head before the Lord
Leviticus4:16 Then the anointed priest is to bring some of the bull’s blood into the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus4:17 and he is to dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord in front of the veil.
Leviticus4:18 He is also to put some of the blood on the horns of the altar that is before the Lord in the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus4:19 And he is to remove all the fat from it and burn it on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He shall offer this bull just as he did the bull for the sin offering; in this way the priest will make atonement on their behalf
Leviticus4:21 Then he is to take the bull outside the camp and burn it
Leviticus4:22 When a leader sins unintentionally and does what is prohibited by any of the commandments of the Lord his God
Leviticus4:23 When he becomes aware of the sin he has committed
Leviticus4:24 He is to lay his hand on the head of the goat and slaughter it at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered before the Lord. It is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest is to take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger
Leviticus4:26 He must burn all its fat on the altar
Leviticus4:27 And if one of the common people sins unintentionally and does what is prohibited by any of the Lord’s commandments
Leviticus4:28 When he becomes aware of the sin he has committed
Leviticus4:29 He is to lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it at the place of the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 Then the priest is to take some of its blood with his finger
Leviticus4:31 Then he is to remove all the fat
Leviticus4:32 If
Leviticus4:33 And he is to lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it as a sin offering at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
Leviticus4:34 Then the priest is to take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger
Leviticus4:35 And he shall remove all the fat
Leviticus5:1 “If someone sins by failing to testify when he hears a public charge about something he has witnessed
Leviticus5:2 Or if a person touches anything unclean—whether the carcass of any unclean wild animal or livestock or crawling creature—even if he is unaware of it
Leviticus5:3 Or if he touches human uncleanness—anything by which one becomes unclean—even if he is unaware of it
Leviticus5:4 Or if someone swears thoughtlessly with his lips to do anything good or evil—in whatever matter a man may rashly pronounce an oath—even if he is unaware of it
Leviticus5:5 If someone incurs guilt in one of these ways
Leviticus5:6 and he must bring his guilt offering to the Lord for the sin he has committed: a female lamb or goat from the flock as a sin offering. And the priest will make atonement for him concerning his sin.
Leviticus5:7 If
Leviticus5:8 He is to bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 then he is to sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 And the priest must prepare the second bird as a burnt offering according to the ordinance. In this way the priest will make atonement for him for the sin he has committed
Leviticus5:11 But if he cannot afford two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 He is to bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 In this way the priest will make atonement for him for any of these sins he has committed
Leviticus5:14 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus5:15 “If someone acts unfaithfully and sins unintentionally against any of the Lord’s holy things
Leviticus5:16 Regarding any holy thing he has harmed
Leviticus5:17 If someone sins and violates any of the Lord’s commandments even though he was unaware
Leviticus5:18 He is to bring to the priest an unblemished ram of proper value from the flock as a guilt offering. Then the priest will make atonement on his behalf for the wrong he has committed in ignorance
Leviticus5:19 It is a guilt offering; he was certainly guilty before the Lord.”
Leviticus6:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:2 “If someone sins and acts unfaithfully against the Lord by deceiving his neighbor in regard to a deposit or security entrusted to him or stolen
Leviticus6:3 or finds lost property and lies about it and swears falsely
Leviticus6:4 once he has sinned and becomes guilty
Leviticus6:5 or anything else about which he has sworn falsely. He must make restitution in full
Leviticus6:6 Then he must bring to the priest his guilt offering to the Lord: an unblemished ram of proper value from the flock.
Leviticus6:7 In this way the priest will make atonement for him before the Lord
Leviticus6:8 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:9 “Command Aaron and his sons that this is the law of the burnt offering: The burnt offering is to remain on the hearth of the altar all night
Leviticus6:10 And the priest shall put on his linen robe and linen undergarments
Leviticus6:11 Then he must take off his garments
Leviticus6:12 The fire on the altar shall be kept burning; it must not be extinguished. Every morning the priest is to add wood to the fire
Leviticus6:13 The fire shall be kept burning on the altar continually; it must not be extinguished.
Leviticus6:14 Now this is the law of the grain offering: Aaron’s sons shall present it before the Lord in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 The priest is to remove a handful of fine flour and olive oil
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons are to eat the remainder. It must be eaten without leaven in a holy place; they are to eat it in the courtyard of the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus6:17 It must not be baked with leaven; I have assigned it as their portion of My offerings made by fire. It is most holy
Leviticus6:18 Any male among the sons of Aaron may eat it. This is a permanent portion from the offerings made by fire to the Lord for the generations to come. Anything that touches them will become holy.”
Leviticus6:19 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:20 “This is the offering that Aaron and his sons must present to the Lord on the day he is anointed: a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a regular grain offering
Leviticus6:21 It shall be prepared with oil on a griddle; you are to bring it well-kneaded and present it as a grain offering broken in pieces
Leviticus6:22 The priest
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering for a priest shall be burned completely; it is not to be eaten.”
Leviticus6:24 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:25 “Tell Aaron and his sons that this is the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it shall eat it; it must be eaten in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Anything that touches its flesh will become holy
Leviticus6:28 The clay pot in which the sin offering is boiled must be broken; if it is boiled in a bronze pot
Leviticus6:29 Any male among the priests may eat it; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering may be eaten if its blood has been brought into the Tent of Meeting to make atonement in the Holy Place; it must be burned.
Leviticus7:1 “Now this is the law of the guilt offering
Leviticus7:2 The guilt offering must be slaughtered in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered
Leviticus7:3 And all the fat from it shall be offered: the fat tail
Leviticus7:4 both kidneys with the fat on them near the loins
Leviticus7:5 The priest shall burn them on the altar as an offering made by fire to the Lord; it is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 Every male among the priests may eat of it. It must be eaten in a holy place; it is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 The guilt offering is like the sin offering; the same law applies to both. It belongs to the priest who makes atonement with it.
Leviticus7:8 As for the priest who presents a burnt offering for anyone
Leviticus7:9 Likewise
Leviticus7:10 and every grain offering
Leviticus7:11 Now this is the law of the peace offering that one may present to the Lord:
Leviticus7:12 If he offers it in thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 Along with his peace offering of thanksgiving he is to present an offering with cakes of leavened bread.
Leviticus7:14 From the cakes he must present one portion of each offering as a contribution to the Lord. It belongs to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the peace offering.
Leviticus7:15 The meat of the sacrifice of his peace offering of thanksgiving must be eaten on the day he offers it; none of it may be left until morning.
Leviticus7:16 If
Leviticus7:17 But any meat of the sacrifice remaining until the third day must be burned up.
Leviticus7:18 If any of the meat from his peace offering is eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 Meat that touches anything unclean must not be eaten; it is to be burned up. As for any other meat
Leviticus7:20 But if anyone who is unclean eats meat from the peace offering that belongs to the Lord
Leviticus7:21 If one touches anything unclean
Leviticus7:22 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:23 “Speak to the Israelites and say
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal found dead or mauled by wild beasts may be used for any other purpose
Leviticus7:25 If anyone eats the fat of an animal from which an offering made by fire may be presented to the Lord
Leviticus7:26 You must not eat the blood of any bird or animal in any of your dwellings.
Leviticus7:27 If anyone eats blood
Leviticus7:28 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:29 “Speak to the Israelites and say
Leviticus7:30 With his own hands he is to bring the offerings made by fire to the Lord; he shall bring the fat
Leviticus7:31 The priest is to burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 And you are to give the right thigh to the priest as a contribution from your peace offering.
Leviticus7:33 The son of Aaron who presents the blood and fat of the peace offering shall have the right thigh as a portion.
Leviticus7:34 I have taken from the sons of Israel the breast of the wave offering and the thigh of the contribution of their peace offerings
Leviticus7:35 This is the portion of the offerings made by fire to the Lord for Aaron and his sons since the day they were presented to serve the Lord as priests.
Leviticus7:36 On the day they were anointed
Leviticus7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which the Lord gave Moses on Mount Sinai on the day He commanded the Israelites to present their offerings to the Lord in the Wilderness of Sinai.
Leviticus8:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus8:2 “Take Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:3 and assemble the whole congregation at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.”
Leviticus8:4 So Moses did as the Lord had commanded him
Leviticus8:5 And Moses said to them
Leviticus8:6 Then Moses presented Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 He put the tunic on Aaron
Leviticus8:8 Then he put the breastpiece on him and placed the Urim and Thummim in the breastpiece.
Leviticus8:9 Moses also put the turban on Aaron’s head and set the gold plate
Leviticus8:10 Next
Leviticus8:11 He sprinkled some of the oil on the altar seven times
Leviticus8:12 He also poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him to consecrate him.
Leviticus8:13 Then Moses presented Aaron’s sons
Leviticus8:14 Moses then brought the bull near for the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 Moses slaughtered the bull
Leviticus8:16 Moses also took all the fat that was on the entrails
Leviticus8:17 But the bull with its hide
Leviticus8:18 Then Moses presented the ram for the burnt offering
Leviticus8:19 Moses slaughtered the ram and sprinkled the blood on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus8:20 He cut the ram into pieces and burned the head
Leviticus8:21 He washed the entrails and legs with water and burned the entire ram on the altar as a burnt offering
Leviticus8:22 After that
Leviticus8:23 Moses slaughtered the ram and took some of its blood and put it on Aaron’s right earlobe
Leviticus8:24 Moses also presented Aaron’s sons and put some of the blood on their right earlobes
Leviticus8:25 And Moses took the fat—the fat tail
Leviticus8:26 And from the basket of unleavened bread that was before the Lord
Leviticus8:27 He put all these in the hands of Aaron and his sons and waved them before the Lord as a wave offering.
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took these from their hands and burned them on the altar with the burnt offering. This was an ordination offering
Leviticus8:29 He also took the breast—Moses’ portion of the ram of ordination—and waved it before the Lord as a wave offering
Leviticus8:30 Next
Leviticus8:31 And Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 Then you must burn up the remainder of the meat and bread.
Leviticus8:33 You must not go outside the entrance to the Tent of Meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 What has been done today has been commanded by the Lord in order to make atonement on your behalf.
Leviticus8:35 You must remain at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting day and night for seven days and keep the Lord’s charge so that you will not die
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything the Lord had commanded through Moses.

Leviticus9:2 He said to Aaron

Leviticus9:4 an ox and a ram for a peace offering to sacrifice before the Lord
Leviticus9:5 So they took what Moses had commanded to the front of the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus9:6 And Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Then Moses said to Aaron

Leviticus9:10 On the altar he burned the fat
Leviticus9:11 But he burned up the flesh and the hide outside the camp.
Leviticus9:12 Then Aaron slaughtered the burnt offering. His sons brought him the blood
Leviticus9:13 They brought him the burnt offering piece by piece
Leviticus9:14 He washed the entrails and the legs and burned them atop the burnt offering on the altar.

Leviticus9:16 He presented the burnt offering and offered it according to the ordinance.
Leviticus9:17 Next he presented the grain offering
Leviticus9:18 Then he slaughtered the ox and the ram as the people’s peace offering. His sons brought him the blood
Leviticus9:19 They also brought the fat portions from the ox and the ram—the fat tail
Leviticus9:20 and placed these on the breasts. Aaron burned the fat portions on the altar
Leviticus9:21 but he waved the breasts and the right thigh as a wave offering before the Lord
Leviticus9:22 Aaron lifted up his hands toward the people and blessed them. And having made the sin offering
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron then entered the Tent of Meeting. When they came out

Leviticus11:1 The Lord spoke again to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 “Say to the Israelites
Leviticus11:3 You may eat any animal that has a split hoof completely divided and that chews the cud.
Leviticus11:4 But of those that only chew the cud or only have a divided hoof
Leviticus11:5 The rock badger
Leviticus11:6 The rabbit
Leviticus11:7 And the pig
Leviticus11:8 You must not eat their meat or touch their carcasses; they are unclean for you.
Leviticus11:9 Of all the creatures that live in the water
Leviticus11:10 But the following among all the teeming life and creatures in the water are detestable to you: everything in the seas or streams that does not have fins and scales.
Leviticus11:11 They shall be an abomination to you; you must not eat their meat
Leviticus11:12 Everything in the water that does not have fins and scales shall be detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 Additionally
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 any kind of raven
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the little owl
Leviticus11:18 the white owl
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 All flying insects that walk on all fours are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:21 However
Leviticus11:22 Of these you may eat any kind of locust
Leviticus11:23 All other flying insects that have four legs are detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 These creatures will make you unclean. Whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean until evening
Leviticus11:25 and whoever picks up one of their carcasses must wash his clothes
Leviticus11:26 Every animal with hooves not completely divided or that does not chew the cud is unclean for you. Whoever touches any of them will be unclean.
Leviticus11:27 All the four-footed animals that walk on their paws are unclean for you; whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean until evening
Leviticus11:28 and anyone who picks up a carcass must wash his clothes
Leviticus11:29 The following creatures that move along the ground are unclean for you: the mole
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 These animals are unclean for you among all the crawling creatures. Whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:32 When one of them dies and falls on something
Leviticus11:33 If any of them falls into a clay pot
Leviticus11:34 Any food coming into contact with water from that pot will be unclean
Leviticus11:35 Anything upon which one of their carcasses falls will be unclean. If it is an oven or cooking pot
Leviticus11:36 Nevertheless
Leviticus11:37 If a carcass falls on any seed for sowing
Leviticus11:38 but if water has been put on the seed and a carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 If an animal that you may eat dies
Leviticus11:40 Whoever eats from the carcass must wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening
Leviticus11:41 Every creature that moves along the ground is detestable; it must not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Do not eat any creature that moves along the ground
Leviticus11:43 Do not defile yourselves by any crawling creature; do not become unclean or defiled by them.
Leviticus11:44 For I am the Lord your God; consecrate yourselves
Leviticus11:45 For I am the Lord
Leviticus11:46 This is the law regarding animals
Leviticus11:47 You must distinguish between the unclean and the clean
