Leviticus13:1 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 “When someone has a swelling or rash or bright spot on his skin that could become an infectious skin disease
Leviticus13:3 The priest is to examine the infection on his skin
Leviticus13:4 If
Leviticus13:5 On the seventh day the priest is to reexamine him
Leviticus13:6 The priest will examine him again on the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But if the rash spreads further on his skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing
Leviticus13:8 The priest will reexamine him
Leviticus13:9 When anyone develops a skin disease
Leviticus13:10 The priest will examine him
Leviticus13:11 it is a chronic skin disease and the priest must pronounce him unclean. He need not isolate him
Leviticus13:12 But if the skin disease breaks out all over his skin so that it covers all the skin of the infected person from head to foot
Leviticus13:13 the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:14 But whenever raw flesh appears on someone
Leviticus13:15 When the priest sees the raw flesh
Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh changes and turns white
Leviticus13:17 The priest will reexamine him
Leviticus13:18 When a boil appears on someone’s skin and it heals
Leviticus13:19 and a white swelling or a reddish-white spot develops where the boil was
Leviticus13:20 The priest shall examine it
Leviticus13:21 But when the priest examines it
Leviticus13:22 If it spreads any further on the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the spot remains unchanged and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 When there is a burn on someone’s skin and the raw area of the burn becomes reddish-white or white
Leviticus13:25 the priest must examine it. If the hair in the spot has turned white and the spot appears to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest examines it and there is no white hair in the spot
Leviticus13:27 On the seventh day the priest is to reexamine him
Leviticus13:28 But if the spot is unchanged and has not spread on the skin but has faded
Leviticus13:29 If a man or woman has an infection on the head or chin
Leviticus13:30 the priest shall examine the infection
Leviticus13:31 But if the priest examines the scaly infection and it does not appear to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:32 On the seventh day the priest is to reexamine the infection
Leviticus13:33 then the person must shave himself except for the scaly area. Then the priest shall isolate him for another seven days.
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day the priest shall examine the scaly outbreak
Leviticus13:35 If
Leviticus13:36 the priest is to examine him
Leviticus13:37 If
Leviticus13:38 When a man or a woman has white spots on the skin
Leviticus13:39 the priest shall examine them
Leviticus13:40 Now if a man loses his hair and is bald
Leviticus13:41 Or if his hairline recedes and he is bald on his forehead
Leviticus13:42 But if there is a reddish-white sore on the bald head or forehead
Leviticus13:43 The priest is to examine him
Leviticus13:44 the man is diseased; he is unclean. The priest must pronounce him unclean because of the infection on his head.
Leviticus13:45 A diseased person must wear torn clothes and let his hair hang loose
Leviticus13:46 As long as he has the infection
Leviticus13:47 If any fabric is contaminated with mildew —any wool or linen garment
Leviticus13:48 any weave or knit of linen or wool
Leviticus13:49 and if the mark in the fabric
Leviticus13:50 And the priest is to examine the mildew and isolate the contaminated fabric for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 On the seventh day the priest shall reexamine it
Leviticus13:52 He is to burn the fabric
Leviticus13:53 But when the priest reexamines it
Leviticus13:54 the priest is to order the contaminated article to be washed and isolated for another seven days.
Leviticus13:55 After it has been washed
Leviticus13:56 If the priest examines it and the mildew has faded after it has been washed
Leviticus13:57 But if it reappears in the fabric
Leviticus13:58 If the mildew disappears from the fabric
Leviticus13:59 This is the law concerning a mildew contamination in wool or linen fabric
Leviticus14:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus14:2 “This is the law for the one afflicted with a skin disease on the day of his cleansing
Leviticus14:3 The priest is to go outside the camp to examine him
Leviticus14:4 the priest shall order that two live clean birds
Leviticus14:5 Then the priest shall command that one of the birds be slaughtered over fresh water in a clay pot.
Leviticus14:6 And he is to take the live bird together with the cedar wood
Leviticus14:7 Seven times he shall sprinkle the one to be cleansed of the skin disease. Then he shall pronounce him clean and release the live bird into the open field.
Leviticus14:8 The one being cleansed must wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 On the seventh day he must shave off all his hair—his head
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day he is to bring two unblemished male lambs
Leviticus14:11 The priest who performs the cleansing shall present the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:12 Then the priest is to take one of the male lambs and present it as a guilt offering
Leviticus14:13 Then he is to slaughter the lamb in the sanctuary area where the sin offering and burnt offering are slaughtered. Like the sin offering
Leviticus14:14 The priest is to take some of the blood from the guilt offering and put it on the right earlobe of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:15 Then the priest shall take some of the log of olive oil
Leviticus14:16 dip his right forefinger into the oil in his left palm
Leviticus14:17 And the priest is to put some of the oil remaining in his palm on the right earlobe of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:18 The rest of the oil in his palm
Leviticus14:19 Then the priest is to sacrifice the sin offering and make atonement for the one to be cleansed from his uncleanness. After that
Leviticus14:20 and offer it on the altar
Leviticus14:21 If
Leviticus14:22 and two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus14:23 On the eighth day he is to bring them for his cleansing to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting before the Lord.
Leviticus14:24 The priest shall take the lamb for the guilt offering
Leviticus14:25 And after he slaughters the lamb for the guilt offering
Leviticus14:26 Then the priest is to pour some of the oil into his left palm
Leviticus14:27 and sprinkle with his right forefinger some of the oil in his left palm seven times before the Lord.
Leviticus14:28 The priest shall also put some of the oil in his palm on the right earlobe of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:29 The rest of the oil in his palm
Leviticus14:30 Then he must sacrifice the turtledoves or young pigeons
Leviticus14:31 one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for someone who has a skin disease and cannot afford the cost of his cleansing.”
Leviticus14:33 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 “When you enter the land of Canaan
Leviticus14:35 the owner of the house shall come and tell the priest
Leviticus14:36 The priest must order that the house be cleared before he enters it to examine the mildew
Leviticus14:37 He is to examine the house
Leviticus14:38 the priest shall go outside the doorway of the house and close it up for seven days.
Leviticus14:39 On the seventh day the priest is to return and inspect the house. If the mildew has spread on the walls
Leviticus14:40 he must order that the contaminated stones be pulled out and thrown into an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 And he shall have the inside of the house scraped completely and the plaster that is scraped off dumped into an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:42 So different stones must be obtained to replace the contaminated ones
Leviticus14:43 If the mildew reappears in the house after the stones have been torn out and the house has been scraped and replastered
Leviticus14:44 the priest must come and inspect it. If the mildew has spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 It must be torn down with its stones
Leviticus14:46 Anyone who enters the house during any of the days that it is closed up will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 And anyone who sleeps in the house or eats in it must wash his clothes.
Leviticus14:48 If
Leviticus14:49 He is to take two birds
Leviticus14:50 and he shall slaughter one of the birds over fresh water in a clay pot.
Leviticus14:51 Then he shall take the cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 And he shall cleanse the house with the bird’s blood
Leviticus14:53 Finally
Leviticus14:54 This is the law for any infectious skin disease
Leviticus14:55 for mildew in clothing or in a house
Leviticus14:56 and for a swelling
Leviticus14:57 to determine when something is clean or unclean. This is the law regarding skin diseases and mildew. ”
Leviticus15:1 And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 “Say to the Israelites
Leviticus15:3 This uncleanness is from his discharge
Leviticus15:4 Any bed on which the man with the discharge lies will be unclean
Leviticus15:5 Anyone who touches his bed must wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:6 Whoever sits on furniture on which the man with the discharge was sitting must wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:7 Whoever touches the body of the man with a discharge must wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:8 If the man with the discharge spits on one who is clean
Leviticus15:9 Any saddle on which the man with the discharge rides will be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Whoever touches anything that was under him will be unclean until evening
Leviticus15:11 If the man with the discharge touches anyone without first rinsing his hands with water
Leviticus15:12 Any clay pot that the man with the discharge touches must be broken
Leviticus15:13 When the man has been cleansed from his discharge
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day he is to take two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus15:15 The priest is to sacrifice them
Leviticus15:16 When a man has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:17 Any clothing or leather on which there is an emission of semen must be washed with water
Leviticus15:18 If a man lies with a woman and there is an emission of semen
Leviticus15:19 When a woman has a discharge consisting of blood from her body
Leviticus15:20 Anything on which she lies or sits during her menstruation will be unclean
Leviticus15:21 and anyone who touches her bed must wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:22 Whoever touches any furniture on which she was sitting must wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:23 And whether it is a bed or furniture on which she was sitting
Leviticus15:24 If a man lies with her and her menstrual flow touches him
Leviticus15:25 When a woman has a discharge of her blood for many days at a time other than her menstrual period
Leviticus15:26 Any bed on which she lies or any furniture on which she sits during the days of her discharge will be unclean
Leviticus15:27 Anyone who touches these things will be unclean; he must wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:28 When a woman is cleansed of her discharge
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day she is to take two turtledoves or two young pigeons and bring them to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus15:30 The priest is to sacrifice one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make atonement for her before the Lord for her unclean discharge.
Leviticus15:31 You must keep the children of Israel separate from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law of him who has a discharge
Leviticus15:33 of a woman in her menstrual period
Leviticus16:1 Now the Lord spoke to Moses after the death of two of Aaron’s sons when they approached the presence of the Lord.
Leviticus16:2 And the Lord said to Moses: “Tell your brother Aaron not to enter freely into the Most Holy Place behind the veil in front of the mercy seat on the ark
Leviticus16:3 This is how Aaron is to enter the Holy Place: with a young bull for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He is to wear the sacred linen tunic
Leviticus16:5 And he shall take from the congregation of Israel two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Aaron is to present the bull for his sin offering and make atonement for himself and his household.
Leviticus16:7 Then he shall take the two goats and present them before the Lord at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus16:8 After Aaron casts lots for the two goats
Leviticus16:9 he shall present the goat chosen by lot for the Lord and sacrifice it as a sin offering.
Leviticus16:10 But the goat chosen by lot as the scapegoat shall be presented alive before the Lord to make atonement by sending it into the wilderness as the scapegoat.
Leviticus16:11 When Aaron presents the bull for his sin offering and makes atonement for himself and his household
Leviticus16:12 Then he must take a censer full of burning coals from the altar before the Lord
Leviticus16:13 He is to put the incense on the fire before the Lord
Leviticus16:14 And he is to take some of the bull’s blood and sprinkle it with his finger on the east side of the mercy seat; then he shall sprinkle some of it with his finger seven times before the mercy seat.
Leviticus16:15 Aaron shall then slaughter the goat for the sin offering for the people and bring its blood behind the veil
Leviticus16:16 So he shall make atonement for the Most Holy Place because of the impurities and rebellious acts of the Israelites in regard to all their sins. He is to do the same for the Tent of Meeting which abides among them
Leviticus16:17 No one may be in the Tent of Meeting from the time Aaron goes in to make atonement in the Most Holy Place until he leaves
Leviticus16:18 Then he shall go out to the altar that is before the Lord and make atonement for it. He is to take some of the bull’s blood and some of the goat’s blood and put it on all the horns of the altar.
Leviticus16:19 He is to sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven times to cleanse it and consecrate it from the uncleanness of the Israelites.
Leviticus16:20 When Aaron has finished purifying the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:21 Then he is to lay both hands on the head of the live goat and confess over it all the iniquities and rebellious acts of the Israelites in regard to all their sins. He is to put them on the goat’s head and send it away into the wilderness by the hand of a man appointed for the task.
Leviticus16:22 The goat will carry on itself all their iniquities into a solitary place
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron is to enter the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus16:24 He is to bathe himself with water in a holy place and put on his own clothes. Then he must go out and sacrifice his burnt offering and the people’s burnt offering to make atonement for himself and for the people.
Leviticus16:25 He is also to burn the fat of the sin offering on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 The man who released the goat as the scapegoat must wash his clothes and bathe himself with water; afterward he may reenter the camp.
Leviticus16:27 The bull for the sin offering and the goat for the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 The one who burns them must wash his clothes and bathe himself with water
Leviticus16:29 This is to be a permanent statute for you: On the tenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 because on this day atonement will be made for you to cleanse you
Leviticus16:31 It is a Sabbath of complete rest for you
Leviticus16:32 The priest who is anointed and ordained to succeed his father as high priest shall make atonement. He will put on the sacred linen garments
Leviticus16:33 and make atonement for the Most Holy Place
Leviticus16:34 This is to be a permanent statute for you
Leviticus17:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus17:2 “Speak to Aaron
Leviticus17:3 ‘Anyone from the house of Israel who slaughters an ox
Leviticus17:4 instead of bringing it to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting to present it as an offering to the Lord before His tabernacle—that man shall incur bloodguilt. He has shed blood and must be cut off from among his people.
Leviticus17:5 For this reason the Israelites will bring to the Lord the sacrifices they have been offering in the open fields. They are to bring them to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting and offer them as sacrifices of peace to the Lord.
Leviticus17:6 The priest will then sprinkle the blood on the altar of the Lord at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting and burn the fat as a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
Leviticus17:7 They must no longer offer their sacrifices to the goat demons to which they have prostituted themselves. This will be a permanent statute for them for the generations to come.’
Leviticus17:8 Tell them that if anyone from the house of Israel or any foreigner living among them offers a burnt offering or a sacrifice
Leviticus17:9 but does not bring it to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting to sacrifice it to the Lord
Leviticus17:10 If anyone from the house of Israel or a foreigner living among them eats any blood
Leviticus17:11 For the life of the flesh is in the blood
Leviticus17:12 Therefore I say to the Israelites
Leviticus17:13 And if any Israelite or foreigner living among them hunts down a wild animal or bird that may be eaten
Leviticus17:14 For the life of all flesh is its blood. Therefore I have told the Israelites
Leviticus17:15 And any person
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash his clothes and bathe himself
Leviticus18:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus18:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:3 You must not follow the practices of the land of Egypt
Leviticus18:4 You are to practice My judgments and keep My statutes by walking in them. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:5 Keep My statutes and My judgments
Leviticus18:6 None of you are to approach any close relative to have sexual relations. I am the Lord.
Leviticus18:7 You must not expose the nakedness of your father by having sexual relations with your mother. She is your mother; you must not have sexual relations with her.
Leviticus18:8 You must not have sexual relations with your father’s wife; it would dishonor your father.
Leviticus18:9 You must not have sexual relations with your sister
Leviticus18:10 You must not have sexual relations with your son’s daughter or your daughter’s daughter
Leviticus18:11 You must not have sexual relations with the daughter of your father’s wife
Leviticus18:12 You must not have sexual relations with your father’s sister; she is your father’s close relative.
Leviticus18:13 You must not have sexual relations with your mother’s sister
Leviticus18:14 You must not dishonor your father’s brother by approaching his wife to have sexual relations with her; she is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You must not have sexual relations with your daughter-in-law. She is your son’s wife; you are not to have sexual relations with her.
Leviticus18:16 You must not have sexual relations with your brother’s wife; that would shame your brother.
Leviticus18:17 You must not have sexual relations with both a woman and her daughter. You are not to marry her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter and have sexual relations with her. They are close relatives; it is depraved.
Leviticus18:18 You must not take your wife’s sister as a rival wife and have sexual relations with her while your wife is still alive.
Leviticus18:19 You must not approach a woman to have sexual relations with her during her menstrual period.
Leviticus18:20 You must not lie carnally with your neighbor’s wife and thus defile yourself with her.
Leviticus18:21 You must not give any of your children to be sacrificed to Molech
Leviticus18:22 You must not lie with a man as with a woman; that is an abomination.
Leviticus18:23 You must not lie carnally with any animal
Leviticus18:24 Do not defile yourselves by any of these practices
Leviticus18:25 Even the land has become defiled
Leviticus18:26 But you are to keep My statutes and ordinances
Leviticus18:27 For the men who were in the land before you committed all these abominations
Leviticus18:28 So if you defile the land
Leviticus18:29 Therefore anyone who commits any of these abominations must be cut off from among his people.
Leviticus18:30 You must keep My charge not to practice any of the abominable customs that were practiced before you
Leviticus19:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus19:2 “Speak to the whole congregation of Israel and tell them: Be holy because I
Leviticus19:3 Each of you must respect his mother and father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols or make for yourselves molten gods. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:5 When you sacrifice a peace offering to the Lord
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten on the day you sacrifice it
Leviticus19:7 If any of it is eaten on the third day
Leviticus19:8 Whoever eats it will bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 You must not strip your vineyard bare or gather its fallen grapes. Leave them for the poor and the foreigner. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:11 You must not steal. You must not lie or deceive one another.
Leviticus19:12 You must not swear falsely by My name and so profane the name of your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:13 You must not defraud your neighbor or rob him. You must not withhold until morning the wages due a hired hand.
Leviticus19:14 You must not curse the deaf or place a stumbling block before the blind
Leviticus19:15 You must not pervert justice; you must not show partiality to the poor or favoritism to the rich; you are to judge your neighbor fairly.
Leviticus19:16 You must not go about spreading slander among your people. You must not endanger the life of your neighbor. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:17 You must not harbor hatred against your brother in your heart. Directly rebuke your neighbor
Leviticus19:18 Do not seek revenge or bear a grudge against any of your people
Leviticus19:19 You are to keep My statutes. You shall not crossbreed two different kinds of livestock; you shall not sow your fields with two kinds of seed; and you shall not wear clothing made of two kinds of material.
Leviticus19:20 If a man lies carnally with a slave girl promised to another man but who has not been redeemed or given her freedom
Leviticus19:21 The man
Leviticus19:22 The priest shall make atonement on his behalf before the Lord with the ram of the guilt offering for the sin he has committed
Leviticus19:23 When you enter the land and plant any kind of tree for food
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year all its fruit must be consecrated as a praise offering to the Lord.
Leviticus19:25 But in the fifth year you may eat its fruit; thus your harvest will be increased. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:26 You must not eat anything with blood still in it. You must not practice divination or sorcery.
Leviticus19:27 You must not cut off the hair at the sides of your head or clip off the edges of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 You must not make any cuts in your bodies for the dead or put tattoo marks on yourselves. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:29 You must not defile your daughter by making her a prostitute
Leviticus19:30 You must keep My Sabbaths and have reverence for My sanctuary. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 You must not turn to mediums or spiritists; do not seek them out
Leviticus19:32 You are to rise in the presence of the elderly
Leviticus19:33 When a foreigner resides with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 You must treat the foreigner living among you as native-born and love him as yourself
Leviticus19:35 You must not use dishonest measures of length
Leviticus19:36 You shall maintain honest scales and weights
Leviticus19:37 You must keep all My statutes and all My ordinances and follow them. I am the Lord.”
Leviticus20:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus20:2 “Tell the Israelites
Leviticus20:3 And I will set My face against that man and cut him off from his people
Leviticus20:4 And if the people of the land ever hide their eyes and fail to put to death the man who gives one of his children to Molech
Leviticus20:5 then I will set My face against that man and his family and cut off from among their people both him and all who follow him in prostituting themselves with Molech.
Leviticus20:6 Whoever turns to mediums or spiritists to prostitute himself with them
Leviticus20:7 Consecrate yourselves
Leviticus20:8 And you shall keep My statutes and practice them. I am the Lord who sanctifies you.
Leviticus20:9 If anyone curses his father or mother
Leviticus20:10 If a man commits adultery with another man’s wife—with the wife of his neighbor—both the adulterer and the adulteress must surely be put to death.
Leviticus20:11 If a man lies with his father’s wife
Leviticus20:12 If a man lies with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 If a man lies with a man as with a woman
Leviticus20:14 If a man marries both a woman and her mother
Leviticus20:15 If a man lies carnally with an animal
Leviticus20:16 If a woman approaches any animal to mate with it
Leviticus20:17 If a man marries his sister
Leviticus20:18 If a man lies with a menstruating woman and has sexual relations with her
Leviticus20:19 You must not have sexual relations with the sister of your mother or your father
Leviticus20:20 If a man lies with his uncle’s wife
Leviticus20:21 If a man marries his brother’s wife
Leviticus20:22 You are therefore to keep all My statutes and ordinances
Leviticus20:23 You must not follow the statutes of the nations I am driving out before you. Because they did all these things
Leviticus20:24 But I have told you that you will inherit their land
Leviticus20:25 You are therefore to distinguish between clean and unclean animals and birds. Do not become contaminated by any animal or bird
Leviticus20:26 You are to be holy to Me because I
Leviticus20:27 A man or a woman who is a medium or spiritist must surely be put to death. They shall be stoned; their blood is upon them.’”
Leviticus21:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus21:2 except for his immediate family—his mother
Leviticus21:3 or his unmarried sister who is near to him
Leviticus21:4 He is not to defile himself for those related to him by marriage
Leviticus21:5 Priests must not make bald spots on their heads
Leviticus21:6 They must be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God. Because they present to the Lord the offerings made by fire
Leviticus21:7 A priest must not marry a woman defiled by prostitution or divorced by her husband
Leviticus21:8 You are to regard him as holy
Leviticus21:9 If a priest’s daughter defiles herself by prostituting herself
Leviticus21:10 The priest who is highest among his brothers
Leviticus21:11 He must not go near any dead body; he must not defile himself
Leviticus21:12 He must not leave or desecrate the sanctuary of his God
Leviticus21:13 The woman he marries must be a virgin.
Leviticus21:14 He is not to marry a widow
Leviticus21:15 so that he does not defile his offspring among his people
Leviticus21:16 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus21:17 “Say to Aaron
Leviticus21:18 No man who has any defect may approach—no man who is blind
Leviticus21:19 no man who has a broken foot or hand
Leviticus21:20 or who is a hunchback or dwarf
Leviticus21:21 No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a defect shall approach to present the offerings made by fire to the Lord. Since he has a defect
Leviticus21:22 He may eat the most holy food of his God as well as the holy food
Leviticus21:23 but because he has a defect
Leviticus21:24 Moses told this to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites.
Leviticus22:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:2 “Tell Aaron and his sons to treat with respect the sacred offerings that the Israelites have consecrated to Me
Leviticus22:3 Tell them that for the generations to come
Leviticus22:4 If a descendant of Aaron has a skin disease or a discharge
Leviticus22:5 or whoever touches a crawling creature or a person that makes him unclean
Leviticus22:6 the man who touches any of these will remain unclean until evening. He must not eat from the sacred offerings unless he has bathed himself with water.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun has set
Leviticus22:8 He must not eat anything found dead or torn by wild animals
Leviticus22:9 The priests must keep My charge
Leviticus22:10 No one outside a priest’s family may eat the sacred offering
Leviticus22:11 But if a priest buys a slave with his own money
Leviticus22:12 If the priest’s daughter is married to a man other than a priest
Leviticus22:13 But if a priest’s daughter with no children becomes widowed or divorced and returns to her father’s house
Leviticus22:14 If anyone eats a sacred offering in error
Leviticus22:15 The priests must not profane the sacred offerings that the Israelites present to the Lord
Leviticus22:16 by allowing the people to eat the sacred offerings and thus to bear the punishment for guilt. For I am the Lord who sanctifies them.”
Leviticus22:17 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:18 “Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the Israelites and tell them
Leviticus22:19 must offer an unblemished male from the cattle
Leviticus22:20 You must not present anything with a defect
Leviticus22:21 When a man presents a peace offering to the Lord from the herd or flock to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering
Leviticus22:22 You are not to present to the Lord any animal that is blind
Leviticus22:23 You may present as a freewill offering an ox or sheep that has a deformed or stunted limb
Leviticus22:24 You are not to present to the Lord an animal whose testicles are bruised
Leviticus22:25 Neither you nor a foreigner shall present food to your God from any such animal. They will not be accepted on your behalf
Leviticus22:26 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:27 “When an ox
Leviticus22:28 But you must not slaughter an ox or a sheep on the same day as its young.
Leviticus22:29 When you sacrifice a thank offering to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 It must be eaten that same day. Do not leave any of it until morning. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:31 You are to keep My commandments and practice them. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:32 You must not profane My holy name. I must be acknowledged as holy among the Israelites. I am the Lord who sanctifies you
Leviticus22:33 who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord.”
Leviticus23:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:2 “Speak to the Israelites and say to them
Leviticus23:3 For six days work may be done
Leviticus23:4 These are the Lord’s appointed feasts
Leviticus23:5 The Passover to the Lord begins at twilight on the fourteenth day of the first month.
Leviticus23:6 On the fifteenth day of the same month begins the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the Lord. For seven days you must eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you are to hold a sacred assembly; you are not to do any regular work.
Leviticus23:8 For seven days you are to present an offering made by fire to the Lord. On the seventh day there shall be a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work.’”
Leviticus23:9 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:10 “Speak to the Israelites and say
Leviticus23:11 And he shall wave the sheaf before the Lord so that it may be accepted on your behalf; the priest is to wave it on the day after the Sabbath.
Leviticus23:12 On the day you wave the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 along with its grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil—an offering made by fire to the Lord
Leviticus23:14 You must not eat any bread or roasted or new grain until the very day you have brought this offering to your God. This is to be a permanent statute for the generations to come
Leviticus23:15 From the day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You shall count off fifty days until the day after the seventh Sabbath
Leviticus23:17 Bring two loaves of bread from your dwellings as a wave offering
Leviticus23:18 Along with the bread you are to present seven unblemished male lambs a year old
Leviticus23:19 You shall also prepare one male goat as a sin offering and two male lambs a year old as a peace offering.
Leviticus23:20 The priest is to wave the lambs as a wave offering before the Lord
Leviticus23:21 On that same day you are to proclaim a sacred assembly
Leviticus23:22 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 The Lord also said to Moses
Leviticus23:24 “Speak to the Israelites and say
Leviticus23:25 You must not do any regular work
Leviticus23:26 Again the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:27 “The tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. You shall hold a sacred assembly and humble yourselves
Leviticus23:28 On this day you are not to do any work
Leviticus23:29 If anyone does not humble himself on this day
Leviticus23:30 I will destroy from among his people anyone who does any work on this day.
Leviticus23:31 You are not to do any work at all. This is a permanent statute for the generations to come
Leviticus23:32 It will be a Sabbath of complete rest for you
Leviticus23:33 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:34 “Speak to the Israelites and say
Leviticus23:35 On the first day there shall be a sacred assembly. You must not do any regular work.
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you are to present an offering made by fire to the Lord. On the eighth day you are to hold a sacred assembly and present an offering made by fire to the Lord. It is a solemn assembly; you must not do any regular work.
Leviticus23:37 These are the Lord’s appointed feasts
Leviticus23:38 These offerings are in addition to the offerings for the Lord’s Sabbaths
Leviticus23:39 On the fifteenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus23:40 On the first day you are to gather the fruit of majestic trees
Leviticus23:41 You are to celebrate this as a feast to the Lord for seven days each year. This is a permanent statute for the generations to come; you are to celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You are to dwell in booths for seven days. All the native-born of Israel must dwell in booths
Leviticus23:43 so that your descendants may know that I made the Israelites dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.’”
Leviticus23:44 So Moses announced to the Israelites the appointed feasts of the Lord.
Leviticus24:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:2 “Command the Israelites to bring you pure oil of pressed olives for the light
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus24:4 He shall tend the lamps on the pure gold lampstand before the Lord continually.
Leviticus24:5 You are also to take fine flour and bake twelve loaves
Leviticus24:6 and set them in two rows—six per row—on the table of pure gold before the Lord.
Leviticus24:7 And you are to place pure frankincense near each row
Leviticus24:8 Every Sabbath day the bread is to be set out before the Lord on behalf of the Israelites as a permanent covenant.
Leviticus24:9 It belongs to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Now the son of an Israelite mother and an Egyptian father went out among the Israelites
Leviticus24:11 The son of the Israelite woman blasphemed the Name with a curse. So they brought him to Moses. (His mother’s name was Shelomith daughter of Dibri
Leviticus24:12 They placed him in custody until the will of the Lord should be made clear to them.
Leviticus24:13 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:14 “Take the blasphemer outside the camp
Leviticus24:15 And you are to tell the Israelites
Leviticus24:16 Whoever blasphemes the name of the Lord must surely be put to death; the whole assembly must surely stone him
Leviticus24:17 And if a man takes the life of anyone else
Leviticus24:18 Whoever kills an animal must make restitution—life for life.
Leviticus24:19 If anyone injures his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 Whoever kills an animal must make restitution
Leviticus24:22 You are to have the same standard of law for the foreign resident and the native; for I am the Lord your God.’”
Leviticus24:23 Then Moses spoke to the Israelites
Leviticus25:1 Then the Lord said to Moses on Mount Sinai
Leviticus25:2 “Speak to the Israelites and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you
Leviticus25:3 For six years you may sow your field and prune your vineyard and gather its crops.
Leviticus25:4 But in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of complete rest for the land—a Sabbath to the Lord. You are not to sow your field or prune your vineyard.
Leviticus25:5 You are not to reap the aftergrowth of your harvest or gather the grapes of your untended vines. The land must have a year of complete rest.
Leviticus25:6 Whatever the land yields during the Sabbath year shall be food for you—for yourself
Leviticus25:7 and for your livestock and the wild animals in your land. All its growth may serve as food.
Leviticus25:8 And you shall count off seven Sabbaths of years—seven times seven years—so that the seven Sabbaths of years amount to forty-nine years.
Leviticus25:9 Then you are to sound the horn far and wide on the tenth day of the seventh month
Leviticus25:10 So you are to consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim liberty in the land for all its inhabitants. It shall be your Jubilee
Leviticus25:11 The fiftieth year will be a Jubilee for you; you are not to sow the land or reap its aftergrowth or harvest the untended vines.
Leviticus25:12 For it is a Jubilee; it shall be holy to you. You may eat only the crops taken directly from the field.
Leviticus25:13 In this Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:14 If you make a sale to your neighbor or a purchase from him
Leviticus25:15 You are to buy from your neighbor according to the number of years since the last Jubilee; he is to sell to you according to the number of harvest years remaining.
Leviticus25:16 You shall increase the price in proportion to a greater number of years
Leviticus25:17 Do not take advantage of each other
Leviticus25:18 You are to keep My statutes and carefully observe My judgments
Leviticus25:19 Then the land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 Now you may wonder
Leviticus25:21 But I will send My blessing upon you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 While you are sowing in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land must not be sold permanently
Leviticus25:24 Thus for every piece of property you possess
Leviticus25:25 If your brother becomes impoverished and sells some of his property
Leviticus25:26 Or if a man has no one to redeem it for him
Leviticus25:27 he shall calculate the years since its sale
Leviticus25:28 But if he cannot obtain enough to repay him
Leviticus25:29 If a man sells a house in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed by the end of a full year
Leviticus25:31 But houses in villages with no walls around them are to be considered as open fields. They may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 As for the cities of the Levites
Leviticus25:33 So whatever belongs to the Levites may be redeemed—a house sold in a city they possess—and must be released in the Jubilee
Leviticus25:34 But the open pastureland around their cities may not be sold
Leviticus25:35 Now if your countryman becomes destitute and cannot support himself among you
Leviticus25:36 Do not take any interest or profit from him
Leviticus25:37 You must not lend him your silver at interest or sell him your food for profit.
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus25:39 If a countryman among you becomes destitute and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 Let him stay with you as a hired worker or temporary resident; he is to work for you until the Year of Jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 Then he and his children are to be released
Leviticus25:42 Because the Israelites are My servants
Leviticus25:43 You are not to rule over them harshly
Leviticus25:44 Your menservants and maidservants shall come from the nations around you
Leviticus25:45 You may also purchase them from the foreigners residing among you or their clans living among you who are born in your land. These may become your property.
Leviticus25:46 You may leave them to your sons after you to inherit as property; you can make them slaves for life. But as for your brothers
Leviticus25:47 If a foreigner residing among you prospers
Leviticus25:48 he retains the right of redemption after he has sold himself. One of his brothers may redeem him:
Leviticus25:49 either his uncle or cousin or any close relative from his clan may redeem him. Or if he prospers
Leviticus25:50 He and his purchaser will then count the time from the year he sold himself up to the Year of Jubilee. The price of his sale will be determined by the number of years
Leviticus25:51 If many years remain
Leviticus25:52 If only a few years remain until the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 He shall be treated like a man hired from year to year
Leviticus25:54 Even if he is not redeemed in any of these ways
Leviticus25:55 For the Israelites are My servants. They are My servants
Leviticus26:1 “You must not make idols for yourselves or set up a carved image or sacred pillar; you must not place a sculpted stone in your land to bow down to it. For I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus26:2 You must keep My Sabbaths and have reverence for My sanctuary. I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 If you follow My statutes and carefully keep My commandments
Leviticus26:4 I will give you rains in their season
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing will continue until the grape harvest
Leviticus26:6 And I will give peace to the land
Leviticus26:7 You will pursue your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will pursue a hundred
Leviticus26:9 I will turn toward you and make you fruitful and multiply you
Leviticus26:10 You will still be eating the old supply of grain when you need to clear it out to make room for the new.
Leviticus26:11 And I will make My dwelling place among you
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus26:14 If
Leviticus26:15 and if you reject My statutes
Leviticus26:16 then this is what I will do to you: I will bring upon you sudden terror
Leviticus26:17 And I will set My face against you
Leviticus26:18 And if after all this you will not obey Me
Leviticus26:19 I will break down your stubborn pride and make your sky like iron and your land like bronze
Leviticus26:20 and your strength will be spent in vain. For your land will not yield its produce
Leviticus26:21 If you walk in hostility toward Me and refuse to obey Me
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals against you to rob you of your children
Leviticus26:23 And if in spite of these things you do not accept My discipline
Leviticus26:24 then I will act with hostility toward you
Leviticus26:25 And I will bring a sword against you to execute the vengeance of the covenant. Though you withdraw into your cities
Leviticus26:26 When I cut off your supply of bread
Leviticus26:27 But if in spite of all this you do not obey Me
Leviticus26:28 then I will walk in fury against you
Leviticus26:29 You will eat the flesh of your own sons and daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places
Leviticus26:31 I will reduce your cities to rubble and lay waste your sanctuaries
Leviticus26:32 And I will lay waste the land
Leviticus26:33 But I will scatter you among the nations and will draw out a sword after you as your land becomes desolate and your cities are laid waste.
Leviticus26:34 Then the land shall enjoy its Sabbaths all the days it lies desolate
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:36 As for those of you who survive
Leviticus26:37 They will stumble over one another as before the sword
Leviticus26:38 You will perish among the nations
Leviticus26:39 Those of you who survive in the lands of your enemies will waste away in their iniquity and will decay in the sins of their fathers.
Leviticus26:40 But if they will confess their iniquity and that of their fathers in the unfaithfulness that they practiced against Me
Leviticus26:41 and I acted with hostility toward them and brought them into the land of their enemies—and if their uncircumcised hearts will be humbled and they will make amends for their iniquity
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember My covenant with Jacob and My covenant with Isaac and My covenant with Abraham
Leviticus26:43 For the land will be abandoned by them
Leviticus26:44 Yet in spite of this
Leviticus26:45 But for their sake I will remember the covenant with their fathers
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes
Leviticus27:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus27:2 “Speak to the Israelites and say to them
Leviticus27:3 if the valuation concerns a male from twenty to sixty years of age
Leviticus27:4 Or if it is a female
Leviticus27:5 And if the person is from five to twenty years of age
Leviticus27:6 Now if the person is from one month to five years of age
Leviticus27:7 And if the person is sixty years of age or older
Leviticus27:8 But if the one making the vow is too poor to pay the valuation
Leviticus27:9 If he vows an animal that may be brought as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus27:10 He must not replace it or exchange it
Leviticus27:11 But if the vow involves any of the unclean animals that may not be brought as an offering to the Lord
Leviticus27:12 The priest shall set its value
Leviticus27:13 If
Leviticus27:14 Now if a man consecrates his house as holy to the Lord
Leviticus27:15 But if he who consecrated his house redeems it
Leviticus27:16 If a man consecrates to the Lord a parcel of his land
Leviticus27:17 If he consecrates his field during the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 But if he consecrates his field after the Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 And if the one who consecrated the field decides to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 If
Leviticus27:21 When the field is released in the Jubilee
Leviticus27:22 Now if a man consecrates to the Lord a field he has purchased
Leviticus27:23 then the priest shall calculate for him the value up to the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:24 In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was bought—the original owner of the land.
Leviticus27:25 Every valuation will be according to the sanctuary shekel
Leviticus27:26 But no one may consecrate a firstborn of the livestock
Leviticus27:27 But if it is among the unclean animals
Leviticus27:28 Nothing that a man sets apart to the Lord from all he owns—whether a man
Leviticus27:29 No person set apart for destruction may be ransomed; he must surely be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 Thus any tithe from the land
Leviticus27:31 If a man wishes to redeem part of his tithe
Leviticus27:32 Every tenth animal from the herd or flock that passes under the shepherd’s rod will be holy to the Lord.
Leviticus27:33 He must not inspect whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments that the Lord gave to Moses for the Israelites on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 On the first day of the second month of the second year after the Israelites had come out of the land of Egypt
Numbers1:2 “Take a census of the whole congregation of Israel by their clans and families
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron are to number those who are twenty years of age or older by their divisions—everyone who can serve in Israel’s army.
Numbers1:4 And one man from each tribe
Numbers1:5 These are the names of the men who are to assist you: From the tribe of Reuben
Numbers1:6 from Simeon
Numbers1:7 from Judah
Numbers1:8 from Issachar
Numbers1:9 from Zebulun
Numbers1:10 from the sons of Joseph: from Ephraim
Numbers1:11 from Benjamin
Numbers1:12 from Dan
Numbers1:13 from Asher
Numbers1:14 from Gad
Numbers1:15 and from Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These men were appointed from the congregation; they were the leaders of the tribes of their fathers
Numbers1:17 So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name
Numbers1:18 and on the first day of the second month they assembled the whole congregation and recorded their ancestry by clans and families
Numbers1:19 just as the Lord had commanded Moses. So Moses numbered them in the Wilderness of Sinai:
Numbers1:20 From the sons of Reuben
Numbers1:21 those registered to the tribe of Reuben numbered 46
Numbers1:22 From the sons of Simeon
Numbers1:23 those registered to the tribe of Simeon numbered 59
Numbers1:24 From the sons of Gad
Numbers1:25 those registered to the tribe of Gad numbered 45
Numbers1:26 From the sons of Judah
Numbers1:27 those registered to the tribe of Judah numbered 74
Numbers1:28 From the sons of Issachar
Numbers1:29 those registered to the tribe of Issachar numbered 54
Numbers1:30 From the sons of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 those registered to the tribe of Zebulun numbered 57
Numbers1:32 From the sons of Joseph: From the sons of Ephraim
Numbers1:33 those registered to the tribe of Ephraim numbered 40
Numbers1:34 And from the sons of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 those registered to the tribe of Manasseh numbered 32
Numbers1:36 From the sons of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 those registered to the tribe of Benjamin numbered 35
Numbers1:38 From the sons of Dan
Numbers1:39 those registered to the tribe of Dan numbered 62
Numbers1:40 From the sons of Asher
Numbers1:41 those registered to the tribe of Asher numbered 41
Numbers1:42 From the sons of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 those registered to the tribe of Naphtali numbered 53
Numbers1:44 These were the men numbered by Moses and Aaron
Numbers1:45 So all the Israelites twenty years of age or older who could serve in Israel’s army were counted according to their families.
Numbers1:46 And all those counted totaled 603
Numbers1:47 The Levites
Numbers1:48 For the Lord had said to Moses:
Numbers1:49 “Do not number the tribe of Levi in the census with the other Israelites.
Numbers1:50 Instead
Numbers1:51 Whenever the tabernacle is to move
Numbers1:52 The Israelites are to camp by their divisions
Numbers1:53 But the Levites are to camp around the tabernacle of the Testimony and watch over it
Numbers1:54 Thus the Israelites did everything just as the Lord had commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers2:2 “The Israelites are to camp around the Tent of Meeting at a distance from it
Numbers2:3 On the east side
Numbers2:4 and his division numbers 74
Numbers2:5 The tribe of Issachar will camp next to it. The leader of the Issacharites is Nethanel son of Zuar
Numbers2:6 and his division numbers 54
Numbers2:7 Next will be the tribe of Zebulun. The leader of the Zebulunites is Eliab son of Helon
Numbers2:8 and his division numbers 57
Numbers2:9 The total number of men in the divisions of the camp of Judah is 186
Numbers2:10 On the south side
Numbers2:11 and his division numbers 46
Numbers2:12 The tribe of Simeon will camp next to it. The leader of the Simeonites is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai
Numbers2:13 and his division numbers 59
Numbers2:14 Next will be the tribe of Gad. The leader of the Gadites is Eliasaph son of Deuel
Numbers2:15 and his division numbers 45
Numbers2:16 The total number of men in the divisions of the camp of Reuben is 151
Numbers2:17 In the middle of the camps
Numbers2:18 On the west side
Numbers2:19 and his division numbers 40
Numbers2:20 The tribe of Manasseh will be next to it. The leader of the Manassites is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur
Numbers2:21 and his division numbers 32
Numbers2:22 Next will be the tribe of Benjamin. The leader of the Benjamites is Abidan son of Gideoni
Numbers2:23 and his division numbers 35
Numbers2:24 The total number of men in the divisions of the camp of Ephraim is 108
Numbers2:25 On the north side
Numbers2:26 and his division numbers 62
Numbers2:27 The tribe of Asher will camp next to it. The leader of the Asherites is Pagiel son of Ocran
Numbers2:28 and his division numbers 41
Numbers2:29 Next will be the tribe of Naphtali. The leader of the Naphtalites is Ahira son of Enan
Numbers2:30 and his division numbers 53
Numbers2:31 The total number of men in the camp of Dan is 157
Numbers2:32 These are the Israelites
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not counted among the other Israelites
Numbers2:34 So the Israelites did everything the Lord commanded Moses; they camped under their standards in this way and set out in the same way
Numbers3:1 This is the account of Aaron and Moses at the time the Lord spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn
Numbers3:3 These were Aaron’s sons
Numbers3:4 Nadab and Abihu
Numbers3:5 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:6 “Bring the tribe of Levi and present them to Aaron the priest to assist him.
Numbers3:7 They are to perform duties for him and for the whole congregation before the Tent of Meeting
Numbers3:8 They shall take care of all the furnishings of the Tent of Meeting and fulfill obligations for the Israelites by attending to the service of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:9 Assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they have been given exclusively to him from among the Israelites.
Numbers3:10 So you shall appoint Aaron and his sons to carry out the duties of the priesthood; but any outsider who approaches the tabernacle must be put to death.”
Numbers3:11 Again the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:12 “Behold
Numbers3:13 for all the firstborn are Mine. On the day I struck down every firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 Then the Lord spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 “Number the Levites by their families and clans. You are to count every male a month old or more.”
Numbers3:16 So Moses numbered them according to the word of the Lord
Numbers3:17 These were the sons of Levi by name: Gershon
Numbers3:18 These were the names of the sons of Gershon by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 The sons of Kohath by their clans were Amram
Numbers3:20 And the sons of Merari by their clans were Mahli and Mushi. These were the clans of the Levites
Numbers3:21 From Gershon came the Libnite clan and the Shimeite clan; these were the Gershonite clans.
Numbers3:22 The number of all the males a month old or more was 7
Numbers3:23 The Gershonite clans were to camp on the west
Numbers3:24 and the leader of the families of the Gershonites was Eliasaph son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 The duties of the Gershonites at the Tent of Meeting were the tabernacle and tent
Numbers3:26 the curtains of the courtyard
Numbers3:27 From Kohath came the clans of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 The number of all the males a month old or more was 8
Numbers3:29 The clans of the Kohathites were to camp on the south side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 and the leader of the families of the Kohathites was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their duties were the ark
Numbers3:32 The chief of the leaders of the Levites was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest; he oversaw those responsible for the duties of the sanctuary.
Numbers3:33 From Merari came the clans of the Mahlites and Mushites; these were the Merarite clans.
Numbers3:34 The number of all the males a month old or more was 6
Numbers3:35 The leader of the families of the Merarites was Zuriel son of Abihail; they were to camp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 The duties assigned to the sons of Merari were the tabernacle’s frames
Numbers3:37 as well as the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases
Numbers3:38 Moses
Numbers3:39 The total number of Levites that Moses and Aaron counted by their clans at the Lord’s command
Numbers3:40 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:41 You are to take the Levites for Me—I am the Lord—in place of all the firstborn of Israel
Numbers3:42 So Moses numbered all the firstborn of the Israelites
Numbers3:43 The total number of the firstborn males a month old or more
Numbers3:44 Again the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers3:45 “Take the Levites in place of all the firstborn of Israel
Numbers3:46 To redeem the 273 firstborn Israelites who outnumber the Levites
Numbers3:47 you are to collect five shekels for each one
Numbers3:48 Give the money to Aaron and his sons as the redemption price for the excess among the Israelites.”
Numbers3:49 So Moses collected the redemption money from those in excess of the number redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 He collected the money from the firstborn of the Israelites: 1
Numbers3:51 And Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons in obedience to the word of the Lord
Numbers4:1 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 “Take a census of the Kohathites among the Levites by their clans and families
Numbers4:3 men from thirty to fifty years old—everyone who is qualified to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers4:4 This service of the Kohathites at the Tent of Meeting regards the most holy things.
Numbers4:5 Whenever the camp sets out
Numbers4:6 They are to place over this a covering of fine leather
Numbers4:7 Over the table of the Presence they are to spread a blue cloth and place the plates and cups on it
Numbers4:8 And they shall spread a scarlet cloth over them
Numbers4:9 They are to take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand used for light
Numbers4:10 Then they shall wrap it and all its utensils inside a covering of fine leather and put it on the carrying frame.
Numbers4:11 Over the gold altar they are to spread a blue cloth
Numbers4:12 They are to take all the utensils for serving in the sanctuary
Numbers4:13 Then they shall remove the ashes from the bronze altar
Numbers4:14 and place on it all the vessels used to serve there: the firepans
Numbers4:15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the holy objects and all their equipment
Numbers4:16 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall oversee the oil for the light
Numbers4:17 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 “Do not allow the Kohathite tribal clans to be cut off from among the Levites.
Numbers4:19 In order that they may live and not die when they come near the most holy things
Numbers4:20 But the Kohathites are not to go in and look at the holy objects
Numbers4:21 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers4:22 “Take a census of the Gershonites as well
Numbers4:23 from thirty to fifty years old
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the Gershonite clans regarding work and transport:
Numbers4:25 They are to carry the curtains of the tabernacle
Numbers4:26 the curtains of the courtyard
Numbers4:27 All the service of the Gershonites—all their transport duties and other work—is to be done at the direction of Aaron and his sons; you are to assign to them all that they are responsible to carry.
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the Gershonite clans at the Tent of Meeting
Numbers4:29 As for the sons of Merari
Numbers4:30 from thirty to fifty years old
Numbers4:31 This is the duty for all their service at the Tent of Meeting: to carry the frames of the tabernacle with its crossbars
Numbers4:32 and the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases
Numbers4:33 This is the service of the Merarite clans according to all their work at the Tent of Meeting
Numbers4:34 So Moses
Numbers4:35 everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers4:36 And those numbered by their clans totaled 2
Numbers4:37 These were counted from the Kohathite clans
Numbers4:38 Then the Gershonites were numbered by their clans and families
Numbers4:39 everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers4:40 And those numbered by their clans and families totaled 2
Numbers4:41 These were counted from the Gershonite clans who served at the Tent of Meeting
Numbers4:42 And the Merarites were numbered by their clans and families
Numbers4:43 everyone from thirty to fifty years old who came to serve in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers4:44 The men registered by their clans numbered 3
Numbers4:45 These were counted from the Merarite clans
Numbers4:46 So Moses
Numbers4:47 from thirty to fifty years old who came to do the work of serving and carrying the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers4:48 And the number of men was 8
Numbers4:49 At the Lord’s command they were numbered through Moses and each one was assigned his work and burden
Numbers5:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:2 “Command the Israelites to send away from the camp anyone with a skin disease
Numbers5:3 You must send away male and female alike; send them outside the camp so they will not defile their camp
Numbers5:4 So the Israelites did this
Numbers5:5 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:6 “Tell the Israelites that when a man or woman acts unfaithfully against the Lord by committing any sin against another
Numbers5:7 and must confess the sin he has committed. He must make full restitution
Numbers5:8 But if the man has no relative to whom restitution can be made for the wrong
Numbers5:9 Every sacred contribution the Israelites bring to the priest shall belong to him.
Numbers5:10 Each man’s sacred gifts are his own
Numbers5:11 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:12 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them that if any man’s wife goes astray and is unfaithful to him
Numbers5:13 by sleeping with another man
Numbers5:14 and if a feeling of jealousy comes over her husband and he suspects his wife who has defiled herself—or if a feeling of jealousy comes over him and he suspects her even though she has not defiled herself—
Numbers5:15 then he is to bring his wife to the priest. He must also bring for her an offering of a tenth of an ephah of barley flour. He is not to pour oil over it or put frankincense on it
Numbers5:16 The priest is to bring the wife forward and have her stand before the Lord.
Numbers5:17 Then he is to take some holy water in a clay jar and put some of the dust from the tabernacle floor into the water.
Numbers5:18 After the priest has the woman stand before the Lord
Numbers5:19 And he is to put the woman under oath and say to her
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under your husband’s authority and have defiled yourself and lain carnally with a man other than your husband’—
Numbers5:21 and the priest shall have the woman swear under the oath of the curse—‘then may the Lord make you an attested curse among your people by making your thigh shrivel and your belly swell.
Numbers5:22 May this water that brings a curse enter your stomach and cause your belly to swell and your thigh to shrivel.’ Then the woman is to say
Numbers5:23 And the priest shall write these curses on a scroll and wash them off into the bitter water.
Numbers5:24 He is to have the woman drink the bitter water that brings a curse
Numbers5:25 The priest shall take from her hand the grain offering for jealousy
Numbers5:26 Then the priest is to take a handful of the grain offering as a memorial portion and burn it on the altar; after that he is to have the woman drink the water.
Numbers5:27 When he has made her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself and is clean
Numbers5:29 This is the law of jealousy when a wife goes astray and defiles herself while under her husband’s authority
Numbers5:30 or when a feeling of jealousy comes over a husband and he suspects his wife. He is to have the woman stand before the Lord
Numbers5:31 The husband will be free from guilt
Numbers6:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them that if a man or woman makes a special vow
Numbers6:3 he is to abstain from wine and strong drink. He must not drink vinegar made from wine or strong drink
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation
Numbers6:5 For the entire period of his vow of separation
Numbers6:6 Throughout the days of his separation to the Lord
Numbers6:7 Even if his father or mother or brother or sister should die
Numbers6:8 Throughout the time of his separation
Numbers6:9 If someone suddenly dies in his presence and defiles his consecrated head of hair
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he must bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers6:11 And the priest is to offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to make atonement for him
Numbers6:12 He must rededicate his time of separation to the Lord and bring a year-old male lamb as a guilt offering. But the preceding days shall not be counted
Numbers6:13 Now this is the law of the Nazirite when his time of separation is complete: He must be brought to the entrance to the Tent of Meeting
Numbers6:14 and he is to present an offering to the Lord of an unblemished year-old male lamb as a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 together with their grain offerings and drink offerings—and a basket of unleavened cakes made from fine flour mixed with oil and unleavened wafers coated with oil.
Numbers6:16 The priest is to present all these before the Lord and make the sin offering and the burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 He shall also offer the ram as a peace offering to the Lord
Numbers6:18 Then at the entrance to the Tent of Meeting
Numbers6:19 And the priest is to take the boiled shoulder from the ram
Numbers6:20 The priest shall then wave them as a wave offering before the Lord. This is a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the law of the Nazirite who vows his offering to the Lord for his separation
Numbers6:22 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:23 “Tell Aaron and his sons: This is how you are to bless the Israelites. Say to them:
Numbers6:24 ‘May the Lord bless you and keep you;
Numbers6:25 may the Lord cause His face to shine upon you and be gracious to you;
Numbers6:26 may the Lord lift up His countenance toward you and give you peace.’
Numbers6:27 So they shall put My name on the Israelites

Numbers7:2 And the leaders of Israel

Numbers7:4 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers7:5 “Accept these gifts from them
Numbers7:6 So Moses took the carts and oxen and gave them to the Levites.

Numbers7:8 and he gave the Merarites four carts and eight oxen
Numbers7:9 But he did not give any to the Kohathites
Numbers7:10 When the altar was anointed

Numbers7:83 and a peace offering of two oxen

Numbers7:85 Each silver platter weighed a hundred and thirty shekels
Numbers7:86 The twelve gold dishes filled with incense weighed ten shekels each
Numbers7:87 All the livestock for the burnt offering totaled twelve bulls
Numbers7:88 All the livestock sacrificed for the peace offering totaled twenty-four bulls
Numbers7:89 When Moses entered the Tent of Meeting to speak with the Lord
Numbers8:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:2 “Speak to Aaron and tell him: ‘When you set up the seven lamps
Numbers8:3 And Aaron did so; he set up the lamps facing toward the front of the lampstand
Numbers8:4 This is how the lampstand was constructed: it was made of hammered gold from its base to its blossoms
Numbers8:5 Again the Lord spoke to Moses
Numbers8:6 “Take the Levites from among the Israelites and make them ceremonially clean.
Numbers8:7 This is what you must do to cleanse them: Sprinkle them with the water of purification. Have them shave their whole bodies and wash their clothes
Numbers8:8 Then have them take a young bull with its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers8:9 Bring the Levites before the Tent of Meeting and assemble the whole congregation of Israel.
Numbers8:10 You are to present the Levites before the Lord and have the Israelites lay their hands upon them.
Numbers8:11 Aaron is to present the Levites before the Lord as a wave offering from the sons of Israel
Numbers8:12 And the Levites are to lay their hands on the heads of the bulls
Numbers8:13 You are to have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons and then present them before the Lord as a wave offering.
Numbers8:14 In this way you shall separate the Levites from the rest of the Israelites
Numbers8:15 After you have cleansed them and presented them as a wave offering
Numbers8:16 For the Levites have been wholly given to Me from among the sons of Israel. I have taken them for Myself in place of all who come first from the womb
Numbers8:17 For every firstborn male in Israel is Mine
Numbers8:18 But I have taken the Levites in place of all the firstborn among the sons of Israel.
Numbers8:19 And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and his sons from among the Israelites
Numbers8:20 So Moses
Numbers8:21 The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes
Numbers8:22 After that
Numbers8:23 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:24 “This applies to the Levites: Men twenty-five years of age or older shall enter to perform the service in the work at the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers8:25 But at the age of fifty
Numbers8:26 After that
Numbers9:1 In the first month of the second year after Israel had come out of the land of Egypt
Numbers9:2 “The Israelites are to observe the Passover at its appointed time.
Numbers9:3 You are to observe it at the appointed time
Numbers9:4 So Moses told the Israelites to observe the Passover
Numbers9:5 and they did so in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers9:6 But there were some men who were unclean due to a dead body
Numbers9:7 and said to Moses
Numbers9:8 “Wait here until I find out what the Lord commands concerning you
Numbers9:9 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers9:10 “Tell the Israelites: ‘When any one of you or your descendants is unclean because of a dead body
Numbers9:11 Such people are to observe it at twilight on the fourteenth day of the second month. They are to eat the lamb
Numbers9:12 they may not leave any of it until morning or break any of its bones. They must observe the Passover according to all its statutes.
Numbers9:13 But if a man who is ceremonially clean and is not on a journey still fails to observe the Passover
Numbers9:14 If a foreigner dwelling among you wants to observe the Passover to the Lord
Numbers9:15 On the day that the tabernacle
Numbers9:16 It remained that way continually; the cloud would cover the tabernacle by day
Numbers9:17 Whenever the cloud was lifted from above the Tent
Numbers9:18 At the Lord’s command the Israelites set out
Numbers9:19 Even when the cloud lingered over the tabernacle for many days
Numbers9:20 Sometimes the cloud remained over the tabernacle for only a few days
Numbers9:21 Sometimes the cloud remained only from evening until morning
Numbers9:22 Whether the cloud lingered for two days
Numbers9:23 They camped at the Lord’s command
Numbers10:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers10:2 “Make two trumpets of hammered silver to be used for calling the congregation and for having the camps set out.
Numbers10:3 When both are sounded
Numbers10:4 But if only one is sounded
Numbers10:5 When you sound short blasts
Numbers10:6 When you sound the short blasts a second time
Numbers10:7 To convene the assembly
Numbers10:8 The sons of Aaron
Numbers10:9 When you enter into battle in your land against an adversary who attacks you
Numbers10:10 And on your joyous occasions
Numbers10:11 On the twentieth day of the second month of the second year
Numbers10:12 and the Israelites set out from the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers10:13 They set out this first time according to the Lord’s command through Moses.
Numbers10:14 First
Numbers10:15 Nethanel son of Zuar was over the division of the tribe of Issachar
Numbers10:16 and Eliab son of Helon was over the division of the tribe of Zebulun.
Numbers10:17 Then the tabernacle was taken down
Numbers10:18 Then the divisions of the camp of Reuben set out under their standard
Numbers10:19 Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai was over the division of the tribe of Simeon
Numbers10:20 and Eliasaph son of Deuel was over the division of the tribe of Gad.
Numbers10:21 Then the Kohathites set out
Numbers10:22 Next
Numbers10:23 Gamaliel son of Pedahzur was over the division of the tribe of Manasseh
Numbers10:24 and Abidan son of Gideoni was over the division of the tribe of Benjamin.
Numbers10:25 Finally
Numbers10:26 Pagiel son of Ocran was over the division of the tribe of Asher
Numbers10:27 and Ahira son of Enan was over the division of the tribe of Naphtali.
Numbers10:28 This was the order of march for the Israelite divisions as they set out.
Numbers10:29 Then Moses said to Hobab
Numbers10:30 “I will not go
Numbers10:31 “Please do not leave us
Numbers10:32 If you come with us
Numbers10:33 So they set out on a three-day journey from the mountain of the Lord
Numbers10:34 And the cloud of the Lord was over them by day when they set out from the camp.
Numbers10:35 Whenever the ark set out
Numbers10:36 And when it came to rest
Numbers11:1 Soon the people began to complain about their hardship in the hearing of the Lord
Numbers11:2 And the people cried out to Moses
Numbers11:3 So that place was called Taberah
Numbers11:4 Meanwhile
Numbers11:5 We remember the fish we ate freely in Egypt
Numbers11:6 But now our appetite is gone; there is nothing to see but this manna!”
Numbers11:7 Now the manna resembled coriander seed
Numbers11:8 The people walked around and gathered it
Numbers11:9 When the dew fell on the camp at night
Numbers11:10 Then Moses heard the people of family after family weeping at the entrances to their tents
Numbers11:11 So Moses asked the Lord
Numbers11:12 Did I conceive all these people? Did I give them birth
Numbers11:13 Where can I get meat for all these people? For they keep crying out to me
Numbers11:14 I cannot carry all these people by myself; it is too burdensome for me.
Numbers11:15 If this is how You are going to treat me
Numbers11:16 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers11:17 And I will come down and speak with you there
Numbers11:18 And say to the people: Consecrate yourselves for tomorrow
Numbers11:19 You will eat it not for one or two days
Numbers11:20 but for a whole month—until it comes out of your nostrils and makes you nauseous—because you have rejected the Lord
Numbers11:21 But Moses replied
Numbers11:22 If all our flocks and herds were slaughtered for them
Numbers11:23 The Lord answered Moses
Numbers11:24 So Moses went out and relayed to the people the words of the Lord
Numbers11:25 Then the Lord came down in the cloud and spoke to him
Numbers11:26 Two men
Numbers11:27 A young man ran and reported to Moses
Numbers11:28 Joshua son of Nun
Numbers11:29 But Moses replied
Numbers11:30 Then Moses returned to the camp
Numbers11:31 Now a wind sent by the Lord came up
Numbers11:32 All that day and night
Numbers11:33 But while the meat was still between their teeth
Numbers11:34 So they called that place Kibroth-hattaavah
Numbers11:35 From Kibroth-hattaavah the people moved on to Hazeroth
Numbers12:1 Then Miriam and Aaron criticized Moses because of the Cushite woman he had married
Numbers12:2 “Does the Lord speak only through Moses?” they said. “Does He not also speak through us?” And the Lord heard this.
Numbers12:3 Now Moses was a very humble man
Numbers12:4 And suddenly the Lord said to Moses
Numbers12:5 and the Lord came down in a pillar of cloud
Numbers12:6 He said
Numbers12:7 But this is not so with My servant Moses; he is faithful in all My house.
Numbers12:8 I speak with him face to face
Numbers12:9 So the anger of the Lord burned against them
Numbers12:10 As the cloud lifted from above the Tent
Numbers12:11 and said to Moses
Numbers12:12 Please do not let her be like a stillborn infant whose flesh is half consumed when he comes out of his mother’s womb.”
Numbers12:13 So Moses cried out to the Lord
Numbers12:14 But the Lord answered Moses
Numbers12:15 So Miriam was confined outside the camp for seven days
Numbers12:16 After that
Numbers13:1 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers13:2 “Send out for yourself men to spy out the land of Canaan
Numbers13:3 So at the consent of the Lord
Numbers13:4 and these were their names: From the tribe of Reuben
Numbers13:5 from the tribe of Simeon
Numbers13:6 from the tribe of Judah
Numbers13:7 from the tribe of Issachar
Numbers13:8 from the tribe of Ephraim
Numbers13:9 from the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers13:10 from the tribe of Zebulun
Numbers13:11 from the tribe of Manasseh (a tribe of Joseph)
Numbers13:12 from the tribe of Dan
Numbers13:13 from the tribe of Asher
Numbers13:14 from the tribe of Naphtali
Numbers13:15 and from the tribe of Gad
Numbers13:16 These were the names of the men Moses sent to spy out the land; and Moses gave to Hoshea son of Nun the name Joshua.
Numbers13:17 When Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan
Numbers13:18 See what the land is like and whether its people are strong or weak
Numbers13:19 Is the land where they live good or bad? Are the cities where they dwell open camps or fortifications?
Numbers13:20 Is the soil fertile or unproductive? Are there trees in it or not? Be courageous
Numbers13:21 So they went up and spied out the land from the Wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob
Numbers13:22 They went up through the Negev and came to Hebron
Numbers13:23 When they came to the Valley of Eshcol
Numbers13:24 Because of the cluster of grapes the Israelites cut there

Numbers13:26 and they went back to Moses

Numbers13:28 Nevertheless
Numbers13:29 The Amalekites live in the land of the Negev; the Hittites

Numbers13:33 We even saw the Nephilim there—the descendants of Anak that come from the Nephilim! We seemed like grasshoppers in our own sight
Numbers14:1 Then the whole congregation lifted up their voices and cried out
Numbers14:2 All the Israelites grumbled against Moses and Aaron
Numbers14:3 Why is the Lord bringing us into this land to fall by the sword? Our wives and children will become plunder. Would it not be better for us to go back to Egypt?”
Numbers14:4 So they said to one another
Numbers14:5 Then Moses and Aaron fell facedown before the whole assembly of the congregation of Israel.
Numbers14:6 Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh
Numbers14:7 and said to the whole congregation of Israel
Numbers14:8 If the Lord delights in us
Numbers14:9 Only do not rebel against the Lord
Numbers14:10 But the whole congregation threatened to stone Joshua and Caleb. Then the glory of the Lord appeared to all the Israelites at the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers14:11 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers14:12 I will strike them with a plague and destroy them—and I will make you into a nation greater and mightier than they are.”
Numbers14:13 But Moses said to the Lord
Numbers14:14 And they will tell it to the inhabitants of this land. They have already heard that You
Numbers14:15 If You kill this people as one man
Numbers14:16 ‘Because the Lord was unable to bring this people into the land He swore to give them
Numbers14:17 So now I pray
Numbers14:18 ‘The Lord is slow to anger and abounding in loving devotion
Numbers14:19 Pardon
Numbers14:20 “I have pardoned them as you requested
Numbers14:21 “Yet as surely as I live and as surely as the whole earth is filled with the glory of the Lord
Numbers14:22 not one of the men who have seen My glory and the signs I performed in Egypt and in the wilderness—yet have tested Me and disobeyed Me these ten times—
Numbers14:23 not one will ever see the land that I swore to give their fathers. None of those who have treated Me with contempt will see it.
Numbers14:24 But because My servant Caleb has a different spirit and has followed Me wholeheartedly
Numbers14:25 Now since the Amalekites and Canaanites are living in the valleys

Numbers14:29 Your bodies will fall in this wilderness—all who were numbered in the census
Numbers14:30 Surely none of you will enter the land in which I swore to settle you
Numbers14:31 But I will bring your children
Numbers14:32 As for you

Numbers14:34 In keeping with the forty days you spied out the land
Numbers14:35 I
