Numbers15:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:2 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: After you enter the land that I am giving you as a home
Numbers15:3 and you present an offering made by fire to the Lord from the herd or flock to produce a pleasing aroma to the Lord—either a burnt offering or a sacrifice
Numbers15:4 then the one presenting his offering to the Lord shall also present a grain offering of a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a quarter hin of olive oil.
Numbers15:5 With the burnt offering or sacrifice of each lamb
Numbers15:6 With a ram you are to prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a third of a hin of olive oil
Numbers15:7 and a third of a hin of wine as a drink offering
Numbers15:8 When you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or sacrifice to fulfill a vow or as a peace offering to the Lord
Numbers15:9 present with the bull a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with half a hin of olive oil.
Numbers15:10 Also present half a hin of wine as a drink offering. It is an offering made by fire
Numbers15:11 This is to be done for each bull
Numbers15:12 This is how you must prepare each one
Numbers15:13 Everyone who is native-born shall prepare these things in this way when he presents an offering made by fire as a pleasing aroma to the Lord.
Numbers15:14 And for the generations to come
Numbers15:15 The assembly is to have the same statute both for you and for the foreign resident; it is a permanent statute for the generations to come. You and the foreigner shall be the same before the Lord.
Numbers15:16 The same law and the same ordinance will apply both to you and to the foreigner residing with you.”
Numbers15:17 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:18 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land to which I am bringing you
Numbers15:19 and you eat the food of the land
Numbers15:20 From the first of your dough
Numbers15:21 Throughout your generations
Numbers15:22 Now if you stray unintentionally and do not obey all these commandments that the Lord has spoken to Moses—
Numbers15:23 all that the Lord has commanded you through Moses from the day the Lord gave them and continuing through the generations to come—
Numbers15:24 and if it was done unintentionally without the knowledge of the congregation
Numbers15:25 The priest is to make atonement for the whole congregation of Israel
Numbers15:26 Then the whole congregation of Israel and the foreigners residing among them will be forgiven
Numbers15:27 Also
Numbers15:28 And the priest shall make atonement before the Lord on behalf of the person who erred by sinning unintentionally; and when atonement has been made for him
Numbers15:29 You shall have the same law for the one who acts in error
Numbers15:30 But the person who sins defiantly
Numbers15:31 He shall certainly be cut off
Numbers15:32 While the Israelites were in the wilderness
Numbers15:33 Those who found the man gathering wood brought him to Moses
Numbers15:34 and because it had not been declared what should be done to him
Numbers15:35 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:36 So the whole congregation took the man outside the camp and stoned him to death
Numbers15:37 Later
Numbers15:38 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them that throughout the generations to come they are to make for themselves tassels for the corners of their garments
Numbers15:39 These will serve as tassels for you to look at
Numbers15:40 Then you will remember and obey all My commandments
Numbers15:41 I am the Lord your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord your God.”

Numbers19:1 Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 “This is the statute of the law that the Lord has commanded: Instruct the Israelites to bring you an unblemished red heifer that has no defect and has never been placed under a yoke.
Numbers19:3 Give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 Eleazar the priest is to take some of its blood on his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the Tent of Meeting.
Numbers19:5 Then the heifer must be burned in his sight. Its hide
Numbers19:6 The priest is to take cedar wood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and bathe his body in water; after that he may enter the camp
Numbers19:8 The one who burned the heifer must also wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:9 Then a man who is ceremonially clean is to gather up the ashes of the heifer and store them in a ceremonially clean place outside the camp. They must be kept by the congregation of Israel for preparing the water of purification; this is for purification from sin.
Numbers19:10 The man who has gathered up the ashes of the heifer must also wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 Whoever touches any dead body will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 He must purify himself with the water on the third day and on the seventh day; then he will be clean. But if he does not purify himself on the third and seventh days
Numbers19:13 Anyone who touches a human corpse and fails to purify himself defiles the tabernacle of the Lord. That person must be cut off from Israel. He remains unclean
Numbers19:14 This is the law when a person dies in a tent: Everyone who enters the tent and everyone already in the tent will be unclean for seven days
Numbers19:15 and any open container without a lid fastened on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 Anyone in the open field who touches someone who has been killed by the sword or has died of natural causes
Numbers19:17 For the purification of the unclean person
Numbers19:18 Then a man who is ceremonially clean is to take some hyssop
Numbers19:19 The man who is ceremonially clean is to sprinkle the unclean person on the third day and on the seventh day. After he purifies the unclean person on the seventh day
Numbers19:20 But if a person who is unclean does not purify himself
Numbers19:21 This is a permanent statute for the people: The one who sprinkles the water of purification must wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 Anything the unclean person touches will become unclean

Numbers28:1 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers28:2 “Command the Israelites and say to them: See that you present to Me at its appointed time the food for My offerings by fire
Numbers28:3 And tell them that this is the offering made by fire you are to present to the Lord as a regular burnt offering each day: two unblemished year-old male lambs.
Numbers28:4 Offer one lamb in the morning and the other at twilight
Numbers28:5 along with a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a grain offering
Numbers28:6 This is a regular burnt offering established at Mount Sinai as a pleasing aroma
Numbers28:7 The drink offering accompanying each lamb shall be a quarter hin. Pour out the offering of fermented drink to the Lord in the sanctuary area.
Numbers28:8 And offer the second lamb at twilight
Numbers28:9 On the Sabbath day
Numbers28:10 This is the burnt offering for every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 At the beginning of every month
Numbers28:12 along with three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering with each bull
Numbers28:13 and a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering with each lamb. This is a burnt offering
Numbers28:14 Their drink offerings shall be half a hin of wine with each bull
Numbers28:15 In addition to the regular burnt offering with its drink offering
Numbers28:16 The fourteenth day of the first month is the Lord’s Passover.
Numbers28:17 On the fifteenth day of this month
Numbers28:18 On the first day there is to be a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work.
Numbers28:19 Present to the Lord an offering made by fire
Numbers28:20 The grain offering shall consist of fine flour mixed with oil; offer three-tenths of an ephah with each bull
Numbers28:21 and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.
Numbers28:22 Include one male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for you.
Numbers28:23 You are to present these in addition to the regular morning burnt offering.
Numbers28:24 Offer the same food each day for seven days as an offering made by fire
Numbers28:25 On the seventh day you shall hold a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work.
Numbers28:26 On the day of firstfruits
Numbers28:27 Present a burnt offering of two young bulls
Numbers28:28 together with their grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil—three-tenths of an ephah with each bull
Numbers28:29 and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.
Numbers28:30 Include one male goat to make atonement for you.
Numbers28:31 Offer them with their drink offerings in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering. The animals must be unblemished.
Numbers29:1 “On the first day of the seventh month
Numbers29:2 As a pleasing aroma to the Lord
Numbers29:3 together with their grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil—three-tenths of an ephah with the bull
Numbers29:4 and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven male lambs.
Numbers29:5 Include one male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for you.
Numbers29:6 These are in addition to the monthly and daily burnt offerings with their prescribed grain offerings and drink offerings. They are a pleasing aroma

Numbers29:28 Include one male goat as a sin offering

Numbers29:30 along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:31 Include one male goat as a sin offering
Numbers29:32 On the seventh day you are to present seven bulls
Numbers29:33 along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:34 Include one male goat as a sin offering
Numbers29:35 On the eighth day you are to hold a solemn assembly; you must not do any regular work.
Numbers29:36 As a pleasing aroma to the Lord
Numbers29:37 along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:38 Include one male goat as a sin offering

Numbers30:1 Then Moses said to the heads of the tribes of Israel
Numbers30:2 If a man makes a vow to the Lord or swears an oath to obligate himself by a pledge
Numbers30:3 And if a woman in her father’s house during her youth makes a vow to the Lord or obligates herself by a pledge
Numbers30:4 and her father hears about her vow or pledge but says nothing to her
Numbers30:5 But if her father prohibits her on the day he hears about it
Numbers30:6 If a woman marries while under a vow or rash promise by which she has bound herself
Numbers30:7 and her husband hears of it but says nothing to her on that day
Numbers30:8 But if her husband prohibits her when he hears of it
Numbers30:9 Every vow a widow or divorced woman pledges to fulfill is binding on her.
Numbers30:10 If a woman in her husband’s house has made a vow or put herself under an obligation with an oath
Numbers30:11 and her husband hears of it but says nothing to her and does not prohibit her
Numbers30:12 But if her husband nullifies them on the day he hears of them
Numbers30:13 Her husband may confirm or nullify any vow or any sworn pledge to deny herself.
Numbers30:14 But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day
Numbers30:15 But if he nullifies them after he hears of them
Numbers30:16 These are the statutes that the Lord commanded Moses concerning the relationship between a man and his wife

Numbers35:1 Again the Lord spoke to Moses on the plains of Moab by the Jordan across from Jericho:
Numbers35:2 “Command the Israelites to give
Numbers35:3 The cities will be for them to live in
Numbers35:4 The pasturelands around the cities you are to give the Levites will extend a thousand cubits from the wall on every side.
Numbers35:5 You are also to measure two thousand cubits outside the city on the east
Numbers35:6 Six of the cities you give the Levites are to be appointed as cities of refuge
Numbers35:7 The total number of cities you give the Levites will be forty-eight
Numbers35:8 The cities that you apportion from the territory of the Israelites should be given to the Levites in proportion to the inheritance of each tribe: more from a larger tribe and less from a smaller one.”
Numbers35:9 Then the Lord said to Moses
Numbers35:10 “Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you cross the Jordan into the land of Canaan
Numbers35:11 designate cities to serve as your cities of refuge
Numbers35:12 You are to have these cities as a refuge from the avenger
Numbers35:13 The cities you select will be your six cities of refuge.
Numbers35:14 Select three cities across the Jordan and three in the land of Canaan as cities of refuge.
Numbers35:15 These six cities will serve as a refuge for the Israelites and for the foreigner or stranger among them
Numbers35:16 If
Numbers35:17 Or if anyone has in his hand a stone of deadly size
Numbers35:18 If anyone has in his hand a deadly object of wood
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood is to put the murderer to death; when he finds him
Numbers35:20 Likewise
Numbers35:21 or if in hostility he strikes him with his hand and he dies
Numbers35:22 But if anyone pushes a person suddenly
Numbers35:23 or without looking drops a heavy stone that kills him
Numbers35:24 then the congregation must judge between the slayer and the avenger of blood according to these ordinances.
Numbers35:25 The assembly is to protect the manslayer from the hand of the avenger of blood. Then the assembly will return him to the city of refuge to which he fled
Numbers35:26 But if the manslayer ever goes outside the limits of the city of refuge to which he fled
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside of his city of refuge and kills him
Numbers35:28 because the manslayer must remain in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may he return to the land he owns.
Numbers35:29 This will be a statutory ordinance for you for the generations to come
Numbers35:30 If anyone kills a person
Numbers35:31 You are not to accept a ransom for the life of a murderer who deserves to die; he must surely be put to death.
Numbers35:32 Nor should you accept a ransom for the person who flees to a city of refuge and allow him to return and live on his own land before the death of the high priest.
Numbers35:33 Do not pollute the land where you live
Numbers35:34 Do not defile the land where you live and where I dwell. For I

Deuteronomy1:1 These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel in the wilderness east of the Jordan—in the Arabah opposite Suph—between Paran and Tophel
Deuteronomy1:2 It is an eleven-day journey from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea by way of Mount Seir.
Deuteronomy1:3 In the fortieth year
Deuteronomy1:4 This was after he had defeated Sihon king of the Amorites
Deuteronomy1:5 On the east side of the Jordan in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy1:6 The Lord our God said to us at Horeb: “You have stayed at this mountain long enough.
Deuteronomy1:7 Resume your journey and go to the hill country of the Amorites; go to all the neighboring peoples in the Arabah
Deuteronomy1:8 See
Deuteronomy1:9 At that time I said to you
Deuteronomy1:10 The Lord your God has multiplied you
Deuteronomy1:11 May the Lord
Deuteronomy1:12 But how can I bear your troubles
Deuteronomy1:13 Choose for yourselves wise
Deuteronomy1:14 And you answered me and said
Deuteronomy1:15 So I took the leaders of your tribes
Deuteronomy1:16 At that time I charged your judges: “Hear the disputes between your brothers
Deuteronomy1:17 Show no partiality in judging; hear both small and great alike. Do not be intimidated by anyone
Deuteronomy1:18 And at that time I commanded you all the things you were to do.

Deuteronomy12:31 You must not worship the Lord your God in this way
Deuteronomy12:32 See that you do everything I command you; do not add to it or subtract from it.
Deuteronomy13:1 If a prophet or dreamer of dreams arises among you and proclaims a sign or wonder to you
Deuteronomy13:2 and if the sign or wonder he has spoken to you comes about
Deuteronomy13:3 you must not listen to the words of that prophet or dreamer. For the Lord your God is testing you to find out whether you love Him with all your heart and with all your soul.
Deuteronomy13:4 You are to follow the Lord your God and fear Him. Keep His commandments and listen to His voice; serve Him and hold fast to Him.
Deuteronomy13:5 Such a prophet or dreamer must be put to death
Deuteronomy13:6 If your very own brother
Deuteronomy13:7 the gods of the peoples around you
Deuteronomy13:8 you must not yield to him or listen to him. Show him no pity
Deuteronomy13:9 Instead
Deuteronomy13:10 Stone him to death for trying to turn you away from the Lord your God
Deuteronomy13:11 Then all Israel will hear and be afraid
Deuteronomy13:12 If
Deuteronomy13:13 that wicked men have arisen from among you and have led the people of their city astray
Deuteronomy13:14 then you must inquire
Deuteronomy13:15 you must surely put the inhabitants of that city to the sword. Devote to destruction all its people and livestock.
Deuteronomy13:16 And you are to gather all its plunder in the middle of the public square
Deuteronomy13:17 Nothing devoted to destruction shall cling to your hands
Deuteronomy13:18 because you obey the Lord your God
Deuteronomy14:1 You are sons of the Lord your God; do not cut yourselves or shave your foreheads on behalf of the dead
Deuteronomy14:2 for you are a people holy to the Lord your God. The Lord has chosen you to be a people for His prized possession out of all the peoples on the face of the earth.
Deuteronomy14:3 You must not eat any detestable thing.
Deuteronomy14:4 These are the animals that you may eat: The ox
Deuteronomy14:5 the deer
Deuteronomy14:6 You may eat any animal that has a split hoof divided in two and that chews the cud.
Deuteronomy14:7 But of those that chew the cud or have a completely divided hoof
Deuteronomy14:8 as well as the pig; though it has a divided hoof
Deuteronomy14:9 Of all the creatures that live in the water
Deuteronomy14:10 but you may not eat anything that does not have fins and scales; it is unclean for you.
Deuteronomy14:11 You may eat any clean bird
Deuteronomy14:12 but these you may not eat: the eagle
Deuteronomy14:13 the red kite
Deuteronomy14:14 any kind of raven
Deuteronomy14:15 the ostrich
Deuteronomy14:16 the little owl
Deuteronomy14:17 the desert owl
Deuteronomy14:18 the stork
Deuteronomy14:19 All flying insects are unclean for you; they may not be eaten.
Deuteronomy14:20 But you may eat any clean bird.
Deuteronomy14:21 You are not to eat any carcass; you may give it to the foreigner residing within your gates
Deuteronomy14:22 You must be sure to set aside a tenth of all the produce brought forth each year from your fields.
Deuteronomy14:23 And you are to eat a tenth of your grain
Deuteronomy14:24 But if the distance is too great for you to carry that with which the Lord your God has blessed you
Deuteronomy14:25 then exchange it for money
Deuteronomy14:26 Then you may spend the money on anything you desire: cattle
Deuteronomy14:27 And do not neglect the Levite within your gates
Deuteronomy14:28 At the end of every three years
Deuteronomy14:29 Then the Levite (because he has no portion or inheritance among you)
Deuteronomy15:1 At the end of every seven years you must cancel debts.
Deuteronomy15:2 This is the manner of remission: Every creditor shall cancel what he has loaned to his neighbor. He is not to collect anything from his neighbor or brother
Deuteronomy15:3 You may collect something from a foreigner
Deuteronomy15:4 There will be no poor among you
Deuteronomy15:5 if only you obey the Lord your God and are careful to follow all these commandments I am giving you today.
Deuteronomy15:6 When the Lord your God blesses you as He has promised
Deuteronomy15:7 If there is a poor man among your brothers within any of the gates in the land that the Lord your God is giving you
Deuteronomy15:8 Instead
Deuteronomy15:9 Be careful not to harbor this wicked thought in your heart: “The seventh year
Deuteronomy15:10 Give generously to him
Deuteronomy15:11 For there will never cease to be poor in the land; that is why I am commanding you to open wide your hand to your brother and to the poor and needy in your land.
Deuteronomy15:12 If a fellow Hebrew
Deuteronomy15:13 And when you release him
Deuteronomy15:14 You are to furnish him liberally from your flock
Deuteronomy15:15 Remember that you were slaves in the land of Egypt
Deuteronomy15:16 But if your servant says to you
Deuteronomy15:17 then take an awl and pierce it through his ear into the door
Deuteronomy15:18 Do not regard it as a hardship to set your servant free
Deuteronomy15:19 You must set apart to the Lord your God every firstborn male produced by your herds and flocks. You are not to put the firstborn of your oxen to work
Deuteronomy15:20 Each year you and your household are to eat it before the Lord your God in the place the Lord will choose.
Deuteronomy15:21 But if an animal has a defect
Deuteronomy15:22 Eat it within your gates; both the ceremonially unclean and clean may eat it as they would a gazelle or a deer.
Deuteronomy15:23 But you must not eat the blood; pour it on the ground like water.
Deuteronomy16:1 Observe the month of Abib and celebrate the Passover to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:2 You are to offer to the Lord your God the Passover sacrifice from the herd or flock in the place the Lord will choose as a dwelling for His Name.
Deuteronomy16:3 You must not eat leavened bread with it; for seven days you are to eat with it unleavened bread
Deuteronomy16:4 No leaven is to be found in all your land for seven days
Deuteronomy16:5 You are not to sacrifice the Passover animal in any of the towns that the Lord your God is giving you.
Deuteronomy16:6 You must only offer the Passover sacrifice at the place the Lord your God will choose as a dwelling for His Name. Do this in the evening as the sun sets
Deuteronomy16:7 And you shall roast it and eat it in the place the Lord your God will choose
Deuteronomy16:8 For six days you must eat unleavened bread
Deuteronomy16:9 You are to count off seven weeks from the time you first put the sickle to the standing grain.
Deuteronomy16:10 And you shall celebrate the Feast of Weeks to the Lord your God with a freewill offering that you give in proportion to how the Lord your God has blessed you
Deuteronomy16:11 and you shall rejoice before the Lord your God in the place He will choose as a dwelling for His Name—you
Deuteronomy16:12 Remember that you were slaves in Egypt
Deuteronomy16:13 You are to celebrate the Feast of Tabernacles for seven days after you have gathered the produce of your threshing floor and your winepress.
Deuteronomy16:14 And you shall rejoice in your feast—you
Deuteronomy16:15 For seven days you shall celebrate a feast to the Lord your God in the place He will choose
Deuteronomy16:16 Three times a year all your men are to appear before the Lord your God in the place He will choose: at the Feast of Unleavened Bread
Deuteronomy16:17 Everyone must appear with a gift as he is able
Deuteronomy16:18 You are to appoint judges and officials for your tribes in every town that the Lord your God is giving you. They are to judge the people with righteous judgment.
Deuteronomy16:19 Do not deny justice or show partiality. Do not accept a bribe
Deuteronomy16:20 Pursue justice
Deuteronomy16:21 Do not set up any wooden Asherah pole next to the altar you will build for the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:22 and do not set up for yourselves a sacred pillar
Deuteronomy17:1 You shall not sacrifice to the Lord your God an ox or a sheep with any defect or serious flaw
Deuteronomy17:2 If a man or woman among you in one of the towns that the Lord your God gives you is found doing evil in the sight of the Lord your God by transgressing His covenant
Deuteronomy17:3 and going to worship other gods
Deuteronomy17:4 and if it is reported and you hear about it
Deuteronomy17:5 you must bring out to your gates the man or woman who has done this evil thing
Deuteronomy17:6 On the testimony of two or three witnesses a man shall be put to death
Deuteronomy17:7 The hands of the witnesses shall be the first in putting him to death
Deuteronomy17:8 If a case is too difficult for you to judge
Deuteronomy17:9 You are to go to the Levitical priests and to the judge who presides at that time. Inquire of them
Deuteronomy17:10 You must abide by the verdict they give you at the place the Lord will choose. Be careful to do everything they instruct you
Deuteronomy17:11 according to the terms of law they give and the verdict they proclaim. Do not turn aside to the right or to the left from the decision they declare to you.
Deuteronomy17:12 But the man who acts presumptuously
Deuteronomy17:13 Then all the people will hear and be afraid
Deuteronomy17:14 When you enter the land that the Lord your God is giving you and have taken possession of it and settled in it
Deuteronomy17:15 you are to appoint over yourselves the king whom the Lord your God shall choose. Appoint a king from among your brothers; you are not to set over yourselves a foreigner who is not one of your brothers.
Deuteronomy17:16 But the king must not acquire many horses for himself or send the people back to Egypt to acquire more horses
Deuteronomy17:17 He must not take many wives for himself
Deuteronomy17:18 When he is seated on his royal throne
Deuteronomy17:19 It is to remain with him
Deuteronomy17:20 Then his heart will not be exalted above his countrymen
Deuteronomy18:1 The Levitical priests—indeed the whole tribe of Levi—shall have no portion or inheritance with Israel. They are to eat the offerings made by fire to the Lord; that is their inheritance.
Deuteronomy18:2 Although they have no inheritance among their brothers
Deuteronomy18:3 This shall be the priests’ share from the people who offer a sacrifice
Deuteronomy18:4 You are to give them the firstfruits of your grain
Deuteronomy18:5 For the Lord your God has chosen Levi and his sons out of all your tribes to stand and minister in His name for all time.
Deuteronomy18:6 Now if a Levite moves from any town of residence throughout Israel and comes in all earnestness to the place the Lord will choose
Deuteronomy18:7 then he shall serve in the name of the Lord his God like all his fellow Levites who stand there before the Lord.
Deuteronomy18:8 They shall eat equal portions
Deuteronomy18:9 When you enter the land that the Lord your God is giving you
Deuteronomy18:10 Let no one be found among you who sacrifices his son or daughter in the fire
Deuteronomy18:11 casts spells
Deuteronomy18:12 For whoever does these things is detestable to the Lord. And because of these detestable things
Deuteronomy18:13 You must be blameless before the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy18:14 Though these nations
Deuteronomy18:15 The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet like me from among your brothers. You must listen to him.
Deuteronomy18:16 This is what you asked of the Lord your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly
Deuteronomy18:17 Then the Lord said to me
Deuteronomy18:18 I will raise up for them a prophet like you from among their brothers. I will put My words in his mouth
Deuteronomy18:19 And I will hold accountable anyone who does not listen to My words that the prophet speaks in My name.
Deuteronomy18:20 But if any prophet dares to speak a message in My name that I have not commanded him to speak
Deuteronomy18:21 You may ask in your heart
Deuteronomy18:22 When a prophet speaks in the name of the Lord and the message does not come to pass or come true
Deuteronomy19:1 When the Lord your God has cut off the nations whose land He is giving you
Deuteronomy19:2 then you are to set apart for yourselves three cities within the land that the Lord your God is giving you to possess.
Deuteronomy19:3 You are to build roads for yourselves and divide into three regions the land that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance
Deuteronomy19:4 Now this is the situation regarding the manslayer who flees to one of these cities to save his life
Deuteronomy19:5 If he goes into the forest with his neighbor to cut timber and swings his axe to chop down a tree
Deuteronomy19:6 Otherwise
Deuteronomy19:7 This is why I am commanding you to set apart for yourselves three cities.
Deuteronomy19:8 And if the Lord your God enlarges your territory
Deuteronomy19:9 and if you carefully keep all these commandments I am giving you today
Deuteronomy19:10 Thus innocent blood will not be shed in the land that the Lord your God is giving you as an inheritance
Deuteronomy19:11 If
Deuteronomy19:12 the elders of his city must send for him
Deuteronomy19:13 You must show him no pity. You are to purge from Israel the guilt of shedding innocent blood
Deuteronomy19:14 You must not move your neighbor’s boundary marker
Deuteronomy19:15 A lone witness is not sufficient to establish any wrongdoing or sin against a man
Deuteronomy19:16 If a false witness testifies against someone
Deuteronomy19:17 both parties to the dispute must stand in the presence of the Lord
Deuteronomy19:18 The judges shall investigate thoroughly
Deuteronomy19:19 you must do to him as he intended to do to his brother. So you must purge the evil from among you.
Deuteronomy19:20 Then the rest of the people will hear and be afraid
Deuteronomy19:21 You must show no pity: life for life

Deuteronomy20:2 When you are about to go into battle
Deuteronomy20:3 saying to them

Deuteronomy20:12 But if they refuse to make peace with you and wage war against you

Deuteronomy20:14 But the women

Deuteronomy20:17 For you must devote them to complete destruction —the Hittites

Deuteronomy21:1 If one is found slain
Deuteronomy21:2 your elders and judges must come out and measure the distance from the victim to the neighboring cities.
Deuteronomy21:3 Then the elders of the city nearest the victim shall take a heifer that has never been yoked or used for work
Deuteronomy21:4 bring the heifer to a valley with running water that has not been plowed or sown
Deuteronomy21:5 And the priests
Deuteronomy21:6 Then all the elders of the city nearest the victim shall wash their hands by the stream over the heifer whose neck has been broken
Deuteronomy21:7 and they shall declare
Deuteronomy21:8 Accept this atonement
Deuteronomy21:9 So you shall purge from among you the guilt of shedding innocent blood
Deuteronomy21:10 When you go to war against your enemies and the Lord your God delivers them into your hand and you take them captive
Deuteronomy21:11 if you see a beautiful woman among them
Deuteronomy21:12 then you shall bring her into your house. She must shave her head
Deuteronomy21:13 and put aside the clothing of her captivity. After she has lived in your house a full month and mourned her father and mother
Deuteronomy21:14 And if you are not pleased with her
Deuteronomy21:15 If a man has two wives
Deuteronomy21:16 when that man assigns his inheritance to his sons he must not appoint the son of the beloved wife as the firstborn over the son of the unloved wife.
Deuteronomy21:17 Instead
Deuteronomy21:18 If a man has a stubborn and rebellious son who does not obey his father and mother and does not listen to them when disciplined
Deuteronomy21:19 his father and mother are to lay hold of him and bring him to the elders of his city
Deuteronomy21:20 and say to the elders
Deuteronomy21:21 Then all the men of his city will stone him to death. So you must purge the evil from among you
Deuteronomy21:22 If a man has committed a sin worthy of death
Deuteronomy21:23 you must not leave the body on the tree overnight
Deuteronomy22:1 If you see your brother’s ox or sheep straying
Deuteronomy22:2 If your brother does not live near you
Deuteronomy22:3 And you shall do the same for his donkey
Deuteronomy22:4 If you see your brother’s donkey or ox fallen on the road
Deuteronomy22:5 A woman must not wear men’s clothing
Deuteronomy22:6 If you come across a bird’s nest with chicks or eggs
Deuteronomy22:7 You may take the young
Deuteronomy22:8 If you build a new house
Deuteronomy22:9 Do not plant your vineyard with two types of seed; if you do
Deuteronomy22:10 Do not plow with an ox and a donkey yoked together.
Deuteronomy22:11 Do not wear clothes of wool and linen woven together.
Deuteronomy22:12 You are to make tassels on the four corners of the cloak you wear.
Deuteronomy22:13 Suppose a man marries a woman
Deuteronomy22:14 and he then accuses her of shameful conduct and gives her a bad name
Deuteronomy22:15 Then the young woman’s father and mother shall bring the proof of her virginity to the city elders at the gate
Deuteronomy22:16 and say to the elders
Deuteronomy22:17 And now he has accused her of shameful conduct
Deuteronomy22:18 Then the elders of that city shall take the man and punish him.
Deuteronomy22:19 They are also to fine him a hundred shekels of silver and give them to the young woman’s father
Deuteronomy22:20 If
Deuteronomy22:21 she shall be brought to the door of her father’s house
Deuteronomy22:22 If a man is found lying with another man’s wife
Deuteronomy22:23 If there is a virgin pledged in marriage to a man
Deuteronomy22:24 you must take both of them out to the gate of that city and stone them to death—the young woman because she did not cry out in the city
Deuteronomy22:25 But if the man encounters a betrothed woman in the open country
Deuteronomy22:26 Do nothing to the young woman
Deuteronomy22:27 When he found her in the field
Deuteronomy22:28 If a man encounters a virgin who is not pledged in marriage
Deuteronomy22:29 then the man who lay with her must pay the young woman’s father fifty shekels of silver
Deuteronomy22:30 A man is not to marry his father’s wife
Deuteronomy23:1 No man with crushed or severed genitals may enter the assembly of the Lord.
Deuteronomy23:2 No one of illegitimate birth may enter the assembly of the Lord
Deuteronomy23:3 No Ammonite or Moabite or any of their descendants may enter the assembly of the Lord
Deuteronomy23:4 For they did not meet you with food and water on your way out of Egypt
Deuteronomy23:5 Yet the Lord your God would not listen to Balaam
Deuteronomy23:6 You are not to seek peace or prosperity from them as long as you live.
Deuteronomy23:7 Do not despise an Edomite
Deuteronomy23:8 The third generation of children born to them may enter the assembly of the Lord.
Deuteronomy23:9 When you are encamped against your enemies
Deuteronomy23:10 If any man among you becomes unclean because of a nocturnal emission
Deuteronomy23:11 When evening approaches
Deuteronomy23:12 You must have a place outside the camp to go and relieve yourself.
Deuteronomy23:13 And you must have a digging tool in your equipment so that when you relieve yourself you can dig a hole and cover up your excrement.
Deuteronomy23:14 For the Lord your God walks throughout your camp to protect you and deliver your enemies to you. Your camp must be holy
Deuteronomy23:15 Do not return a slave to his master if he has taken refuge with you.
Deuteronomy23:16 Let him live among you wherever he chooses
Deuteronomy23:17 No daughter or son of Israel is to be a shrine prostitute.
Deuteronomy23:18 You must not bring the wages of a prostitute
Deuteronomy23:19 Do not charge your brother interest on money
Deuteronomy23:20 You may charge a foreigner interest
Deuteronomy23:21 If you make a vow to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy23:22 But if you refrain from making a vow
Deuteronomy23:23 Be careful to follow through on what comes from your lips
Deuteronomy23:24 When you enter your neighbor’s vineyard
Deuteronomy23:25 When you enter your neighbor’s grainfield

Deuteronomy24:2 If
Deuteronomy24:3 and the second man hates her
Deuteronomy24:4 then the husband who divorced her first may not remarry her after she has been defiled
Deuteronomy24:5 If a man is newly married

Deuteronomy24:7 If a man is caught kidnapping one of his Israelite brothers
Deuteronomy24:8 In cases of infectious skin diseases

Deuteronomy24:20 When you beat the olives from your trees
Deuteronomy24:21 When you gather the grapes of your vineyard
Deuteronomy24:22 Remember that you were slaves in the land of Egypt. Therefore I am commanding you to do this.

Deuteronomy28:1 “Now if you faithfully obey the voice of the Lord your God and are careful to follow all His commandments I am giving you today
Deuteronomy28:2 And all these blessings will come upon you and overtake you
Deuteronomy28:3 You will be blessed in the city and blessed in the country.
Deuteronomy28:4 The fruit of your womb will be blessed
Deuteronomy28:5 Your basket and kneading bowl will be blessed.
Deuteronomy28:6 You will be blessed when you come in and blessed when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:7 The Lord will cause the enemies who rise up against you to be defeated before you. They will march out against you in one direction but flee from you in seven.
Deuteronomy28:8 The Lord will decree a blessing on your barns and on everything to which you put your hand; the Lord your God will bless you in the land He is giving you.
Deuteronomy28:9 The Lord will establish you as His holy people
Deuteronomy28:10 Then all the peoples of the earth will see that you are called by the name of the Lord
Deuteronomy28:11 The Lord will make you prosper abundantly—in the fruit of your womb
Deuteronomy28:12 The Lord will open the heavens
Deuteronomy28:13 The Lord will make you the head and not the tail; you will only move upward and never downward
Deuteronomy28:14 Do not turn aside to the right or to the left from any of the words I command you today
Deuteronomy28:15 If
Deuteronomy28:16 You will be cursed in the city and cursed in the country.
Deuteronomy28:17 Your basket and kneading bowl will be cursed.
Deuteronomy28:18 The fruit of your womb will be cursed
Deuteronomy28:19 You will be cursed when you come in and cursed when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:20 The Lord will send curses upon you
Deuteronomy28:21 The Lord will make the plague cling to you until He has exterminated you from the land that you are entering to possess.
Deuteronomy28:22 The Lord will strike you with wasting disease
Deuteronomy28:23 The sky over your head will be bronze
Deuteronomy28:24 The Lord will turn the rain of your land into dust and powder; it will descend on you from the sky until you are destroyed.
Deuteronomy28:25 The Lord will cause you to be defeated before your enemies. You will march out against them in one direction but flee from them in seven. You will be an object of horror to all the kingdoms of the earth.
Deuteronomy28:26 Your corpses will be food for all the birds of the air and beasts of the earth
Deuteronomy28:27 The Lord will afflict you with the boils of Egypt
Deuteronomy28:28 The Lord will afflict you with madness
Deuteronomy28:29 and at noon you will grope about like a blind man in the darkness. You will not prosper in your ways. Day after day you will be oppressed and plundered
Deuteronomy28:30 You will be pledged in marriage to a woman
Deuteronomy28:31 Your ox will be slaughtered before your eyes
Deuteronomy28:32 Your sons and daughters will be given to another nation
Deuteronomy28:33 A people you do not know will eat the produce of your land and of all your toil. All your days you will be oppressed and crushed.
Deuteronomy28:34 You will be driven mad by the sights you see.
Deuteronomy28:35 The Lord will afflict you with painful
Deuteronomy28:36 The Lord will bring you and the king you appoint to a nation neither you nor your fathers have known
Deuteronomy28:37 You will become an object of horror
Deuteronomy28:38 You will sow much seed in the field but harvest little
Deuteronomy28:39 You will plant and cultivate vineyards
Deuteronomy28:40 You will have olive trees throughout your territory but will never anoint yourself with oil
Deuteronomy28:41 You will father sons and daughters
Deuteronomy28:42 Swarms of locusts will consume all your trees and the produce of your land.
Deuteronomy28:43 The foreigner living among you will rise higher and higher above you
Deuteronomy28:44 He will lend to you
Deuteronomy28:45 All these curses will come upon you. They will pursue you and overtake you until you are destroyed
Deuteronomy28:46 These curses will be a sign and a wonder upon you and your descendants forever.
Deuteronomy28:47 Because you did not serve the Lord your God with joy and gladness of heart in all your abundance
Deuteronomy28:48 you will serve your enemies the Lord will send against you in famine
Deuteronomy28:49 The Lord will bring a nation from afar
Deuteronomy28:50 a ruthless nation with no respect for the old and no pity for the young.
Deuteronomy28:51 They will eat the offspring of your livestock and the produce of your land until you are destroyed. They will leave you no grain or new wine or oil
Deuteronomy28:52 They will besiege all the cities throughout your land
Deuteronomy28:53 Then you will eat the fruit of your womb
Deuteronomy28:54 The most gentle and refined man among you will begrudge his brother
Deuteronomy28:55 refusing to share with any of them the flesh of his children he will eat because he has nothing left in the siege and distress that your enemy will inflict on you within all your gates.
Deuteronomy28:56 The most gentle and refined woman among you
Deuteronomy28:57 the afterbirth that comes from between her legs and the children she bears
Deuteronomy28:58 If you are not careful to observe all the words of this law which are written in this book
Deuteronomy28:59 He will bring upon you and your descendants extraordinary disasters
Deuteronomy28:60 He will afflict you again with all the diseases you dreaded in Egypt
Deuteronomy28:61 The Lord will also bring upon you every sickness and plague not recorded in this Book of the Law
Deuteronomy28:62 You who were as numerous as the stars in the sky will be left few in number
Deuteronomy28:63 Just as it pleased the Lord to make you prosper and multiply
Deuteronomy28:64 Then the Lord will scatter you among all the nations
Deuteronomy28:65 Among those nations you will find no repose
Deuteronomy28:66 So your life will hang in doubt before you
Deuteronomy28:67 In the morning you will say
Deuteronomy28:68 The Lord will return you to Egypt in ships by a route that I said you should never see again. There you will sell yourselves to your enemies as male and female slaves

Deuteronomy30:11 For this commandment I give you today is not too difficult for you or beyond your reach.

Deuteronomy30:13 And it is not beyond the sea

Deuteronomy32:22 For a fire has been kindled by My anger
Deuteronomy32:23 I will heap disasters upon them; I will spend My arrows against them.
Deuteronomy32:24 They will be wasted from hunger and ravaged by pestilence and bitter plague; I will send the fangs of wild beasts against them
Deuteronomy32:25 Outside
Deuteronomy32:26 I would have said that I would cut them to pieces and blot out their memory from mankind

Deuteronomy32:31 For their rock is not like our Rock

Deuteronomy32:33 Their wine is the venom of serpents
Deuteronomy32:34 “Have I not stored up these things
Deuteronomy32:35 Vengeance is Mine; I will repay. In due time their foot will slip; for their day of disaster is near

Deuteronomy32:37 He will say: “Where are their gods
Deuteronomy32:38 which ate the fat of their sacrifices and drank the wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise up and help you; let them give you shelter!
Deuteronomy32:39 See now that I am He; there is no God besides Me. I bring death and I give life; I wound and I heal
Deuteronomy32:40 For I lift up My hand to heaven and declare: As surely as I live forever

Deuteronomy32:42 I will make My arrows drunk with blood

Joshua6:18 But keep away from the things devoted to destruction
Joshua6:19 For all the silver and gold and all the articles of bronze and iron are holy to the Lord; they must go into His treasury.”

Joshua8:3 So Joshua and the whole army set out to attack Ai. Joshua chose 30
Joshua8:4 with these orders: “Pay attention. You are to lie in ambush behind the city
Joshua8:5 Then I and all the troops with me will advance on the city. When they come out against us as they did the first time
Joshua8:6 They will pursue us until we have drawn them away from the city
Joshua8:7 you are to rise from the ambush and seize the city
Joshua8:8 And when you have taken the city
Joshua8:9 So Joshua sent them out
Joshua8:10 Joshua got up early the next morning and mobilized his men
Joshua8:11 Then all the troops who were with him marched up and approached the city. They arrived in front of Ai and camped to the north of it
Joshua8:12 Now Joshua had taken about five thousand men and set up an ambush between Bethel and Ai
Joshua8:13 So the forces were stationed with the main camp to the north of the city and the rear guard to the west of the city. And that night Joshua went into the valley.
Joshua8:14 When the king of Ai saw the Israelites
Joshua8:15 Joshua and all Israel let themselves be beaten back before them
Joshua8:16 Then all the men of Ai were summoned to pursue them
Joshua8:17 Not a man was left in Ai or Bethel who did not go out after Israel
Joshua8:18 Then the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua8:19 and as soon as he did so
Joshua8:20 When the men of Ai turned and looked back
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the men in ambush had captured the city and that smoke was rising from it
Joshua8:22 Meanwhile
Joshua8:23 But they took the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 When Israel had finished killing all the men of Ai who had pursued them into the field and wilderness
Joshua8:25 A total of twelve thousand men and women fell that day—all the people of Ai.
Joshua8:26 Joshua did not draw back the hand that held his battle lance until he had devoted to destruction all who lived in Ai.
Joshua8:27 Israel took for themselves only the cattle and plunder of that city
Joshua8:28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a permanent heap of ruins
Joshua8:29 He hung the king of Ai on a tree until evening
Joshua8:30 At that time Joshua built an altar on Mount Ebal to the Lord
Joshua8:31 just as Moses the servant of the Lord had commanded the Israelites. He built it according to what is written in the Book of the Law of Moses: “an altar of uncut stones on which no iron tool has been used.” And on it they offered burnt offerings to the Lord
Joshua8:32 And there in the presence of the Israelites
Joshua8:33 All Israel
Joshua8:34 Afterward
Joshua8:35 There was not a word of all that Moses had commanded that Joshua failed to read before the whole assembly of Israel
Joshua9:1 Now when news of this reached all the kings west of the Jordan—those in the hill country
Joshua9:2 they came together to wage war against Joshua and Israel.
Joshua9:3 But the people of Gibeon
Joshua9:4 acted deceptively and set out as envoys
Joshua9:5 They put worn
Joshua9:6 They went to Joshua in the camp at Gilgal and said to him and the men of Israel
Joshua9:7 But the men of Israel said to the Hivites
Joshua9:8 “We are your servants
Joshua9:9 “Your servants have come from a very distant land
Joshua9:10 and all that He did to the two kings of the Amorites beyond the Jordan—Sihon king of Heshbon and Og king of Bashan
Joshua9:11 So the elders and inhabitants of our land told us
Joshua9:12 This bread of ours was warm when we packed it at home on the day we left to come to you. But take a look
Joshua9:13 These wineskins were new when we filled them
Joshua9:14 Then the men of Israel sampled their provisions
Joshua9:15 And Joshua made a treaty of peace with them to let them live
Joshua9:16 Three days after they had made the treaty with the Gibeonites
Joshua9:17 So the Israelites set out and on the third day arrived at their cities—Gibeon
Joshua9:18 But the Israelites did not attack them
Joshua9:19 All the leaders answered
Joshua9:20 This is how we will treat them: We will let them live
Joshua9:21 They continued
Joshua9:22 Then Joshua summoned the Gibeonites and said
Joshua9:23 Now therefore you are under a curse and will perpetually serve as woodcutters and water carriers for the house of my God.”
Joshua9:24 The Gibeonites answered
Joshua9:25 Now we are in your hands. Do to us whatever seems good and right to you.”
Joshua9:26 So Joshua did this and delivered them from the hands of the Israelites
Joshua9:27 On that day he made them woodcutters and water carriers
Joshua10:1 Now Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem heard that Joshua had captured Ai and devoted it to destruction —doing to Ai and its king as he had done to Jericho and its king—and that the people of Gibeon had made peace with Israel and were living near them.
Joshua10:2 So Adoni-zedek and his people were greatly alarmed
Joshua10:3 Therefore Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem sent word to Hoham king of Hebron
Joshua10:4 “Come up and help me. We will attack Gibeon
Joshua10:5 So the five kings of the Amorites—the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:6 Then the men of Gibeon sent word to Joshua in the camp at Gilgal: “Do not abandon your servants. Come quickly and save us! Help us
Joshua10:7 So Joshua and his whole army
Joshua10:8 The Lord said to Joshua
Joshua10:9 After marching all night from Gilgal
Joshua10:10 And the Lord threw them into confusion before Israel
Joshua10:11 As they fled before Israel along the descent from Beth-horon to Azekah
Joshua10:12 On the day that the Lord gave the Amorites over to the Israelites
Joshua10:13 So the sun stood still and the moon stopped until the nation took vengeance upon its enemies. Is this not written in the Book of Jashar? “So the sun stopped in the middle of the sky and delayed going down about a full day.”
Joshua10:14 There has been no day like it before or since
Joshua10:15 Then Joshua returned with all Israel to the camp at Gilgal.
Joshua10:16 Now the five kings had fled and hidden in the cave at Makkedah.
Joshua10:17 And Joshua was informed: “The five kings have been found; they are hiding in the cave at Makkedah.”
Joshua10:18 So Joshua said
Joshua10:19 But you
Joshua10:20 So Joshua and the Israelites continued to inflict a terrible slaughter until they had finished them off
Joshua10:21 The whole army returned safely to Joshua in the camp at Makkedah
Joshua10:22 Then Joshua said
Joshua10:23 So they brought the five kings out of the cave—the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 When they had brought the kings to Joshua
Joshua10:25 “Do not be afraid or discouraged
Joshua10:26 After this
Joshua10:27 At sunset Joshua ordered that they be taken down from the trees and thrown into the cave in which they had hidden. Then large stones were placed against the mouth of the cave
Joshua10:28 On that day Joshua captured Makkedah and put it to the sword
Joshua10:29 Then Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Makkedah to Libnah and fought against Libnah.
Joshua10:30 And the Lord also delivered that city and its king into the hand of Israel
Joshua10:31 And Joshua and all Israel with him moved on from Libnah to Lachish. They laid siege to it and fought against it.
Joshua10:32 And the Lord delivered Lachish into the hand of Israel
Joshua10:33 At that time Horam king of Gezer went to help Lachish
Joshua10:34 So Joshua moved on from Lachish to Eglon
Joshua10:35 That day they captured Eglon and put it to the sword
Joshua10:36 Then Joshua and all Israel with him went up from Eglon to Hebron and fought against it.
Joshua10:37 They captured it and put to the sword its king
Joshua10:38 Finally Joshua and all Israel with him turned toward Debir and fought against it.
Joshua10:39 And they captured Debir
Joshua10:40 So Joshua conquered the whole region—the hill country
Joshua10:41 Joshua conquered the area from Kadesh-barnea to Gaza
Joshua10:42 And because the Lord
Joshua10:43 Then Joshua returned with all Israel to the camp at Gilgal.

Joshua20:2 “Tell the Israelites to designate the cities of refuge
Joshua20:3 so that anyone who kills another unintentionally or accidentally may flee there. These will be your refuge from the avenger of blood.

Joshua20:5 Now if the avenger of blood pursues him
Joshua20:6 He is to stay in that city until he stands trial before the assembly and until the death of the high priest serving at that time. Then the manslayer may return to his own home in the city from which he fled.”

1Samuel2:1 At that time Hannah prayed: “My heart rejoices in the Lord in whom my horn is exalted. My mouth speaks boldly against my enemies
1Samuel2:2 There is no one holy like the Lord. Indeed
1Samuel2:3 Do not boast so proudly
1Samuel2:4 The bows of the mighty are broken
1Samuel2:5 The well-fed hire themselves out for food
1Samuel2:6 The Lord brings death and gives life; He brings down to Sheol and raises up.
1Samuel2:7 The Lord sends poverty and wealth; He humbles and He exalts.
1Samuel2:8 He raises the poor from the dust and lifts the needy from the ash heap. He seats them among princes and bestows on them a throne of honor. For the foundations of the earth are the Lord’s
1Samuel2:9 He guards the steps of His faithful ones
1Samuel2:10 Those who oppose the Lord will be shattered. He will thunder from heaven against them. The Lord will judge the ends of the earth and will give power to His king. He will exalt the horn of His anointed.”

1Samuel2:31 Behold

1Samuel17:50 Thus David prevailed over the Philistine with a sling and a stone; without a sword in his hand he struck down the Philistine and killed him.
1Samuel17:51 David ran and stood over him. He grabbed the Philistine’s sword and pulled it from its sheath and killed him; and he cut off his head with the sword. When the Philistines saw that their hero was dead
1Samuel17:52 Then the men of Israel and Judah charged forward with a shout and pursued the Philistines to the entrance of Gath and to the gates of Ekron. And the bodies of the Philistines were strewn along the Shaaraim road to Gath and Ekron.

2Samuel22:1 And David sang this song to the Lord on the day the Lord had delivered him from the hand of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
2Samuel22:2 He said: “The Lord is my rock
2Samuel22:3 My God is my rock
2Samuel22:4 I will call upon the Lord
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death engulfed me; the torrents of chaos overwhelmed me.
2Samuel22:6 The cords of Sheol entangled me; the snares of death confronted me.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon the Lord; I cried out to my God. And from His temple He heard my voice
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth shook and quaked; the foundations of the heavens trembled; they were shaken because He burned with anger.
2Samuel22:9 Smoke rose from His nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He parted the heavens and came down with dark clouds beneath His feet.
2Samuel22:11 He mounted a cherub and flew; He soared on the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness a canopy around Him
2Samuel22:13 From the brightness of His presence coals of fire blazed forth.
2Samuel22:14 The Lord thundered from heaven; the voice of the Most High resounded.
2Samuel22:15 He shot His arrows and scattered the foes; He hurled lightning and routed them.
2Samuel22:16 The channels of the sea appeared
2Samuel22:17 He reached down from on high and took hold of me; He drew me out of deep waters.
2Samuel22:18 He rescued me from my powerful enemy
2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in my day of calamity
2Samuel22:20 He brought me out into the open; He rescued me because He delighted in me.
2Samuel22:21 The Lord has rewarded me according to my righteousness; He has repaid me according to the cleanness of my hands.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the Lord and have not wickedly departed from my God.
2Samuel22:23 For all His ordinances are before me; I have not disregarded His statutes.
2Samuel22:24 And I have been blameless before Him and kept myself from iniquity.
2Samuel22:25 So the Lord has repaid me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 To the faithful You show Yourself faithful
2Samuel22:27 to the pure You show Yourself pure
2Samuel22:28 You save an afflicted people
2Samuel22:29 For You
2Samuel22:30 For in You I can charge an army; with my God I can scale a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God besides the Lord? And who is the Rock except our God?
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong fortress and He makes my way clear.
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like those of a deer and stations me upon the heights.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for battle; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.
2Samuel22:36 You have given me Your shield of salvation
2Samuel22:37 You broaden the path beneath me so that my ankles do not give way.
2Samuel22:38 I pursued my enemies and destroyed them; I did not turn back until they were consumed.
2Samuel22:39 I devoured and crushed them so they could not rise; they have fallen under my feet.
2Samuel22:40 You have armed me with strength for battle; You have subdued my foes beneath me.
2Samuel22:41 You have made my enemies retreat before me; I put an end to those who hated me.
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 I ground them as the dust of the earth; I crushed and trampled them like mud in the streets.
2Samuel22:44 You have delivered me from the strife of my people; You have preserved me as the head of nations; a people I had not known shall serve me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners cower before me; when they hear me
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners lose heart and come trembling from their strongholds.
2Samuel22:47 The Lord lives
2Samuel22:48 the God who avenges me and brings down nations beneath me
2Samuel22:49 who frees me from my enemies. You exalt me above my foes; You rescue me from violent men.
2Samuel22:50 Therefore I will praise You
2Samuel22:51 Great salvation He brings to His king. He shows loving devotion to His anointed

1Kings4:20 The people of Judah and Israel were as numerous as the sand on the seashore
1Kings4:21 And Solomon reigned over all the kingdoms from the Euphrates to the land of the Philistines
1Kings4:22 Solomon’s provisions for a single day were thirty cors of fine flour
1Kings4:23 ten fat oxen
1Kings4:24 For Solomon had dominion over everything west of the Euphrates —over all the kingdoms from Tiphsah to Gaza—and he had peace on all sides.
1Kings4:25 Throughout the days of Solomon
1Kings4:26 Solomon had 4
1Kings4:27 Each month the governors in turn provided food for King Solomon and all who came to his table. They saw to it that nothing was lacking.
1Kings4:28 Each one also brought to the required place their quotas of barley and straw for the chariot horses and other horses.
1Kings4:29 And God gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings4:30 Solomon’s wisdom was greater than that of all the men of the East
1Kings4:31 He was wiser than all men—wiser than Ethan the Ezrahite
1Kings4:32 Solomon composed three thousand proverbs
1Kings4:33 He spoke of trees
1Kings4:34 So men of all nations came to listen to Solomon’s wisdom

1Kings6:1 In the four hundred and eightieth year after the Israelites had come out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The house that King Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits long
1Kings6:3 The portico at the front of the main hall of the temple was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:4 He also had narrow windows framed high in the temple.
1Kings6:5 Against the walls of the temple and the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:6 The bottom floor was five cubits wide
1Kings6:7 The temple was constructed using finished stones cut at the quarry
1Kings6:8 The entrance to the bottom floor was on the south side of the temple. A stairway led up to the middle level
1Kings6:9 So Solomon built the temple and finished it
1Kings6:10 He built chambers all along the temple
1Kings6:11 Then the word of the Lord came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 “As for this temple you are building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the Israelites and will not abandon My people Israel.”
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the temple and finished it.
1Kings6:15 He lined the interior walls with cedar paneling from the floor of the temple to the ceiling
1Kings6:16 He partitioned off the twenty cubits at the rear of the temple with cedar boards from floor to ceiling to form within the temple an inner sanctuary
1Kings6:17 And the main hall in front of this room was forty cubits long.
1Kings6:18 The cedar paneling inside the temple was carved with gourds and open flowers. Everything was cedar; not a stone could be seen.
1Kings6:19 Solomon also prepared the inner sanctuary within the temple to set the ark of the covenant of the Lord there.
1Kings6:20 The inner sanctuary was twenty cubits long
1Kings6:21 So Solomon overlaid the inside of the temple with pure gold
1Kings6:22 So he overlaid with gold the whole interior of the temple
1Kings6:23 In the inner sanctuary he made two cherubim
1Kings6:24 One wing of the first cherub was five cubits long
1Kings6:25 The second cherub also measured ten cubits; both cherubim had the same size and shape
1Kings6:26 and the height of each cherub was ten cubits.
1Kings6:27 And he placed the cherubim inside the innermost room of the temple. Since their wings were spread out
1Kings6:28 He also overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 Then he carved the walls all around the temple
1Kings6:30 And he overlaid the temple floor with gold in both the inner and outer sanctuaries.
1Kings6:31 For the entrance to the inner sanctuary
1Kings6:32 The double doors were made of olive wood
1Kings6:33 In the same way he made four-sided doorposts of olive wood for the sanctuary entrance.
1Kings6:34 The two doors were made of cypress wood
1Kings6:35 He carved into them cherubim
1Kings6:36 Solomon built the inner courtyard with three rows of dressed stone and one row of trimmed cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 The foundation of the house of the Lord was laid in the fourth year of Solomon’s reign
1Kings6:38 In his eleventh year and eighth month

1Kings7:3 The house was roofed with cedar above the beams that rested on the pillars—forty-five beams

1Kings7:25 The Sea stood on twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 It was a handbreadth thick
1Kings7:27 In addition

1Kings7:29 and on the panels between the uprights were lions

1Kings7:31 The opening to each stand inside the crown at the top was one cubit deep
1Kings7:32 There were four wheels under the panels
1Kings7:33 The wheels were made like chariot wheels; their axles
1Kings7:34 Each stand had four handles
1Kings7:35 At the top of each stand was a circular band half a cubit high. The supports and panels were cast as a unit with the top of the stand.
1Kings7:36 He engraved cherubim
1Kings7:37 In this way he made the ten stands
1Kings7:38 He also made ten bronze basins
1Kings7:39 He set five stands on the south side of the temple and five on the north
1Kings7:40 Additionally

1Kings7:42 the four hundred pomegranates for the two sets of network (two rows of pomegranates for each network covering both the bowl-shaped capitals atop the pillars);
1Kings7:43 the ten stands; the ten basins on the stands;
1Kings7:44 the Sea; the twelve oxen underneath the Sea;
1Kings7:45 and the pots

1Kings7:50 the pure gold basins

1Kings8:1 At that time Solomon assembled before him in Jerusalem the elders of Israel—all the tribal heads and family leaders of the Israelites—to bring up the ark of the covenant of the Lord from Zion
1Kings8:2 And all the men of Israel came together to King Solomon at the feast in the seventh month
1Kings8:3 When all the elders of Israel had arrived
1Kings8:4 and they brought up the ark of the Lord and the Tent of Meeting with all its sacred furnishings. So the priests and Levites carried them up.
1Kings8:5 There
1Kings8:6 Then the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place in the inner sanctuary of the temple
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread their wings over the place of the ark and overshadowed the ark and its poles.
1Kings8:8 The poles extended far enough that their ends were visible from the Holy Place in front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two stone tablets that Moses had placed in it at Horeb
1Kings8:10 And when the priests came out of the Holy Place
1Kings8:11 so that the priests could not stand there to minister because of the cloud; for the glory of the Lord filled the house of the Lord.
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon declared: “The Lord has said that He would dwell in the thick cloud.
1Kings8:13 I have indeed built You an exalted house
1Kings8:14 And as the whole assembly of Israel stood there
1Kings8:15 and said: “Blessed be the Lord
1Kings8:16 ‘Since the day I brought My people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 Now it was in the heart of my father David to build a house for the Name of the Lord
1Kings8:18 But the Lord said to my father David
1Kings8:19 Nevertheless
1Kings8:20 Now the Lord has fulfilled the word that He spoke. I have succeeded my father David
1Kings8:21 And there I have provided a place for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord in front of the whole assembly of Israel
1Kings8:23 and said: “O Lord
1Kings8:24 You have kept Your promise to Your servant
1Kings8:25 Therefore now
1Kings8:26 And now
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed dwell upon the earth? Even heaven
1Kings8:28 Yet regard the prayer and plea of Your servant
1Kings8:29 May Your eyes be open toward this temple night and day
1Kings8:30 Hear the plea of Your servant and of Your people Israel when they pray toward this place. May You hear from heaven
1Kings8:31 When a man sins against his neighbor and is required to take an oath
1Kings8:32 then may You hear from heaven and act. May You judge Your servants
1Kings8:33 When Your people Israel are defeated before an enemy because they have sinned against You
1Kings8:34 then may You hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel. May You restore them to the land You gave to their fathers.
1Kings8:35 When the skies are shut and there is no rain because Your people have sinned against You
1Kings8:36 then may You hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants
1Kings8:37 When famine or plague comes upon the land
1Kings8:38 then may whatever prayer or petition Your people Israel make—each knowing his own afflictions and spreading out his hands toward this temple—
1Kings8:39 be heard by You from heaven
1Kings8:40 so that they may fear You all the days they live in the land that You gave to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 And as for the foreigner who is not of Your people Israel but has come from a distant land because of Your name—
1Kings8:42 for they will hear of Your great name and mighty hand and outstretched arm—when he comes and prays toward this temple
1Kings8:43 then may You hear from heaven
1Kings8:44 When Your people go to war against their enemies
1Kings8:45 then may You hear from heaven their prayer and their plea
1Kings8:46 When they sin against You—for there is no one who does not sin—and You become angry with them and deliver them to an enemy who takes them as captives to his own land
1Kings8:47 and when they come to their senses in the land to which they were taken
1Kings8:48 and when they return to You with all their heart and soul in the land of the enemies who took them captive
1Kings8:49 then may You hear from heaven
1Kings8:50 May You forgive Your people who have sinned against You and all the transgressions they have committed against You
1Kings8:51 For they are Your people and Your inheritance; You brought them out of Egypt
1Kings8:52 May Your eyes be open to the pleas of Your servant and of Your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For You
1Kings8:54 Now when Solomon had finished praying this entire prayer and petition to the Lord
1Kings8:55 And he stood and blessed the whole assembly of Israel in a loud voice
1Kings8:56 “Blessed be the Lord
1Kings8:57 May the Lord our God be with us
1Kings8:58 May He incline our hearts to Himself
1Kings8:59 And may these words with which I have made my petition before the Lord be near to the Lord our God day and night
1Kings8:60 so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the Lord is God. There is no other!
1Kings8:61 So let your heart be fully devoted to the Lord our God
1Kings8:62 Then the king and all Israel with him offered sacrifices before the Lord.
1Kings8:63 And Solomon offered as peace offerings to the Lord 22
1Kings8:64 On that same day the king consecrated the middle of the courtyard in front of the house of the Lord
1Kings8:65 So at that time Solomon and all Israel with him—a great assembly of people from Lebo-hamath to the Brook of Egypt—kept the feast before the Lord our God for seven days and seven more days—fourteen days in all.
1Kings8:66 On the fifteenth day Solomon sent the people away. So they blessed the king and went home

1Kings13:8 But the man of God replied

1Kings13:10 So the man of God went another way and did not return by the way he had come to Bethel.
1Kings13:11 Now a certain old prophet was living in Bethel
1Kings13:12 “Which way did he go?” their father asked. And his sons showed him the way taken by the man of God
1Kings13:13 So the prophet said to his sons

1Kings13:15 So the prophet said to the man of God

1Kings13:19 but the man of God went back with him

1Kings13:23 And after the man of God had finished eating and drinking
1Kings13:24 As he went on his way

1Kings13:31 After he had buried him
1Kings13:32 for the message that he cried out by the word of the Lord against the altar in Bethel and against all the shrines on the high places in the cities of Samaria will surely come to pass.”
1Kings13:33 Even after these events
1Kings13:34 And this was the sin of the house of Jeroboam that led to its extermination and destruction from the face of the earth.
1Kings14:1 At that time Abijah son of Jeroboam became ill
1Kings14:2 and Jeroboam said to his wife
1Kings14:3 Take with you ten loaves of bread
1Kings14:4 Jeroboam’s wife did as instructed; she arose and went to Shiloh and arrived at Ahijah’s house. Now Ahijah could not see
1Kings14:5 But the Lord had said to Ahijah
1Kings14:6 So when Ahijah heard the sound of her feet entering the door
1Kings14:7 Go
1Kings14:8 I tore the kingdom away from the house of David and gave it to you. But you were not like My servant David
1Kings14:9 You have done more evil than all who came before you. You have proceeded to make for yourself other gods and molten images to provoke Me
1Kings14:10 Because of all this
1Kings14:11 Anyone belonging to Jeroboam who dies in the city will be eaten by dogs
1Kings14:12 As for you
1Kings14:13 All Israel will mourn for him and bury him. For this is the only one belonging to Jeroboam who will receive a proper burial
1Kings14:14 Moreover
1Kings14:15 For the Lord will strike Israel as a reed is shaken in the water. He will uproot Israel from this good land that He gave their fathers
1Kings14:16 So He will give Israel over on account of the sins Jeroboam has committed and has caused Israel to commit.”

1Kings14:21 Meanwhile

1Kings14:23 They also built for themselves high places
1Kings14:24 There were even male shrine prostitutes in the land. They imitated all the abominations of the nations the Lord had driven out before the Israelites.

1Kings14:31 And Rehoboam rested with his fathers and was buried with them in the City of David; his mother’s name was Naamah the Ammonite. And his son Abijam reigned in his place.
1Kings15:1 In the eighteenth year of the reign of Jeroboam son of Nebat

1Kings15:14 The high places were not removed

2Kings17:7 All this happened because the people of Israel had sinned against the Lord their God
2Kings17:8 and walked in the customs of the nations that the Lord had driven out before the Israelites
2Kings17:9 The Israelites secretly did things against the Lord their God that were not right. From watchtower to fortified city
2Kings17:10 They set up for themselves sacred pillars and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.

2Kings17:15 They rejected His statutes and the covenant He had made with their fathers

2Kings18:1 In the third year of the reign of Hoshea son of Elah over Israel

2Kings18:13 In the fourteenth year of Hezekiah’s reign

1Chronicles15:4 Then he gathered together the descendants of Aaron and the Levites:
1Chronicles15:5 From the Kohathites
1Chronicles15:6 from the Merarites
1Chronicles15:7 from the Gershomites
1Chronicles15:8 from the Elizaphanites
1Chronicles15:9 from the Hebronites
1Chronicles15:10 and from the Uzzielites
1Chronicles15:11 David summoned the priests Zadok and Abiathar and the Levites Uriel
1Chronicles15:12 And he said to them
1Chronicles15:13 It was because you Levites were not with us the first time that the Lord our God burst forth in anger against us. For we did not consult Him about the proper order.”
1Chronicles15:14 So the priests and Levites consecrated themselves to bring up the ark of the Lord
1Chronicles15:15 And the Levites carried the ark of God on their shoulders with the poles
1Chronicles15:16 David also told the leaders of the Levites to appoint their relatives as singers to lift up their voices with joy
1Chronicles15:17 So the Levites appointed Heman son of Joel; from his brothers
1Chronicles15:18 and with them their brothers next in rank: Zechariah
1Chronicles15:19 The musicians Heman
1Chronicles15:20 Zechariah
1Chronicles15:21 And Mattithiah
1Chronicles15:22 Chenaniah the head Levite was the director of the music because he was highly skilled.

1Chronicles15:24 Shebaniah
1Chronicles15:25 So David

1Chronicles15:29 As the ark of the covenant of the Lord was entering the City of David
1Chronicles16:1 So they brought the ark of God and placed it inside the tent that David had pitched for it. And they presented burnt offerings and peace offerings before God.
1Chronicles16:2 When David had finished sacrificing the burnt offerings and peace offerings
1Chronicles16:3 Then he distributed to every man and woman of Israel a loaf of bread
1Chronicles16:4 David appointed some of the Levites to minister before the ark of the Lord
1Chronicles16:5 Asaph was the chief
1Chronicles16:6 and the priests Benaiah and Jahaziel blew the trumpets regularly before the ark of the covenant of God.
1Chronicles16:7 On that day David first committed to Asaph and his brothers this song of thanksgiving to the Lord:
1Chronicles16:8 “Give thanks to the Lord; call upon His name; make known His deeds among the nations.
1Chronicles16:9 Sing to Him
1Chronicles16:10 Glory in His holy name; let the hearts of those who seek the Lord rejoice.
1Chronicles16:11 Seek out the Lord and His strength; seek His face always.
1Chronicles16:12 Remember the wonders He has done
1Chronicles16:13 O offspring of His servant Israel
1Chronicles16:14 He is the Lord our God; His judgments carry throughout the earth.
1Chronicles16:15 Remember His covenant forever
1Chronicles16:16 the covenant He made with Abraham
1Chronicles16:17 He confirmed it to Jacob as a decree
1Chronicles16:18 ‘I will give you the land of Canaan as the portion of your inheritance.’
1Chronicles16:19 When they were few in number
1Chronicles16:20 they wandered from nation to nation
1Chronicles16:21 He let no man oppress them; He rebuked kings on their behalf:
1Chronicles16:22 ‘Do not touch My anointed ones! Do no harm to My prophets!’
1Chronicles16:23 Sing to the Lord
1Chronicles16:24 Declare His glory among the nations
1Chronicles16:25 For great is the Lord
1Chronicles16:26 For all the gods of the nations are idols
1Chronicles16:27 Splendor and majesty are before Him; strength and joy fill His dwelling.
1Chronicles16:28 Ascribe to the Lord
1Chronicles16:29 Ascribe to the Lord the glory due His name; bring an offering and come before Him. Worship the Lord in the splendor of His holiness;
1Chronicles16:30 tremble before Him
1Chronicles16:31 Let the heavens be glad
1Chronicles16:32 Let the sea resound
1Chronicles16:33 Then the trees of the forest will sing for joy before the Lord
1Chronicles16:34 Give thanks to the Lord
1Chronicles16:35 Then cry out: ‘Save us
1Chronicles16:36 Blessed be the Lord
1Chronicles16:37 So David left Asaph and his brothers there before the ark of the covenant of the Lord
1Chronicles16:38 along with Obed-edom and his sixty-eight relatives. Obed-edom son of Jeduthun
1Chronicles16:39 And David left Zadok the priest and his fellow priests before the tabernacle of the Lord at the high place in Gibeon
1Chronicles16:40 to regularly present burnt offerings to the Lord on the altar of burnt offerings
1Chronicles16:41 With them were Heman
1Chronicles16:42 Heman and Jeduthun had with them trumpets and cymbals for the music and instruments for the songs of God. And the sons of Jeduthun were stationed at the gate.
1Chronicles16:43 Then all the people departed for their homes
1Chronicles17:1 After David had settled into his palace
1Chronicles17:2 And Nathan replied to David
1Chronicles17:3 But that night the word of God came to Nathan
1Chronicles17:4 “Go and tell My servant David that this is what the Lord says: You are not the one to build Me a house in which to dwell.
1Chronicles17:5 For I have not dwelt in a house from the day I brought Israel up out of Egypt until this day
1Chronicles17:6 In all My journeys with all the Israelites
1Chronicles17:7 Now then
1Chronicles17:8 I have been with you wherever you have gone
1Chronicles17:9 And I will provide a place for My people Israel and will plant them so that they may dwell in a place of their own and be disturbed no more. No longer will the sons of wickedness oppress them as they did at the beginning
1Chronicles17:10 and have done since the day I appointed judges over My people Israel. And I will subdue all your enemies. Moreover
1Chronicles17:11 And when your days are fulfilled and you go to be with your fathers
1Chronicles17:12 He will build a house for Me
1Chronicles17:13 I will be his Father
1Chronicles17:14 But I will set him over My house and My kingdom forever
1Chronicles17:15 So Nathan relayed to David all the words of this entire vision.
1Chronicles17:16 Then King David went in
1Chronicles17:17 And as if this was a small thing in Your eyes
1Chronicles17:18 What more can David say to You for so honoring Your servant? For You know Your servant
1Chronicles17:19 O LORD. For the sake of Your servant and according to Your own heart
1Chronicles17:20 O Lord
1Chronicles17:21 And who is like Your people Israel—the one nation on earth whom God went out to redeem as a people for Himself? You made a name for Yourself through great and awesome wonders by driving out nations from before Your people
1Chronicles17:22 For You have made Your people Israel Your very own forever
1Chronicles17:23 And now
1Chronicles17:24 so that Your name will be established and magnified forever when it is said
1Chronicles17:25 For You
1Chronicles17:26 And now
1Chronicles17:27 So now You have been pleased to bless the house of Your servant

1Chronicles18:8 And from Tibhath and Cun

1Chronicles18:12 Moreover

1Chronicles18:15 Joab son of Zeruiah was over the army; Jehoshaphat son of Ahilud was the recorder;
1Chronicles18:16 Zadok son of Ahitub and Ahimelech son of Abiathar were priests; Shavsha was the scribe;
1Chronicles18:17 Benaiah son of Jehoiada was over the Cherethites and Pelethites; and David’s sons were chief officials at the king’s side.

1Chronicles27:27 Shimei the Ramathite was in charge of the vineyards. Zabdi the Shiphmite was in charge of the produce of the vineyards for the wine vats.
1Chronicles27:28 Baal-hanan the Gederite was in charge of the olive and sycamore trees in the foothills. Joash was in charge of the stores of olive oil.
1Chronicles27:29 Shitrai the Sharonite was in charge of the herds grazing in Sharon. Shaphat son of Adlai was in charge of the herds in the valleys.
1Chronicles27:30 Obil the Ishmaelite was in charge of the camels. Jehdeiah the Meronothite was in charge of the donkeys.
1Chronicles27:31 Jaziz the Hagrite was in charge of the flocks. All these officials were in charge of King David’s property.
1Chronicles27:32 David’s uncle Jonathan was a counselor; he was a man of insight and a scribe. Jehiel son of Hachmoni attended to the sons of the king.
1Chronicles27:33 Ahithophel was the king’s counselor. Hushai the Archite was the king’s friend.
1Chronicles27:34 Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah
