Psalms22:1 My God
Psalms22:2 I cry out by day
Psalms22:3 Yet You are holy
Psalms22:4 In You our fathers trusted; they trusted and You delivered them.
Psalms22:5 They cried out to You and were set free; they trusted in You and were not disappointed.
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm and not a man
Psalms22:7 All who see me mock me; they sneer and shake their heads:
Psalms22:8 “He trusts in the Lord
Psalms22:9 Yet You brought me forth from the womb; You made me secure at my mother’s breast.
Psalms22:10 From birth I was cast upon You; from my mother’s womb You have been my God.
Psalms22:11 Be not far from me
Psalms22:12 Many bulls surround me; strong bulls of Bashan encircle me.
Psalms22:13 They open their jaws against me like lions that roar and maul.
Psalms22:14 I am poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength is dried up like a potsherd
Psalms22:16 For dogs surround me; a band of evil men encircles me; they have pierced my hands and feet.
Psalms22:17 I can count all my bones; they stare and gloat over me.
Psalms22:18 They divide my garments among them and cast lots for my clothing.
Psalms22:19 But You
Psalms22:20 Deliver my soul from the sword
Psalms22:21 Save me from the mouth of the lion; at the horns of the wild oxen You have answered me!
Psalms22:22 I will proclaim Your name to my brothers; I will praise You in the assembly.
Psalms22:23 You who fear the Lord
Psalms22:24 For He has not despised or detested the torment of the afflicted. He has not hidden His face from him
Psalms22:25 My praise for You resounds in the great assembly; I will fulfill my vows before those who fear You.
Psalms22:26 The poor will eat and be satisfied; those who seek the Lord will praise Him. May your hearts live forever!
Psalms22:27 All the ends of the earth will remember and turn to the Lord. All the families of the nations will bow down before Him.
Psalms22:28 For dominion belongs to the Lord and He rules over the nations.
Psalms22:29 All the rich of the earth will feast and worship; all who go down to the dust will kneel before Him— even those unable to preserve their lives.
Psalms22:30 Posterity will serve Him; they will declare the Lord to a new generation.
Psalms22:31 They will come and proclaim His righteousness to a people yet unborn— all that He has done.

Psalms33:5 The Lord loves righteousness and justice; the earth is full of His loving devotion.

Psalms33:7 He piles up the waters of the sea; He puts the depths into storehouses.

Psalms33:9 For He spoke

Psalms33:11 The counsel of the Lord stands forever
Psalms33:12 Blessed is the nation whose God is the Lord

Psalms33:14 From His dwelling place He gazes on all who inhabit the earth.
Psalms33:15 He shapes the hearts of each; He considers all their works.
Psalms33:16 No king is saved by his vast army; no warrior is delivered by his great strength.
Psalms33:17 A horse is a vain hope for salvation; even its great strength cannot save.

Psalms33:19 to deliver them from death and keep them alive in famine.

Psalms34:19 Many are the afflictions of the righteous
Psalms34:20 He protects all his bones; not one of them will be broken.
Psalms34:21 Evil will slay the wicked
Psalms34:22 The Lord redeems His servants
Psalms35:1 Contend with my opponents
Psalms35:2 Take up Your shield and buckler; arise and come to my aid.
Psalms35:3 Draw the spear and javelin against my pursuers; say to my soul: “I am your salvation.”
Psalms35:4 May those who seek my life be disgraced and put to shame; may those who plan to harm me be driven back and confounded.
Psalms35:5 May they be like chaff in the wind
Psalms35:6 May their path be dark and slick
Psalms35:7 For without cause they laid their net for me; without reason they dug a pit for my soul.
Psalms35:8 May ruin befall them by surprise; may the net they hid ensnare them; may they fall into the hazard they created.
Psalms35:9 Then my soul will rejoice in the Lord and exult in His salvation.
Psalms35:10 All my bones will exclaim
Psalms35:11 Hostile witnesses come forward; they make charges I know nothing about.
Psalms35:12 They repay me evil for good
Psalms35:13 Yet when they were ill
Psalms35:14 I paced about as for my friend or brother; I was bowed down with grief
Psalms35:15 But when I stumbled
Psalms35:16 Like godless jesters at a feast
Psalms35:17 How long
Psalms35:18 Then I will give You thanks in the great assembly; I will praise You among many people.
Psalms35:19 Let not my enemies gloat over me without cause
Psalms35:20 For they do not speak peace
Psalms35:21 They gape at me and say
Psalms35:22 O Lord
Psalms35:23 Awake and rise to my defense
Psalms35:24 Vindicate me by Your righteousness
Psalms35:25 Let them not say in their hearts
Psalms35:26 May those who gloat in my distress be ashamed and confounded; may those who exalt themselves over me be clothed in shame and reproach.
Psalms35:27 May those who favor my vindication shout for joy and gladness; may they always say
Psalms35:28 Then my tongue will proclaim Your righteousness and Your praises all day long.
Psalms36:1 An oracle is in my heart regarding the transgression of the wicked man: There is no fear of God before his eyes.
Psalms36:2 For his eyes are too full of conceit to detect or hate his own sin.
Psalms36:3 The words of his mouth are wicked and deceitful; he has ceased to be wise and well-doing.
Psalms36:4 Even on his bed he plots wickedness; he sets himself on a path that is not good; he fails to reject evil.
Psalms36:5 Your loving devotion
Psalms36:6 Your righteousness is like the highest mountains; Your judgments are like the deepest sea. O Lord
Psalms36:7 How precious is Your loving devotion
Psalms36:8 They feast on the abundance of Your house
Psalms36:9 For with You is the fountain of life; in Your light we see light.
Psalms36:10 Extend Your loving devotion to those who know You
Psalms36:11 Let not the foot of the proud come against me
Psalms36:12 There the evildoers lie fallen
Psalms37:1 Do not fret over those who do evil; do not envy those who do wrong.
Psalms37:2 For they wither quickly like grass and wilt like tender plants.
Psalms37:3 Trust in the Lord and do good; dwell in the land and cultivate faithfulness.
Psalms37:4 Delight yourself in the Lord
Psalms37:5 Commit your way to the Lord; trust in Him
Psalms37:6 He will bring forth your righteousness like the dawn
Psalms37:7 Be still before the Lord and wait patiently for Him; do not fret when men prosper in their ways
Psalms37:8 Refrain from anger and abandon wrath; do not fret—it can only bring harm.
Psalms37:9 For the evildoers will be cut off
Psalms37:10 Yet a little while
Psalms37:11 But the meek will inherit the land and delight in abundant prosperity.
Psalms37:12 The wicked scheme against the righteous and gnash their teeth at them
Psalms37:13 but the Lord laughs
Psalms37:14 The wicked have drawn the sword and bent the bow to bring down the poor and needy
Psalms37:15 But their swords will pierce their own hearts
Psalms37:16 Better is the little of the righteous than the abundance of many who are wicked.
Psalms37:17 For the arms of the wicked will be broken
Psalms37:18 The Lord knows the days of the blameless
Psalms37:19 In the time of evil they will not be ashamed
Psalms37:20 But the wicked and enemies of the Lord will perish like the glory of the fields. They will vanish; like smoke they will fade away.
Psalms37:21 The wicked borrow and do not repay
Psalms37:22 Surely those He blesses will inherit the land
Psalms37:23 The steps of a man are ordered by the Lord who takes delight in his journey.
Psalms37:24 Though he falls
Psalms37:25 I once was young and now am old
Psalms37:26 They are ever generous and quick to lend
Psalms37:27 Turn away from evil and do good
Psalms37:28 For the Lord loves justice and will not forsake His saints. They are preserved forever
Psalms37:29 The righteous will inherit the land and dwell in it forever.
Psalms37:30 The mouth of the righteous man utters wisdom
Psalms37:31 The law of his God is in his heart; his steps do not falter.
Psalms37:32 Though the wicked lie in wait for the righteous
Psalms37:33 the Lord will not leave them in their power or let them be condemned under judgment.
Psalms37:34 Wait for the Lord and keep His way
Psalms37:35 I have seen a wicked
Psalms37:36 yet he passed away and was no more; though I searched
Psalms37:37 Consider the blameless and observe the upright
Psalms37:38 But the transgressors will all be destroyed; the future of the wicked will be cut off.
Psalms37:39 The salvation of the righteous is from the Lord; He is their stronghold in time of trouble.
Psalms37:40 The Lord helps and delivers them; He rescues and saves them from the wicked
Psalms38:1 O Lord
Psalms38:2 For Your arrows have pierced me deeply
Psalms38:3 There is no soundness in my body because of Your anger; there is no rest in my bones because of my sin.
Psalms38:4 For my iniquities have overwhelmed me; they are a burden too heavy to bear.
Psalms38:5 My wounds are foul and festering because of my sinful folly.
Psalms38:6 I am bent and brought low; all day long I go about mourning.
Psalms38:7 For my loins are full of burning pain
Psalms38:8 I am numb and badly crushed; I groan in anguish of heart.
Psalms38:9 O Lord
Psalms38:10 My heart pounds
Psalms38:11 My beloved and friends shun my disease
Psalms38:12 Those who seek my life lay snares; those who wish me harm speak destruction
Psalms38:13 But like a deaf man
Psalms38:14 I am like a man who cannot hear
Psalms38:15 I wait for You
Psalms38:16 For I said
Psalms38:17 For I am ready to fall
Psalms38:18 Yes
Psalms38:19 Many are my enemies without cause
Psalms38:20 Those who repay my good with evil attack me for pursuing the good.
Psalms38:21 Do not forsake me
Psalms38:22 Come quickly to help me
Psalms39:1 I said
Psalms39:2 I was speechless and still; I remained silent
Psalms39:3 My heart grew hot within me; as I mused
Psalms39:4 “Show me
Psalms39:5 You
Psalms39:6 Surely every man goes about like a phantom; surely he bustles in vain; he heaps up riches not knowing who will haul them away.
Psalms39:7 And now
Psalms39:8 Deliver me from all my transgressions; do not make me the reproach of fools.
Psalms39:9 I have become mute; I do not open my mouth because of what You have done.
Psalms39:10 Remove Your scourge from me; I am perishing by the force of Your hand.
Psalms39:11 You discipline and correct a man for his iniquity
Psalms39:12 Hear my prayer
Psalms39:13 Turn Your gaze away from me
Psalms40:1 I waited patiently for the Lord; He inclined to me and heard my cry.
Psalms40:2 He lifted me up from the pit of despair
Psalms40:3 He put a new song in my mouth
Psalms40:4 Blessed is the man who has made the Lord his trust
Psalms40:5 Many
Psalms40:6 Sacrifice and offering You did not desire
Psalms40:7 Then I said
Psalms40:8 I delight to do Your will
Psalms40:9 I proclaim righteousness in the great assembly; behold
Psalms40:10 I have not covered up Your righteousness in my heart; I have declared Your faithfulness and salvation; I have not concealed Your loving devotion and faithfulness from the great assembly.
Psalms40:11 O Lord
Psalms40:12 For evils without number surround me; my sins have overtaken me
Psalms40:13 Be pleased
Psalms40:14 May those who seek my life be ashamed and confounded; may those who wish me harm be repelled and humiliated.
Psalms40:15 May those who say to me
Psalms40:16 May all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; may those who love Your salvation always say
Psalms40:17 But I am poor and needy; may the Lord think of me. You are my helper and deliverer; O my God
Psalms41:1 Blessed is the one who cares for the poor; the Lord will deliver him in the day of trouble.
Psalms41:2 The Lord will protect and preserve him; He will bless him in the land and refuse to surrender him to the will of his foes.
Psalms41:3 The Lord will sustain him on his bed of illness and restore him from his bed of sickness.
Psalms41:4 I said
Psalms41:5 My enemies say with malice: “When will he die and be forgotten?”
Psalms41:6 My visitor speaks falsehood; he gathers slander in his heart; he goes out and spreads it abroad.
Psalms41:7 All who hate me whisper against me; they imagine the worst for me:
Psalms41:8 “A vile disease has been poured into him; he will never get up from where he lies!”
Psalms41:9 Even my close friend whom I trusted
Psalms41:10 But You
Psalms41:11 By this I know that You delight in me
Psalms41:12 In my integrity You uphold me and set me in Your presence forever.
Psalms41:13 Blessed be the Lord
Psalms42:1 As the deer pants for streams of water
Psalms42:2 My soul thirsts for God
Psalms42:3 My tears have been my food both day and night
Psalms42:4 These things come to mind as I pour out my soul: how I walked with the multitude
Psalms42:5 Why are you downcast
Psalms42:6 O my God
Psalms42:7 Deep calls to deep in the roar of Your waterfalls; all Your breakers and waves have rolled over me.
Psalms42:8 The Lord decrees His loving devotion by day
Psalms42:9 I say to God my Rock
Psalms42:10 Like the crushing of my bones
Psalms42:11 Why are you downcast
Psalms43:1 Vindicate me
Psalms43:2 For You are the God of my refuge. Why have You rejected me? Why must I walk in sorrow because of the enemy’s oppression?
Psalms43:3 Send out Your light and Your truth; let them lead me. Let them bring me to Your holy mountain
Psalms43:4 Then I will go to the altar of God
Psalms43:5 Why are you downcast
Psalms44:1 We have heard with our ears
Psalms44:2 With Your hand You drove out the nations and planted our fathers there; You crushed the peoples and cast them out.
Psalms44:3 For it was not by their sword that they took the land; their arm did not bring them victory. It was by Your right hand
Psalms44:4 You are my King
Psalms44:5 Through You we repel our foes; through Your name we trample our enemies.
Psalms44:6 For I do not trust in my bow
Psalms44:7 For You save us from our enemies; You put those who hate us to shame.
Psalms44:8 In God we have boasted all day long
Psalms44:9 But You have rejected and humbled us; You no longer go forth with our armies.
Psalms44:10 You have made us retreat from the foe
Psalms44:11 You have given us up as sheep to be devoured; You have scattered us among the nations.
Psalms44:12 You sell Your people for nothing; no profit do You gain from their sale.
Psalms44:13 You have made us a reproach to our neighbors
Psalms44:14 You have made us a byword among the nations
Psalms44:15 All day long my disgrace is before me
Psalms44:16 at the voice of the scorner and reviler
Psalms44:17 All this has come upon us
Psalms44:18 Our hearts have not turned back; our steps have not strayed from Your path.
Psalms44:19 But You have crushed us in the lair of jackals; You have covered us with deepest darkness.
Psalms44:20 If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread out our hands to a foreign god
Psalms44:21 would not God have discovered
Psalms44:22 Yet for Your sake we face death all day long; we are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.
Psalms44:23 Wake up
Psalms44:24 Why do You hide Your face and forget our affliction and oppression?
Psalms44:25 For our soul has sunk to the dust; our bodies cling to the earth.
Psalms44:26 Rise up; be our help! Redeem us on account of Your loving devotion.
Psalms45:1 My heart is stirred by a noble theme as I recite my verses to the king; my tongue is the pen of a skillful writer.
Psalms45:2 You are the most handsome of men; grace has anointed your lips
Psalms45:3 Strap your sword at your side
Psalms45:4 In your splendor ride forth in victory on behalf of truth and humility and justice; may your right hand show your awesome deeds.
Psalms45:5 Your arrows pierce the hearts of the king’s foes; the nations fall beneath your feet.
Psalms45:6 Your throne
Psalms45:7 You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God
Psalms45:8 All your garments are fragrant with myrrh and aloes and cassia; from palaces of ivory the harps make you glad.
Psalms45:9 The daughters of kings are among your honored women; the queen stands at your right hand
Psalms45:10 Listen
Psalms45:11 and the king will desire your beauty; bow to him
Psalms45:12 The Daughter of Tyre will come with a gift; men of wealth will seek your favor.
Psalms45:13 All glorious is the princess in her chamber; her gown is embroidered with gold.
Psalms45:14 In colorful garments she is led to the king; her virgin companions are brought before you.
Psalms45:15 They are led in with joy and gladness; they enter the palace of the king.
Psalms45:16 Your sons will succeed your fathers; you will make them princes throughout the land.
Psalms45:17 I will commemorate your name through all generations; therefore the nations will praise you forever and ever.

Psalms49:5 Why should I fear in times of trouble

Psalms49:7 No man can possibly redeem his brother or pay his ransom to God.
Psalms49:8 For the redemption of his soul is costly
Psalms49:9 that he should live on forever and not see decay.
Psalms49:10 For it is clear that wise men die
Psalms49:11 Their graves are their eternal homes— their dwellings for endless generations— even though their lands were their namesakes.

Psalms49:17 For when he dies

Psalms50:10 for every beast of the forest is Mine— the cattle on a thousand hills.

Psalms50:12 If I were hungry
Psalms50:13 Do I eat the flesh of bulls

Psalms59:6 They return in the evening
Psalms59:7 See what they spew from their mouths— sharp words from their lips: “For who can hear us?”
Psalms59:8 But You

Psalms59:17 To You
Psalms60:1 You have rejected us
Psalms60:2 You have shaken the land and torn it open. Heal its fractures
Psalms60:3 You have shown Your people hardship; we are staggered from the wine You made us drink.
Psalms60:4 You have raised a banner for those who fear You
Psalms60:5 Respond and save us with Your right hand

Psalms60:8 Moab is My washbasin; upon Edom I toss My sandal; over Philistia I shout in triumph.”

Psalms60:12 With God we will perform with valor
Psalms61:1 Hear my cry
Psalms61:2 From the ends of the earth I call out to You whenever my heart is faint. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.
Psalms61:3 For You have been my refuge
Psalms61:4 Let me dwell in Your tent forever and take refuge in the shelter of Your wings. Selah
Psalms61:5 For You have heard my vows
Psalms61:6 Increase the days of the king’s life; may his years span many generations.
Psalms61:7 May he sit enthroned in God’s presence forever; appoint Your loving devotion and Your faithfulness to guard him.
Psalms61:8 Then I will ever sing praise to Your name and fulfill my vows day by day.
Psalms62:1 In God alone my soul finds rest; my salvation comes from Him.
Psalms62:2 He alone is my rock and my salvation. He is my fortress; I will never be shaken.
Psalms62:3 How long will you threaten a man? Will all of you throw him down like a leaning wall or a tottering fence?
Psalms62:4 They fully intend to cast him down from his lofty perch; they delight in lies; with their mouths they bless
Psalms62:5 Rest in God alone
Psalms62:6 He alone is my rock and my salvation; He is my fortress; I will not be shaken.
Psalms62:7 My salvation and my honor rest on God
Psalms62:8 Trust in Him at all times
Psalms62:9 Lowborn men are but a vapor
Psalms62:10 Place no trust in extortion
Psalms62:11 God has spoken once; I have heard this twice: that power belongs to God
Psalms62:12 and loving devotion to You
Psalms63:1 O God
Psalms63:2 So I have seen You in the sanctuary and beheld Your power and glory.
Psalms63:3 Because Your loving devotion is better than life
Psalms63:4 So I will bless You as long as I live; in Your name I will lift my hands.
Psalms63:5 My soul is satisfied as with the richest of foods; with joyful lips my mouth will praise You.
Psalms63:6 When I remember You on my bed
Psalms63:7 For You are my help; I will sing for joy in the shadow of Your wings.
Psalms63:8 My soul clings to You; Your right hand upholds me.
Psalms63:9 But those who seek my life to destroy it will go into the depths of the earth.
Psalms63:10 They will fall to the power of the sword; they will become a portion for foxes.
Psalms63:11 But the king will rejoice in God; all who swear by Him will exult
Psalms64:1 Hear
Psalms64:2 Hide me from the scheming of the wicked
Psalms64:3 who sharpen their tongues like swords and aim their bitter words like arrows
Psalms64:4 ambushing the innocent in seclusion
Psalms64:5 They hold fast to their evil purpose; they speak of hiding their snares. “Who will see them?” they say.
Psalms64:6 They devise injustice and say
Psalms64:7 But God will shoot them with arrows; suddenly they will be wounded.
Psalms64:8 They will be made to stumble
Psalms64:9 Then all mankind will fear and proclaim the work of God; so they will ponder what He has done.
Psalms64:10 Let the righteous rejoice in the Lord and take refuge in Him; let all the upright in heart exult.
Psalms65:1 Praise awaits You
Psalms65:2 O You who listen to prayer
Psalms65:3 When iniquities prevail against me
Psalms65:4 Blessed is the one You choose and bring near to dwell in Your courts! We are filled with the goodness of Your house
Psalms65:5 With awesome deeds of righteousness You answer us
Psalms65:6 You formed the mountains by Your power
Psalms65:7 You stilled the roaring of the seas
Psalms65:8 Those who live far away fear Your wonders; You make the dawn and sunset shout for joy.
Psalms65:9 You attend to the earth and water it; with abundance You enrich it. The streams of God are full of water
Psalms65:10 You soak its furrows and level its ridges; You soften it with showers and bless its growth.
Psalms65:11 You crown the year with Your bounty
Psalms65:12 The pastures of the wilderness overflow; the hills are robed with joy.
Psalms65:13 The pastures are clothed with flocks

Psalms66:5 Come and see the works of God; how awesome are His deeds toward mankind.
Psalms66:6 He turned the sea into dry land; they passed through the waters on foot; there we rejoiced in Him.
Psalms66:7 He rules forever by His power; His eyes watch the nations. Do not let the rebellious exalt themselves. Selah
Psalms66:8 Bless our God
Psalms66:9 He preserves our lives and keeps our feet from slipping.
Psalms66:10 For You
Psalms66:11 You led us into the net; You laid burdens on our backs.
Psalms66:12 You let men ride over our heads; we went through fire and water
Psalms66:13 I will enter Your house with burnt offerings; I will fulfill my vows to You—
Psalms66:14 the vows that my lips promised and my mouth spoke in my distress.

Psalms66:16 Come and listen
Psalms66:17 I cried out to Him with my mouth and praised Him with my tongue.
Psalms66:18 If I had cherished iniquity in my heart
Psalms66:19 But God has surely heard; He has attended to the sound of my prayer.

Psalms68:12 “Kings and their armies flee in haste; she who waits at home divides the plunder.
Psalms68:13 Though you lie down among the sheepfolds
Psalms68:14 When the Almighty scattered the kings in the land
Psalms68:15 A mountain of God is Mount Bashan; a mountain of many peaks is Mount Bashan.
Psalms68:16 Why do you gaze in envy
Psalms68:17 The chariots of God are tens of thousands— thousands of thousands are they; the Lord is in His sanctuary as He was at Sinai.

Psalms68:35 O God
Psalms69:1 Save me
Psalms69:2 I have sunk into the miry depths
Psalms69:3 I am weary from my crying; my throat is parched. My eyes fail
Psalms69:4 Those who hate me without cause outnumber the hairs of my head; many are those who would destroy me— my enemies for no reason. Though I did not steal
Psalms69:5 You know my folly
Psalms69:6 May those who hope in You not be ashamed through me
Psalms69:7 For I have endured scorn for Your sake
Psalms69:8 I have become a stranger to my brothers and a foreigner to my mother’s sons
Psalms69:9 because zeal for Your house has consumed me
Psalms69:10 I wept and fasted
Psalms69:11 I made sackcloth my clothing
Psalms69:12 Those who sit at the gate mock me
Psalms69:13 But my prayer to You
Psalms69:14 Rescue me from the mire and do not let me sink; deliver me from my foes and out of the deep waters.
Psalms69:15 Do not let the floods engulf me or the depths swallow me up; let not the Pit close its mouth over me.
Psalms69:16 Answer me
Psalms69:17 Hide not Your face from Your servant
Psalms69:18 Draw near to my soul and redeem me; ransom me because of my foes.
Psalms69:19 You know my reproach
Psalms69:20 Insults have broken my heart
Psalms69:21 They poisoned my food with gall and gave me vinegar to quench my thirst.
Psalms69:22 May their table become a snare; may it be a retribution and a trap.
Psalms69:23 May their eyes be darkened so they cannot see
Psalms69:24 Pour out Your wrath upon them
Psalms69:25 May their place be deserted; let there be no one to dwell in their tents.
Psalms69:26 For they persecute the one You struck and recount the pain of those You wounded.
Psalms69:27 Add iniquity to their iniquity; let them not share in Your righteousness.
Psalms69:28 May they be blotted out of the Book of Life and not listed with the righteous.
Psalms69:29 But I am in pain and distress; let Your salvation protect me
Psalms69:30 I will praise God’s name in song and exalt Him with thanksgiving.
Psalms69:31 And this will please the Lord more than an ox
Psalms69:32 The humble will see and rejoice. You who seek God
Psalms69:33 For the Lord listens to the needy and does not despise His captive people.
Psalms69:34 Let heaven and earth praise Him
Psalms69:35 For God will save Zion and rebuild the cities of Judah
Psalms69:36 The descendants of His servants will inherit it

Psalms71:1 In You
Psalms71:2 In Your justice
Psalms71:3 Be my rock of refuge
Psalms71:4 Deliver me
Psalms71:5 For You are my hope
Psalms71:6 I have leaned on You since birth; You pulled me from my mother’s womb. My praise is always for You.
Psalms71:7 I have become a portent to many
Psalms71:8 My mouth is filled with Your praise and with Your splendor all day long.
Psalms71:9 Do not discard me in my old age; do not forsake me when my strength fails.
Psalms71:10 For my enemies speak against me
Psalms71:11 saying
Psalms71:12 Be not far from me
Psalms71:13 May the accusers of my soul be ashamed and consumed; may those who seek my harm be covered with scorn and disgrace.
Psalms71:14 But I will always hope and will praise You more and more.
Psalms71:15 My mouth will declare Your righteousness and Your salvation all day long
Psalms71:16 I will enter in the strength of the Lord GOD; I will proclaim Your righteousness—Yours alone.
Psalms71:17 O God
Psalms71:18 Even when I am old and gray
Psalms71:19 Your righteousness reaches to the heavens
Psalms71:20 Though You have shown me many troubles and misfortunes
Psalms71:21 You will increase my honor and comfort me once again.
Psalms71:22 So I will praise You with the harp for Your faithfulness
Psalms71:23 When I sing praise to You my lips will shout for joy
Psalms71:24 My tongue will indeed proclaim Your righteousness all day long
Psalms72:1 Endow the king with Your justice
Psalms72:2 May he judge Your people with righteousness and Your afflicted with justice.
Psalms72:3 May the mountains bring peace to the people
Psalms72:4 May he vindicate the afflicted among the people; may he save the children of the needy and crush the oppressor.
Psalms72:5 May they fear him as long as the sun shines
Psalms72:6 May he be like rain that falls on freshly cut grass
Psalms72:7 May the righteous flourish in his days and prosperity abound
Psalms72:8 May he rule from sea to sea
Psalms72:9 May the nomads bow before him
Psalms72:10 May the kings of Tarshish and distant shores bring tribute; may the kings of Sheba and Seba offer gifts.
Psalms72:11 May all kings bow down to him and all nations serve him.
Psalms72:12 For he will deliver the needy who cry out and the afflicted who have no helper.
Psalms72:13 He will take pity on the poor and needy and save the lives of the oppressed.
Psalms72:14 He will redeem them from oppression and violence
Psalms72:15 Long may he live! May gold from Sheba be given him. May people ever pray for him; may they bless him all day long.
Psalms72:16 May there be an abundance of grain in the land; may it sway atop the hills. May its fruit trees flourish like the forests of Lebanon
Psalms72:17 May his name endure forever; may his name continue as long as the sun shines. In him may all nations be blessed; may they call him blessed.
Psalms72:18 Blessed be the Lord God
Psalms72:19 And blessed be His glorious name forever; may all the earth be filled with His glory. Amen and amen.
Psalms72:20 Thus conclude the prayers of David son of Jesse.
Psalms73:1 Surely God is good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I envied the arrogant when I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
Psalms73:4 They have no struggle in their death; their bodies are well-fed.
Psalms73:5 They are free of the burdens others carry; they are not afflicted like other men.
Psalms73:6 Therefore pride is their necklace; a garment of violence covers them.
Psalms73:7 From their prosperity proceeds iniquity; the imaginations of their hearts run wild.
Psalms73:8 They mock and speak with malice; with arrogance they threaten oppression.
Psalms73:9 They set their mouths against the heavens
Psalms73:10 So their people return to this place and drink up waters in abundance.
Psalms73:11 The wicked say
Psalms73:12 Behold
Psalms73:13 Surely in vain I have kept my heart pure; in innocence I have washed my hands.
Psalms73:14 For I am afflicted all day long and punished every morning.
Psalms73:15 If I had said
Psalms73:16 When I tried to understand all this
Psalms73:17 until I entered God’s sanctuary; then I discerned their end.
Psalms73:18 Surely You set them on slick ground; You cast them down into ruin.
Psalms73:19 How suddenly they are laid waste
Psalms73:20 Like one waking from a dream
Psalms73:21 When my heart was grieved and I was pierced within
Psalms73:22 I was senseless and ignorant; I was a brute beast before You.
Psalms73:23 Yet I am always with You; You hold my right hand.
Psalms73:24 You guide me with Your counsel
Psalms73:25 Whom have I in heaven but You? And on earth I desire no one besides You.
Psalms73:26 My flesh and my heart may fail
Psalms73:27 Those far from You will surely perish; You destroy all who are unfaithful to You.
Psalms73:28 But as for me
Psalms74:1 Why have You rejected us forever
Psalms74:2 Remember Your congregation
Psalms74:3 Turn Your steps to the everlasting ruins
Psalms74:4 Your foes have roared within Your meeting place; they have unfurled their banners as signs
Psalms74:5 like men wielding axes in a thicket of trees
Psalms74:6 and smashing all the carvings with hatchets and picks.
Psalms74:7 They have burned Your sanctuary to the ground; they have defiled the dwelling place of Your Name.
Psalms74:8 They said in their hearts
Psalms74:9 There are no signs for us to see. There is no longer any prophet. And none of us knows how long this will last.
Psalms74:10 How long
Psalms74:11 Why do You withdraw Your strong right hand? Stretch it out to destroy them!
Psalms74:12 Yet God is my King from ancient times
Psalms74:13 You divided the sea by Your strength; You smashed the heads of the dragons of the sea;
Psalms74:14 You crushed the heads of Leviathan; You fed him to the creatures of the desert.
Psalms74:15 You broke open the fountain and the flood; You dried up the ever-flowing rivers.
Psalms74:16 The day is Yours
Psalms74:17 You set all the boundaries of the earth; You made the summer and winter.
Psalms74:18 Remember how the enemy has mocked You
Psalms74:19 Do not deliver the soul of Your dove to beasts; do not forget the lives of Your afflicted forever.
Psalms74:20 Consider Your covenant
Psalms74:21 Do not let the oppressed retreat in shame; may the poor and needy praise Your name.
Psalms74:22 Rise up
Psalms74:23 Do not disregard the clamor of Your adversaries

Psalms75:3 When the earth and all its dwellers quake

Psalms75:6 For exaltation comes neither from east nor west
Psalms75:7 but it is God who judges; He brings down one and exalts another.
Psalms75:8 For a cup is in the hand of the Lord

Psalms76:1 God is known in Judah; His name is great in Israel.
Psalms76:2 His tent is in Salem
Psalms76:3 There He shattered the flaming arrows
Psalms76:4 You are resplendent with light
Psalms76:5 The valiant lie plundered; they sleep their last sleep. No men of might could lift a hand.
Psalms76:6 At Your rebuke
Psalms76:7 You alone are to be feared. When You are angry
Psalms76:8 From heaven You pronounced judgment
Psalms76:9 when God rose up to judge
Psalms76:10 Even the wrath of man shall praise You; with the survivors of wrath You will clothe Yourself.
Psalms76:11 Make and fulfill your vows to the Lord your God; let all the neighboring lands bring tribute to Him who is to be feared.
Psalms76:12 He breaks the spirits of princes; He is feared by the kings of the earth.
Psalms77:1 I cried out to God; I cried aloud to God to hear me.
Psalms77:2 In the day of trouble I sought the Lord; through the night my outstretched hands did not grow weary; my soul refused to be comforted.
Psalms77:3 I remembered You
Psalms77:4 You have kept my eyes from closing; I am too troubled to speak.
Psalms77:5 I considered the days of old
Psalms77:6 At night I remembered my song; in my heart I mused
Psalms77:7 “Will the Lord spurn us forever and never show His favor again?
Psalms77:8 Is His loving devotion gone forever? Has His promise failed for all time?
Psalms77:9 Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has His anger shut off His compassion?” Selah
Psalms77:10 So I said
Psalms77:11 I will remember the works of the Lord; yes
Psalms77:12 I will reflect on all You have done and ponder Your mighty deeds.
Psalms77:13 Your way
Psalms77:14 You are the God who works wonders; You display Your strength among the peoples.
Psalms77:15 With power You redeemed Your people
Psalms77:16 The waters saw You
Psalms77:17 The clouds poured down water; the skies resounded with thunder; Your arrows flashed back and forth.
Psalms77:18 Your thunder resounded in the whirlwind; the lightning lit up the world; the earth trembled and quaked.
Psalms77:19 Your path led through the sea
Psalms77:20 You led Your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
Psalms78:1 Give ear
Psalms78:2 I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden from the beginning
Psalms78:3 that we have heard and known and our fathers have relayed to us.
Psalms78:4 We will not hide them from their children
Psalms78:5 For He established a testimony in Jacob and appointed a law in Israel
Psalms78:6 that the coming generation would know them— even children yet to be born— to arise and tell their own children
Psalms78:7 that they should put their confidence in God
Psalms78:8 Then they will not be like their fathers
Psalms78:9 The archers of Ephraim turned back on the day of battle.
Psalms78:10 They failed to keep God’s covenant and refused to live by His law.
Psalms78:11 They forgot what He had done
Psalms78:12 He worked wonders before their fathers in the land of Egypt
Psalms78:13 He split the sea and brought them through; He set the waters upright like a wall.
Psalms78:14 He led them with a cloud by day and with a light of fire all night.
Psalms78:15 He split the rocks in the wilderness and gave them drink as abundant as the seas.
Psalms78:16 He brought streams from the stone and made water flow down like rivers.
Psalms78:17 But they continued to sin against Him
Psalms78:18 They willfully tested God by demanding the food they craved.
Psalms78:19 They spoke against God
Psalms78:20 When He struck the rock
Psalms78:21 Therefore the Lord heard and was filled with wrath; so a fire was kindled against Jacob
Psalms78:22 because they did not believe God or rely on His salvation.
Psalms78:23 Yet He commanded the clouds above and opened the doors of the heavens.
Psalms78:24 He rained down manna for them to eat; He gave them grain from heaven.
Psalms78:25 Man ate the bread of angels; He sent them food in abundance.
Psalms78:26 He stirred the east wind from the heavens and drove the south wind by His might.
Psalms78:27 He rained meat on them like dust
Psalms78:28 He felled them in the midst of their camp
Psalms78:29 So they ate and were well filled
Psalms78:30 Yet before they had filled their desire
Psalms78:31 God’s anger flared against them
Psalms78:32 In spite of all this
Psalms78:33 So He ended their days in futility
Psalms78:34 When He slew them
Psalms78:35 And they remembered that God was their Rock
Psalms78:36 But they deceived Him with their mouths
Psalms78:37 Their hearts were disloyal to Him
Psalms78:38 And yet He was compassionate; He forgave their iniquity and did not destroy them. He often restrained His anger and did not unleash His full wrath.
Psalms78:39 He remembered that they were but flesh
Psalms78:40 How often they disobeyed Him in the wilderness and grieved Him in the desert!
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God and provoked the Holy One of Israel.
Psalms78:42 They did not remember His power — the day He redeemed them from the adversary
Psalms78:43 when He performed His signs in Egypt and His wonders in the fields of Zoan.
Psalms78:44 He turned their rivers to blood
Psalms78:45 He sent swarms of flies that devoured them
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to the grasshopper
Psalms78:47 He killed their vines with hailstones and their sycamore-figs with sleet.
Psalms78:48 He abandoned their cattle to the hail and their livestock to bolts of lightning.
Psalms78:49 He unleashed His fury against them
Psalms78:50 He cleared a path for His anger; He did not spare them from death but delivered their lives to the plague.
Psalms78:51 He struck all the firstborn of Egypt
Psalms78:52 He led out His people like sheep and guided them like a flock in the wilderness.
Psalms78:53 He led them safely
Psalms78:54 He brought them to His holy land
Psalms78:55 He drove out nations before them and apportioned their inheritance; He settled the tribes of Israel in their tents.
Psalms78:56 But they tested and disobeyed God Most High
Psalms78:57 They turned back and were faithless like their fathers
Psalms78:58 They enraged Him with their high places and provoked His jealousy with their idols.
Psalms78:59 On hearing it
Psalms78:60 He abandoned the tabernacle of Shiloh
Psalms78:61 He delivered His strength to captivity
Psalms78:62 He surrendered His people to the sword because He was enraged by His heritage.
Psalms78:63 Fire consumed His young men
Psalms78:64 His priests fell by the sword
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord awoke as from sleep
Psalms78:66 He beat back His foes; He put them to everlasting shame.
Psalms78:67 He rejected the tent of Joseph and refused the tribe of Ephraim.
Psalms78:68 But He chose the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 He built His sanctuary like the heights
Psalms78:70 He chose David His servant and took him from the sheepfolds;
Psalms78:71 from tending the ewes He brought him to be shepherd of His people Jacob
Psalms78:72 So David shepherded them with integrity of heart and guided them with skillful hands.

Psalms79:5 How long

Psalms79:13 Then we Your people

Psalms80:3 Restore us

Psalms80:18 Then we will not turn away from You; revive us

Psalms81:2 Lift up a song
Psalms81:3 Sound the ram’s horn at the New Moon
Psalms81:4 For this is a statute for Israel
Psalms81:5 He ordained it as a testimony for Joseph when he went out over the land of Egypt
Psalms81:6 “I relieved his shoulder of the burden; his hands were freed from the basket.
Psalms81:7 You called out in distress
Psalms81:8 Hear
Psalms81:9 There must be no strange god among you
Psalms81:10 I am the Lord your God
Psalms81:11 But My people would not listen to Me
Psalms81:12 So I gave them up to their stubborn hearts to follow their own devices.
Psalms81:13 If only My people would listen to Me
Psalms81:14 how soon I would subdue their enemies and turn My hand against their foes!
Psalms81:15 Those who hate the Lord would feign obedience
Psalms81:16 But I would feed you the finest wheat; with honey from the rock I would satisfy you.”
Psalms82:1 God presides in the divine assembly; He renders judgment among the gods:
Psalms82:2 “How long will you judge unjustly and show partiality to the wicked? Selah
Psalms82:3 Defend the cause of the weak and fatherless; uphold the rights of the afflicted and oppressed.
Psalms82:4 Rescue the weak and needy; save them from the hand of the wicked.
Psalms82:5 They do not know or understand; they wander in the darkness; all the foundations of the earth are shaken.
Psalms82:6 I have said

Psalms89:13 Mighty is Your arm; strong is Your hand. Your right hand is exalted.

Psalms89:16 They rejoice in Your name all day long
Psalms89:17 For You are the glory of their strength

Psalms89:21 My hand will sustain him; surely My arm will strengthen him.
Psalms89:22 No enemy will exact tribute; no wicked man will oppress him.
Psalms89:23 I will crush his foes before him and strike down those who hate him.
Psalms89:24 My faithfulness and loving devotion will be with him
Psalms89:25 I will set his hand over the sea
Psalms89:26 He will call to Me
Psalms89:27 I will indeed appoint him as My firstborn
Psalms89:28 I will forever preserve My loving devotion for him
Psalms89:29 I will establish his line forever
Psalms89:30 If his sons forsake My law and do not walk in My judgments
Psalms89:31 if they violate My statutes and fail to keep My commandments
Psalms89:32 I will attend to their transgression with the rod
Psalms89:33 But I will not withdraw My loving devotion from him
Psalms89:34 I will not violate My covenant or alter the utterance of My lips.
Psalms89:35 Once and for all I have sworn by My holiness— I will not lie to David—
Psalms89:36 his offspring shall endure forever
Psalms89:37 like the moon
Psalms89:38 Now
Psalms89:39 You have renounced the covenant with Your servant and sullied his crown in the dust.
Psalms89:40 You have broken down all his walls; You have reduced his strongholds to rubble.
Psalms89:41 All who pass by plunder him; he has become a reproach to his neighbors.
Psalms89:42 You have exalted the right hand of his foes; You have made all his enemies rejoice.
Psalms89:43 You have bent the edge of his sword and have not sustained him in battle.
Psalms89:44 You have ended his splendor and cast his throne to the ground.
Psalms89:45 You have cut short the days of his youth; You have covered him with shame. Selah

Psalms106:1 Hallelujah! Give thanks to the Lord
Psalms106:2 Who can describe the mighty acts of the Lord or fully proclaim His praise?
Psalms106:3 Blessed are those who uphold justice
Psalms106:4 Remember me
Psalms106:5 that I may see the prosperity of Your chosen ones
Psalms106:6 We have sinned like our fathers; we have done wrong and acted wickedly.
Psalms106:7 Our fathers in Egypt did not grasp Your wonders or remember Your abundant kindness; but they rebelled by the sea
Psalms106:8 Yet He saved them for the sake of His name
Psalms106:9 He rebuked the Red Sea
Psalms106:10 He saved them from the hand that hated them; He redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
Psalms106:11 The waters covered their foes; not one of them remained.
Psalms106:12 Then they believed His promises and sang His praise.
Psalms106:13 Yet they soon forgot His works and failed to wait for His counsel.
Psalms106:14 They craved intensely in the wilderness and tested God in the desert.
Psalms106:15 So He granted their request
Psalms106:16 In the camp they envied Moses
Psalms106:17 The earth opened up and swallowed Dathan; it covered the assembly of Abiram.
Psalms106:18 Then fire blazed through their company; flames consumed the wicked.
Psalms106:19 At Horeb they made a calf and worshiped a molten image.
Psalms106:20 They exchanged their Glory for the image of a grass-eating ox.
Psalms106:21 They forgot God their Savior
Psalms106:22 wondrous works in the land of Ham
Psalms106:23 So He said He would destroy them— had not Moses His chosen one stood before Him in the breach to divert His wrath from destroying them.
Psalms106:24 They despised the pleasant land; they did not believe His promise.
Psalms106:25 They grumbled in their tents and did not listen to the voice of the Lord.
Psalms106:26 So He raised His hand and swore to cast them down in the wilderness
Psalms106:27 to disperse their offspring among the nations and scatter them throughout the lands.
Psalms106:28 They yoked themselves to Baal of Peor and ate sacrifices offered to lifeless gods.
Psalms106:29 So they provoked the Lord to anger with their deeds
Psalms106:30 But Phinehas stood and intervened
Psalms106:31 It was credited to him as righteousness for endless generations to come.
Psalms106:32 At the waters of Meribah they angered the Lord
Psalms106:33 For they rebelled against His Spirit
Psalms106:34 They did not destroy the peoples as the Lord had commanded them
Psalms106:35 but they mingled with the nations and adopted their customs.
Psalms106:36 They worshiped their idols
Psalms106:37 They sacrificed their sons and their daughters to demons.
Psalms106:38 They shed innocent blood— the blood of their sons and daughters
Psalms106:39 They defiled themselves by their actions and prostituted themselves by their deeds.
Psalms106:40 So the anger of the Lord burned against His people
Psalms106:41 He delivered them into the hand of the nations
Psalms106:42 Their enemies oppressed them
Psalms106:43 Many times He rescued them
Psalms106:44 Nevertheless He heard their cry; He took note of their distress.
Psalms106:45 And He remembered His covenant with them
Psalms106:46 He made them objects of compassion to all who held them captive.
Psalms106:47 Save us
Psalms106:48 Blessed be the Lord

Psalms107:11 because they rebelled against the words of God and despised the counsel of the Most High.

Proverbs1:1 These are the proverbs of Solomon son of David
Proverbs1:2 for gaining wisdom and discipline
Proverbs1:3 and for receiving instruction in wise living and in righteousness
Proverbs1:4 To impart prudence to the simple and knowledge and discretion to the young
Proverbs1:5 let the wise listen and gain instruction
Proverbs1:6 by understanding the proverbs and parables
Proverbs1:7 The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge
Proverbs1:8 Listen
Proverbs1:9 For they are a garland of grace on your head and a pendant around your neck.
Proverbs1:10 My son
Proverbs1:11 If they say
Proverbs1:12 let us swallow them alive like Sheol
Proverbs1:13 We will find all manner of precious goods; we will fill our houses with plunder.
Proverbs1:14 Throw in your lot with us; let us all share one purse”—
Proverbs1:15 my son
Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 How futile it is to spread the net where any bird can see it!
Proverbs1:18 But they lie in wait for their own blood; they ambush their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 Such is the fate of all who are greedy
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom calls out in the street
Proverbs1:21 in the main concourse she cries aloud
Proverbs1:22 “How long
Proverbs1:23 If you had repented at my rebuke
Proverbs1:24 Because you refused my call
Proverbs1:25 because you neglected all my counsel
Proverbs1:26 in turn I will mock your calamity; I will sneer when terror strikes you
Proverbs1:27 when your dread comes like a storm
Proverbs1:28 Then they will call on me
Proverbs1:29 For they hated knowledge and chose not to fear the Lord.
Proverbs1:30 They accepted none of my counsel; they despised all my reproof.
Proverbs1:31 So they will eat the fruit of their own way
Proverbs1:32 For the waywardness of the simple will slay them
Proverbs1:33 But whoever listens to me will dwell in safety
Proverbs2:1 My son
Proverbs2:2 if you incline your ear to wisdom and direct your heart to understanding
Proverbs2:3 if you truly call out to insight and lift your voice to understanding
Proverbs2:4 if you seek it like silver and search it out like hidden treasure
Proverbs2:5 then you will discern the fear of the Lord and discover the knowledge of God.
Proverbs2:6 For the Lord gives wisdom; from His mouth come knowledge and understanding.
Proverbs2:7 He stores up sound wisdom for the upright; He is a shield to those who walk with integrity
Proverbs2:8 to guard the paths of justice and protect the way of His saints.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will discern righteousness and justice and equity—every good path.
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart
Proverbs2:11 Discretion will watch over you
Proverbs2:12 to deliver you from the way of evil
Proverbs2:13 from those who leave the straight paths to walk in the ways of darkness
Proverbs2:14 from those who enjoy doing evil and rejoice in the twistedness of evil
Proverbs2:15 whose paths are crooked and whose ways are devious.
Proverbs2:16 It will rescue you from the forbidden woman
Proverbs2:17 who abandons the partner of her youth and forgets the covenant of her God.
Proverbs2:18 For her house sinks down to death
Proverbs2:19 None who go to her return or negotiate the paths of life.
Proverbs2:20 So you will follow in the ways of the good
Proverbs2:21 For the upright will inhabit the land
Proverbs2:22 but the wicked will be cut off from the land
Proverbs3:1 My son
Proverbs3:2 for they will add length to your days
Proverbs3:3 Never let loving devotion or faithfulness leave you; bind them around your neck
Proverbs3:4 Then you will find favor and high regard in the sight of God and man.
Proverbs3:5 Trust in the Lord with all your heart
Proverbs3:6 in all your ways acknowledge Him
Proverbs3:7 Be not wise in your own eyes; fear the Lord and turn away from evil.
Proverbs3:8 This will bring healing to your body and refreshment to your bones.
Proverbs3:9 Honor the Lord with your wealth and with the firstfruits of all your harvest;
Proverbs3:10 then your barns will be filled with plenty
Proverbs3:11 My son
Proverbs3:12 for the Lord disciplines the one He loves
Proverbs3:13 Blessed is the man who finds wisdom
Proverbs3:14 for she is more profitable than silver
Proverbs3:15 She is more precious than rubies; nothing you desire compares with her.
Proverbs3:16 Long life is in her right hand; in her left hand are riches and honor.
Proverbs3:17 All her ways are pleasant
Proverbs3:18 She is a tree of life to those who embrace her
Proverbs3:19 The Lord founded the earth by wisdom and established the heavens by understanding.
Proverbs3:20 By His knowledge the watery depths were broken open
Proverbs3:21 My son
Proverbs3:22 They will be life to your soul and adornment to your neck.
Proverbs3:23 Then you will go on your way in safety
Proverbs3:24 When you lie down
Proverbs3:25 Do not fear sudden danger or the ruin that overtakes the wicked
Proverbs3:26 for the Lord will be your confidence and will keep your foot from the snare.
Proverbs3:27 Do not withhold good from the deserving when it is within your power to act.
Proverbs3:28 Do not tell your neighbor
Proverbs3:29 Do not devise evil against your neighbor
Proverbs3:30 Do not accuse a man without cause
Proverbs3:31 Do not envy a violent man or choose any of his ways;
Proverbs3:32 for the Lord detests the perverse
Proverbs3:33 The curse of the Lord is on the house of the wicked
Proverbs3:34 He mocks the mockers
Proverbs3:35 The wise will inherit honor
Proverbs4:1 Listen
Proverbs4:2 For I give you sound teaching; do not abandon my directive.
Proverbs4:3 When I was a son to my father
Proverbs4:4 he taught me and said
Proverbs4:5 Get wisdom
Proverbs4:6 Do not forsake wisdom
Proverbs4:7 Wisdom is supreme; so acquire wisdom. And whatever you may acquire
Proverbs4:8 Prize her
Proverbs4:9 She will set a garland of grace on your head; she will present you with a crown of beauty.”
Proverbs4:10 Listen
Proverbs4:11 I will guide you in the way of wisdom; I will lead you on straight paths.
Proverbs4:12 When you walk
Proverbs4:13 Hold on to instruction; do not let go. Guard it
Proverbs4:14 Do not set foot on the path of the wicked or walk in the way of evildoers.
Proverbs4:15 Avoid it; do not travel on it. Turn from it and pass on by.
Proverbs4:16 For they cannot sleep unless they do evil; they are deprived of slumber until they make someone fall.
Proverbs4:17 For they eat the bread of wickedness and drink the wine of violence.
Proverbs4:18 The path of the righteous is like the first gleam of dawn
Proverbs4:19 But the way of the wicked is like the darkest gloom; they do not know what makes them stumble.
Proverbs4:20 My son
Proverbs4:21 Do not lose sight of them; keep them within your heart.
Proverbs4:22 For they are life to those who find them
Proverbs4:23 Guard your heart with all diligence
Proverbs4:24 Put away deception from your mouth; keep your lips from perverse speech.
Proverbs4:25 Let your eyes look forward; fix your gaze straight ahead.
Proverbs4:26 Make a level path for your feet
Proverbs4:27 Do not swerve to the right or to the left; turn your feet away from evil.
Proverbs5:1 My son
Proverbs5:2 that you may maintain discretion and your lips may preserve knowledge.
Proverbs5:3 Though the lips of the forbidden woman drip honey and her speech is smoother than oil
Proverbs5:4 in the end she is bitter as wormwood
Proverbs5:5 Her feet go down to death; her steps lead straight to Sheol.
Proverbs5:6 She does not consider the path of life; she does not know that her ways are unstable.
Proverbs5:7 So now
Proverbs5:8 Keep your path far from her; do not go near the door of her house
Proverbs5:9 lest you concede your vigor to others
Proverbs5:10 lest strangers feast on your wealth
Proverbs5:11 At the end of your life you will groan when your flesh and your body are spent
Proverbs5:12 and you will say
Proverbs5:13 I did not listen to the voice of my teachers or incline my ear to my mentors.
Proverbs5:14 I am on the brink of utter ruin in the midst of the whole assembly.”
Proverbs5:15 Drink water from your own cistern

Proverbs7:1 My son
Proverbs7:2 Keep my commandments and live; guard my teachings as the apple of your eye.
Proverbs7:3 Tie them to your fingers; write them on the tablet of your heart.
Proverbs7:4 Say to wisdom
Proverbs7:5 that they may keep you from the adulteress
Proverbs7:6 For at the window of my house I looked through the lattice.
Proverbs7:7 I saw among the simple
Proverbs7:8 crossing the street near her corner
Proverbs7:9 at twilight
Proverbs7:10 Then a woman came out to meet him
Proverbs7:11 She is loud and defiant; her feet do not remain at home.
Proverbs7:12 Now in the street
Proverbs7:13 She seizes him and kisses him; she brazenly says to him:
Proverbs7:14 “I have made my peace offerings; today I have paid my vows.
Proverbs7:15 So I came out to meet you; I sought you
Proverbs7:16 I have decked my bed with coverings
Proverbs7:17 I have perfumed my bed with myrrh
Proverbs7:18 Come
Proverbs7:19 For my husband is not at home; he has gone on a long journey.
Proverbs7:20 He took with him a bag of money and will not return till the moon is full.”
Proverbs7:21 With her great persuasion she entices him; with her flattering lips she lures him.
Proverbs7:22 He follows her on impulse
Proverbs7:23 until an arrow pierces his liver
Proverbs7:24 Now
Proverbs7:25 Do not let your heart turn aside to her ways; do not stray into her paths.
Proverbs7:26 For she has brought many down to death; her slain are many in number.
Proverbs7:27 Her house is the road to Sheol

Proverbs8:2 On the heights overlooking the road
Proverbs8:3 Beside the gates to the city
Proverbs8:4 “To you
Proverbs8:5 O simple ones
Proverbs8:6 Listen
Proverbs8:7 For my mouth will speak the truth
Proverbs8:8 All the words of my mouth are righteous; none are crooked or perverse.

Proverbs8:11 For wisdom is more precious than rubies
Proverbs8:12 I
Proverbs8:13 To fear the Lord is to hate evil; I hate arrogant pride
Proverbs8:14 Counsel and sound judgment are mine; I have insight and strength.

Proverbs8:17 I love those who love me
Proverbs8:18 With me are riches and honor
Proverbs8:19 My fruit is better than gold
Proverbs8:20 I walk in the way of righteousness
Proverbs8:21 bestowing wealth on those who love me and making their treasuries full.
Proverbs8:22 The Lord created me as His first course
Proverbs8:23 From everlasting I was established
Proverbs8:24 When there were no watery depths
Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains were settled
Proverbs8:26 before He made the land or fields
Proverbs8:27 I was there when He established the heavens
Proverbs8:28 when He established the clouds above
Proverbs8:29 when He set a boundary for the sea
Proverbs8:30 Then I was a skilled craftsman at His side
Proverbs8:31 I was rejoicing in His whole world
Proverbs8:32 Now therefore
Proverbs8:33 Listen to instruction and be wise; do not ignore it.
Proverbs8:34 Blessed is the man who listens to me
Proverbs8:35 For whoever finds me finds life and obtains the favor of the Lord.
Proverbs8:36 But he who fails to find me harms himself; all who hate me love death.”

Proverbs31:10 A wife of noble character
Proverbs31:11 The heart of her husband trusts in her
Proverbs31:12 She brings him good and not harm all the days of her life.
Proverbs31:13 She selects wool and flax and works with eager hands.
Proverbs31:14 She is like the merchant ships
Proverbs31:15 She rises while it is still night to provide food for her household and portions for her maidservants.

Proverbs31:17 She girds herself with strength and shows that her arms are strong.
Proverbs31:18 She sees that her gain is good

Proverbs31:21 When it snows

Proverbs31:26 She opens her mouth with wisdom

Ecclesiastes1:1 These are the words of the Teacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 “Futility of futilities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What does a man gain from all his labor
Ecclesiastes1:4 Generations come and generations go
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises and the sun sets; it hurries back to where it rises.
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows southward
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the rivers flow into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are wearisome
Ecclesiastes1:9 What has been will be again
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a case where one can say
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of those who came before
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I set my mind to seek and explore by wisdom all that is done under heaven. What a heavy burden God has laid upon the sons of men to occupy them!
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the things that are done under the sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be straightened
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 So I set my mind to know wisdom and madness and folly; I learned that this
Ecclesiastes1:18 For with much wisdom comes much sorrow
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I sought to cheer my body with wine and to embrace folly—my mind still guiding me with wisdom—until I could see what was worthwhile for men to do under heaven during the few days of their lives.
Ecclesiastes2:4 I expanded my pursuits. I built houses and planted vineyards for myself.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks for myself
Ecclesiastes2:6 I built reservoirs to water my groves of flourishing trees.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I acquired menservants and maidservants
Ecclesiastes2:8 and I accumulated for myself silver and gold and the treasure of kings and provinces. I gathered to myself male and female singers
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became great and surpassed all in Jerusalem who had preceded me; and my wisdom remained with me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 Anything my eyes desired
Ecclesiastes2:11 Yet when I considered all the works that my hands had accomplished and what I had toiled to achieve
Ecclesiastes2:12 Then I turned to consider wisdom and madness and folly; for what more can the king’s successor do than what has already been accomplished?
Ecclesiastes2:13 And I saw that wisdom exceeds folly
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man has eyes in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 So I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:16 For there is no lasting remembrance of the wise
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 I hated all for which I had toiled under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether that man will be wise or foolish? Yet he will take over all the labor at which I have worked skillfully under the sun. This too is futile.
Ecclesiastes2:20 So my heart began to despair over all the labor that I had done under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 When there is a man who has labored with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what does a man get for all the toil and striving with which he labors under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 Indeed
Ecclesiastes2:24 Nothing is better for a man than to eat and drink and enjoy his work. I have also seen that this is from the hand of God.
Ecclesiastes2:25 For apart from Him
Ecclesiastes2:26 To the man who is pleasing in His sight
Ecclesiastes3:1 To everything there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 a time to be born and a time to die
Ecclesiastes3:3 a time to kill and a time to heal
Ecclesiastes3:4 a time to weep and a time to laugh
Ecclesiastes3:5 a time to cast away stones and a time to gather stones together
Ecclesiastes3:6 a time to search and a time to count as lost
Ecclesiastes3:7 a time to tear and a time to mend
Ecclesiastes3:8 a time to love and a time to hate
Ecclesiastes3:9 What does the worker gain from his toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the burden that God has laid upon the sons of men to occupy them.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in the hearts of men
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them than to rejoice and do good while they live
Ecclesiastes3:13 and also that every man should eat and drink and find satisfaction in all his labor—this is the gift of God.
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that everything God does endures forever; nothing can be added to it or taken from it. God does it so that they should fear Him.
Ecclesiastes3:15 What exists has already been
Ecclesiastes3:16 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes3:19 For the fates of both men and beasts are the same: As one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to one place; all come from dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows if the spirit of man rises upward and the spirit of the animal descends into the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 I have seen that there is nothing better for a man than to enjoy his work
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I looked
Ecclesiastes4:2 So I admired the dead
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better than both is he who has not yet existed
Ecclesiastes4:4 I saw that all labor and success spring from a man’s envy of his neighbor. This too is futile and a pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands and consumes his own flesh.
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better one handful with tranquility than two handfuls with toil and pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes4:7 Again
Ecclesiastes4:8 There is a man all alone
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if one falls down
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And though one may be overpowered
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor but wise youth than an old but foolish king who no longer knows how to take a warning.
Ecclesiastes4:14 For the youth has come from the prison to the kingship
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw that all who lived and walked under the sun followed this second one
Ecclesiastes4:16 There is no limit to all the people who were before them. Yet the successor will not be celebrated by those who come even later. This too is futile and a pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes5:1 Guard your steps when you go to the house of God. Draw near to listen rather than to offer the sacrifice of fools
Ecclesiastes5:2 Do not be quick to speak
Ecclesiastes5:3 As a dream comes through many cares
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you make a vow to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better not to vow than to make a vow and not fulfill it.
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not let your mouth cause your flesh to sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 For as many dreams bring futility
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see the oppression of the poor and the denial of justice and righteousness in the province
Ecclesiastes5:9 The produce of the earth is taken by all; the king himself profits from the fields.
Ecclesiastes5:10 He who loves money is never satisfied by money
Ecclesiastes5:11 When good things increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of the worker is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a grievous evil I have seen under the sun: wealth hoarded to the harm of its owner
Ecclesiastes5:14 or wealth lost in a failed venture
Ecclesiastes5:15 As a man came from his mother’s womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This too is a grievous evil: Exactly as a man is born
Ecclesiastes5:17 Moreover
Ecclesiastes5:18 Here is what I have seen to be good and fitting: to eat and drink
Ecclesiastes5:19 Furthermore
Ecclesiastes5:20 For a man seldom considers the days of his life
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is another evil I have seen under the sun
Ecclesiastes6:2 God gives a man riches
Ecclesiastes6:3 A man may father a hundred children and live for many years; yet no matter how long he lives
Ecclesiastes6:4 For a stillborn child enters in futility and departs in darkness
Ecclesiastes6:5 The child
Ecclesiastes6:6 even if he lives a thousand years twice over but fails to enjoy his prosperity. Do not all go to the same place?
Ecclesiastes6:7 All a man’s labor is for his mouth
Ecclesiastes6:8 What advantage
Ecclesiastes6:9 Better what the eye can see than the wandering of desire. This too is futile and a pursuit of the wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Whatever exists was named long ago
Ecclesiastes6:11 For the more words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for a man during the few days in which he passes through his fleeting life like a shadow? Who can tell a man what will come after him under the sun?
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good name is better than fine perfume
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to enter a house of mourning than a house of feasting
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 The heart of the wise is in the house of mourning
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to heed a wise man’s rebuke than to listen to the song of fools.
Ecclesiastes7:6 For like the crackling of thorns under the pot
Ecclesiastes7:7 Surely extortion turns a wise man into a fool
Ecclesiastes7:8 The end of a matter is better than the beginning
Ecclesiastes7:9 Do not be quickly provoked in your spirit
Ecclesiastes7:10 Do not say
Ecclesiastes7:11 Wisdom
Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom
Ecclesiastes7:13 Consider the work of God: Who can straighten what He has bent?
Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity
Ecclesiastes7:15 In my futile life I have seen both of these: A righteous man perishing in his righteousness
Ecclesiastes7:16 Do not be overly righteous
Ecclesiastes7:17 Do not be excessively wicked
Ecclesiastes7:18 It is good to grasp the one and not let the other slip from your hand. For he who fears God will follow both warnings.
Ecclesiastes7:19 Wisdom makes the wise man stronger than ten rulers in a city.
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is no righteous man on earth who does good and never sins.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Do not pay attention to every word that is spoken
Ecclesiastes7:22 For you know in your heart that many times you yourself have cursed others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All this I tested by wisdom
Ecclesiastes7:24 What exists is out of reach and very deep. Who can fathom it?
Ecclesiastes7:25 I directed my mind to understand
Ecclesiastes7:26 And I find more bitter than death the woman who is a snare
Ecclesiastes7:27 “Behold
Ecclesiastes7:28 While my soul was still searching but not finding
Ecclesiastes7:29 Only this have I found: I have discovered that God made men upright
Ecclesiastes8:1 Who is like the wise man? Who knows the interpretation of a matter? A man’s wisdom brightens his face
Ecclesiastes8:2 Keep the king’s command
Ecclesiastes8:3 Do not hasten to leave his presence
Ecclesiastes8:4 For the king’s word is supreme
Ecclesiastes8:5 Whoever keeps his command will come to no harm
Ecclesiastes8:6 For there is a right time and procedure to every purpose
Ecclesiastes8:7 Since no one knows what will happen
Ecclesiastes8:8 As no man has power over the wind to contain it
Ecclesiastes8:9 All this I have seen
Ecclesiastes8:10 Then too
Ecclesiastes8:11 When the sentence for a crime is not speedily executed
Ecclesiastes8:12 Although a sinner does evil a hundred times and still lives long
Ecclesiastes8:13 Yet because the wicked do not fear God
Ecclesiastes8:14 There is a futility that is done on the earth: There are righteous men who get what the actions of the wicked deserve
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I commended the enjoyment of life
Ecclesiastes8:16 When I applied my mind to know wisdom and to observe the task that one performs on the earth—though his eyes do not see sleep in the day or even in the night—
Ecclesiastes8:17 I saw every work of God
Ecclesiastes9:1 So I took all this to heart and concluded that the righteous and the wise
Ecclesiastes9:2 It is the same for all: There is a common fate for the righteous and the wicked
Ecclesiastes9:3 This is an evil in everything that is done under the sun: There is one fate for everyone. Furthermore
Ecclesiastes9:4 There is hope
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love
Ecclesiastes9:7 Go
Ecclesiastes9:8 Let your garments always be white
Ecclesiastes9:9 Enjoy life with your beloved wife all the days of the fleeting life that God has given you under the sun—all your fleeting days. For this is your portion in life and in your labor under the sun.
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever you find to do with your hands
Ecclesiastes9:11 I saw something else under the sun: The race is not to the swift
Ecclesiastes9:12 For surely no man knows his time: Like fish caught in a cruel net or birds trapped in a snare
Ecclesiastes9:13 I have also seen this wisdom under the sun
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small city with few men. A mighty king came against it
Ecclesiastes9:15 Now a poor wise man was found in the city
Ecclesiastes9:16 And I said
Ecclesiastes9:17 The calm words of the wise are heeded over the shouts of a ruler among fools.
Ecclesiastes9:18 Wisdom is better than weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 As dead flies bring a stench to the perfumer’s oil
Ecclesiastes10:2 A wise man’s heart inclines to the right
Ecclesiastes10:3 Even as the fool walks along the road
Ecclesiastes10:4 If the ruler’s temper flares against you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is an evil I have seen under the sun— an error that proceeds from the ruler:
Ecclesiastes10:6 Folly is appointed to great heights
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have seen slaves on horseback
Ecclesiastes10:8 He who digs a pit may fall into it
Ecclesiastes10:9 The one who quarries stones may be injured by them
Ecclesiastes10:10 If the axe is dull and the blade unsharpened
Ecclesiastes10:11 If the snake bites before it is charmed
Ecclesiastes10:12 The words of a wise man’s mouth are gracious
Ecclesiastes10:13 The beginning of his talk is folly
Ecclesiastes10:14 Yet the fool multiplies words. No one knows what is coming
Ecclesiastes10:15 The toil of a fool wearies him
Ecclesiastes10:16 Woe to you
Ecclesiastes10:17 Blessed are you
Ecclesiastes10:18 Through laziness the roof caves in
Ecclesiastes10:19 A feast is prepared for laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Do not curse the king even in your thoughts
Ecclesiastes11:1 Cast your bread upon the waters
Ecclesiastes11:2 Divide your portion among seven
Ecclesiastes11:3 If the clouds are full
Ecclesiastes11:4 He who watches the wind will fail to sow
Ecclesiastes11:5 As you do not know the path of the wind
Ecclesiastes11:6 Sow your seed in the morning
Ecclesiastes11:7 Light is sweet
Ecclesiastes11:8 So if a man lives many years
Ecclesiastes11:9 Rejoice
Ecclesiastes11:10 So banish sorrow from your heart
Ecclesiastes12:1 Remember your Creator in the days of your youth
Ecclesiastes12:2 before the light of the sun
Ecclesiastes12:3 on the day the keepers of the house tremble and the strong men stoop
Ecclesiastes12:4 when the doors to the street are shut and the sound of the mill fades away
Ecclesiastes12:5 when men fear the heights and dangers of the road
Ecclesiastes12:6 Remember Him before the silver cord is snapped and the golden bowl is crushed
Ecclesiastes12:7 before the dust returns to the ground from which it came and the spirit returns to God who gave it.
Ecclesiastes12:8 “Futility of futilities
Ecclesiastes12:9 Not only was the Teacher wise
Ecclesiastes12:10 The Teacher searched to find delightful sayings and to record accurate words of truth.
Ecclesiastes12:11 The words of the wise are like goads
Ecclesiastes12:12 And by these
Ecclesiastes12:13 When all has been heard
Ecclesiastes12:14 For God will bring every deed into judgment
SongOfSongs1:1 This is Solomon’s Song of Songs.
SongOfSongs1:2 Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth! For your love is more delightful than wine.
SongOfSongs1:3 The fragrance of your perfume is pleasing; your name is like perfume poured out. No wonder the maidens adore you.
SongOfSongs1:4 Take me away with you—let us hurry! May the king bring me to his chambers. We will rejoice and delight in you; we will praise your love more than wine. It is only right that they adore you.
SongOfSongs1:5 I am dark
SongOfSongs1:6 Do not stare because I am dark
SongOfSongs1:7 Tell me
SongOfSongs1:8 If you do not know
SongOfSongs1:9 I compare you
SongOfSongs1:10 Your cheeks are beautiful with ornaments
SongOfSongs1:11 We will make you ornaments of gold
SongOfSongs1:12 While the king was at his table
SongOfSongs1:13 My beloved is to me a sachet of myrrh resting between my breasts.
SongOfSongs1:14 My beloved is to me a cluster of henna blossoms in the vineyards of En-gedi.
SongOfSongs1:15 How beautiful you are
SongOfSongs1:16 How handsome you are
SongOfSongs1:17 The beams of our house are cedars; our rafters are fragrant firs.

SongOfSongs2:2 Like a lily among the thorns is my darling among the maidens.

SongOfSongs2:4 He has brought me to the house of wine
SongOfSongs2:5 Sustain me with raisins; refresh me with apples
SongOfSongs2:6 His left hand is under my head
SongOfSongs2:7 O daughters of Jerusalem
SongOfSongs2:8 Listen! My beloved approaches. Look! Here he comes
SongOfSongs2:9 My beloved is like a gazelle or a young stag. Look
SongOfSongs2:10 My beloved calls to me
SongOfSongs2:11 For now the winter is past; the rain is over and gone.
SongOfSongs2:12 The flowers have appeared in the countryside; the season of singing has come
SongOfSongs2:13 The fig tree ripens its figs; the blossoming vines spread their fragrance. Arise
SongOfSongs2:14 O my dove in the clefts of the rock
SongOfSongs2:15 Catch for us the foxes— the little foxes that ruin the vineyards— for our vineyards are in bloom.
SongOfSongs2:16 My beloved is mine and I am his; he pastures his flock among the lilies.
SongOfSongs2:17 Before the day breaks and shadows flee
