Isaiah1:1 This is the vision concerning Judah and Jerusalem that Isaiah son of Amoz saw during the reigns of Uzziah
Isaiah1:2 Listen
Isaiah1:3 The ox knows its owner
Isaiah1:4 Alas
Isaiah1:5 Why do you want more beatings? Why do you keep rebelling? Your head has a massive wound
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of your foot to the top of your head
Isaiah1:7 Your land is desolate; your cities are burned with fire. Foreigners devour your fields before you— a desolation demolished by strangers.
Isaiah1:8 And the Daughter of Zion is abandoned like a shelter in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 Unless the Lord of Hosts had left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the Lord
Isaiah1:11 “What good to Me is your multitude of sacrifices?” says the Lord. “I am full from the burnt offerings of rams and the fat of well-fed cattle; I take no delight in the blood of bulls and lambs and goats.
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before Me
Isaiah1:13 Bring your worthless offerings no more; your incense is detestable to Me— your New Moons
Isaiah1:14 I hate your New Moons and your appointed feasts. They have become a burden to Me; I am weary of bearing them.
Isaiah1:15 When you spread out your hands in prayer
Isaiah1:16 Wash and cleanse yourselves. Remove your evil deeds from My sight. Stop doing evil!
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do right; seek justice and correct the oppressor. Defend the fatherless and plead the case of the widow.”
Isaiah1:18 “Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 But if you resist and rebel
Isaiah1:21 See how the faithful city has become a harlot! She once was full of justice; righteousness resided within her
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross; your fine wine is diluted with water.
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebels
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord GOD of Hosts
Isaiah1:25 I will turn My hand against you; I will thoroughly purge your dross; I will remove all your impurities.
Isaiah1:26 I will restore your judges as at first
Isaiah1:27 Zion will be redeemed with justice
Isaiah1:28 But rebels and sinners will together be shattered
Isaiah1:29 Surely you will be ashamed of the sacred oaks in which you have delighted; you will be embarrassed by the gardens that you have chosen.
Isaiah1:30 For you will become like an oak whose leaves are withered
Isaiah1:31 The strong man will become tinder and his work will be a spark; both will burn together
Isaiah2:1 This is the message that was revealed to Isaiah son of Amoz concerning Judah and Jerusalem:
Isaiah2:2 In the last days the mountain of the house of the Lord will be established as the chief of the mountains; it will be raised above the hills
Isaiah2:3 And many peoples will come and say: “Come
Isaiah2:4 Then He will judge between the nations and arbitrate for many peoples. They will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks. Nation will no longer take up the sword against nation
Isaiah2:5 Come
Isaiah2:6 For You have abandoned Your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land is full of silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Their land is full of idols; they bow down to the work of their hands
Isaiah2:9 So mankind is brought low
Isaiah2:10 Go into the rocks and hide in the dust from the terror of the Lord and the splendor of His majesty.
Isaiah2:11 The proud look of man will be humbled
Isaiah2:12 For the Day of the Lord of Hosts will come against all the proud and lofty
Isaiah2:13 against all the cedars of Lebanon
Isaiah2:14 against all the tall mountains
Isaiah2:15 against every high tower
Isaiah2:16 against every ship of Tarshish
Isaiah2:17 So the pride of man will be brought low
Isaiah2:18 and the idols will vanish completely.
Isaiah2:19 Men will flee to caves in the rocks and holes in the ground
Isaiah2:20 In that day men will cast away to the moles and bats their idols of silver and gold— the idols they made to worship.
Isaiah2:21 They will flee to caverns in the rocks and crevices in the cliffs
Isaiah2:22 Put no more trust in man
Isaiah3:1 For behold
Isaiah3:2 the mighty man and the warrior
Isaiah3:3 the commander of fifty and the dignitary
Isaiah3:4 “I will make mere lads their leaders
Isaiah3:5 The people will oppress one another
Isaiah3:6 A man will seize his brother within his father’s house: “You have a cloak—you be our leader! Take charge of this heap of rubble.”
Isaiah3:7 On that day he will cry aloud: “I am not a healer. I have no food or clothing in my house. Do not make me leader of the people!”
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen because they spoke and acted against the Lord
Isaiah3:9 The expression on their faces testifies against them
Isaiah3:10 Tell the righteous it will be well with them
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked; disaster is upon them! For they will be repaid with what their hands have done.
Isaiah3:12 Youths oppress My people
Isaiah3:13 The Lord arises to contend; He stands to judge the people.
Isaiah3:14 The Lord brings this charge against the elders and leaders of His people: “You have devoured the vineyard; the plunder of the poor is in your houses.
Isaiah3:15 Why do you crush My people and grind the faces of the poor?” declares the Lord GOD of Hosts.
Isaiah3:16 The Lord also says: “Because the daughters of Zion are haughty— walking with heads held high and wanton eyes
Isaiah3:17 the Lord will bring sores on the heads of the daughters of Zion
Isaiah3:18 In that day the Lord will take away their finery: their anklets and headbands and crescents;
Isaiah3:19 their pendants
Isaiah3:20 their headdresses
Isaiah3:21 their signet rings and nose rings;
Isaiah3:22 their festive robes
Isaiah3:23 and their mirrors
Isaiah3:24 Instead of fragrance there will be a stench; instead of a belt
Isaiah3:25 Your men will fall by the sword
Isaiah3:26 And the gates of Zion will lament and mourn; destitute
Isaiah4:1 In that day seven women will take hold of one man and say
Isaiah4:2 On that day the Branch of the Lord will be beautiful and glorious
Isaiah4:3 Whoever remains in Zion and whoever is left in Jerusalem will be called holy— all in Jerusalem who are recorded among the living—
Isaiah4:4 when the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and cleansed the bloodstains from the heart of Jerusalem by a spirit of judgment and a spirit of fire.
Isaiah4:5 Then the Lord will create over all of Mount Zion and over her assemblies a cloud of smoke by day and a glowing flame of fire by night. For over all the glory there will be a canopy
Isaiah4:6 a shelter to give shade from the heat by day
Isaiah5:1 I will sing for my beloved a song of his vineyard: My beloved had a vineyard on a very fertile hill.
Isaiah5:2 He dug it up and cleared the stones and planted the finest vines. He built a watchtower in the middle and dug out a winepress as well. He waited for the vineyard to yield good grapes
Isaiah5:3 “And now
Isaiah5:4 What more could I have done for My vineyard than I already did for it? Why
Isaiah5:5 Now I will tell you what I am about to do to My vineyard: I will take away its hedge
Isaiah5:6 I will make it a wasteland
Isaiah5:7 For the vineyard of the Lord of Hosts is the house of Israel
Isaiah5:8 Woe to you who add house to house and join field to field until no place is left and you live alone in the land.
Isaiah5:9 I heard the Lord of Hosts declare: “Surely many houses will become desolate
Isaiah5:10 For ten acres of vineyard will yield but a bath of wine
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning in pursuit of strong drink
Isaiah5:12 At their feasts are the lyre and harp
Isaiah5:13 Therefore My people will go into exile for their lack of understanding; their dignitaries are starving and their masses are parched with thirst.
Isaiah5:14 Therefore Sheol enlarges its throat and opens wide its enormous jaws
Isaiah5:15 So mankind will be brought low
Isaiah5:16 But the Lord of Hosts will be exalted by His justice
Isaiah5:17 Lambs will graze as in their own pastures
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who draw iniquity with cords of deceit and pull sin along with cart ropes
Isaiah5:19 to those who say
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good and good evil
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own eyes and clever in their own sight.
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are heroes in drinking wine and champions in mixing beer
Isaiah5:23 who acquit the guilty for a bribe and deprive the innocent of justice.
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 Therefore the anger of the Lord burns against His people; His hand is raised against them to strike them down. The mountains quake
Isaiah5:26 He lifts a banner for the distant nations and whistles for those at the ends of the earth. Behold—how speedily and swiftly they come!
Isaiah5:27 None of them grows weary or stumbles; no one slumbers or sleeps. No belt is loose and no sandal strap is broken.
Isaiah5:28 Their arrows are sharpened
Isaiah5:29 Their roaring is like that of a lion; they roar like young lions. They growl and seize their prey; they carry it away from deliverance.
Isaiah5:30 In that day they will roar over it

Isaiah6:2 Above Him stood seraphim
Isaiah6:3 And they were calling out to one another: “Holy
Isaiah6:4 At the sound of their voices the doorposts and thresholds shook
Isaiah6:5 Then I said: “Woe is me
Isaiah6:6 Then one of the seraphim flew to me
Isaiah6:7 And with it he touched my mouth and said: “Now that this has touched your lips
Isaiah6:8 Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying: “Whom shall I send? Who will go for Us?” And I said: “Here am I. Send me!”
Isaiah6:9 And He replied: “Go and tell this people
Isaiah6:10 Make the hearts of this people calloused; deafen their ears and close their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes
Isaiah6:11 Then I asked: “How long
Isaiah6:12 until the Lord has driven men far away and the land is utterly forsaken.
Isaiah6:13 And though a tenth remains in the land
Isaiah7:1 Now in the days that Ahaz son of Jotham
Isaiah7:2 When it was reported to the house of David that Aram was in league with Ephraim
Isaiah7:3 Then the Lord said to Isaiah
Isaiah7:4 and say to him: Calm down and be quiet. Do not be afraid or disheartened over these two smoldering stubs of firewood—over the fierce anger of Rezin and Aram and of the son of Remaliah.
Isaiah7:5 For Aram
Isaiah7:6 ‘Let us invade Judah
Isaiah7:7 But this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘It will not arise; it will not happen.
Isaiah7:8 For the head of Aram is Damascus
Isaiah7:9 The head of Ephraim is Samaria
Isaiah7:10 Again the Lord spoke to Ahaz
Isaiah7:11 “Ask for a sign from the Lord your God
Isaiah7:12 But Ahaz replied
Isaiah7:13 Then Isaiah said
Isaiah7:14 Therefore the Lord Himself will give you a sign: Behold
Isaiah7:15 By the time He knows enough to reject evil and choose good
Isaiah7:16 For before the boy knows enough to reject evil and choose good
Isaiah7:17 The Lord will bring on you and on your people and on the house of your father a time unlike any since the day Ephraim separated from Judah—He will bring the king of Assyria.”
Isaiah7:18 On that day the Lord will whistle to the flies at the farthest streams of the Nile and to the bees in the land of Assyria.
Isaiah7:19 And they will all come and settle in the steep ravines and clefts of the rocks
Isaiah7:20 On that day the Lord will use a razor hired from beyond the Euphrates —the king of Assyria—to shave your head and the hair of your legs
Isaiah7:21 On that day a man will raise a young cow and two sheep
Isaiah7:22 and from the abundance of milk they give
Isaiah7:23 And on that day
Isaiah7:24 Men will go there with bow and arrow
Isaiah7:25 For fear of the briers and thorns
Isaiah8:1 Then the Lord said to me
Isaiah8:2 And I will appoint for Myself trustworthy witnesses—Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberekiah.”

Isaiah8:4 For before the boy knows how to cry ‘Father’ or ‘Mother
Isaiah8:5 And the Lord spoke to me further:
Isaiah8:6 “Because this people has rejected the gently flowing waters of Shiloah and rejoiced in Rezin and the son of Remaliah
Isaiah8:7 the Lord will surely bring against them the mighty floodwaters of the Euphrates — the king of Assyria and all his pomp. It will overflow its channels and overrun its banks.
Isaiah8:8 It will pour into Judah
Isaiah8:9 Huddle together
Isaiah8:10 Devise a plan
Isaiah8:11 For this is what the Lord has spoken to me with a strong hand
Isaiah8:12 “Do not call conspiracy everything these people regard as conspiracy. Do not fear what they fear; do not live in dread.
Isaiah8:13 The Lord of Hosts is the One you shall regard as holy. Only He should be feared; only He should be dreaded.
Isaiah8:14 And He will be a sanctuary— but to both houses of Israel a stone of stumbling and a rock of offense
Isaiah8:15 Many will stumble over these; they will fall and be broken; they will be ensnared and captured.”
Isaiah8:16 Bind up the testimony and seal the law among my disciples.
Isaiah8:17 I will wait for the Lord
Isaiah8:18 Here am I
Isaiah8:19 When men tell you to consult the spirits of the dead and the spiritists who whisper and mutter
Isaiah8:20 To the law and to the testimony! If they do not speak according to this word
Isaiah8:21 They will roam the land
Isaiah8:22 Then they will look to the earth and see only distress and darkness and the gloom of anguish. And they will be driven into utter darkness.
Isaiah9:1 Nevertheless
Isaiah9:2 The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of the shadow of death
Isaiah9:3 You have enlarged the nation and increased its joy. The people rejoice before You as they rejoice at harvest time
Isaiah9:4 For as in the day of Midian You have shattered the yoke of their burden
Isaiah9:5 For every trampling boot of battle and every garment rolled in blood will be burned as fuel for the fire.
Isaiah9:6 For unto us a child is born
Isaiah9:7 Of the increase of His government and peace there will be no end. He will reign on the throne of David and over his kingdom
Isaiah9:8 The Lord has sent a message against Jacob
Isaiah9:9 All the people will know it— Ephraim and the dwellers of Samaria. With pride and arrogance of heart they will say:
Isaiah9:10 “The bricks have fallen
Isaiah9:11 The Lord has raised up the foes of Rezin against him and joined his enemies together.
Isaiah9:12 Aram from the east and Philistia from the west have devoured Israel with open mouths. Despite all this
Isaiah9:13 But the people did not return to Him who struck them; they did not seek the Lord of Hosts.
Isaiah9:14 So the Lord will cut off Israel’s head and tail
Isaiah9:15 The head is the elder and honorable man
Isaiah9:16 For those who guide this people mislead them
Isaiah9:17 Therefore the Lord takes no pleasure in their young men; He has no compassion on their fatherless and widows. For every one of them is godless and wicked
Isaiah9:18 For wickedness burns like a fire that consumes the thorns and briers and kindles the forest thickets which roll upward in billows of smoke.

Isaiah10:1 Woe to those who enact unjust statutes and issue oppressive decrees
Isaiah10:2 to deprive the poor of fair treatment and withhold justice from the oppressed of My people
Isaiah10:3 What will you do on the day of reckoning when devastation comes from afar? To whom will you flee for help? Where will you leave your wealth?
Isaiah10:4 Nothing will remain but to crouch among the captives or fall among the slain. Despite all this
Isaiah10:5 Woe to Assyria
Isaiah10:6 I will send him against a godless nation; I will dispatch him against a people destined for My rage
Isaiah10:7 But this is not his intention; this is not his plan. For it is in his heart to destroy and cut off many nations.
Isaiah10:8 “Are not all my commanders kings?” he says.
Isaiah10:9 “Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?
Isaiah10:10 As my hand seized the idolatrous kingdoms whose images surpassed those of Jerusalem and Samaria
Isaiah10:11 and as I have done to Samaria and its idols
Isaiah10:12 So when the Lord has completed all His work against Mount Zion and Jerusalem
Isaiah10:13 For he says: ‘By the strength of my hand I have done this
Isaiah10:14 My hand reached as into a nest to seize the wealth of the nations. Like one gathering abandoned eggs
Isaiah10:15 Does an axe raise itself above the one who swings it? Does a saw boast over him who saws with it? It would be like a rod waving the one who lifts it
Isaiah10:16 Therefore the Lord GOD of Hosts will send a wasting disease among Assyria’s stout warriors
Isaiah10:17 And the Light of Israel will become a fire
Isaiah10:18 The splendor of its forests and orchards
Isaiah10:19 The remaining trees of its forests will be so few that a child could count them.

Isaiah10:21 A remnant will return —a remnant of Jacob— to the Mighty God.

Isaiah10:23 For the Lord GOD of Hosts will carry out the destruction decreed upon the whole land.

Isaiah11:3 And He will delight in the fear of the Lord. He will not judge by what His eyes see
Isaiah11:4 but with righteousness He will judge the poor
Isaiah11:5 Righteousness will be the belt around His hips
Isaiah11:6 The wolf will live with the lamb
Isaiah11:7 The cow will graze with the bear
Isaiah11:8 The infant will play by the cobra’s den
Isaiah11:9 They will neither harm nor destroy on all My holy mountain

Isaiah14:1 For the Lord will have compassion on Jacob; once again He will choose Israel and settle them in their own land. The foreigner will join them and unite with the house of Jacob.
Isaiah14:2 The nations will escort Israel and bring it to its homeland. Then the house of Israel will possess the nations as menservants and maidservants in the Lord’s land. They will make captives of their captors and rule over their oppressors.
Isaiah14:3 On the day that the Lord gives you rest from your pain and torment
Isaiah14:4 you will sing this song of contempt against the king of Babylon: How the oppressor has ceased
Isaiah14:5 The Lord has broken the staff of the wicked
Isaiah14:6 It struck the peoples in anger with unceasing blows; it subdued the nations in rage with relentless persecution.
Isaiah14:7 All the earth is at peace and at rest; they break out in song.
Isaiah14:8 Even the cypresses and cedars of Lebanon exult over you: “Since you have been laid low
Isaiah14:9 Sheol beneath is eager to meet you upon your arrival. It stirs the spirits of the dead to greet you— all the rulers of the earth. It makes all the kings of the nations rise from their thrones.
Isaiah14:10 They will all respond to you
Isaiah14:11 Your pomp has been brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:12 How you have fallen from heaven
Isaiah14:13 You said in your heart: “I will ascend to the heavens; I will raise my throne above the stars of God. I will sit on the mount of assembly
Isaiah14:14 I will ascend above the tops of the clouds; I will make myself like the Most High.”
Isaiah14:15 But you will be brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:16 Those who see you will stare; they will ponder your fate: “Is this the man who shook the earth and made the kingdoms tremble
Isaiah14:17 who turned the world into a desert and destroyed its cities
Isaiah14:18 All the kings of the nations lie in state
Isaiah14:19 But you are cast out of your grave like a rejected branch
Isaiah14:20 You will not join them in burial
Isaiah14:21 Prepare a place to slaughter his sons for the iniquities of their forefathers. They will never rise up to possess a land or cover the earth with their cities.
Isaiah14:22 “I will rise up against them
Isaiah14:23 “I will make her a place for owls and for swamplands; I will sweep her away with the broom of destruction
Isaiah14:24 The Lord of Hosts has sworn: “Surely
Isaiah14:25 I will break Assyria in My land; I will trample him on My mountain. His yoke will be taken off My people
Isaiah14:26 This is the plan devised for the whole earth
Isaiah14:27 The Lord of Hosts has purposed

Isaiah24:2 people and priest alike
Isaiah24:3 The earth will be utterly laid waste and thoroughly plundered. For the Lord has spoken this word.
Isaiah24:4 The earth mourns and withers; the world languishes and fades; the exalted of the earth waste away.
Isaiah24:5 The earth is defiled by its people; they have transgressed the laws; they have overstepped the decrees and broken the everlasting covenant.
Isaiah24:6 Therefore a curse has consumed the earth
Isaiah24:7 The new wine dries up
Isaiah24:8 The joyful tambourines have ceased; the noise of revelers has stopped; the joyful harp is silent.
Isaiah24:9 They no longer sing and drink wine; strong drink is bitter to those who consume it.
Isaiah24:10 The city of chaos is shattered; every house is closed to entry.
Isaiah24:11 In the streets they cry out for wine. All joy turns to gloom; rejoicing is exiled from the land.
Isaiah24:12 The city is left in ruins; its gate is reduced to rubble.
Isaiah24:13 So will it be on the earth and among the nations
Isaiah24:14 They raise their voices
Isaiah24:15 Therefore glorify the Lord in the east. Extol the name of the Lord

Isaiah24:18 Whoever flees the sound of panic will fall into the pit
Isaiah24:19 The earth is utterly broken apart
Isaiah24:20 The earth staggers like a drunkard and sways like a shack. Earth’s rebellion weighs it down
Isaiah24:21 In that day the Lord will punish the host of heaven above and the kings of the earth below.
Isaiah24:22 They will be gathered together like prisoners in a pit. They will be confined to a dungeon and punished after many days.
Isaiah24:23 The moon will be confounded and the sun will be ashamed; for the Lord of Hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem

Isaiah28:1 Woe to the majestic crown of Ephraim’s drunkards
Isaiah28:2 Behold
Isaiah28:3 The majestic crown of Ephraim’s drunkards will be trampled underfoot.
Isaiah28:4 The fading flower of his beautiful splendor
Isaiah28:5 On that day the Lord of Hosts will be a crown of glory
Isaiah28:6 a spirit of justice to him who sits in judgment
Isaiah28:7 These also stagger from wine and stumble from strong drink: Priests and prophets reel from strong drink and are befuddled by wine. They stumble because of strong drink
Isaiah28:8 For all their tables are covered with vomit; there is not a place without filth.
Isaiah28:9 Whom is He trying to teach? To whom is He explaining His message? To infants just weaned from milk? To babies removed from the breast?
Isaiah28:10 For they hear: “Order on order
Isaiah28:11 Indeed
Isaiah28:12 to whom He has said: “This is the place of rest
Isaiah28:13 Then the word of the Lord to them will become: “Order on order
Isaiah28:14 Therefore hear the word of the Lord
Isaiah28:15 For you said
Isaiah28:16 So this is what the Lord GOD says: “See
Isaiah28:17 I will make justice the measuring line and righteousness the level. Hail will sweep away your refuge of lies
Isaiah28:18 Your covenant with death will be dissolved
Isaiah28:19 As often as it passes through
Isaiah28:20 Indeed
Isaiah28:21 For the Lord will rise up as at Mount Perazim. He will rouse Himself as in the Valley of Gibeon
Isaiah28:22 So now
Isaiah28:23 Listen and hear my voice. Pay attention and hear what I say.
Isaiah28:24 Does the plowman plow for planting every day? Does he continuously loosen and harrow the soil?
Isaiah28:25 When he has leveled its surface
Isaiah28:26 For his God instructs and teaches him properly.
Isaiah28:27 Surely caraway is not threshed with a sledge
Isaiah28:28 Grain for bread must be ground
Isaiah28:29 This also comes from the Lord of Hosts
Isaiah29:1 Woe to you
Isaiah29:2 And I will constrain Ariel
Isaiah29:3 I will camp in a circle around you; I will besiege you with towers and set up siege works against you.
Isaiah29:4 You will be brought low
Isaiah29:5 But your many foes will be like fine dust
Isaiah29:6 you will be visited by the Lord of Hosts with thunder and earthquake and loud noise
Isaiah29:7 All the many nations going out to battle against Ariel— even all who war against her
Isaiah29:8 as when a hungry man dreams he is eating
Isaiah29:9 Stop and be astonished; blind yourselves and be sightless; be drunk
Isaiah29:10 For the Lord has poured out on you a spirit of deep sleep. He has shut your eyes
Isaiah29:11 And the entire vision will be to you like the words sealed in a scroll. If it is handed to someone to read
Isaiah29:12 Or if the scroll is handed to one unable to read
Isaiah29:13 Therefore the Lord said: “These people draw near to Me with their mouths and honor Me with their lips
Isaiah29:14 Therefore I will again confound these people with wonder upon wonder. The wisdom of the wise will vanish
Isaiah29:15 Woe to those who dig deep to hide their plans from the Lord. In darkness they do their works and say
Isaiah29:16 You have turned things upside down
Isaiah29:17 In a very short time
Isaiah29:18 On that day the deaf will hear the words of the scroll
Isaiah29:19 The humble will increase their joy in the Lord
Isaiah29:20 For the ruthless will vanish
Isaiah29:21 those who indict a man with a word
Isaiah29:22 Therefore the Lord who redeemed Abraham says of the house of Jacob: “No longer will Jacob be ashamed and no more will his face grow pale.
Isaiah29:23 For when he sees his children around him
Isaiah29:24 Then the wayward in spirit will come to understanding
Isaiah30:1 “Woe to the rebellious children
Isaiah30:2 They set out to go down to Egypt without asking My advice
Isaiah30:3 But Pharaoh’s protection will become your shame
Isaiah30:4 For though their princes are at Zoan and their envoys have arrived in Hanes
Isaiah30:5 everyone will be put to shame because of a people useless to them. They cannot be of help; they are good for nothing but shame and reproach.”
Isaiah30:6 This is the burden against the beasts of the Negev: Through a land of hardship and distress
Isaiah30:7 Egypt’s help is futile and empty; therefore I have called her Rahab Who Sits Still.
Isaiah30:8 Go now
Isaiah30:9 These are rebellious people
Isaiah30:10 They say to the seers
Isaiah30:11 Get out of the way; turn off the road. Rid us of the Holy One of Israel!”
Isaiah30:12 Therefore this is what the Holy One of Israel says: “Because you have rejected this message
Isaiah30:13 this iniquity of yours is like a breach about to fail
Isaiah30:14 It will break in pieces like a potter’s jar
Isaiah30:15 For the Lord GOD
Isaiah30:16 “No
Isaiah30:17 A thousand will flee at the threat of one; at the threat of five you will all flee
Isaiah30:18 Therefore the Lord longs to be gracious to you; therefore He rises to show you compassion
Isaiah30:19 O people in Zion who dwell in Jerusalem
Isaiah30:20 The Lord will give you the bread of adversity and the water of affliction
Isaiah30:21 And whether you turn to the right or to the left
Isaiah30:22 So you will desecrate your silver-plated idols and your gold-plated images. You will throw them away like menstrual cloths
Isaiah30:23 Then He will send rain for the seed that you have sown in the ground
Isaiah30:24 The oxen and donkeys that work the ground will eat salted fodder
Isaiah30:25 And from every high mountain and every raised hill
Isaiah30:26 The light of the moon will be as bright as the sun
Isaiah30:27 Behold
Isaiah30:28 His breath is like a rushing torrent that rises to the neck. He comes to sift the nations in a sieve of destruction; He bridles the jaws of the peoples to lead them astray.
Isaiah30:29 You will sing as on the night of a holy festival
Isaiah30:30 And the Lord will cause His majestic voice to be heard and His mighty arm to be revealed
Isaiah30:31 For Assyria will be shattered at the voice of the Lord; He will strike them with His scepter.
Isaiah30:32 And with every stroke of the rod of punishment that the Lord brings down on them
Isaiah30:33 For Topheth has long been prepared; it has been made ready for the king. Its funeral pyre is deep and wide
Isaiah31:1 Woe to those who go down to Egypt for help
Isaiah31:2 Yet He too is wise and brings disaster; He does not call back His words. He will rise up against the house of the wicked and against the allies of evildoers.
Isaiah31:3 But the Egyptians are men
Isaiah31:4 For this is what the Lord has said to me: “Like a lion roaring or a young lion over its prey— and though a band of shepherds is called out against it
Isaiah31:5 Like birds hovering overhead
Isaiah31:6 Return to the One against whom you have so blatantly rebelled
Isaiah31:7 For on that day
Isaiah31:8 “Then Assyria will fall
Isaiah31:9 Their rock will pass away for fear
Isaiah32:1 Behold
Isaiah32:2 Each will be like a shelter from the wind
Isaiah32:3 Then the eyes of those who see will no longer be closed
Isaiah32:4 The mind of the rash will know and understand
Isaiah32:5 No longer will a fool be called noble
Isaiah32:6 For a fool speaks foolishness; his mind plots iniquity. He practices ungodliness and speaks falsely about the Lord; he leaves the hungry empty and deprives the thirsty of drink.
Isaiah32:7 The weapons of the scoundrel are destructive; he hatches plots to destroy the poor with lies
Isaiah32:8 But a noble man makes honorable plans; he stands up for worthy causes.
Isaiah32:9 Stand up
Isaiah32:10 In a little more than a year you will tremble
Isaiah32:11 Shudder
Isaiah32:12 Beat your breasts for the pleasant fields
Isaiah32:13 and for the land of my people
Isaiah32:14 For the palace will be forsaken
Isaiah32:15 until the Spirit is poured out upon us from on high. Then the desert will be an orchard
Isaiah32:16 Then justice will inhabit the wilderness
Isaiah32:17 The work of righteousness will be peace; the service of righteousness will be quiet confidence forever.
Isaiah32:18 Then my people will dwell in a peaceful place
Isaiah32:19 But hail will level the forest
Isaiah32:20 Blessed are those who sow beside abundant waters
Isaiah33:1 Woe to you
Isaiah33:2 O Lord
Isaiah33:3 The peoples flee the thunder of Your voice; the nations scatter when You rise.
Isaiah33:4 Your spoil
Isaiah33:5 The Lord is exalted
Isaiah33:6 He will be the sure foundation for your times
Isaiah33:7 Behold
Isaiah33:8 The highways are deserted; travel has ceased. The treaty has been broken
Isaiah33:9 The land mourns and languishes; Lebanon is ashamed and decayed. Sharon is like a desert; Bashan and Carmel shake off their leaves.
Isaiah33:10 “Now I will arise
Isaiah33:11 You conceive chaff; you give birth to stubble. Your breath is a fire that will consume you.
Isaiah33:12 The peoples will be burned to ashes
Isaiah33:13 You who are far off
Isaiah33:14 The sinners in Zion are afraid; trembling grips the ungodly: “Who of us can dwell with a consuming fire? Who of us can dwell with everlasting flames?”
Isaiah33:15 He who walks righteously and speaks with sincerity
Isaiah33:16 he will dwell on the heights; the mountain fortress will be his refuge; his food will be provided and his water assured.
Isaiah33:17 Your eyes will see the King in His beauty and behold a land that stretches afar.
Isaiah33:18 Your mind will ponder the former terror: “Where is he who tallies? Where is he who weighs? Where is he who counts the towers?”
Isaiah33:19 You will no longer see the insolent
Isaiah33:20 Look upon Zion
Isaiah33:21 But there the Majestic One
Isaiah33:22 For the Lord is our Judge
Isaiah33:23 Your ropes are slack; they cannot secure the mast or spread the sail. Then an abundance of spoils will be divided
Isaiah33:24 And no resident of Zion will say
Isaiah34:1 Come near
Isaiah34:2 The Lord is angry with all the nations and furious with all their armies. He will devote them to destruction; He will give them over to slaughter.
Isaiah34:3 Their slain will be left unburied
Isaiah34:4 All the stars of heaven will be dissolved. The skies will be rolled up like a scroll
Isaiah34:5 When My sword has drunk its fill in the heavens
Isaiah34:6 The sword of the Lord is bathed in blood. It drips with fat— with the blood of lambs and goats
Isaiah34:7 And the wild oxen will fall with them
Isaiah34:8 For the Lord has a day of vengeance
Isaiah34:9 Edom’s streams will be turned to tar
Isaiah34:10 It will not be quenched—day or night. Its smoke will ascend forever. From generation to generation it will lie desolate; no one will ever again pass through it.
Isaiah34:11 The desert owl and screech owl will possess it
Isaiah34:12 No nobles will be left to proclaim a king
Isaiah34:13 Her towers will be overgrown with thorns
Isaiah34:14 The desert creatures will meet with hyenas
Isaiah34:15 There the owl will make her nest; she will lay and hatch her eggs and gather her brood under her shadow. Even there the birds of prey will gather
Isaiah34:16 Search and read the scroll of the Lord: Not one of these will go missing
Isaiah34:17 He has allotted their portion; His hand has distributed it by measure. They will possess it forever; they will dwell in it from generation to generation.
Isaiah35:1 The wilderness and the land will be glad; the desert will rejoice and blossom like a rose.
Isaiah35:2 It will bloom profusely and rejoice with joy and singing. The glory of Lebanon will be given to it
Isaiah35:3 Strengthen the limp hands and steady the feeble knees!
Isaiah35:4 Say to those with anxious hearts: “Be strong
Isaiah35:5 Then the eyes of the blind will be opened and the ears of the deaf unstopped.
Isaiah35:6 Then the lame will leap like a deer and the mute tongue will shout for joy. For waters will gush forth in the wilderness
Isaiah35:7 The parched ground will become a pool
Isaiah35:8 And there will be a highway called the Way of Holiness. The unclean will not travel it— only those who walk in the Way— and fools will not stray onto it.
Isaiah35:9 No lion will be there
Isaiah35:10 So the redeemed of the Lord will return and enter Zion with singing
Isaiah36:1 In the fourteenth year of Hezekiah’s reign
Isaiah36:2 And the king of Assyria sent the Rabshakeh
Isaiah36:3 Then Eliakim son of Hilkiah the palace administrator
Isaiah36:4 The Rabshakeh said to them
Isaiah36:5 You claim to have a strategy and strength for war
Isaiah36:6 Look now
Isaiah36:7 But if you say to me
Isaiah36:8 Now
Isaiah36:9 For how can you repel a single officer among the least of my master’s servants when you depend on Egypt for chariots and horsemen?
Isaiah36:10 So now
Isaiah36:11 Then Eliakim
Isaiah36:12 But the Rabshakeh replied
Isaiah36:13 Then the Rabshakeh stood and called out loudly in Hebrew: “Hear the words of the great king
Isaiah36:14 This is what the king says: Do not let Hezekiah deceive you
Isaiah36:15 Do not let Hezekiah persuade you to trust in the Lord when he says
Isaiah36:16 Do not listen to Hezekiah
Isaiah36:17 until I come and take you away to a land like your own—a land of grain and new wine
Isaiah36:18 Do not let Hezekiah mislead you when he says
Isaiah36:19 Where are the gods of Hamath and Arpad? Where are the gods of Sepharvaim? Have they delivered Samaria from my hand?
Isaiah36:20 Who among all the gods of these lands has delivered his land from my hand? How then can the Lord deliver Jerusalem from my hand?”
Isaiah36:21 But the people remained silent and did not answer a word
Isaiah36:22 Then Hilkiah’s son Eliakim the palace administrator

Isaiah37:2 And he sent Eliakim the palace administrator
Isaiah37:3 to tell him

Isaiah37:6 who replied

Isaiah37:13 Where are the kings of Hamath
Isaiah37:14 So Hezekiah received the letter from the messengers
Isaiah37:15 And Hezekiah prayed to the Lord:
Isaiah37:16 “O Lord of Hosts

Isaiah37:18 Truly

Isaiah37:20 And now

Isaiah37:22 this is the word that the Lord has spoken against him: ‘The Virgin Daughter of Zion despises you and mocks you; the Daughter of Jerusalem shakes her head behind you.
Isaiah37:23 Whom have you taunted and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes in pride? Against the Holy One of Israel!
Isaiah37:24 Through your servants you have taunted the Lord
Isaiah37:25 I have dug wells and drunk foreign waters. With the soles of my feet I have dried up all the streams of Egypt.”
Isaiah37:26 Have you not heard? Long ago I ordained it; in days of old I planned it. Now I have brought it to pass
Isaiah37:27 Therefore their inhabitants
Isaiah37:28 But I know your sitting down
Isaiah37:29 Because your rage and arrogance against Me have reached My ears
Isaiah37:30 And this will be a sign to you
Isaiah37:31 And the surviving remnant of the house of Judah will again take root below and bear fruit above.
Isaiah37:32 For a remnant will go forth from Jerusalem
Isaiah37:33 So this is what the Lord says about the king of Assyria: ‘He will not enter this city or shoot an arrow into it. He will not come before it with a shield or build up a siege ramp against it.
Isaiah37:34 He will go back the way he came
Isaiah37:35 ‘I will defend this city and save it for My own sake and for the sake of My servant David.’”
Isaiah37:36 Then the angel of the Lord went out and struck down 185
Isaiah37:37 So Sennacherib king of Assyria broke camp and withdrew. He returned to Nineveh and stayed there.
Isaiah37:38 One day
Isaiah38:1 In those days Hezekiah became mortally ill. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz came to him and said
Isaiah38:2 Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the Lord
Isaiah38:3 saying
Isaiah38:4 And the word of the Lord came to Isaiah
Isaiah38:5 “Go and tell Hezekiah that this is what the Lord
Isaiah38:6 And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria. I will defend this city.
Isaiah38:7 This will be a sign to you from the Lord that He will do what He has promised:
Isaiah38:8 I will make the sun’s shadow that falls on the stairway of Ahaz go back ten steps.’” So the sunlight went back the ten steps it had descended.
Isaiah38:9 This is a writing by Hezekiah king of Judah after his illness and recovery:
Isaiah38:10 I said
Isaiah38:11 I said
Isaiah38:12 My dwelling has been picked up and removed from me like a shepherd’s tent. I have rolled up my life like a weaver; He cuts me off from the loom; from day until night You make an end of me.
Isaiah38:13 I composed myself until the morning. Like a lion He breaks all my bones; from day until night You make an end of me.
Isaiah38:14 I chirp like a swallow or crane; I moan like a dove. My eyes grow weak as I look upward. O Lord
Isaiah38:15 What can I say? He has spoken to me
Isaiah38:16 O Lord
Isaiah38:17 Surely for my own welfare I had such great anguish; but Your love has delivered me from the pit of oblivion
Isaiah38:18 For Sheol cannot thank You; Death cannot praise You. Those who descend to the Pit cannot hope for Your faithfulness.
Isaiah38:19 The living
Isaiah38:20 The Lord will save me; we will play songs on stringed instruments all the days of our lives in the house of the Lord.
Isaiah38:21 Now Isaiah had said
Isaiah38:22 And Hezekiah had asked

Isaiah40:2 “Speak tenderly to Jerusalem
Isaiah40:3 A voice of one calling: “Prepare the way for the Lord in the wilderness; make a straight highway for our God in the desert.
Isaiah40:4 Every valley shall be lifted up
Isaiah40:5 And the glory of the Lord will be revealed
Isaiah40:6 A voice says
Isaiah40:7 The grass withers and the flowers fall when the breath of the Lord blows on them; indeed
Isaiah40:8 The grass withers and the flowers fall
Isaiah40:9 Go up on a high mountain
Isaiah40:10 Behold
Isaiah40:11 He tends His flock like a shepherd; He gathers the lambs in His arms and carries them close to His heart. He gently leads the nursing ewes.
Isaiah40:12 Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand
Isaiah40:13 Who has directed the Spirit of the Lord
Isaiah40:14 Whom did He consult to enlighten Him
Isaiah40:15 Surely the nations are like a drop in a bucket; they are considered a speck of dust on the scales; He lifts up the islands like fine dust.
Isaiah40:16 Lebanon is not sufficient for fuel
Isaiah40:17 All the nations are as nothing before Him; He regards them as nothingness and emptiness.
Isaiah40:18 To whom will you liken God? To what image will you compare Him?
Isaiah40:19 To an idol that a craftsman casts and a metalworker overlays with gold and fits with silver chains?
Isaiah40:20 To one bereft of an offering who chooses wood that will not rot
Isaiah40:21 Do you not know? Have you not heard? Has it not been declared to you from the beginning? Have you not understood since the foundation of the earth?
Isaiah40:22 He sits enthroned above the circle of the earth; its dwellers are like grasshoppers. He stretches out the heavens like a curtain
Isaiah40:23 He brings the princes to nothing and makes the rulers of the earth meaningless.
Isaiah40:24 No sooner are they planted
Isaiah40:25 “To whom will you liken Me
Isaiah40:26 Lift up your eyes on high: Who created all these? He leads forth the starry host by number; He calls each one by name. Because of His great power and mighty strength
Isaiah40:27 Why do you say
Isaiah40:28 Do you not know? Have you not heard? The Lord is the everlasting God
Isaiah40:29 He gives power to the faint and increases the strength of the weak.
Isaiah40:30 Even youths grow tired and weary
Isaiah40:31 But those who wait upon the Lord will renew their strength; they will mount up with wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary

Isaiah42:1 “Here is My Servant
Isaiah42:2 He will not cry out or raise His voice
Isaiah42:3 A bruised reed He will not break and a smoldering wick He will not extinguish; He will faithfully bring forth justice.
Isaiah42:4 He will not grow weak or discouraged before He has established justice on the earth. In His law the islands will put their hope.”
Isaiah42:5 This is what God the Lord says— He who created the heavens and stretched them out
Isaiah42:6 “I
Isaiah42:7 to open the eyes of the blind
Isaiah42:8 I am the Lord; that is My name! I will not yield My glory to another or My praise to idols.
Isaiah42:9 Behold
Isaiah42:10 Sing to the Lord a new song— His praise from the ends of the earth— you who go down to the sea
Isaiah42:11 Let the desert and its cities raise their voices; let the villages of Kedar cry aloud. Let the people of Sela sing for joy; let them cry out from the mountaintops.
Isaiah42:12 Let them give glory to the Lord and declare His praise in the islands.
Isaiah42:13 The Lord goes forth like a mighty one; He stirs up His zeal like a warrior. He shouts; yes
Isaiah42:14 “I have kept silent from ages past; I have remained quiet and restrained. But now I will groan like a woman in labor; I will at once gasp and pant.
Isaiah42:15 I will lay waste the mountains and hills and dry up all their vegetation. I will turn the rivers into dry land and drain the marshes.
Isaiah42:16 I will lead the blind by a way they did not know; I will guide them on unfamiliar paths. I will turn darkness into light before them and rough places into level ground. These things I will do for them
Isaiah42:17 But those who trust in idols and say to molten images
Isaiah42:18 Listen
Isaiah42:19 Who is blind but My servant
Isaiah42:20 Though seeing many things
Isaiah42:21 The Lord was pleased
Isaiah42:22 But this is a people plundered and looted
Isaiah42:23 Who among you will pay attention to this? Who will listen and obey hereafter?
Isaiah42:24 Who gave Jacob up for spoil
Isaiah42:25 So He poured out on them His furious anger and the fierceness of battle. It enveloped them in flames

Isaiah43:2 When you pass through the waters
Isaiah43:3 For I am the Lord your God
Isaiah43:4 Because you are precious and honored in My sight

Isaiah43:6 I will say to the north
Isaiah43:7 everyone called by My name and created for My glory

Isaiah43:9 All the nations gather together and the peoples assemble. Who among them can declare this
Isaiah43:10 “You are My witnesses
Isaiah43:11 I
Isaiah43:12 I alone decreed and saved and proclaimed— I
Isaiah43:13 Even from eternity I am He
Isaiah43:14 Thus says the Lord your Redeemer

Isaiah43:17 who brings out the chariots and horses
Isaiah43:18 “Do not call to mind the former things; pay no attention to the things of old.

Isaiah43:20 The beasts of the field will honor Me

Isaiah43:26 Remind Me

Isaiah44:1 But now listen
Isaiah44:2 This is the word of the Lord
Isaiah44:3 For I will pour water on the thirsty land
Isaiah44:4 They will sprout among the grass like willows by flowing streams.
Isaiah44:5 One will say
Isaiah44:6 Thus says the Lord
Isaiah44:7 Who then is like Me? Let him say so! Let him declare his case before Me
Isaiah44:8 Do not tremble or fear. Have I not told you and declared it long ago? You are My witnesses! Is there any God but Me? There is no other Rock; I know not one.”
Isaiah44:9 All makers of idols are nothing
Isaiah44:10 Who fashions a god or casts an idol which profits him nothing?
Isaiah44:11 Behold
Isaiah44:12 The blacksmith takes a tool and labors over the coals; he fashions an idol with hammers and forges it with his strong arms. Yet he grows hungry and loses his strength; he fails to drink water and grows faint.
Isaiah44:13 The woodworker extends a measuring line; he marks it out with a stylus; he shapes it with chisels and outlines it with a compass. He fashions it in the likeness of man
Isaiah44:14 He cuts down cedars or retrieves a cypress or oak. He lets it grow strong among the trees of the forest. He plants a laurel
Isaiah44:15 It serves as fuel for man. He takes some of it to warm himself
Isaiah44:16 He burns half of it in the fire
Isaiah44:17 From the rest he makes a god
Isaiah44:18 They do not comprehend or discern
Isaiah44:19 And no one considers in his heart
Isaiah44:20 He feeds on ashes. His deluded heart has led him astray
Isaiah44:21 Remember these things
Isaiah44:22 I have blotted out your transgressions like a cloud
Isaiah44:23 Sing for joy
Isaiah44:24 Thus says the Lord
Isaiah44:25 who foils the signs of false prophets and makes fools of diviners
Isaiah44:26 who confirms the message of His servant and fulfills the counsel of His messengers
Isaiah44:27 who says to the depths of the sea
Isaiah44:28 who says of Cyrus
Isaiah45:1 This is what the Lord says to Cyrus His anointed
Isaiah45:2 “I will go before you and level the mountains; I will break down the gates of bronze and cut through the bars of iron.
Isaiah45:3 I will give you the treasures of darkness and the riches hidden in secret places
Isaiah45:4 For the sake of Jacob My servant and Israel My chosen one
Isaiah45:5 I am the Lord
Isaiah45:6 so that all may know

Isaiah47:12 So take your stand with your spells and with your many sorceries
Isaiah47:13 You are wearied by your many counselors; let them come forward now and save you— your astrologers who observe the stars
Isaiah47:14 Surely they are like stubble; the fire will burn them up. They cannot deliver themselves from the power of the flame. There will be no coals to warm them or fire to sit beside.
Isaiah47:15 This is what they are to you— those with whom you have labored and traded from youth— each one strays in his own direction; not one of them can save you.

Isaiah48:3 I foretold the former things long ago; they came out of My mouth and I proclaimed them. Suddenly I acted
Isaiah48:4 For I knew that you are stubborn; your neck is iron and your forehead is bronze.
Isaiah48:5 Therefore I declared it to you long ago; I announced it before it came to pass
Isaiah48:6 You have heard these things; look at them all. Will you not acknowledge them? From now on I will tell you of new things
Isaiah48:7 They are created now
Isaiah48:8 You have never heard; you have never understood; for a long time your ears have not been open. For I knew how deceitful you are; you have been called a rebel from birth.

Isaiah59:4 No one calls for justice; no one pleads his case honestly. They rely on empty pleas; they tell lies; they conceive mischief and give birth to iniquity.

Isaiah59:6 Their cobwebs cannot be made into clothing
Isaiah59:7 Their feet run to evil; they are swift to shed innocent blood. Their thoughts are sinful thoughts; ruin and destruction lie in their wake.

Jeremiah1:1 These are the words of Jeremiah son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah1:2 The word of the Lord came to Jeremiah in the thirteenth year of the reign of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah
Jeremiah1:3 and through the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah
Jeremiah1:4 The word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah1:5 “Before I formed you in the womb I knew you
Jeremiah1:6 “Ah
Jeremiah1:7 But the Lord told me: “Do not say
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of them
Jeremiah1:9 Then the Lord reached out His hand
Jeremiah1:10 See
Jeremiah1:11 And the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah1:12 “You have observed correctly
Jeremiah1:13 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah1:14 Then the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:15 For I am about to summon all the clans and kingdoms of the north
Jeremiah1:16 I will pronounce My judgments against them for all their wickedness
Jeremiah1:17 Get yourself ready. Stand up and tell them everything that I command you. Do not be intimidated by them
Jeremiah1:18 Now behold
Jeremiah1:19 They will fight against you but will never overcome you
Jeremiah2:1 Now the word of the Lord came to me
Jeremiah2:2 “Go and proclaim in the hearing of Jerusalem that this is what the Lord says: ‘I remember the devotion of your youth
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holy to the Lord
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah2:5 This is what the Lord says: “What fault did your fathers find in Me that they strayed so far from Me
Jeremiah2:6 They did not ask
Jeremiah2:7 I brought you into a fertile land to eat its fruit and bounty
Jeremiah2:8 The priests did not ask
Jeremiah2:9 Therefore
Jeremiah2:10 Cross over to the coasts of Cyprus and take a look; send to Kedar and consider carefully; see if there has ever been anything like this:
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation ever changed its gods
Jeremiah2:12 Be stunned by this
Jeremiah2:13 “For My people have committed two evils: They have forsaken Me
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a slave? Was he born into slavery? Why then has he become prey?
Jeremiah2:15 The young lions have roared at him; they have growled with a loud voice. They have laid waste his land; his cities lie in ruins
Jeremiah2:16 The men of Memphis and Tahpanhes have shaved the crown of your head.
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not brought this on yourself by forsaking the Lord your God when He led you in the way?
Jeremiah2:18 Now what will you gain on your way to Egypt to drink the waters of the Nile? What will you gain on your way to Assyria to drink the waters of the Euphrates?

Jeremiah17:1 “The sin of Judah is written with an iron stylus
Jeremiah17:2 Even their children remember their altars and Asherah poles by the green trees and on the high hills.
Jeremiah17:3 O My mountain in the countryside
Jeremiah17:4 And you yourself will relinquish the inheritance that I gave you. I will enslave you to your enemies in a land that you do not know
Jeremiah17:5 This is what the Lord says: “Cursed is the man who trusts in mankind
Jeremiah17:6 He will be like a shrub in the desert; he will not see when prosperity comes. He will dwell in the parched places of the desert
Jeremiah17:7 But blessed is the man who trusts in the Lord
Jeremiah17:8 He is like a tree planted by the waters that sends out its roots toward the stream. It does not fear when the heat comes
Jeremiah17:9 The heart is deceitful above all things and beyond cure. Who can understand it?
Jeremiah17:10 I
Jeremiah17:11 Like a partridge hatching eggs it did not lay is the man who makes a fortune unjustly. In the middle of his days his riches will desert him
Jeremiah17:12 A glorious throne
Jeremiah17:13 O Lord
Jeremiah17:14 Heal me
Jeremiah17:15 Behold
Jeremiah17:16 But I have not run away from being Your shepherd; I have not desired the day of despair. You know that the utterance of my lips was spoken in Your presence.
Jeremiah17:17 Do not become a terror to me; You are my refuge in the day of disaster.
Jeremiah17:18 Let my persecutors be put to shame
Jeremiah17:19 This is what the Lord said to me: “Go and stand at the gate of the people
Jeremiah17:20 Say to them
Jeremiah17:21 This is what the Lord says: Take heed for yourselves; do not carry a load or bring it through the gates of Jerusalem on the Sabbath day.
Jeremiah17:22 You must not carry a load out of your houses or do any work on the Sabbath day
Jeremiah17:23 Yet they would not listen or incline their ear
Jeremiah17:24 If

Jeremiah17:26 And people will come from the cities of Judah and the places around Jerusalem
Jeremiah17:27 But if you do not listen to Me to keep the Sabbath day holy by not carrying a load while entering the gates of Jerusalem on the Sabbath day

Jeremiah18:6 “O house of Israel

Jeremiah18:8 But if that nation I warned turns from its evil
Jeremiah18:9 And if at another time I announce that I will build up and establish a nation or kingdom
Jeremiah18:10 and if it does evil in My sight and does not listen to My voice

Jeremiah18:15 Yet My people have forgotten Me. They burn incense to worthless idols that make them stumble in their ways
Jeremiah18:16 They have made their land a desolation
Jeremiah18:17 I will scatter them before the enemy like the east wind. I will show them My back and not My face in the day of their calamity.”

Jeremiah23:32 “Indeed

Jeremiah23:36 But refer no more to the burden of the Lord

Jeremiah23:38 But if you claim
Jeremiah23:39 therefore I will surely forget you and will cast you out of My presence
Jeremiah23:40 And I will bring upon you everlasting shame and perpetual humiliation that will never be forgotten.”
