Jeremiah46:17 There they will cry out: ‘Pharaoh king of Egypt was all noise; he has let the appointed time pass him by.’
Jeremiah46:18 As surely as I live
Jeremiah46:19 Pack your bags for exile
Jeremiah46:20 Egypt is a beautiful heifer
Jeremiah46:21 Even the mercenaries among her are like fattened calves. They too will turn back; together they will flee
Jeremiah46:22 Egypt will hiss like a fleeing serpent
Jeremiah46:23 They will chop down her forest
Jeremiah46:24 The Daughter of Egypt will be put to shame; she will be delivered into the hands of the people of the north.”
Jeremiah46:25 The Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah46:26 I will deliver them into the hands of those who seek their lives—of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and his officers. But after this
Jeremiah46:27 But you
Jeremiah46:28 And you
Jeremiah47:1 This is the word of the Lord that came to Jeremiah the prophet about the Philistines before Pharaoh struck down Gaza.
Jeremiah47:2 This is what the Lord says: “See how the waters are rising from the north and becoming an overflowing torrent. They will overflow the land and its fullness
Jeremiah47:3 at the sound of the galloping hooves of stallions
Jeremiah47:4 For the day has come to destroy all the Philistines
Jeremiah47:5 The people of Gaza will shave their heads in mourning; Ashkelon will be silenced. O remnant of their valley
Jeremiah47:6 ‘Alas
Jeremiah47:7 How can it rest when the Lord has commanded it? He has appointed it against Ashkelon and the shore of its coastland.”
Jeremiah48:1 Concerning Moab
Jeremiah48:2 There is no longer praise for Moab; in Heshbon they devise evil against her: ‘Come
Jeremiah48:3 A voice cries out from Horonaim: ‘Devastation and great destruction!’
Jeremiah48:4 Moab will be shattered; her little ones will cry out.
Jeremiah48:5 For on the ascent to Luhith they weep bitterly as they go
Jeremiah48:6 ‘Flee! Run for your lives! Become like a juniper in the desert. ’
Jeremiah48:7 Because you trust in your works and treasures
Jeremiah48:8 The destroyer will move against every city
Jeremiah48:9 Put salt on Moab
Jeremiah48:10 Cursed is the one who is remiss in doing the work of the Lord
Jeremiah48:11 Moab has been at ease from youth
Jeremiah48:12 Therefore behold
Jeremiah48:13 Then Moab will be ashamed of Chemosh
Jeremiah48:14 How can you say
Jeremiah48:15 Moab has been destroyed and its towns have been invaded; the best of its young men have gone down in the slaughter
Jeremiah48:16 Moab’s calamity is at hand
Jeremiah48:17 Mourn for him
Jeremiah48:18 Come down from your glory; sit on parched ground
Jeremiah48:19 Stand by the road and watch
Jeremiah48:20 Moab is put to shame
Jeremiah48:21 Judgment has come upon the high plain— upon Holon
Jeremiah48:22 upon Dibon
Jeremiah48:23 upon Kiriathaim
Jeremiah48:24 upon Kerioth
Jeremiah48:25 The horn of Moab has been cut off
Jeremiah48:26 “Make him drunk
Jeremiah48:27 Was not Israel your object of ridicule? Was he ever found among thieves? For whenever you speak of him you shake your head.
Jeremiah48:28 Abandon the towns and settle among the rocks
Jeremiah48:29 We have heard of Moab’s pomposity
Jeremiah48:30 I know his insolence
Jeremiah48:31 Therefore I will wail for Moab; I will cry out for all of Moab; I will moan for the men of Kir-heres.
Jeremiah48:32 I will weep for you
Jeremiah48:33 Joy and gladness are removed from the orchard and from the fields of Moab. I have stopped the flow of wine from the presses; no one treads them with shouts of joy; their shouts are not for joy.
Jeremiah48:34 There is a cry from Heshbon to Elealeh; they raise their voices to Jahaz
Jeremiah48:35 In Moab
Jeremiah48:36 Therefore My heart laments like a flute for Moab; it laments like a flute for the men of Kir-heres
Jeremiah48:37 For every head is shaved and every beard is clipped; on every hand is a gash
Jeremiah48:38 On all the rooftops of Moab and in the public squares
Jeremiah48:39 “How shattered it is! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! Moab has become an object of ridicule and horror to all those around him.”
Jeremiah48:40 For this is what the Lord says: “Behold
Jeremiah48:41 Kirioth has been taken
Jeremiah48:42 Moab will be destroyed as a nation because he vaunted himself against the Lord.
Jeremiah48:43 Terror and pit and snare await you
Jeremiah48:44 “Whoever flees the panic will fall into the pit
Jeremiah48:45 “Those who flee will stand helpless in Heshbon’s shadow
Jeremiah48:46 Woe to you
Jeremiah48:47 Yet in the latter days I will restore Moab from captivity
Jeremiah49:1 Concerning the Ammonites
Jeremiah49:2 Therefore
Jeremiah49:3 Wail
Jeremiah49:4 Why do you boast of your valleys— your valleys so fruitful
Jeremiah49:5 Behold
Jeremiah49:6 Yet afterward I will restore the Ammonites from captivity
Jeremiah49:7 Concerning Edom
Jeremiah49:8 Turn and run! Lie low
Jeremiah49:9 If grape gatherers came to you
Jeremiah49:10 But I will strip Esau bare; I will uncover his hiding places
Jeremiah49:11 Abandon your orphans; I will preserve their lives. Let your widows trust in Me.”
Jeremiah49:12 For this is what the Lord says: “If those who do not deserve to drink the cup must drink it
Jeremiah49:13 For by Myself I have sworn
Jeremiah49:14 I have heard a message from the Lord; an envoy has been sent to the nations: “Assemble yourselves to march against her! Rise up for battle!”
Jeremiah49:15 “For behold
Jeremiah49:16 The terror you cause and the pride of your heart have deceived you
Jeremiah49:17 “Edom will become an object of horror. All who pass by will be appalled and will scoff at all her wounds.
Jeremiah49:18 As Sodom and Gomorrah were overthrown along with their neighbors
Jeremiah49:19 Behold
Jeremiah49:20 Therefore hear the plans that the Lord has drawn up against Edom and the strategies He has devised against the people of Teman: Surely the little ones of the flock will be dragged away; certainly their pasture will be made desolate because of them.
Jeremiah49:21 At the sound of their fall the earth will quake; their cry will resound to the Red Sea.
Jeremiah49:22 Look! An eagle will soar and swoop down
Jeremiah49:23 Concerning Damascus: “Hamath and Arpad are put to shame
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus has become feeble; she has turned to flee. Panic has gripped her; anguish and pain have seized her like a woman in labor.
Jeremiah49:25 How is the city of praise not forsaken
Jeremiah49:26 For her young men will fall in the streets
Jeremiah49:27 “I will set fire to the walls of Damascus; it will consume the fortresses of Ben-hadad.”
Jeremiah49:28 Concerning Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor
Jeremiah49:29 They will take their tents and flocks
Jeremiah49:30 Run! Escape quickly! Lie low
Jeremiah49:31 Rise up
Jeremiah49:32 Their camels will become plunder
Jeremiah49:33 “Hazor will become a haunt for jackals
Jeremiah49:34 This is the word of the Lord that came to Jeremiah the prophet concerning Elam at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah.
Jeremiah49:35 This is what the Lord of Hosts says: “Behold
Jeremiah49:36 I will bring the four winds against Elam from the four corners of the heavens
Jeremiah49:37 So I will shatter Elam before their foes
Jeremiah49:38 I will set My throne in Elam
Jeremiah49:39 “Yet in the last days
Jeremiah50:1 This is the word that the Lord spoke through Jeremiah the prophet concerning Babylon and the land of the Chaldeans:
Jeremiah50:2 “Announce and declare to the nations; lift up a banner and proclaim it; hold nothing back when you say
Jeremiah50:3 For a nation from the north will come against her; it will make her land a desolation. No one will live in it; both man and beast will flee.”
Jeremiah50:4 “In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:5 They will ask the way to Zion and turn their faces toward it. They will come and join themselves to the Lord in an everlasting covenant that will never be forgotten.
Jeremiah50:6 My people are lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray
Jeremiah50:7 All who found them devoured them
Jeremiah50:8 Flee from the midst of Babylon; depart from the land of the Chaldeans; be like the he-goats that lead the flock.
Jeremiah50:9 For behold
Jeremiah50:10 Chaldea will be plundered; all who plunder her will have their fill
Jeremiah50:11 “Because you rejoice
Jeremiah50:12 your mother will be greatly ashamed; she who bore you will be disgraced. Behold
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of the Lord
Jeremiah50:14 Line up in formation around Babylon
Jeremiah50:15 Raise a war cry against her on every side! She has thrown up her hands in surrender; her towers have fallen; her walls are torn down. Since this is the vengeance of the Lord
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off the sower from Babylon
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a scattered flock
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore this is what the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah50:19 I will return Israel to his pasture
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:21 Go up against the land of Merathaim
Jeremiah50:22 “The noise of battle is in the land— the noise of great destruction.
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth lies broken and shattered! What a horror Babylon has become among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I laid a snare for you
Jeremiah50:25 The Lord has opened His armory and brought out His weapons of wrath
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from the farthest border. Break open her granaries; pile her up like mounds of grain. Devote her to destruction; leave her no survivors.
Jeremiah50:27 Kill all her young bulls; let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them
Jeremiah50:28 Listen to the fugitives and refugees from the land of Babylon
Jeremiah50:29 Summon the archers against Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore
Jeremiah50:31 “Behold
Jeremiah50:32 The arrogant one will stumble and fall with no one to pick him up. And I will kindle a fire in his cities to consume all those around him.”
Jeremiah50:33 This is what the Lord of Hosts says: “The sons of Israel are oppressed
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; the Lord of Hosts is His name. He will fervently plead their case so that He may bring rest to the earth
Jeremiah50:35 A sword is against the Chaldeans
Jeremiah50:36 A sword is against her false prophets
Jeremiah50:37 A sword is against her horses and chariots and against all the foreigners in her midst
Jeremiah50:38 A drought is upon her waters
Jeremiah50:39 So the desert creatures and hyenas will live there and ostriches will dwell there. It will never again be inhabited or lived in from generation to generation.
Jeremiah50:40 As God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah along with their neighbors
Jeremiah50:41 Behold
Jeremiah50:42 They grasp the bow and spear; they are cruel and merciless. Their voice roars like the sea
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon has heard the report
Jeremiah50:44 Behold
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore hear the plans that the Lord has drawn up against Babylon and the strategies He has devised against the land of the Chaldeans: Surely the little ones of the flock will be dragged away; certainly their pasture will be made desolate because of them.
Jeremiah50:46 At the sound of Babylon’s capture the earth will quake; a cry will be heard among the nations.
Jeremiah51:1 This is what the Lord says: “Behold
Jeremiah51:2 I will send strangers to Babylon to winnow her and empty her land; for they will come against her from every side in her day of disaster.
Jeremiah51:3 Do not let the archer bend his bow or put on his armor. Do not spare her young men; devote all her army to destruction!
Jeremiah51:4 And they will fall slain in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel and Judah have not been abandoned by their God
Jeremiah51:6 Flee from Babylon! Escape with your lives! Do not be destroyed in her punishment. For this is the time of the Lord’s vengeance; He will pay her what she deserves.
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a gold cup in the hand of the Lord
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly Babylon has fallen and been shattered. Wail for her; get her balm for her pain; perhaps she can be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 “We tried to heal Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 “The Lord has brought forth our vindication; come
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers! The Lord has aroused the spirit of the kings of the Medes
Jeremiah51:12 Raise a banner against the walls of Babylon; post the guard; station the watchmen; prepare the ambush. For the Lord has both devised and accomplished what He spoke against the people of Babylon.
Jeremiah51:13 You who dwell by many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The Lord of Hosts has sworn by Himself: “Surely I will fill you up with men as with locusts
Jeremiah51:15 The Lord made the earth by His power; He established the world by His wisdom and stretched out the heavens by His understanding.
Jeremiah51:16 When He thunders
Jeremiah51:17 Every man is senseless and devoid of knowledge; every goldsmith is put to shame by his idols. For his molten images are a fraud
Jeremiah51:18 They are worthless
Jeremiah51:19 The Portion of Jacob is not like these
Jeremiah51:20 “You are My war club
Jeremiah51:21 With you I shatter the horse and rider; with you I shatter the chariot and driver.
Jeremiah51:22 With you I shatter man and woman; with you I shatter the old man and the youth; with you I shatter the young man and the maiden.
Jeremiah51:23 With you I shatter the shepherd and his flock; with you I shatter the farmer and his oxen; with you I shatter the governors and officials.
Jeremiah51:24 Before your very eyes I will repay Babylon and all the dwellers of Chaldea for all the evil they have done in Zion
Jeremiah51:25 “Behold
Jeremiah51:26 No one shall retrieve from you a cornerstone or a foundation stone
Jeremiah51:27 “Raise a banner in the land! Blow the ram’s horn among the nations! Prepare the nations against her. Summon the kingdoms against her— Ararat
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare the nations for battle against her— the kings of the Medes
Jeremiah51:29 The earth quakes and writhes because the Lord’s intentions against Babylon stand: to make the land of Babylon a desolation
Jeremiah51:30 The warriors of Babylon have stopped fighting; they sit in their strongholds. Their strength is exhausted; they have become like women. Babylon’s homes have been set ablaze
Jeremiah51:31 One courier races to meet another
Jeremiah51:32 The fords have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For this is what the Lord of Hosts
Jeremiah51:34 “Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon has devoured me; he has crushed me. He has set me aside like an empty vessel; he has swallowed me like a monster; he filled his belly with my delicacies and vomited me out.
Jeremiah51:35 May the violence done to me and to my flesh be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore this is what the Lord says: “Behold
Jeremiah51:37 Babylon will become a heap of rubble
Jeremiah51:38 They will roar together like young lions; they will growl like lion cubs.
Jeremiah51:39 While they are flushed with heat
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Sheshach has been captured! The praise of all the earth has been seized. What a horror Babylon has become among the nations!
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has come up over Babylon; she is covered in turbulent waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon. I will make him spew out what he swallowed. The nations will no longer stream to him; even the wall of Babylon will fall.
Jeremiah51:45 Come out of her
Jeremiah51:46 Do not let your heart grow faint
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore
Jeremiah51:48 Then heaven and earth and all that is in them will shout for joy over Babylon because the destroyers from the north will come against her
Jeremiah51:49 “Babylon must fall on account of the slain of Israel
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 “We are ashamed because we have heard reproach; disgrace has covered our faces
Jeremiah51:52 “Therefore
Jeremiah51:53 Even if Babylon ascends to the heavens and fortifies her lofty stronghold
Jeremiah51:54 “The sound of a cry comes from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 For the Lord will destroy Babylon; He will silence her mighty voice. The waves will roar like great waters; the tumult of their voices will resound.
Jeremiah51:56 For a destroyer is coming against her— against Babylon. Her warriors will be captured
Jeremiah51:57 I will make her princes and wise men drunk
Jeremiah51:58 This is what the Lord of Hosts says: “Babylon’s thick walls will be leveled
Jeremiah51:59 This is the message that Jeremiah the prophet gave to the quartermaster Seraiah son of Neriah
Jeremiah51:60 Jeremiah had written on a single scroll about all the disaster that would come upon Babylon—all these words that had been written concerning Babylon.
Jeremiah51:61 And Jeremiah said to Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 and say
Jeremiah51:63 When you finish reading this scroll
Jeremiah51:64 Then you are to say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was twenty-one years old when he became king
Jeremiah52:2 And Zedekiah did evil in the sight of the Lord
Jeremiah52:3 For because of the anger of the Lord
Jeremiah52:4 So in the ninth year of Zedekiah’s reign
Jeremiah52:5 And the city was kept under siege until King Zedekiah’s eleventh year.
Jeremiah52:6 By the ninth day of the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city was breached; and though the Chaldeans had surrounded the city
Jeremiah52:8 but the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho
Jeremiah52:9 The Chaldeans seized the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 There at Riblah the king of Babylon slaughtered the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes
Jeremiah52:11 Then he put out Zedekiah’s eyes
Jeremiah52:12 On the tenth day of the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 He burned down the house of the Lord
Jeremiah52:14 And the whole army of the Chaldeans under the captain of the guard broke down all the walls around Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan captain of the guard carried into exile some of the poorest people and those who remained in the city
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan captain of the guard left behind some of the poorest of the land to tend the vineyards and fields.
Jeremiah52:17 Moreover
Jeremiah52:18 They also took away the pots
Jeremiah52:19 The captain of the guard also took away the basins
Jeremiah52:20 As for the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 Each pillar was eighteen cubits tall and twelve cubits in circumference; each was hollow
Jeremiah52:22 The bronze capital atop one pillar was five cubits high
Jeremiah52:23 Each capital had ninety-six pomegranates on the sides
Jeremiah52:24 The captain of the guard also took away Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 Of those still in the city
Jeremiah52:26 Nebuzaradan captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 There at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:28 These are the people Nebuchadnezzar carried away: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 in Nebuchadnezzar’s eighteenth year
Jeremiah52:30 in Nebuchadnezzar’s twenty-third year
Jeremiah52:31 On the twenty-fifth day of the twelfth month of the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Jehoiachin king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 And he spoke kindly to Jehoiachin and set his throne above the thrones of the other kings who were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin changed out of his prison clothes
Jeremiah52:34 And the king of Babylon provided Jehoiachin a daily portion for the rest of his life
Lamentations1:1 How lonely lies the city
Lamentations1:2 She weeps aloud in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile under affliction and harsh slavery; she dwells among the nations but finds no place to rest. All her pursuers have overtaken her in the midst of her distress.
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her foes have become her masters; her enemies are at ease. For the Lord has brought her grief because of her many transgressions. Her children have gone away as captives before the enemy.
Lamentations1:6 All the splendor has departed from the Daughter of Zion. Her princes are like deer that find no pasture; they lack the strength to flee in the face of the hunter.
Lamentations1:7 In the days of her affliction and wandering Jerusalem remembers all the treasures that were hers in days of old. When her people fell into enemy hands she received no help. Her enemies looked upon her
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has sinned greatly; therefore she has become an object of scorn. All who honored her now despise her
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness stains her skirts; she did not consider her end. Her downfall was astounding; there was no one to comfort her. Look
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has seized all her treasures. For she has seen the nations enter her sanctuary— those You had forbidden to enter Your assembly.
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan as they search for bread. They have traded their treasures for food to keep themselves alive. Look
Lamentations1:12 Is this nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 He sent fire from on high
Lamentations1:14 My transgressions are bound into a yoke
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has rejected all the mighty men in my midst; He has summoned an army against me to crush my young warriors. Like grapes in a winepress
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; my eyes flow with tears. For there is no one nearby to comfort me
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands
Lamentations1:18 The Lord is righteous
Lamentations1:19 I called out to my lovers
Lamentations1:20 See
Lamentations1:21 People have heard my groaning
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before You
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord has covered the Daughter of Zion with the cloud of His anger! He has cast the glory of Israel from heaven to earth. He has abandoned His footstool in the day of His anger.
Lamentations2:2 Without pity the Lord has swallowed up all the dwellings of Jacob. In His wrath He has demolished the fortified cities of the Daughter of Judah. He brought to the ground and defiled her kingdom and its princes.
Lamentations2:3 In fierce anger He has cut off every horn of Israel and withdrawn His right hand at the approach of the enemy. He has burned in Jacob like a flaming fire that consumes everything around it.
Lamentations2:4 He has bent His bow like an enemy; His right hand is positioned. Like a foe He has killed all who were pleasing to the eye; He has poured out His wrath like fire on the tent of the Daughter of Zion.
Lamentations2:5 The Lord is like an enemy; He has swallowed up Israel. He has swallowed up all her palaces and destroyed her strongholds. He has multiplied mourning and lamentation for the Daughter of Judah.
Lamentations2:6 He has laid waste His tabernacle like a garden booth; He has destroyed His place of meeting. The Lord has made Zion forget her appointed feasts and Sabbaths. In His fierce anger He has despised both king and priest.
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected His altar; He has abandoned His sanctuary; He has delivered the walls of her palaces into the hand of the enemy. They have raised a shout in the house of the Lord as on the day of an appointed feast.
Lamentations2:8 The Lord determined to destroy the wall of the Daughter of Zion. He stretched out a measuring line and did not withdraw His hand from destroying. He made the ramparts and walls lament; together they waste away.
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; He has destroyed and shattered their bars. Her king and her princes are exiled among the nations
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the Daughter of Zion sit on the ground in silence. They have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth. The young women of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes fail from weeping; I am churning within. My heart is poured out in grief over the destruction of the daughter of my people
Lamentations2:12 They cry out to their mothers: “Where is the grain and wine?” as they faint like the wounded in the streets of the city
Lamentations2:13 What can I say for you? To what can I compare you
Lamentations2:14 The visions of your prophets were empty and deceptive; they did not expose your guilt to ward off your captivity. The burdens they envisioned for you were empty and misleading.
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by clap their hands at you in scorn. They hiss and shake their heads at the Daughter of Jerusalem: “Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies open their mouths against you. They hiss and gnash their teeth
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has done what He planned; He has accomplished His decree
Lamentations2:18 The hearts of the people cry out to the Lord. O wall of the Daughter of Zion
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 Look
Lamentations2:21 Both young and old lie together in the dust of the streets. My young men and maidens have fallen by the sword. You have slain them in the day of Your anger; You have slaughtered them without compassion.
Lamentations2:22 You summoned my attackers on every side
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction under the rod of God’s wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has driven me away and made me walk in darkness instead of light.
Lamentations3:3 Indeed
Lamentations3:4 He has worn away my flesh and skin; He has shattered my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has besieged me and surrounded me with bitterness and hardship.
Lamentations3:6 He has made me dwell in darkness like those dead for ages.
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in so I cannot escape; He has weighed me down with chains.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry out and plead for help
Lamentations3:9 He has barred my ways with cut stones; He has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He forced me off my path and tore me to pieces; He left me without help.
Lamentations3:12 He bent His bow and set me as the target for His arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He pierced my kidneys with His arrows.
Lamentations3:14 I am a laughingstock to all my people; they mock me in song all day long.
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness; He has intoxicated me with wormwood.
Lamentations3:16 He has ground my teeth with gravel and trampled me in the dust.
Lamentations3:17 My soul has been deprived of peace; I have forgotten what prosperity is.
Lamentations3:18 So I say
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and wandering
Lamentations3:20 Surely my soul remembers and is humbled within me.
Lamentations3:21 Yet I call this to mind
Lamentations3:22 Because of the loving devotion of the Lord we are not consumed
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness!
Lamentations3:24 “The Lord is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is good to those who wait for Him
Lamentations3:26 It is good to wait quietly for the salvation of the Lord.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man to bear the yoke while he is still young.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone in silence
Lamentations3:29 Let him bury his face in the dust— perhaps there is still hope.
Lamentations3:30 Let him offer his cheek to the one who would strike him; let him be filled with reproach.
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast us off forever.
Lamentations3:32 Even if He causes grief
Lamentations3:33 For He does not willingly afflict or grieve the sons of men.
Lamentations3:34 To crush underfoot all the prisoners of the land
Lamentations3:35 to deny a man justice before the Most High
Lamentations3:36 to subvert a man in his lawsuit— of these the Lord does not approve.
Lamentations3:37 Who has spoken and it came to pass
Lamentations3:38 Do not both adversity and good come from the mouth of the Most High?
Lamentations3:39 Why should any mortal man complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us examine and test our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and hands to God in heaven:
Lamentations3:42 “We have sinned and rebelled; You have not forgiven.”
Lamentations3:43 You have covered Yourself in anger and pursued us; You have killed without pity.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered Yourself with a cloud that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us scum and refuse among the nations.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies open their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 Panic and pitfall have come upon us— devastation and destruction.
Lamentations3:48 Streams of tears flow from my eyes over the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes overflow unceasingly
Lamentations3:50 until the Lord looks down from heaven and sees.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes bring grief to my soul because of all the daughters of my city.
Lamentations3:52 Without cause my enemies hunted me like a bird.
Lamentations3:53 They dropped me alive into a pit and cast stones upon me.
Lamentations3:54 The waters flowed over my head
Lamentations3:55 I called on Your name
Lamentations3:56 You heard my plea: “Do not ignore my cry for relief.”
Lamentations3:57 You drew near when I called on You; You said
Lamentations3:58 You defend my cause
Lamentations3:59 You have seen
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their malice
Lamentations3:61 O Lord
Lamentations3:62 the slander and murmuring of my assailants against me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 When they sit and when they rise
Lamentations3:64 You will pay them back what they deserve
Lamentations3:65 Put a veil of anguish over their hearts; may Your curse be upon them!
Lamentations3:66 You will pursue them in anger and exterminate them from under Your heavens
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has become tarnished
Lamentations4:2 How the precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even jackals offer their breasts to nurse their young
Lamentations4:4 The nursing infant’s tongue clings in thirst to the roof of his mouth. Little children beg for bread
Lamentations4:5 Those who once ate delicacies are destitute in the streets; those brought up in crimson huddle in ash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 The punishment of the daughter of my people is greater than that of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her dignitaries were brighter than snow
Lamentations4:8 But now their appearance is blacker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones; it has become as dry as a stick.
Lamentations4:9 Those slain by the sword are better off than those who die of hunger
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women have cooked their own children
Lamentations4:11 The Lord has exhausted His wrath; He has poured out His fierce anger; He has kindled a fire in Zion
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth did not believe
Lamentations4:13 But this was for the sins of her prophets and the guilt of her priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered blind in the streets
Lamentations4:15 “Go away! Unclean!” men shouted at them. “Away
Lamentations4:16 The presence of the Lord has scattered them; He regards them no more. The priests are shown no honor; the elders find no favor.
Lamentations4:17 All the while our eyes were failing as we looked in vain for help. We watched from our towers for a nation that could not save us.
Lamentations4:18 They stalked our every step
Lamentations4:19 Those who chased us were swifter than the eagles in the sky; they pursued us over the mountains and ambushed us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 The Lord’s anointed
Lamentations4:21 So rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 O Daughter of Zion
Lamentations5:1 Remember
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become fatherless orphans; our mothers are widows.
Lamentations5:4 We must buy the water we drink; our wood comes at a price.
Lamentations5:5 We are closely pursued; we are weary and find no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We submitted to Egypt and Assyria to get enough bread.
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned and are no more
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; there is no one to deliver us from their hands.
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the risk of our lives because of the sword in the wilderness.
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is as hot as an oven with fever from our hunger.
Lamentations5:11 Women have been ravished in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes have been hung up by their hands; elders receive no respect.
Lamentations5:13 Young men toil at millstones; boys stagger under loads of wood.
Lamentations5:14 The elders have left the city gate; the young men have stopped their music.
Lamentations5:15 Joy has left our hearts; our dancing has turned to mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head. Woe to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this
Lamentations5:18 because of Mount Zion
Lamentations5:19 You
Lamentations5:20 Why have You forgotten us forever? Why have You forsaken us for so long?
Lamentations5:21 Restore us to Yourself
Lamentations5:22 unless You have utterly rejected us and remain angry with us beyond measure.
Ezekiel1:1 In the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month—it was the fifth year of the exile of King Jehoiachin—
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the Lord came directly to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 I looked and saw a whirlwind coming from the north
Ezekiel1:5 and within it was the form of four living creatures. And this was their appearance: They had a human form
Ezekiel1:6 but each had four faces and four wings.
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 Under their wings on their four sides they had human hands. All four living creatures had faces and wings
Ezekiel1:9 and their wings were touching one another. They did not turn as they moved; each one went straight ahead.
Ezekiel1:10 The form of their faces was that of a man
Ezekiel1:11 Such were their faces. Their wings were spread upward; each had two wings touching the wings of the creature on either side
Ezekiel1:12 Each creature went straight ahead. Wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:13 In the midst of the living creatures was the appearance of glowing coals of fire
Ezekiel1:14 The creatures were darting back and forth as quickly as flashes of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 When I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The workmanship of the wheels looked like the gleam of beryl
Ezekiel1:17 As they moved
Ezekiel1:18 Their rims were high and awesome
Ezekiel1:19 So as the living creatures moved
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:21 When the creatures moved
Ezekiel1:22 Spread out above the heads of the living creatures was the likeness of an awesome expanse
Ezekiel1:23 And under the expanse
Ezekiel1:24 When the creatures moved
Ezekiel1:25 And there came a voice from above the expanse over their heads as they stood still with their wings lowered.
Ezekiel1:26 Above the expanse over their heads was the likeness of a throne with the appearance of sapphire
Ezekiel1:27 From what seemed to be His waist up
Ezekiel1:28 The appearance of the brilliant light all around Him was like that of a rainbow in a cloud on a rainy day. This was the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the Lord. And when I saw it

Ezekiel4:1 “Now you
Ezekiel4:2 Then lay siege against it: Construct a siege wall

Ezekiel7:6 The end has come! The end has come! It has roused itself against you. Behold
Ezekiel7:7 Doom has come to you
Ezekiel7:8 Very soon I will pour out My wrath upon you and vent My anger against you; I will judge you according to your ways and repay you for all your abominations.
Ezekiel7:9 I will not look on you with pity
Ezekiel7:10 Behold
Ezekiel7:11 Their violence has grown into a rod to punish their wickedness. None of them will remain: none of their multitude
Ezekiel7:12 The time has come; the day has arrived. Let the buyer not rejoice and the seller not mourn
Ezekiel7:13 The seller will surely not recover what he sold while both remain alive. For the vision concerning the whole multitude will not be revoked
Ezekiel7:14 They have blown the trumpet and made everything ready
Ezekiel7:15 The sword is outside; plague and famine are within. Those in the country will die by the sword
Ezekiel7:16 The survivors will escape and live in the mountains
Ezekiel7:17 Every hand will go limp
Ezekiel7:18 They will put on sackcloth
Ezekiel7:19 They will throw their silver into the streets
Ezekiel7:20 His beautiful ornaments they transformed into pride and used them to fashion their vile images and detestable idols. Therefore I will make these into something unclean for them.
Ezekiel7:21 And I will hand these things over as plunder to foreigners and loot to the wicked of the earth
Ezekiel7:22 I will turn My face away from them
Ezekiel7:23 Forge the chain
Ezekiel7:24 So I will bring the most wicked of nations to take possession of their houses. I will end the pride of the mighty
Ezekiel7:25 Anguish is coming! They will seek peace
Ezekiel7:26 Disaster upon disaster will come
Ezekiel7:27 The king will mourn

Ezekiel11:8 You fear the sword

Ezekiel13:6 They see false visions and speak lying divinations. They claim
Ezekiel13:7 Haven’t you seen a false vision and spoken a lying divination when you proclaim
Ezekiel13:8 Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: Because you have uttered vain words and seen false visions
Ezekiel13:9 My hand will be against the prophets who see false visions and speak lying divinations. They will not belong to the council of My people or be recorded in the register of the house of Israel
Ezekiel13:10 Because they have led My people astray
Ezekiel13:11 tell those whitewashing the wall that it will fall. Rain will come in torrents

Ezekiel13:13 Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: In My wrath I will release a windstorm
Ezekiel13:14 I will tear down the wall you whitewashed and level it to the ground
Ezekiel13:15 And after I have vented My wrath against the wall and against those who whitewashed it
Ezekiel13:16 those prophets of Israel who prophesied to Jerusalem and saw a vision of peace for her when there was no peace
Ezekiel13:17 Now

Ezekiel13:19 You have profaned Me among My people for handfuls of barley and scraps of bread. By lying to My people who would listen
Ezekiel13:20 Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: See
Ezekiel13:21 I will also tear off your veils and deliver My people from your hands
Ezekiel13:22 Because you have disheartened the righteous with your lies
Ezekiel13:23 therefore you will no longer see false visions or practice divination. I will deliver My people from your hands. Then you will know that I am the Lord.”

Ezekiel14:6 Therefore tell the house of Israel that this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘Repent and turn away from your idols; turn your faces away from all your abominations.
Ezekiel14:7 For when any Israelite or any foreigner dwelling in Israel separates himself from Me
Ezekiel14:8 I will set My face against that man and make him a sign and a proverb; I will cut him off from among My people. Then you will know that I am the Lord.
Ezekiel14:9 But if the prophet is enticed to speak a message
Ezekiel14:10 They will bear their punishment—the punishment of the inquirer will be the same as that of the prophet—
Ezekiel14:11 in order that the house of Israel may no longer stray from Me and no longer defile themselves with all their transgressions. Then they will be My people and I will be their God

Ezekiel14:20 then as surely as I live
Ezekiel14:21 For this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘How much worse will it be when I send against Jerusalem My four dire judgments—sword

Ezekiel15:1 Then the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel15:2 “Son of man

Ezekiel15:4 No
Ezekiel15:5 Even when it was whole

Ezekiel16:1 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel16:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel16:3 and tell her that this is what the Lord GOD says to Jerusalem: Your origin and your birth were in the land of the Canaanites. Your father was an Amorite and your mother a Hittite.
Ezekiel16:4 On the day of your birth your cord was not cut
Ezekiel16:5 No one cared enough for you to do even one of these things out of compassion for you. Instead
Ezekiel16:6 Then I passed by and saw you wallowing in your blood
Ezekiel16:7 I made you thrive like a plant of the field. You grew up and matured and became very beautiful. Your breasts were formed and your hair grew
Ezekiel16:8 Then I passed by and saw you
Ezekiel16:9 Then I bathed you with water
Ezekiel16:10 I clothed you in embroidered cloth and gave you sandals of fine leather. I wrapped you in fine linen and covered you with silk.
Ezekiel16:11 I adorned you with jewelry
Ezekiel16:12 I put a ring in your nose
Ezekiel16:13 So you were adorned with gold and silver
Ezekiel16:14 Your fame spread among the nations on account of your beauty
Ezekiel16:15 But because of your fame
Ezekiel16:16 You took some of your garments and made colorful high places for yourself
Ezekiel16:17 You also took the fine jewelry of gold and silver I had given you
Ezekiel16:18 You took your embroidered garments to cover them
Ezekiel16:19 And you set before them as a pleasing aroma the food I had given you—the fine flour
Ezekiel16:20 You even took the sons and daughters you bore to Me and sacrificed them as food to idols. Was your prostitution not enough?
Ezekiel16:21 You slaughtered My children and delivered them up through the fire to idols.
Ezekiel16:22 And in all your abominations and acts of prostitution
Ezekiel16:23 Woe! Woe to you
Ezekiel16:24 you built yourself a mound and made yourself a lofty shrine in every public square.
Ezekiel16:25 At the head of every street you built your lofty shrines and degraded your beauty. With increasing promiscuity
Ezekiel16:26 You prostituted yourself with your lustful neighbors
Ezekiel16:27 Therefore I stretched out My hand against you and reduced your portion. I gave you over to the desire of those who hate you
Ezekiel16:28 Then you prostituted yourself with the Assyrians
Ezekiel16:29 So you extended your promiscuity to Chaldea
Ezekiel16:30 How weak-willed is your heart
Ezekiel16:31 But when you built your mounds at the head of every street and made your lofty shrines in every public square
Ezekiel16:32 You adulterous wife! You receive strangers instead of your own husband!
Ezekiel16:33 Men give gifts to all their prostitutes
Ezekiel16:34 So your prostitution is the opposite of that of other women: No one solicited your favors
Ezekiel16:35 Therefore
Ezekiel16:36 This is what the Lord GOD says: Because you poured out your wealth and exposed your nakedness in your promiscuity with your lovers and with all your detestable idols
Ezekiel16:37 therefore I will surely gather all the lovers with whom you found pleasure
Ezekiel16:38 And I will sentence you to the punishment of women who commit adultery and those who shed blood; so I will bring upon you the wrath of your bloodshed and jealousy.
Ezekiel16:39 Then I will deliver you into the hands of your lovers
Ezekiel16:40 They will bring a mob against you
Ezekiel16:41 Then they will burn down your houses and execute judgment against you in the sight of many women. I will put an end to your prostitution
Ezekiel16:42 So I will lay to rest My wrath against you
Ezekiel16:43 Because you did not remember the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:44 Behold
Ezekiel16:45 You are the daughter of your mother
Ezekiel16:46 Your older sister was Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 And you not only walked in their ways and practiced their abominations
Ezekiel16:48 As surely as I live
Ezekiel16:49 Now this was the iniquity of your sister Sodom: She and her daughters were arrogant
Ezekiel16:50 Thus they were haughty and committed abominations before Me. Therefore I removed them
Ezekiel16:51 Furthermore
Ezekiel16:52 So now you must bear your disgrace
Ezekiel16:53 But I will restore Sodom and her daughters from captivity
Ezekiel16:54 So you will bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all you did to comfort them.
Ezekiel16:55 And your sisters
Ezekiel16:56 Did you not treat your sister Sodom as an object of scorn in the day of your pride
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness was uncovered? Even so
Ezekiel16:58 You will bear the consequences of your lewdness and your abominations
Ezekiel16:59 For this is what the Lord GOD says: I will deal with you according to your deeds
Ezekiel16:60 But I will remember the covenant I made with you in the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:61 Then you will remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your older and younger sisters. I will give them to you as daughters
Ezekiel16:62 So I will establish My covenant with you
Ezekiel16:63 so that when I make atonement for all you have done
Ezekiel17:1 Now the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel17:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel17:3 and tell them that this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘A great eagle with great wings and long pinions
Ezekiel17:4 He plucked off its topmost shoot
Ezekiel17:5 He took some of the seed of the land and planted it in fertile soil; he placed it by abundant waters and set it out like a willow.
Ezekiel17:6 It sprouted and became a spreading vine
Ezekiel17:7 But there was another great eagle with great wings and many feathers. And behold
Ezekiel17:8 It had been planted in good soil by abundant waters in order to yield branches and bear fruit and become a splendid vine.’
Ezekiel17:9 So you are to tell them that this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘Will it flourish? Will it not be uprooted and stripped of its fruit so that it shrivels? All its foliage will wither! It will not take a strong arm or many people to pull it up by its roots.
Ezekiel17:10 Even if it is transplanted
Ezekiel17:11 Then the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel17:12 “Now say to this rebellious house: ‘Do you not know what these things mean?’ Tell them
Ezekiel17:13 He took a member of the royal family and made a covenant with him
Ezekiel17:14 so that the kingdom would be brought low
Ezekiel17:15 But this king rebelled against Babylon by sending his envoys to Egypt to ask for horses and a large army. Will he flourish? Will the one who does such things escape? Can he break a covenant and yet escape?’
Ezekiel17:16 ‘As surely as I live
Ezekiel17:17 Pharaoh with his mighty army and vast horde will not help him in battle
Ezekiel17:18 He despised the oath by breaking the covenant. Seeing that he gave his hand in pledge yet did all these things
Ezekiel17:19 Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘As surely as I live
Ezekiel17:20 I will spread My net over him and catch him in My snare. I will bring him to Babylon and execute judgment upon him there for the treason he committed against Me.
Ezekiel17:21 All his choice troops will fall by the sword
Ezekiel17:22 This is what the Lord GOD says: ‘I will take a shoot from the lofty top of the cedar
Ezekiel17:23 I will plant it on the mountain heights of Israel so that it will bear branches; it will yield fruit and become a majestic cedar. Birds of every kind will nest under it
Ezekiel17:24 Then all the trees of the field will know that I am the Lord. I bring the tall tree down and make the low tree tall. I dry up the green tree and make the withered tree flourish. I
Ezekiel18:1 Then the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel18:2 “What do you people mean by quoting this proverb about the land of Israel: ‘The fathers have eaten sour grapes
Ezekiel18:3 As surely as I live
Ezekiel18:4 Behold
Ezekiel18:5 Now suppose a man is righteous and does what is just and right:
Ezekiel18:6 He does not eat at the mountain or look to the idols of the house of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor’s wife or approach a woman during her period.
Ezekiel18:7 He does not oppress another
Ezekiel18:8 He does not engage in usury or take excess interest
Ezekiel18:9 He follows My statutes and faithfully keeps My ordinances. That man is righteous; surely he will live
Ezekiel18:10 Now suppose that man has a violent son
Ezekiel18:11 though the father has done none of them: Indeed
Ezekiel18:12 He oppresses the poor and needy; he commits robbery and does not restore a pledge. He lifts his eyes to idols; he commits abominations.
Ezekiel18:13 He engages in usury and takes excess interest. Will this son live? He will not! Since he has committed all these abominations
Ezekiel18:14 Now suppose this son has a son who sees all the sins his father has committed
Ezekiel18:15 He does not eat at the mountain or look to the idols of the house of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor’s wife.
Ezekiel18:16 He does not oppress another
Ezekiel18:17 He withholds his hand from harming the poor and takes no interest or usury. He keeps My ordinances and follows My statutes. Such a man will not die for his father’s iniquity. He will surely live.
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 Yet you may ask
Ezekiel18:20 The soul who sins is the one who will die. A son will not bear the iniquity of his father
Ezekiel18:21 But if the wicked man turns from all the sins he has committed
Ezekiel18:22 None of the transgressions he has committed will be held against him. Because of the righteousness he has practiced
Ezekiel18:23 Do I take any pleasure in the death of the wicked? declares the Lord GOD. Wouldn’t I prefer that he turn from his ways and live?
Ezekiel18:24 But if a righteous man turns from his righteousness and practices iniquity
Ezekiel18:25 Yet you say
Ezekiel18:26 If a righteous man turns from his righteousness and practices iniquity
Ezekiel18:27 But if a wicked man turns from the wickedness he has committed and does what is just and right
Ezekiel18:28 Because he considered and turned from all the transgressions he had committed
Ezekiel18:29 Yet the house of Israel says
Ezekiel18:30 Therefore
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from yourselves all the transgressions you have committed
Ezekiel18:32 For I take no pleasure in anyone’s death
Ezekiel19:1 “As for you
Ezekiel19:2 and say: ‘What was your mother? A lioness among the lions! She lay down among the young lions; she reared her cubs.
Ezekiel19:3 She brought up one of her cubs
Ezekiel19:4 When the nations heard of him
Ezekiel19:5 When she saw that she had waited in vain
Ezekiel19:6 He prowled among the lions
Ezekiel19:7 He broke down their strongholds and devastated their cities. The land and everything in it shuddered at the sound of his roaring.
Ezekiel19:8 Then the nations set out against him from the provinces on every side. They spread their net over him; he was trapped in their pit.
Ezekiel19:9 With hooks they caged him and brought him to the king of Babylon. They brought him into captivity so that his roar was heard no longer on the mountains of Israel.
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother was like a vine in your vineyard
Ezekiel19:11 It had strong branches
Ezekiel19:12 But it was uprooted in fury
Ezekiel19:13 Now it is planted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 Fire has gone out from its main branch and devoured its fruit; on it no strong branch remains fit for a ruler’s scepter.’ This is a lament and shall be used as a lament.”
Ezekiel20:1 In the seventh year
Ezekiel20:2 Then the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel20:3 “Son of man
Ezekiel20:4 Will you judge them
Ezekiel20:5 and tell them that this is what the Lord GOD says: On the day I chose Israel
Ezekiel20:6 On that day I swore to bring them out of the land of Egypt into a land that I had searched out for them
Ezekiel20:7 And I said to them: ‘Each of you must throw away the abominations before his eyes
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against Me and refused to listen. None of them cast away the abominations before their eyes
Ezekiel20:9 But I acted for the sake of My name
Ezekiel20:10 So I brought them out of the land of Egypt and led them into the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:11 And I gave them My statutes and made known to them My ordinances—for the man who does these things will live by them.
Ezekiel20:12 I also gave them My Sabbaths as a sign between us
Ezekiel20:13 Yet the house of Israel rebelled against Me in the wilderness. They did not follow My statutes and they rejected My ordinances—though the man who does these things will live by them—and they utterly profaned My Sabbaths. Then I resolved to pour out My wrath upon them and put an end to them in the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:14 But I acted for the sake of My name
Ezekiel20:15 Moreover
Ezekiel20:16 because they kept rejecting My ordinances
Ezekiel20:17 Yet I looked on them with pity and did not destroy them or bring them to an end in the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:18 In the wilderness I said to their children: ‘Do not walk in the statutes of your fathers or keep their ordinances or defile yourselves with their idols.
Ezekiel20:19 I am the Lord your God; walk in My statutes
Ezekiel20:20 Keep My Sabbaths holy
Ezekiel20:21 But the children rebelled against Me. They did not walk in My statutes or carefully observe My ordinances—though the man who does these things will live by them—and they profaned My Sabbaths. So I resolved to pour out My wrath upon them and vent My anger against them in the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:22 But I withheld My hand and acted for the sake of My name
Ezekiel20:23 However
Ezekiel20:24 For they did not practice My ordinances
Ezekiel20:25 I also gave them over to statutes that were not good and ordinances by which they could not live.
Ezekiel20:26 And I pronounced them unclean through their gifts—the sacrifice of every firstborn in the fire—so that I might devastate them
Ezekiel20:27 Therefore

Ezekiel20:38 And I will purge you of those who rebel and transgress against Me. I will bring them out of the land in which they dwell
Ezekiel20:39 And as for you

Ezekiel22:9 Among you are slanderous men bent on bloodshed; within you are those who eat on the mountain shrines and commit acts of indecency.
Ezekiel22:10 In you they have uncovered the nakedness of their fathers; in you they violate women during their menstrual impurity.
Ezekiel22:11 One man commits an abomination with his neighbor’s wife; another wickedly defiles his daughter-in-law; and yet another violates his sister

Ezekiel23:17 Then the Babylonians came to her

Ezekiel23:19 Yet she multiplied her promiscuity
Ezekiel23:20 and lusted after their lovers

Ezekiel23:22 Therefore
Ezekiel23:23 the Babylonians and all the Chaldeans

Ezekiel23:26 They will strip off your clothes and take your fine jewelry.
Ezekiel23:27 So I will put an end to your indecency and prostitution

Ezekiel23:29 They will treat you with hatred
Ezekiel23:30 have brought these things upon you
Ezekiel23:31 Because you have followed the path of your sister
Ezekiel23:32 This is what the Lord GOD says: ‘You will drink your sister’s cup
Ezekiel23:33 You will be filled with drunkenness and grief
Ezekiel23:34 You will drink it and drain it; you will dash it to pieces

Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 They have also done this to Me: On that very same day
Ezekiel23:39 On the very day they slaughtered their children for their idols
Ezekiel23:40 Furthermore
Ezekiel23:41 You sat on a couch of luxury with a table spread before it
Ezekiel23:42 accompanied by the sound of a carefree crowd. Drunkards were brought in from the desert along with men from the rabble
Ezekiel23:43 Then I said of her who had grown old in adulteries: ‘Now let them use her as a prostitute
Ezekiel23:44 And they slept with her as with a prostitute; they slept with Oholah and Oholibah
Ezekiel23:45 But righteous men will sentence them to the punishment of those who commit adultery and bloodshed
Ezekiel23:46 This is what the Lord GOD says: ‘Bring a mob against them and consign them to terror and plunder.

Ezekiel24:4 Put in the pieces of meat
Ezekiel24:5 Take the choicest of the flock and pile the fuel beneath it. Bring it to a boil and cook the bones in it.’
Ezekiel24:6 Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘Woe to the city of bloodshed

Ezekiel24:8 In order to stir up wrath and take vengeance
Ezekiel24:9 Yes
Ezekiel24:10 Pile on the logs and kindle the fire; cook the meat well and mix in the spices; let the bones be burned.

Ezekiel27:7 Of embroidered fine linen from Egypt they made your sail

Ezekiel27:10 Men of Persia

Ezekiel27:13 Javan
Ezekiel27:14 The men of Beth-togarmah exchanged horses

Ezekiel27:23 Haran
Ezekiel27:24 In your marketplace they traded with you fine garments of blue

Ezekiel27:26 Your oarsmen have brought you onto the high seas
Ezekiel27:27 Your wealth
Ezekiel27:28 The countryside will shake when your sailors cry out.
Ezekiel27:29 All who handle the oars will abandon their ships. The sailors and all the captains of the sea will stand on the shore.
Ezekiel27:30 They will raise their voices for you and cry out bitterly. They will throw dust on their heads and roll in ashes.
Ezekiel27:31 They will shave their heads for you and wrap themselves in sackcloth. They will weep over you with anguish of soul and bitter mourning.
Ezekiel27:32 As they wail and mourn over you
Ezekiel27:33 When your wares went out to sea
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are shattered by the seas in the depths of the waters; your merchandise and the people among you have gone down with you.
Ezekiel27:35 All the people of the coastlands are appalled over you. Their kings shudder with fear; their faces are contorted.
Ezekiel27:36 Those who trade among the nations hiss at you; you have come to a horrible end and will be no more.’”
Ezekiel28:1 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel28:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel28:3 Behold
Ezekiel28:4 By your wisdom and understanding you have gained your wealth and amassed gold and silver for your treasuries.
Ezekiel28:5 By your great skill in trading you have increased your wealth
Ezekiel28:6 Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: Because you regard your heart as the heart of a god
Ezekiel28:7 behold
Ezekiel28:8 They will bring you down to the Pit
Ezekiel28:9 Will you still say
Ezekiel28:10 You will die the death of the uncircumcised at the hands of foreigners. For I have spoken
Ezekiel28:11 Again the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel28:12 “Son of man
Ezekiel28:13 You were in Eden
Ezekiel28:14 You were anointed as a guardian cherub
Ezekiel28:15 From the day you were created you were blameless in your ways— until wickedness was found in you.
Ezekiel28:16 By the vastness of your trade
Ezekiel28:17 Your heart grew proud of your beauty; you corrupted your wisdom because of your splendor; so I cast you to the earth; I made you a spectacle before kings.
Ezekiel28:18 By the multitude of your iniquities and the dishonesty of your trading you have profaned your sanctuaries. So I made fire come from within you
Ezekiel28:19 All the nations who know you are appalled over you. You have come to a horrible end and will be no more.’”

Ezekiel28:21 “Son of man
Ezekiel28:22 And you are to declare that this is what the Lord GOD says: ‘Behold
Ezekiel28:23 I will send a plague against her and shed blood in her streets; the slain will fall within her
Ezekiel28:24 For the people of Israel will no longer face a pricking brier or a painful thorn from all around them who treat them with contempt. Then they will know that I am the Lord GOD.’
Ezekiel28:25 This is what the Lord GOD says: ‘When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they have been scattered
Ezekiel28:26 And there they will dwell securely

Ezekiel29:15 Egypt will be the lowliest of kingdoms and will never again exalt itself above the nations. For I will diminish Egypt so that it will never again rule over the nations.

Ezekiel31:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel31:2 “Son of man
Ezekiel31:3 Look at Assyria
Ezekiel31:4 The waters made it grow; the deep springs made it tall
Ezekiel31:5 Therefore it towered higher than all the trees of the field. Its branches multiplied
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of the air nested in its branches
Ezekiel31:7 It was beautiful in its greatness
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in the garden of God could not rival it; the cypresses could not compare with its branches

Ezekiel32:1 In the twelfth year

Ezekiel32:22 Assyria is there with her whole company; her graves are all around her. All of them are slain
Ezekiel32:23 Her graves are set in the depths of the Pit

Ezekiel32:26 Meshech and Tubal are there with all their multitudes
Ezekiel32:27 They do not lie down with the fallen warriors of old
Ezekiel32:28 But you too will be shattered and lie down among the uncircumcised
Ezekiel32:29 Edom is there
Ezekiel32:30 All the leaders of the north and all the Sidonians are there; they went down in disgrace with the slain

Ezekiel39:20 And at My table you will eat your fill of horses and riders
Ezekiel39:21 I will display My glory among the nations
Ezekiel39:22 From that day forward the house of Israel will know that I am the Lord their God.
Ezekiel39:23 And the nations will know that the house of Israel went into exile for their iniquity
Ezekiel39:24 I dealt with them according to their uncleanness and transgressions

Ezekiel39:26 They will forget their disgrace and all the treachery they committed against Me

Ezekiel39:28 Then they will know that I am the Lord their God

Ezekiel40:1 In the twenty-fifth year of our exile
Ezekiel40:2 In visions of God He took me to the land of Israel and set me on a very high mountain
Ezekiel40:3 So He took me there
Ezekiel40:4 “Son of man
Ezekiel40:5 And I saw a wall surrounding the temple area. Now the length of the measuring rod in the man’s hand was six long cubits (each measuring a cubit and a handbreadth)
Ezekiel40:6 Then he came to the gate facing east and climbed its steps. He measured the threshold of the gate to be one rod deep.
Ezekiel40:7 Each gate chamber was one rod long and one rod wide
Ezekiel40:8 Then he measured the portico of the gateway inside;
Ezekiel40:9 it was eight cubits deep
Ezekiel40:10 There were three gate chambers on each side of the east gate
Ezekiel40:11 And he measured the width of the gateway entrance to be ten cubits
Ezekiel40:12 In front of each gate chamber was a wall one cubit high
Ezekiel40:13 Then he measured the gateway from the roof of one gate chamber to the roof of the opposite one; the distance was twenty-five cubits from doorway to doorway.
Ezekiel40:14 Next he measured the gateposts to be sixty cubits high. The gateway extended around to the gatepost of the courtyard.
Ezekiel40:15 And the distance from the entrance of the gateway to the far end of its inner portico was fifty cubits.
Ezekiel40:16 The gate chambers and their side pillars had beveled windows all around the inside of the gateway. The porticos also had windows all around on the inside. Each side pillar was decorated with palm trees.
Ezekiel40:17 Then he brought me into the outer court
Ezekiel40:18 which flanked the gateways and corresponded to the length of the gates; this was the lower pavement.
Ezekiel40:19 Then he measured the distance from the front of the lower gateway to the outside of the inner court; it was a hundred cubits on the east side as well as on the north.
Ezekiel40:20 He also measured the length and width of the gateway of the outer court facing north.
Ezekiel40:21 Its three gate chambers on each side
Ezekiel40:22 Its windows
Ezekiel40:23 There was a gate to the inner court facing the north gate
Ezekiel40:24 Then he led me to the south side
Ezekiel40:25 Both the gateway and its portico had windows all around
Ezekiel40:26 Seven steps led up to it
Ezekiel40:27 The inner court also had a gate facing south
Ezekiel40:28 Next he brought me into the inner court through the south gate
Ezekiel40:29 Its gate chambers
Ezekiel40:30 (The porticoes around the inner court were twenty-five cubits long and five cubits deep. )
Ezekiel40:31 Its portico faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:32 And he brought me to the inner court on the east side
Ezekiel40:33 Its gate chambers
Ezekiel40:34 Its portico faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:35 Then he brought me to the north gate and measured it. It had the same measurements as the others
Ezekiel40:36 as did its gate chambers
Ezekiel40:37 Its portico faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:38 There was a chamber with a doorway by the portico in each of the inner gateways. There the burnt offering was to be washed.
Ezekiel40:39 Inside the portico of the gateway were two tables on each side
Ezekiel40:40 Outside
Ezekiel40:41 So there were four tables inside the gateway and four outside—eight tables in all—on which the sacrifices were to be slaughtered.
Ezekiel40:42 There were also four tables of dressed stone for the burnt offering
Ezekiel40:43 The double-pronged hooks
Ezekiel40:44 Outside the inner gate
Ezekiel40:45 Then the man said to me: “The chamber that faces south is for the priests who keep charge of the temple
Ezekiel40:46 and the chamber that faces north is for the priests who keep charge of the altar. These are the sons of Zadok
Ezekiel40:47 Next he measured the court. It was square
Ezekiel40:48 Then he brought me to the portico of the temple and measured the side pillars of the portico to be five cubits on each side. The width of the gateway was fourteen cubits and its sidewalls were three cubits on either side.
Ezekiel40:49 The portico was twenty cubits wide and twelve cubits deep
Ezekiel41:1 Then the man brought me into the outer sanctuary and measured the side pillars to be six cubits wide on each side.
Ezekiel41:2 The width of the entrance was ten cubits
Ezekiel41:3 And he went into the inner sanctuary and measured the side pillars at the entrance to be two cubits wide. The entrance was six cubits wide
Ezekiel41:4 Then he measured the room adjacent to the inner sanctuary to be twenty cubits long and twenty cubits wide. And he said to me
Ezekiel41:5 Next he measured the wall of the temple to be six cubits thick
Ezekiel41:6 The side rooms were arranged one above another in three levels of thirty rooms each. There were ledges all around the wall of the temple to serve as supports for the side rooms
Ezekiel41:7 The side rooms surrounding the temple widened at each successive level
Ezekiel41:8 I saw that the temple had a raised base all around it
Ezekiel41:9 The outer wall of the side rooms was five cubits thick
Ezekiel41:10 and the outer chambers was twenty cubits wide all around the temple.
Ezekiel41:11 The side rooms opened into this area
Ezekiel41:12 Now the building that faced the temple courtyard on the west was seventy cubits wide
Ezekiel41:13 Then he measured the temple to be a hundred cubits long
Ezekiel41:14 The width of the temple courtyard on the east
Ezekiel41:15 Next he measured the length of the building facing the temple courtyard at the rear of the temple
Ezekiel41:16 as well as the thresholds and the beveled windows and the galleries all around with their three levels opposite the threshold
Ezekiel41:17 In the space above the outside of the entrance to the inner sanctuary on all the walls
Ezekiel41:18 were alternating carved cherubim and palm trees. Each cherub had two faces:
Ezekiel41:19 the face of a man was toward the palm tree on one side
Ezekiel41:20 Cherubim and palm trees were carved on the wall of the outer sanctuary from the floor to the space above the entrance.
Ezekiel41:21 The outer sanctuary had a rectangular doorframe
Ezekiel41:22 There was an altar of wood three cubits high and two cubits square. Its corners
Ezekiel41:23 Both the outer sanctuary and the inner sanctuary had double doors
Ezekiel41:24 and each door had two swinging panels. There were two panels for one door and two for the other.
Ezekiel41:25 Cherubim and palm trees like those on the walls were carved on the doors of the outer sanctuary
Ezekiel41:26 There were beveled windows and palm trees on the sidewalls of the portico. The side rooms of the temple also had canopies.
Ezekiel42:1 Then the man led me out northward into the outer court
Ezekiel42:2 The building with the door facing north was a hundred cubits long and fifty cubits wide.
Ezekiel42:3 Gallery faced gallery in three levels opposite the twenty cubits that belonged to the inner court and opposite the pavement that belonged to the outer court.
Ezekiel42:4 In front of the chambers was an inner walkway ten cubits wide and a hundred cubits long. Their doors were on the north.
Ezekiel42:5 Now the upper chambers were smaller because the galleries took more space from the chambers on the lower and middle floors of the building.
Ezekiel42:6 For they were arranged in three stories
Ezekiel42:7 An outer wall in front of the chambers was fifty cubits long and ran parallel to the chambers and the outer court.
Ezekiel42:8 For the chambers on the outer court were fifty cubits long

Ezekiel43:1 Then the man brought me back to the gate that faces east
Ezekiel43:2 and I saw the glory of the God of Israel coming from the east. His voice was like the roar of many waters
Ezekiel43:3 The vision I saw was like the vision I had seen when He came to destroy the city and like the visions I had seen by the River Kebar. I fell facedown
Ezekiel43:4 and the glory of the Lord entered the temple through the gate facing east.
Ezekiel43:5 Then the Spirit lifted me up and brought me into the inner court

Ezekiel43:7 and He said to me

Ezekiel43:11 and if they are ashamed of all they have done

Ezekiel43:14 The space from the gutter on the ground to the lower ledge shall be two cubits
Ezekiel43:15 The altar hearth shall be four cubits high

Ezekiel43:23 When you have finished the purification
Ezekiel43:24 You must present them before the Lord; the priests are to sprinkle salt on them and sacrifice them as a burnt offering to the Lord.

Ezekiel43:26 For seven days the priests are to make atonement for the altar and cleanse it; so they shall consecrate it.
Ezekiel43:27 At the end of these days
Ezekiel44:1 The man then brought me back to the outer gate of the sanctuary that faced east

Ezekiel44:4 Then the man brought me to the front of the temple by way of the north gate. I looked and saw the glory of the Lord filling His temple
Ezekiel44:5 The Lord said to me: “Son of man

Ezekiel44:7 In addition to all your other abominations
Ezekiel44:8 And you have not kept charge of My holy things
Ezekiel44:9 This is what the Lord GOD says: No foreigner uncircumcised in heart and flesh may enter My sanctuary—not even a foreigner who lives among the Israelites.
Ezekiel44:10 Surely the Levites who wandered away from Me when Israel went astray
Ezekiel44:11 Yet they shall be ministers in My sanctuary
Ezekiel44:12 Because they ministered before their idols and became a stumbling block of iniquity to the house of Israel
Ezekiel44:13 They must not approach Me to serve Me as priests or come near any of My holy things or the most holy things. They will bear the shame of the abominations they have committed.
Ezekiel44:14 Yet I will appoint them to keep charge of all the work for the temple and everything to be done in it.
Ezekiel44:15 But the Levitical priests
Ezekiel44:16 They alone shall enter My sanctuary and draw near to My table to minister before Me. They will keep My charge.
Ezekiel44:17 When they enter the gates of the inner court
Ezekiel44:18 They are to wear linen turbans on their heads and linen undergarments around their waists. They must not wear anything that makes them perspire.
Ezekiel44:19 When they go out to the outer court
Ezekiel44:20 They must not shave their heads or let their hair grow long
Ezekiel44:21 No priest may drink wine before he enters the inner court.
Ezekiel44:22 And they shall not marry a widow or a divorced woman
Ezekiel44:23 They are to teach My people the difference between the holy and the common
Ezekiel44:24 In any dispute
Ezekiel44:25 A priest must not defile himself by going near a dead person. However

Ezekiel44:27 And on the day he goes into the sanctuary

Ezekiel44:31 The priests may not eat any bird or animal found dead or torn by wild beasts.

Daniel1:1 In the third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 And the Lord delivered into his hand Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:3 Then the king ordered Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 young men without blemish
Daniel1:5 The king assigned them daily provisions of the royal food and wine. They were to be trained for three years
Daniel1:6 Among these young men were some from Judah: Daniel
Daniel1:7 The chief official gave them new names: To Daniel he gave the name Belteshazzar; to Hananiah
Daniel1:8 But Daniel made up his mind that he would not defile himself with the king’s food or wine. So he asked the chief official for permission not to defile himself.
Daniel1:9 Now God had granted Daniel favor and compassion from the chief official
Daniel1:10 but he said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then Daniel said to the steward whom the chief official had appointed over Daniel
Daniel1:12 “Please test your servants for ten days. Let us be given only vegetables to eat and water to drink.
Daniel1:13 Then compare our appearances with those of the young men who are eating the royal food
Daniel1:14 So he consented to this and tested them for ten days.
Daniel1:15 And at the end of ten days
Daniel1:16 So the steward continued to withhold their choice food and the wine they were to drink
Daniel1:17 To these four young men God gave knowledge and understanding in every kind of literature and wisdom. And Daniel had insight into all kinds of visions and dreams.
Daniel1:18 Now at the end of the time specified by the king
Daniel1:19 And the king spoke with them
Daniel1:20 In every matter of wisdom and understanding about which the king consulted them
Daniel1:21 And Daniel remained there until the first year of King Cyrus.

Daniel6:1 Now it pleased Darius to appoint 120 satraps to rule throughout the kingdom

Daniel6:28 So Daniel prospered during the reign of Darius and the reign of Cyrus the Persian.

Daniel8:1 In the third year of the reign of King Belshazzar
Daniel8:2 And in the vision I saw myself in the citadel of Susa
Daniel8:3 Then I lifted up my eyes and saw a ram with two horns standing beside the canal. The horns were long
Daniel8:4 I saw the ram charging toward the west and the north and the south. No animal could stand against him
Daniel8:5 As I was contemplating all this
Daniel8:6 He came toward the two-horned ram I had seen standing beside the canal and rushed at him with furious power.
Daniel8:7 I saw him approach the ram in a rage against him
Daniel8:8 Thus the goat became very great
Daniel8:9 From one of these horns a little horn emerged and grew extensively toward the south and the east and toward the Beautiful Land.
Daniel8:10 It grew as high as the host of heaven
Daniel8:11 It magnified itself
Daniel8:12 And in the rebellion

Joel1:2 Hear this

Joel1:5 Wake up
Joel1:6 For a nation has invaded My land
Joel1:7 It has laid waste My grapevine and splintered My fig tree. It has stripped off the bark and thrown it away; the branches have turned white.
Joel1:8 Wail like a virgin dressed in sackcloth
Joel1:9 Grain and drink offerings have been cut off from the house of the Lord; the priests are in mourning
Joel1:10 The field is ruined; the land mourns. For the grain is destroyed
Joel1:11 Be dismayed
Joel1:12 The grapevine is dried up
Joel1:13 Put on sackcloth and lament

Joel1:15 Alas for the day! For the Day of the Lord is near
Joel1:16 Has not the food been cut off before our very eyes— joy and gladness from the house of our God?
Joel1:17 The seeds lie shriveled beneath the clods; the storehouses are in ruins; the granaries are broken down
Joel1:18 How the cattle groan! The herds wander in confusion because they have no pasture. Even the flocks of sheep are suffering.
Joel1:19 To You
Joel1:20 Even the beasts of the field pant for You

Joel2:18 Then the Lord became jealous for His land
Joel2:19 And the Lord answered His people: “Behold
Joel2:20 The northern army I will drive away from you
Joel2:21 Do not be afraid
Joel2:22 Do not be afraid
Joel2:23 Be glad
Joel2:24 The threshing floors will be full of grain
Joel2:25 I will repay you for the years eaten by locusts— the swarming locust
Joel2:26 You will have plenty to eat
Joel2:27 Then you will know that I am present in Israel and that I am the Lord your God
Joel2:28 And afterward
Joel2:29 Even on My menservants and maidservants
Joel2:30 I will show wonders in the heavens and on the earth
Joel2:31 The sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood before the coming of the great and awesome Day of the Lord.
Joel2:32 And everyone who calls on the name of the Lord will be saved; for on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there will be deliverance
Joel3:1 “Yes
Joel3:2 I will gather all the nations and bring them down to the Valley of Jehoshaphat. There I will enter into judgment against them concerning My people
Joel3:3 They cast lots for My people; they bartered a boy for a prostitute and sold a girl for wine to drink.
Joel3:4 Now what do you have against Me
Joel3:5 For you took My silver and gold and carried off My finest treasures to your temples.
Joel3:6 You sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks
Joel3:7 Behold
Joel3:8 I will sell your sons and daughters into the hands of the people of Judah
Joel3:9 Proclaim this among the nations: “Prepare for war; rouse the mighty men; let all the men of war advance and attack!
Joel3:10 Beat your plowshares into swords and your pruning hooks into spears. Let the weak say
Joel3:11 Come quickly
Joel3:12 Let the nations be roused and advance to the Valley of Jehoshaphat
Joel3:13 Swing the sickle
Joel3:14 Multitudes
Joel3:15 The sun and moon will grow dark
Joel3:16 The Lord will roar from Zion and raise His voice from Jerusalem; heaven and earth will tremble. But the Lord will be a refuge for His people
Joel3:17 Then you will know that I am the Lord your God
Joel3:18 And in that day the mountains will drip with sweet wine
Joel3:19 Egypt will become desolate
Joel3:20 But Judah will be inhabited forever
