Amos1:1 These are the words of Amos
Amos1:2 He said: “The Lord roars from Zion and raises His voice from Jerusalem; the pastures of the shepherds mourn
Amos1:3 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of Damascus
Amos1:4 So I will send fire upon the house of Hazael to consume the citadels of Ben-hadad.
Amos1:5 I will break down the gates of Damascus; I will cut off the ruler of the Valley of Aven and the one who wields the scepter in Beth-eden. The people of Aram will be exiled to Kir
Amos1:6 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of Gaza
Amos1:7 So I will send fire upon the walls of Gaza
Amos1:8 I will cut off the ruler of Ashdod and the one who wields the scepter in Ashkelon. I will turn My hand against Ekron
Amos1:9 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of Tyre
Amos1:10 So I will send fire upon the walls of Tyre to consume its citadels.”
Amos1:11 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of Edom
Amos1:12 So I will send fire upon Teman to consume the citadels of Bozrah.”
Amos1:13 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of the Ammonites
Amos1:14 So I will kindle a fire in the walls of Rabbah to consume its citadels amid war cries on the day of battle and a violent wind on the day of tempest.
Amos1:15 Their king will go into exile — he and his princes together
Amos2:1 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of Moab
Amos2:2 So I will send fire against Moab to consume the citadels of Kerioth. Moab will die in tumult
Amos2:3 I will cut off the ruler of Moab and kill all the officials with him
Amos2:4 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of Judah
Amos2:5 So I will send fire upon Judah to consume the citadels of Jerusalem.”
Amos2:6 This is what the Lord says: “For three transgressions of Israel
Amos2:7 They trample on the heads of the poor as on the dust of the earth; they push the needy out of their way. A man and his father have relations with the same girl and so profane My holy name.
Amos2:8 They lie down beside every altar on garments taken in pledge. And in the house of their God
Amos2:9 Yet it was I who destroyed the Amorite before them
Amos2:10 And I brought you up from the land of Egypt and led you forty years in the wilderness
Amos2:11 I raised up prophets from your sons and Nazirites from your young men. Is this not true
Amos2:12 “But you made the Nazirites drink wine and commanded the prophets not to prophesy.
Amos2:13 Behold
Amos2:14 Escape will fail the swift
Amos2:15 The archer will not stand his ground
Amos2:16 Even the bravest of mighty men will flee naked on that day
Amos3:1 Hear this word that the Lord has spoken against you
Amos3:2 “Only you have I known from all the families of the earth; therefore I will punish you for all your iniquities.”
Amos3:3 Can two walk together without agreeing where to go?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest when he has no prey? Does a young lion growl in his den if he has caught nothing?
Amos3:5 Does a bird land in a snare where no bait has been set? Does a trap spring from the ground when it has nothing to catch?
Amos3:6 If a ram’s horn sounds in a city
Amos3:7 Surely the Lord GOD does nothing without revealing His plan to His servants the prophets.
Amos3:8 The lion has roared— who will not fear? The Lord GOD has spoken— who will not prophesy?
Amos3:9 Proclaim to the citadels of Ashdod and to the citadels of Egypt: “Assemble on the mountains of Samaria; see the great unrest in the city and the acts of oppression in her midst.”
Amos3:10 “For they know not how to do right
Amos3:11 Therefore this is what the Lord GOD says: “An enemy will surround the land; he will pull down your strongholds and plunder your citadels.”
Amos3:12 This is what the Lord says: “As the shepherd snatches from the mouth of the lion two legs or a piece of an ear
Amos3:13 Hear and testify against the house of Jacob
Amos3:14 On the day I punish Israel for their transgressions
Amos3:15 I will tear down the winter house along with the summer house; the houses of ivory will also perish
Amos4:1 Hear this word
Amos4:2 The Lord GOD has sworn by His holiness: “Behold
Amos4:3 You will go out through broken walls
Amos4:4 “Go to Bethel and transgress; rebel even more at Gilgal! Bring your sacrifices every morning
Amos4:5 Offer leavened bread as a thank offering
Amos4:6 “I beset all your cities with cleanness of teeth and all your towns with lack of bread
Amos4:7 “I also withheld the rain from you when the harvest was three months away. I sent rain on one city but withheld it from another. One field received rain; another without rain withered.
Amos4:8 People staggered from city to city for water to drink
Amos4:9 “I struck you with blight and mildew in your growing gardens and vineyards; the locust devoured your fig and olive trees
Amos4:10 “I sent plagues among you like those of Egypt; I killed your young men with the sword
Amos4:11 “Some of you I overthrew as I overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah
Amos4:12 “Therefore

Amos5:12 For I know that your transgressions are many and your sins are numerous. You oppress the righteous by taking bribes; you deprive the poor of justice in the gate.

Amos5:14 Seek good

Amos5:20 Will not the Day of the Lord be darkness and not light
Amos5:21 “I hate
Amos5:22 Even though you offer Me burnt offerings and grain offerings
Amos5:23 Take away from Me the noise of your songs! I will not listen to the music of your harps.
Amos5:24 But let justice roll on like a river
Amos5:25 Did you bring Me sacrifices and offerings forty years in the wilderness
Amos5:26 You have taken along Sakkuth your king and Kaiwan your star god
Amos5:27 Therefore I will send you into exile beyond Damascus

Amos6:4 You lie on beds inlaid with ivory
Amos6:5 You improvise songs on the harp like David and invent your own musical instruments.

Obadiah1:1 This is the vision of Obadiah: This is what the Lord GOD says about Edom— We have heard a message from the Lord; an envoy has been sent among the nations to say
Obadiah1:2 “Behold
Obadiah1:3 The pride of your heart has deceived you
Obadiah1:4 Though you soar like the eagle and make your nest among the stars
Obadiah1:5 “If thieves came to you
Obadiah1:6 But how Esau will be pillaged
Obadiah1:7 All the men allied with you will drive you to the border; the men at peace with you will deceive and overpower you. Those who eat your bread will set a trap for you without your awareness of it.
Obadiah1:8 In that day
Obadiah1:9 Then your mighty men
Obadiah1:10 Because of the violence against your brother Jacob
Obadiah1:11 On the day you stood aloof while strangers carried off his wealth and foreigners entered his gate and cast lots for Jerusalem
Obadiah1:12 But you should not gloat in that day
Obadiah1:13 You should not enter the gate of My people in the day of their disaster
Obadiah1:14 Nor should you stand at the crossroads to cut off their fugitives
Obadiah1:15 For the Day of the Lord is near for all the nations. As you have done
Obadiah1:16 For as you drank on My holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But on Mount Zion there will be deliverance
Obadiah1:18 Then the house of Jacob will be a blazing fire
Obadiah1:19 Those from the Negev will possess the mountains of Esau; those from the foothills will possess the land of the Philistines. They will occupy the fields of Ephraim and Samaria
Obadiah1:20 And the exiles of this host of the Israelites will possess the land of the Canaanites as far as Zarephath; and the exiles from Jerusalem who are in Sepharad will possess the cities of the Negev.
Obadiah1:21 The deliverers will ascend Mount Zion to rule over the mountains of Esau. And the kingdom will belong to the Lord.

Jonah1:2 “Get up! Go to the great city of Nineveh and preach against it
Jonah1:3 Jonah
Jonah1:4 Then the Lord hurled a great wind upon the sea
Jonah1:5 The sailors were afraid
Jonah1:6 The captain approached him and said
Jonah1:7 “Come!” said the sailors to one another. “Let us cast lots to find out who is responsible for this calamity that is upon us.” So they cast lots
Jonah1:8 “Tell us now
Jonah1:9 “I am a Hebrew
Jonah1:10 Then the men were even more afraid and said to him
Jonah1:11 Now the sea was growing worse and worse
Jonah1:12 “Pick me up
Jonah1:13 Nevertheless
Jonah1:14 So they cried out to the Lord: “Please
Jonah1:15 At this
Jonah1:16 Then the men feared the Lord greatly

Jonah2:4 At this
Jonah2:5 The waters engulfed me to take my life; the watery depths closed around me; the seaweed wrapped around my head.
Jonah2:6 To the roots of the mountains I descended; the earth beneath me barred me in forever! But You raised my life from the pit
Jonah2:7 As my life was fading away
Jonah2:8 Those who cling to worthless idols forsake His loving devotion.
Jonah2:9 But I
Jonah2:10 And the Lord commanded the fish

Jonah3:9 Who knows? God may turn and relent; He may turn from His fierce anger
Jonah3:10 When God saw their actions—that they had turned from their evil ways—He relented from the disaster He had threatened to bring upon them.

Micah1:1 This is the word of the Lord that came to Micah the Moreshite in the days of Jotham
Micah1:2 Hear
Micah1:3 For behold
Micah1:4 The mountains will melt beneath Him
Micah1:5 All this is for the transgression of Jacob and the sins of the house of Israel. What is the transgression of Jacob? Is it not Samaria? And what is the high place of Judah? Is it not Jerusalem?
Micah1:6 Therefore I will make Samaria a heap of rubble in the open field
Micah1:7 All her carved images will be smashed to pieces; all her wages will be burned in the fire
Micah1:8 Because of this I will lament and wail; I will walk barefoot and naked. I will howl like a jackal and mourn like an ostrich.
Micah1:9 For her wound is incurable; it has reached even Judah; it has approached the gate of my people
Micah1:10 Do not tell it in Gath; do not weep at all. Roll in the dust in Beth-leaphrah.
Micah1:11 Depart in shameful nakedness
Micah1:12 For the dwellers of Maroth pined for good
Micah1:13 Harness your chariot horses
Micah1:14 Therefore
Micah1:15 I will again bring a conqueror against you
Micah1:16 Shave yourselves bald and cut off your hair in mourning for your precious children; make yourselves as bald as an eagle
Micah2:1 Woe to those who devise iniquity and plot evil on their beds! At morning’s light they accomplish it because the power is in their hands.
Micah2:2 They covet fields and seize them; they take away houses. They deprive a man of his home
Micah2:3 Therefore this is what the Lord says: “I am planning against this nation a disaster from which you cannot free your necks. Then you will not walk so proudly
Micah2:4 In that day they will take up a proverb against you and taunt you with this bitter lamentation: ‘We are utterly ruined! He has changed the portion of my people. How He has removed it from me! He has allotted our fields to traitors.’”
Micah2:5 Therefore
Micah2:6 “Do not preach
Micah2:7 Should it be said
Micah2:8 But of late My people have risen up like an enemy: You strip off the splendid robe from unsuspecting passersby like men returning from battle.
Micah2:9 You drive the women of My people from their pleasant homes. You take away My blessing from their children forever.
Micah2:10 Arise and depart
Micah2:11 If a man of wind were to come and say falsely
Micah2:12 I will surely gather all of you
Micah2:13 One who breaks open the way will go up before them; they will break through the gate

Micah3:2 You hate good and love evil. You tear the skin from my people and strip the flesh from their bones.

Micah3:7 Then the seers will be ashamed and the diviners will be disgraced. They will all cover their mouths because there is no answer from God.”

Micah3:10 who build Zion with bloodshed and Jerusalem with iniquity.

Micah4:2 And many nations will come and say: “Come
Micah4:3 Then He will judge between many peoples and arbitrate for strong nations far and wide. Then they will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks. Nation will no longer take up the sword against nation
Micah4:4 And each man will sit under his own vine and under his own fig tree
Micah4:5 Though each of the peoples may walk in the name of his god
Micah4:6 “On that day
Micah4:7 And I will make the lame into a remnant
Micah4:8 And you

Micah4:11 But now many nations have assembled against you
Micah4:12 But they do not know the thoughts of the Lord or understand His plan
Micah4:13 Rise and thresh

Micah5:2 But you

Micah5:5 And He will be our peace when Assyria invades our land and tramples our citadels. We will raise against it seven shepherds

Micah5:12 I will cut the sorceries from your hand
Micah5:13 I will also cut off the carved images and sacred pillars from among you
Micah5:14 I will root out the Asherah poles from your midst and demolish your cities.
Micah5:15 I will take vengeance in anger and wrath upon the nations that have not obeyed Me.”

Micah6:2 Hear
Micah6:3 ‘My people
Micah6:4 For I brought you up from the land of Egypt and redeemed you from the house of slavery. I sent Moses before you
Micah6:5 My people
Micah6:6 With what shall I come before the Lord when I bow before the God on high? Should I come to Him with burnt offerings
Micah6:7 Would the Lord be pleased with thousands of rams
Micah6:8 He has shown you
Micah6:9 The voice of the Lord calls out to the city (and it is sound wisdom to fear Your name): “Heed the rod and the One who ordained it.
Micah6:10 Can I forget any longer
Micah6:11 Can I excuse dishonest scales or bags of false weights?
Micah6:12 For the wealthy of the city are full of violence
Micah6:13 Therefore I am striking you severely
Micah6:14 You will eat but not be satisfied
Micah6:15 You will sow but not reap; you will press olives but not anoint yourselves with oil; you will tread grapes but not drink the wine.
Micah6:16 You have kept the statutes of Omri and all the practices of Ahab’s house; you have followed their counsel. Therefore I will make you a desolation
Micah7:1 Woe is me! For I am like one gathering summer fruit at the gleaning of the vineyard; there is no cluster to eat
Micah7:2 The godly man has perished from the earth; there is no one upright among men. They all lie in wait for blood; they hunt one another with a net.
Micah7:3 Both hands are skilled at evil; the prince and the judge demand a bribe. When the powerful utters his evil desire
Micah7:4 The best of them is like a brier; the most upright is sharper than a hedge of thorns. The day for your watchmen has come
Micah7:5 Do not rely on a friend; do not trust in a companion. Seal the doors of your mouth from her who lies in your arms.
Micah7:6 For a son dishonors his father
Micah7:7 But as for me
Micah7:8 Do not gloat over me
Micah7:9 Because I have sinned against Him
Micah7:10 Then my enemy will see and will be covered with shame— she who said to me
Micah7:11 The day for rebuilding your walls will come— the day for extending your boundary.
Micah7:12 On that day they will come to you from Assyria and the cities of Egypt
Micah7:13 Then the earth will become desolate because of its inhabitants
Micah7:14 Shepherd with Your staff Your people
Micah7:15 As in the days when you came out of Egypt
Micah7:16 Nations will see and be ashamed
Micah7:17 They will lick the dust like a snake
Micah7:18 Who is a God like You
Micah7:19 He will again have compassion on us; He will vanquish our iniquities. You will cast out all our sins into the depths of the sea.
Micah7:20 You will show faithfulness to Jacob and loving devotion to Abraham
Nahum1:1 This is the burden against Nineveh
Nahum1:2 The Lord is a jealous and avenging God; the Lord is avenging and full of wrath. The Lord takes vengeance on His foes and reserves wrath for His enemies.
Nahum1:3 The Lord is slow to anger and great in power; the Lord will by no means leave the guilty unpunished. His path is in the whirlwind and storm
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea and dries it up; He makes all the rivers run dry. Bashan and Carmel wither
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before Him
Nahum1:6 Who can withstand His indignation? Who can endure His burning anger? His wrath is poured out like fire; even rocks are shattered before Him.
Nahum1:7 The Lord is good
Nahum1:8 But with an overwhelming flood He will make an end of Nineveh and pursue His enemies into darkness.
Nahum1:9 Whatever you plot against the Lord
Nahum1:10 For they will be entangled as with thorns and consumed like the drink of a drunkard— like stubble that is fully dry.
Nahum1:11 From you
Nahum1:12 This is what the Lord says: “Though they are allied and numerous
Nahum1:13 For I will now break their yoke from your neck and tear away your shackles.”
Nahum1:14 The Lord has issued a command concerning you
Nahum1:15 Look to the mountains— the feet of one who brings good news
Nahum2:1 One who scatters advances against you
Nahum2:2 For the Lord will restore the splendor of Jacob like the splendor of Israel
Nahum2:3 The shields of his mighty men are red; the valiant warriors are dressed in scarlet. The fittings of the chariots flash like fire on the day they are prepared
Nahum2:4 The chariots dash through the streets; they rush around the plazas
Nahum2:5 He summons his nobles; they stumble as they advance. They race to its wall; the protective shield is set in place.
Nahum2:6 The river gates are thrown open and the palace collapses.
Nahum2:7 It is decreed that the city be exiled and carried away; her maidservants moan like doves
Nahum2:8 Nineveh has been like a pool of water throughout her days
Nahum2:9 “Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold!” There is no end to the treasure
Nahum2:10 She is emptied! Yes
Nahum2:11 Where is the lions’ lair or the feeding ground of the young lions
Nahum2:12 The lion mauled enough for its cubs and strangled prey for the lioness. It filled its dens with the kill
Nahum2:13 “Behold
Nahum3:1 Woe to the city of blood
Nahum3:2 The crack of the whip
Nahum3:3 Charging horseman
Nahum3:4 because of the many harlotries of the harlot
Nahum3:5 “Behold
Nahum3:6 I will pelt you with filth and treat you with contempt; I will make a spectacle of you.
Nahum3:7 Then all who see you will recoil from you and say
Nahum3:8 Are you better than Thebes
Nahum3:9 Cush and Egypt were her boundless strength; Put and Libya were her allies.
Nahum3:10 Yet she became an exile; she went into captivity. Her infants were dashed to pieces at the head of every street. They cast lots for her dignitaries
Nahum3:11 You too will become drunk; you will go into hiding and seek refuge from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses are fig trees with the first ripe figs; when shaken
Nahum3:13 Look at your troops— they are like your women! The gates of your land are wide open to your enemies; fire consumes their bars.
Nahum3:14 Draw your water for the siege; strengthen your fortresses. Work the clay and tread the mortar; repair the brick kiln!
Nahum3:15 There the fire will devour you; the sword will cut you down and consume you like a young locust. Make yourself many like the young locust; make yourself many like the swarming locust!
Nahum3:16 You have multiplied your merchants more than the stars of the sky. The young locust strips the land and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your guards are like the swarming locust
Nahum3:18 O king of Assyria
Nahum3:19 There is no healing for your injury; your wound is severe. All who hear the news of you applaud your downfall
Habakkuk1:1 This is the burden that Habakkuk the prophet received in a vision:
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do You make me see iniquity? Why do You tolerate wrongdoing? Destruction and violence are before me. Strife is ongoing
Habakkuk1:4 Therefore the law is paralyzed
Habakkuk1:5 “Look at the nations and observe— be utterly astounded! For I am doing a work in your days that you would never believe even if someone told you.
Habakkuk1:6 For behold
Habakkuk1:7 They are dreaded and feared; from themselves they derive justice and sovereignty.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards
Habakkuk1:9 All of them come bent on violence; their hordes advance like the east wind; they gather prisoners like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They scoff at kings and make rulers an object of scorn. They laugh at every fortress and build up siege ramps to seize it.
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep by like the wind and pass on through. They are guilty; their own strength is their god.”
Habakkuk1:12 Are You not from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 Your eyes are too pure to look upon evil
Habakkuk1:14 You have made men like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The foe pulls all of them up with a hook; he catches them in his dragnet
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore he sacrifices to his dragnet and burns incense to his fishing net
Habakkuk1:17 Will he
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand at my guard post and station myself on the ramparts. I will watch to see what He will say to me
Habakkuk2:2 Then the Lord answered me: “Write down this vision and clearly inscribe it on tablets
Habakkuk2:3 For the vision awaits an appointed time; it testifies of the end and does not lie. Though it lingers
Habakkuk2:4 Look at the proud one; his soul is not upright — but the righteous will live by faith —
Habakkuk2:5 and wealth indeed betrays him. He is an arrogant man never at rest. He enlarges his appetite like Sheol
Habakkuk2:6 Will not all of these take up a taunt against him
Habakkuk2:7 Will not your creditors suddenly arise and those who disturb you awaken? Then you will become their prey.
Habakkuk2:8 Because you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Woe to him who builds his house by unjust gain
Habakkuk2:10 You have plotted shame for your house by cutting off many peoples and forfeiting your life.
Habakkuk2:11 For the stones will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 Woe to him who builds a city with bloodshed and establishes a town by iniquity!
Habakkuk2:13 Is it not indeed from the Lord of Hosts that the labor of the people only feeds the fire
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the glory of the Lord as the waters cover the sea.
Habakkuk2:15 Woe to him who gives drink to his neighbors
Habakkuk2:16 You will be filled with shame instead of glory. You too must drink and expose your uncircumcision! The cup in the Lord’s right hand will come around to you
Habakkuk2:17 For your violence against Lebanon will overwhelm you
Habakkuk2:18 What use is an idol
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to wood
Habakkuk2:20 But the Lord is in His holy temple; let all the earth be silent before Him.
Habakkuk3:1 This is a prayer of Habakkuk the prophet
Habakkuk3:2 O Lord
Habakkuk3:3 God came from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 His radiance was like the sunlight; rays flashed from His hand
Habakkuk3:5 Plague went before Him
Habakkuk3:6 He stood and measured the earth; He looked and startled the nations; the ancient mountains crumbled; the perpetual hills collapsed. His ways are everlasting.
Habakkuk3:7 I saw the tents of Cushan in distress; the curtains of Midian were trembling.
Habakkuk3:8 Were You angry at the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 You brandished Your bow; You called for many arrows. Selah You split the earth with rivers.
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw You and quaked; torrents of water swept by. The deep roared with its voice and lifted its hands on high.
Habakkuk3:11 Sun and moon stood still in their places at the flash of Your flying arrows
Habakkuk3:12 You marched across the earth with fury; You threshed the nations in wrath.
Habakkuk3:13 You went forth for the salvation of Your people
Habakkuk3:14 With his own spear You pierced his head
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled the sea with Your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I heard and trembled within; my lips quivered at the sound. Decay entered my bones; I trembled where I stood. Yet I must wait patiently for the day of distress to come upon the people who invade us.
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree does not bud and no fruit is on the vines
Habakkuk3:18 yet I will exult in the Lord; I will rejoice in the God of my salvation!
Habakkuk3:19 GOD the Lord is my strength; He makes my feet like those of a deer; He makes me walk upon the heights! For the choirmaster. With stringed instruments.

Zechariah14:8 And on that day living water will flow out from Jerusalem
Zechariah14:9 On that day the Lord will become King over all the earth—the Lord alone
Zechariah14:10 All the land from Geba to Rimmon south of Jerusalem will be turned into a plain
Zechariah14:11 People will live there
Zechariah14:12 And this will be the plague with which the Lord strikes all the peoples who have warred against Jerusalem: Their flesh will rot while they stand on their feet
Zechariah14:13 On that day a great panic from the Lord will come upon them
Zechariah14:14 Judah will also fight at Jerusalem
Zechariah14:15 And a similar plague will strike the horses and mules

Zechariah14:21 Indeed

Matthew1:1 This is the record of the genealogy of Jesus Christ
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac
Matthew1:3 Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah by Tamar
Matthew1:4 Ram was the father of Amminadab
Matthew1:5 Salmon was the father of Boaz by Rahab
Matthew1:6 and Jesse the father of David the king. Next: David was the father of Solomon by Uriah’s wife
Matthew1:7 Solomon the father of Rehoboam
Matthew1:8 Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat
Matthew1:9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh
Matthew1:11 and Josiah the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon.
Matthew1:12 After the exile to Babylon: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel the father of Abiud
Matthew1:14 Azor was the father of Zadok
Matthew1:15 Eliud was the father of Eleazar
Matthew1:16 and Jacob the father of Joseph
Matthew1:17 In all
Matthew1:18 This is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about: His mother Mary was pledged in marriage to Joseph
Matthew1:19 Because Joseph her husband was a righteous man and was unwilling to disgrace her publicly
Matthew1:20 But after he had pondered these things
Matthew1:21 She will give birth to a Son
Matthew1:22 All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet:
Matthew1:23 “Behold
Matthew1:24 When Joseph woke up
Matthew1:25 But he had no union with her until she gave birth to a Son. And he gave Him the name Jesus.

Matthew5:23 So if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother has something against you

Matthew5:26 Truly I tell you

Matthew5:31 It has also been said

Matthew5:33 Again
Matthew5:34 But I tell you not to swear at all: either by heaven

Matthew5:37 Simply let your ‘Yes’ be ‘Yes

Matthew5:39 But I tell you not to resist an evil person. If someone slaps you on your right cheek

Matthew5:41 and if someone forces you to go one mile
Matthew5:42 Give to the one who asks you

Matthew6:28 And why do you worry about clothes? Consider how the lilies of the field grow: They do not labor or spin.

Matthew6:31 Therefore do not worry

Matthew6:34 Therefore do not worry about tomorrow

Matthew10:6 Go rather to the lost sheep of Israel.
Matthew10:7 As you go
Matthew10:8 Heal the sick
Matthew10:9 Do not carry any gold or silver or copper in your belts.

Matthew10:20 For it will not be you speaking

Matthew10:22 You will be hated by everyone because of My name
Matthew10:23 When they persecute you in one town
Matthew10:24 A disciple is not above his teacher

Matthew10:26 So do not be afraid of them. For there is nothing concealed that will not be disclosed
Matthew10:27 What I tell you in the dark
Matthew10:28 Do not be afraid of those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Instead
Matthew10:29 Are not two sparrows sold for a penny? Yet not one of them will fall to the ground apart from the will of your Father.
Matthew10:30 And even the very hairs of your head are all numbered.
Matthew10:31 So do not be afraid; you are worth more than many sparrows.
Matthew10:32 Therefore everyone who confesses Me before men
Matthew10:33 But whoever denies Me before men
Matthew10:34 Do not assume that I have come to bring peace to the earth; I have not come to bring peace
Matthew10:35 For I have come to turn ‘a man against his father
Matthew10:36 A man’s enemies will be the members of his own household.’
Matthew10:37 Anyone who loves his father or mother more than Me is not worthy of Me; anyone who loves his son or daughter more than Me is not worthy of Me;
Matthew10:38 and anyone who does not take up his cross and follow Me is not worthy of Me.
Matthew10:39 Whoever finds his life will lose it
Matthew10:40 He who receives you receives Me
Matthew10:41 Whoever receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward
Matthew10:42 And if anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because he is My disciple

Matthew12:19 He will not quarrel or cry out; no one will hear His voice in the streets.

Matthew12:29 Or again

Matthew12:31 Therefore I tell you
Matthew12:32 Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven
Matthew12:33 Make a tree good and its fruit will be good
Matthew12:34 You brood of vipers
Matthew12:35 The good man brings good things out of his good store of treasure
Matthew12:36 But I tell you that men will give an account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.
Matthew12:37 For by your words you will be acquitted
Matthew12:38 Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him

Matthew12:40 For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish
Matthew12:41 The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah
Matthew12:42 The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon

Matthew12:44 Then it says

Matthew12:47 Someone told Him
Matthew12:48 But Jesus replied
Matthew12:49 Pointing to His disciples
Matthew12:50 For whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.”
Matthew13:1 That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the sea.
Matthew13:2 Such large crowds gathered around Him that He got into a boat and sat down
Matthew13:3 And He told them many things in parables
Matthew13:4 And as he was sowing
Matthew13:5 Some fell on rocky ground
Matthew13:6 But when the sun rose
Matthew13:7 Other seed fell among thorns
Matthew13:8 Still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop—a hundredfold
Matthew13:9 He who has ears
Matthew13:10 Then the disciples came to Jesus and asked
Matthew13:11 He replied
Matthew13:12 Whoever has will be given more
Matthew13:13 This is why I speak to them in parables: ‘Though seeing
Matthew13:14 In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled: ‘You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.
Matthew13:15 For this people’s heart has grown callous; they hardly hear with their ears
Matthew13:16 But blessed are your eyes because they see
Matthew13:17 For truly I tell you
Matthew13:18 Consider
Matthew13:19 When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it
Matthew13:20 The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.
Matthew13:21 But since he has no root
Matthew13:22 The seed sown among the thorns is the one who hears the word
Matthew13:23 But the seed sown on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and produces a crop—a hundredfold
Matthew13:24 Jesus put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.
Matthew13:25 But while everyone was asleep
Matthew13:26 When the wheat sprouted and bore grain
Matthew13:27 The owner’s servants came to him and said
Matthew13:28 ‘An enemy did this

Matthew13:34 Jesus spoke all these things to the crowds in parables. He did not tell them anything without using a parable.
Matthew13:35 So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet: “I will open My mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden since the foundation of the world.”

Matthew25:44 And they too will reply
Matthew25:45 Then the King will answer

Matthew26:42 A second time He went away and prayed

Matthew26:46 Rise

Matthew26:51 At this

Matthew26:53 Are you not aware that I can call on My Father

Matthew26:56 But this has all happened so that the writings of the prophets would be fulfilled.” Then all the disciples deserted Him and fled.

Matthew27:3 When Judas

Matthew27:13 Then Pilate asked Him
Matthew27:14 But Jesus gave no answer
Matthew27:15 Now it was the governor’s custom at the feast to release to the crowd a prisoner of their choosing.
Matthew27:16 At that time they were holding a notorious prisoner named Barabbas.
Matthew27:17 So when the crowd had assembled
Matthew27:18 For he knew it was out of envy that they had handed Jesus over to him.
Matthew27:19 While Pilate was sitting on the judgment seat
Matthew27:20 But the chief priests and elders persuaded the crowds to ask for Barabbas and to have Jesus put to death.
Matthew27:21 “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?” asked the governor. “Barabbas
Matthew27:22 “What then should I do with Jesus who is called Christ?” Pilate asked. They all answered
Matthew27:23 “Why?” asked Pilate. “What evil has He done?” But they shouted all the louder
Matthew27:24 When Pilate saw that he was accomplishing nothing
Matthew27:25 All the people answered
Matthew27:26 So Pilate released Barabbas to them. But he had Jesus flogged
Matthew27:27 Then the governor’s soldiers took Jesus into the Praetorium and gathered the whole company around Him.

Matthew27:29 And they twisted together a crown of thorns and set it on His head. They put a staff in His right hand and knelt down before Him to mock Him
Matthew27:30 Then they spit on Him and took the staff and struck Him on the head repeatedly.

Matthew27:32 Along the way they found a man from Cyrene

Matthew27:39 And those who passed by heaped abuse on Him

Matthew27:42 “He saved others
Matthew27:43 He trusts in God. Let God deliver Him now if He wants Him. For He said
Matthew27:44 In the same way
Matthew27:45 From the sixth hour until the ninth hour darkness came over all the land.

Matthew27:47 When some of those standing there heard this
Matthew27:48 One of them quickly ran and brought a sponge. He filled it with sour wine
Matthew27:49 But the others said
Matthew27:50 When Jesus had cried out again in a loud voice
Matthew27:51 At that moment the veil of the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth quaked and the rocks were split.
Matthew27:52 The tombs broke open
Matthew27:53 After Jesus’ resurrection

Matthew27:56 Among them were Mary Magdalene
Matthew27:57 When it was evening

Matthew27:59 So Joseph took the body
Matthew27:60 and placed it in his own new tomb that he had cut into the rock. Then he rolled a great stone across the entrance to the tomb and went away.

Matthew27:63 “Sir
Matthew27:64 So give the order that the tomb be secured until the third day. Otherwise
Matthew27:65 “You have a guard
Matthew27:66 So they went and secured the tomb by sealing the stone and posting the guard.

Mark1:1 This is the beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: “Behold
Mark1:3 “A voice of one calling in the wilderness
Mark1:4 John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness
Mark1:5 People went out to him from all of Jerusalem and the countryside of Judea. Confessing their sins
Mark1:6 John was clothed in camel’s hair
Mark1:7 And he began to proclaim: “After me will come One more powerful than I
Mark1:8 I baptize you with water
Mark1:9 In those days Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
Mark1:10 As soon as Jesus came up out of the water
Mark1:11 And a voice came from heaven: “You are My beloved Son; in You I am well pleased.”
Mark1:12 At once the Spirit drove Jesus into the wilderness
Mark1:13 and He was there for forty days

Mark1:15 “The time is fulfilled
Mark1:16 As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee

Mark1:24 “What do You want with us
Mark1:25 But Jesus rebuked the spirit. “Be silent!” He said. “Come out of him!”
Mark1:26 At this
Mark1:27 All the people were amazed and began to ask one another
Mark1:28 And the news about Jesus spread quickly through the whole region of Galilee.
Mark1:29 As soon as Jesus and His companions had left the synagogue
Mark1:30 Simon’s mother-in-law was sick in bed with a fever
Mark1:31 So He went to her
Mark1:32 That evening
Mark1:33 and the whole town gathered at the door.
Mark1:34 And He healed many who were ill with various diseases and drove out many demons. But He would not allow the demons to speak

Mark1:38 But Jesus answered
Mark1:39 So He went throughout Galilee

Mark1:41 Moved with compassion
Mark1:42 And immediately the leprosy left him
Mark1:43 Jesus promptly sent him away with a stern warning:
Mark1:44 “See that you don’t tell anyone. But go

Mark4:5 Some fell on rocky ground
Mark4:6 But when the sun rose
Mark4:7 Other seed fell among thorns
Mark4:8 Still other seed fell on good soil

Mark4:17 But they themselves have no root
Mark4:18 Others are like the seeds sown among the thorns. They hear the word
Mark4:19 but the worries of this life
Mark4:20 Still others are like the seeds sown on good soil. They hear the word

Luke4:7 So if You worship me
Luke4:8 But Jesus answered
Luke4:9 Then the devil led Him to Jerusalem and set Him on the pinnacle of the temple. “If You are the Son of God
Luke4:10 For it is written: ‘He will command His angels concerning You to guard You carefully;
Luke4:11 and they will lift You up in their hands
Luke4:12 But Jesus answered
Luke4:13 When the devil had finished every temptation
Luke4:14 Jesus returned to Galilee in the power of the Spirit
Luke4:15 He taught in their synagogues and was glorified by everyone.
Luke4:16 Then Jesus came to Nazareth
Luke4:17 the scroll of the prophet Isaiah was handed to Him. Unrolling it
Luke4:18 “The Spirit of the Lord is on Me
Luke4:19 to proclaim the year of the Lord’s favor.”
Luke4:20 Then He rolled up the scroll
Luke4:21 and He began by saying
Luke4:22 All spoke well of Him and marveled at the gracious words that came from His lips. “Isn’t this the son of Joseph?” they asked.
Luke4:23 Jesus said to them
Luke4:24 Then He added
Luke4:25 But I tell you truthfully that there were many widows in Israel in the time of Elijah
Luke4:26 Yet Elijah was not sent to any of them
Luke4:27 And there were many lepers in Israel in the time of Elisha the prophet. Yet not one of them was cleansed—only Naaman the Syrian.”
Luke4:28 On hearing this
Luke4:29 They got up
Luke4:30 But Jesus passed through the crowd and went on His way.
Luke4:31 Then He went down to Capernaum
Luke4:32 They were astonished at His teaching
Luke4:33 In the synagogue there was a man possessed by the spirit of an unclean demon. He cried out in a loud voice
Luke4:34 “Ha! What do You want with us
Luke4:35 But Jesus rebuked the demon. “Be silent!” He said. “Come out of him!” At this
Luke4:36 All the people were overcome with amazement and asked one another
Luke4:37 And the news about Jesus spread throughout the surrounding region.
Luke4:38 After Jesus had left the synagogue
Luke4:39 and He stood over her and rebuked the fever
Luke4:40 At sunset
Luke4:41 Demons also came out of many people
Luke4:42 At daybreak
Luke4:43 But Jesus told them
Luke4:44 And He continued to preach in the synagogues of Judea.
Luke5:1 On one occasion
Luke5:2 He saw two boats at the edge of the lake. The fishermen had left them and were washing their nets.

Luke5:5 “Master

Luke6:42 How can you say

Luke8:6 Some fell on rocky ground
Luke8:7 Other seed fell among thorns
Luke8:8 Still other seed fell on good soil

Luke8:10 He replied
Luke8:11 Now this is the meaning of the parable: The seed is the word of God.
Luke8:12 The seeds along the path are those who hear
Luke8:13 The seeds on rocky ground are those who hear the word and receive it with joy
Luke8:14 The seeds that fell among the thorns are those who hear
Luke8:15 But the seeds on good soil are those with a noble and good heart

Luke12:26 So if you cannot do such a small thing

Luke12:30 For the Gentiles of the world strive after all these things

Luke12:40 You also must be ready
Luke12:41 “Lord

Luke12:44 Truly I tell you
Luke12:45 But suppose that servant says in his heart
Luke12:46 The master of that servant will come on a day he does not expect and at an hour he does not anticipate. Then he will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
Luke12:47 That servant who knows his master’s will but does not get ready or follow his instructions will be beaten with many blows.
Luke12:48 But the one who unknowingly does things worthy of punishment will be beaten with few blows. From everyone who has been given much

Luke12:50 But I have a baptism to undergo
Luke12:51 Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? No
Luke12:52 From now on
Luke12:53 They will be divided

Luke12:55 And when the south wind blows

Luke12:57 And why don’t you judge for yourselves what is right?
Luke12:58 Make every effort to reconcile with your adversary while you are on your way to the magistrate. Otherwise
Luke12:59 I tell you

John3:1 Now there was a man of the Pharisees named Nicodemus
John3:2 He came to Jesus at night and said
John3:3 Jesus replied
John3:4 “How can a man be born when he is old?” Nicodemus asked. “Can he enter his mother’s womb a second time to be born?”
John3:5 Jesus answered
John3:6 Flesh is born of flesh
John3:7 Do not be amazed that I said
John3:8 The wind blows where it wishes. You hear its sound
John3:9 “How can this be?” Nicodemus asked.
John3:10 “You are Israel’s teacher
John3:11 Truly
John3:12 If I have told you about earthly things and you do not believe
John3:13 No one has ascended into heaven except the One who descended from heaven—the Son of Man.
John3:14 Just as Moses lifted up the snake in the wilderness
John3:15 that everyone who believes in Him may have eternal life.
John3:16 For God so loved the world that He gave His one and only Son
John3:17 For God did not send His Son into the world to condemn the world
John3:18 Whoever believes in Him is not condemned
John3:19 And this is the verdict: The Light has come into the world
John3:20 Everyone who does evil hates the Light
John3:21 But whoever practices the truth comes into the Light
John3:22 After this
John3:23 Now John was also baptizing at Aenon near Salim
John3:24 (For John had not yet been thrown into prison.)
John3:25 Then a dispute arose between John’s disciples and a certain Jew over the issue of ceremonial washing.
John3:26 So John’s disciples came to him and said
John3:27 John replied
John3:28 You yourselves can testify that I said
John3:29 The bride belongs to the bridegroom. The friend of the bridegroom stands and listens for him
John3:30 He must increase; I must decrease.
John3:31 The One who comes from above is above all. The one who is from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks as one from the earth. The One who comes from heaven is above all.
John3:32 He testifies to what He has seen and heard
John3:33 Whoever accepts His testimony has certified that God is truthful.
John3:34 For the One whom God has sent speaks the words of God
John3:35 The Father loves the Son and has placed all things in His hands.
John3:36 Whoever believes in the Son has eternal life. Whoever rejects the Son will not see life. Instead

John5:1 Some time later there was a feast of the Jews
John5:2 Now there is in Jerusalem near the Sheep Gate a pool with five covered colonnades
John5:3 On these walkways lay a great number of the sick
John5:5 One man there had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.
John5:6 When Jesus saw him lying there and realized that he had spent a long time in this condition
John5:7 “Sir
John5:8 Then Jesus told him
John5:9 Immediately the man was made well
John5:10 so the Jews said to the man who had been healed
John5:11 But he answered

John5:13 But the man who was healed did not know who it was
John5:14 Afterward
John5:15 And the man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had made him well.
John5:16 Now because Jesus was doing these things on the Sabbath
John5:17 But Jesus answered them
John5:18 Because of this
John5:19 So Jesus replied
John5:20 The Father loves the Son and shows Him all He does. And to your amazement
John5:21 For just as the Father raises the dead and gives them life
John5:22 Furthermore
John5:23 so that all may honor the Son just as they honor the Father. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent Him.
John5:24 Truly
John5:25 Truly
John5:26 For as the Father has life in Himself
John5:27 And He has given Him authority to execute judgment
John5:28 Do not be amazed at this
John5:29 and come out—those who have done good to the resurrection of life
John5:30 I can do nothing by Myself; I judge only as I hear. And My judgment is just
John5:31 If I testify about Myself
John5:32 There is another who testifies about Me
John5:33 You have sent to John
John5:34 Even though I do not accept human testimony
John5:35 John was a lamp that burned and gave light
John5:36 But I have testimony more substantial than that of John. For the works that the Father has given Me to accomplish—the very works I am doing—testify about Me that the Father has sent Me.
John5:37 And the Father who sent Me has Himself testified about Me. You have never heard His voice nor seen His form
John5:38 nor does His word abide in you
John5:39 You pore over the Scriptures because you presume that by them you possess eternal life. These are the very words that testify about Me
John5:40 yet you refuse to come to Me to have life.
John5:41 I do not accept glory from men
John5:42 but I know you
John5:43 I have come in My Father’s name
John5:44 How can you believe if you accept glory from one another
John5:45 Do not think that I will accuse you before the Father. Your accuser is Moses
John5:46 If you had believed Moses
John5:47 But since you do not believe what he wrote

John10:10 The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I have come that they may have life
John10:11 I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep.
John10:12 The hired hand is not the shepherd
John10:13 The man runs away because he is a hired servant and is unconcerned for the sheep.
John10:14 I am the good shepherd. I know My sheep and My sheep know Me
John10:15 just as the Father knows Me and I know the Father. And I lay down My life for the sheep.

John10:17 The reason the Father loves Me is that I lay down My life in order to take it up again.
John10:18 No one takes it from Me

John12:28 Father

Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 the gospel He promised beforehand through His prophets in the Holy Scriptures
Romans1:3 regarding His Son
Romans1:4 and who through the Spirit of holiness was declared with power to be the Son of God by His resurrection from the dead: Jesus Christ our Lord.
Romans1:5 Through Him and on behalf of His name
Romans1:6 And you also are among those who are called to belong to Jesus Christ.
Romans1:7 To all in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints: Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 God
Romans1:10 in my prayers at all times
Romans1:11 For I long to see you so that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to strengthen you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 I do not want you to be unaware
Romans1:14 I am obligated both to Greeks and non-Greeks
Romans1:15 That is why I am so eager to preach the gospel also to you who are in Rome.
Romans1:16 I am not ashamed of the gospel
Romans1:17 For the gospel reveals the righteousness of God that comes by faith from start to finish
Romans1:18 The wrath of God is being revealed from heaven against all the godlessness and wickedness of men who suppress the truth by their wickedness.
Romans1:19 For what may be known about God is plain to them
Romans1:20 For since the creation of the world God’s invisible qualities
Romans1:21 For although they knew God
Romans1:22 Although they claimed to be wise
Romans1:23 and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images of mortal man and birds and animals and reptiles.
Romans1:24 Therefore God gave them over in the desires of their hearts to impurity for the dishonoring of their bodies with one another.
Romans1:25 They exchanged the truth of God for a lie
Romans1:26 For this reason God gave them over to dishonorable passions. Even their women exchanged natural relations for unnatural ones.
Romans1:27 Likewise
Romans1:28 Furthermore
Romans1:29 They have become filled with every kind of wickedness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 They are senseless
Romans1:32 Although they know God’s righteous decree that those who do such things are worthy of death
Romans2:1 You
Romans2:2 And we know that God’s judgment against those who do such things is based on truth.
Romans2:3 So when you
Romans2:4 Or do you disregard the riches of His kindness
Romans2:5 But because of your hard and unrepentant heart
Romans2:6 God “will repay each one according to his deeds.”
Romans2:7 To those who by perseverance in doing good seek glory
Romans2:8 But for those who are self-seeking and who reject the truth and follow wickedness
Romans2:9 There will be trouble and distress for every human being who does evil
Romans2:10 but glory
Romans2:11 For God does not show favoritism.
Romans2:12 All who sin apart from the law will also perish apart from the law
Romans2:13 For it is not the hearers of the law who are righteous before God
Romans2:14 Indeed
Romans2:15 So they show that the work of the law is written on their hearts
Romans2:16 on the day when God will judge men’s secrets through Christ Jesus
Romans2:17 Now you
Romans2:18 if you know His will and approve of what is superior because you are instructed by the law;
Romans2:19 if you are convinced that you are a guide for the blind
Romans2:20 an instructor of the foolish
Romans2:21 you
Romans2:22 You who forbid adultery
Romans2:23 You who boast in the law
Romans2:24 As it is written: “God’s name is blasphemed among the Gentiles because of you.”
Romans2:25 Circumcision has value if you observe the law
Romans2:26 If a man who is not circumcised keeps the requirements of the law
Romans2:27 The one who is physically uncircumcised yet keeps the law will condemn you who
Romans2:28 A man is not a Jew because he is one outwardly

Romans3:1 What
Romans3:2 Much in every way. First of all
Romans3:3 What if some did not have faith? Will their lack of faith nullify God’s faithfulness?
Romans3:4 Certainly not! Let God be true and every man a liar. As it is written: “So that You may be proved right when You speak and victorious when You judge.”
Romans3:5 But if our unrighteousness highlights the righteousness of God
Romans3:6 Certainly not! In that case
Romans3:7 However
Romans3:8 Why not say
Romans3:9 What then? Are we any better? Not at all. For we have already made the charge that Jews and Greeks alike are all under sin.
Romans3:10 As it is written: “There is no one righteous
Romans3:11 There is no one who understands
Romans3:12 All have turned away
Romans3:13 “Their throats are open graves; their tongues practice deceit.” “The venom of vipers is on their lips.”
Romans3:14 “Their mouths are full of cursing and bitterness.”
Romans3:15 “Their feet are swift to shed blood;
Romans3:16 ruin and misery lie in their wake
Romans3:17 and the way of peace they have not known.”
Romans3:18 “There is no fear of God before their eyes.”
Romans3:19 Now we know that whatever the law says
Romans3:20 Therefore no one will be justified in His sight by works of the law. For the law merely brings awareness of sin.
Romans3:21 But now
Romans3:22 And this righteousness from God comes through faith in Jesus Christ to all who believe. There is no distinction
Romans3:23 for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God
Romans3:24 and are justified freely by His grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus.
Romans3:25 God presented Him as the atoning sacrifice through faith in His blood
Romans3:26 He did this to demonstrate His righteousness at the present time
Romans3:27 Where
Romans3:28 For we maintain that a man is justified by faith apart from works of the law.
Romans3:29 Is God the God of Jews only? Is He not the God of Gentiles too? Yes
Romans3:30 since there is only one God
Romans3:31 Do we
Romans4:1 What then shall we say that Abraham
Romans4:2 If Abraham was indeed justified by works
Romans4:3 For what does the Scripture say? “Abraham believed God
Romans4:4 Now the wages of the worker are not credited as a gift
Romans4:5 However
Romans4:6 And David speaks likewise of the blessedness of the man to whom God credits righteousness apart from works:
Romans4:7 “Blessed are they whose lawless acts are forgiven
Romans4:8 Blessed is the man whose sin the Lord will never count against him.”
Romans4:9 Is this blessing only on the circumcised
Romans4:10 In what context was it credited? Was it after his circumcision
Romans4:11 And he received the sign of circumcision as a seal of the righteousness that he had by faith while he was still uncircumcised. So then
Romans4:12 And he is also the father of the circumcised who not only are circumcised
Romans4:13 For the promise to Abraham and his offspring that he would be heir of the world was not given through the law
Romans4:14 For if those who live by the law are heirs
Romans4:15 because the law brings wrath. And where there is no law
Romans4:16 Therefore
Romans4:17 As it is written: “I have made you a father of many nations.” He is our father in the presence of God
Romans4:18 Against all hope
Romans4:19 Without weakening in his faith
Romans4:20 Yet he did not waver through disbelief in the promise of God
Romans4:21 being fully persuaded that God was able to do what He had promised.
Romans4:22 This is why “it was credited to him as righteousness.”
Romans4:23 Now the words “it was credited to him” were written not only for Abraham
Romans4:24 but also for us
Romans4:25 He was delivered over to death for our trespasses and was raised to life for our justification.

Romans5:2 through whom we have gained access by faith into this grace in which we stand. And we rejoice in the hope of the glory of God.

Romans5:4 perseverance
Romans5:5 And hope does not disappoint us
Romans5:6 For at just the right time

Romans5:12 Therefore

Romans5:14 Nevertheless

Romans5:16 Again
Romans5:17 For if
Romans5:18 So then

Romans6:1 What then shall we say? Shall we continue in sin so that grace may increase?
Romans6:2 Certainly not! How can we who died to sin live in it any longer?
Romans6:3 Or aren’t you aware that all of us who were baptized into Christ Jesus were baptized into His death?
Romans6:4 We were therefore buried with Him through baptism into death
Romans6:5 For if we have been united with Him like this in His death
Romans6:6 We know that our old self was crucified with Him so that the body of sin might be rendered powerless
Romans6:7 For anyone who has died has been freed from sin.
Romans6:8 Now if we died with Christ
Romans6:9 For we know that since Christ was raised from the dead
Romans6:10 The death He died
Romans6:11 So you too must count yourselves dead to sin
Romans6:12 Therefore do not let sin reign in your mortal body so that you obey its desires.
Romans6:13 Do not present the parts of your body to sin as instruments of wickedness
Romans6:14 For sin shall not be your master
Romans6:15 What then? Shall we sin because we are not under law
Romans6:16 Do you not know that when you offer yourselves as obedient slaves
Romans6:17 But thanks be to God that
Romans6:18 You have been set free from sin and have become slaves to righteousness.
Romans6:19 I am speaking in human terms because of the weakness of your flesh. Just as you used to offer the parts of your body in slavery to impurity and to escalating wickedness
Romans6:20 For when you were slaves to sin
Romans6:21 What fruit did you reap at that time from the things of which you are now ashamed? The outcome of those things is death.
Romans6:22 But now that you have been set free from sin and have become slaves to God
Romans6:23 For the wages of sin is death

Romans7:2 For instance
Romans7:3 So then
Romans7:4 Therefore
Romans7:5 For when we lived according to the flesh
Romans7:6 But now
Romans7:7 What then shall we say? Is the law sin? Certainly not! Indeed
Romans7:8 But sin
Romans7:9 Once I was alive apart from the law; but when the commandment came
Romans7:10 So I discovered that the very commandment that was meant to bring life actually brought death.
Romans7:11 For sin
Romans7:12 So then
Romans7:13 Did that which is good
Romans7:14 We know that the law is spiritual; but I am unspiritual
Romans7:15 I do not understand what I do. For what I want to do
Romans7:16 And if I do what I do not want to do
Romans7:17 In that case
Romans7:18 I know that nothing good lives in me
Romans7:19 For I do not do the good I want to do. Instead
Romans7:20 And if I do what I do not want
Romans7:21 So this is the principle I have discovered: When I want to do good
Romans7:22 For in my inner being I delight in God’s law.
Romans7:23 But I see another law at work in my body
Romans7:24 What a wretched man I am! Who will rescue me from this body of death?
Romans7:25 Thanks be to God
Romans8:1 Therefore
Romans8:2 For in Christ Jesus the law of the Spirit of life set you free from the law of sin and death.
Romans8:3 For what the law was powerless to do in that it was weakened by the flesh
Romans8:4 so that the righteous standard of the law might be fulfilled in us
Romans8:5 Those who live according to the flesh set their minds on the things of the flesh; but those who live according to the Spirit set their minds on the things of the Spirit.
Romans8:6 The mind of the flesh is death
Romans8:7 because the mind of the flesh is hostile to God: It does not submit to God’s law
Romans8:8 Those controlled by the flesh cannot please God.
Romans8:9 You
Romans8:10 But if Christ is in you
Romans8:11 And if the Spirit of Him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you
Romans8:12 Therefore
Romans8:13 For if you live according to the flesh
Romans8:14 For all who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
Romans8:15 For you did not receive a spirit of slavery that returns you to fear
Romans8:16 The Spirit Himself testifies with our spirit that we are God’s children.
Romans8:17 And if we are children
Romans8:18 I consider that our present sufferings are not comparable to the glory that will be revealed in us.
Romans8:19 The creation waits in eager expectation for the revelation of the sons of God.
Romans8:20 For the creation was subjected to futility
Romans8:21 that the creation itself will be set free from its bondage to decay and brought into the glorious freedom of the children of God.
Romans8:22 We know that the whole creation has been groaning together in the pains of childbirth until the present time.
Romans8:23 Not only that
Romans8:24 For in this hope we were saved; but hope that is seen is no hope at all. Who hopes for what he can already see?
Romans8:25 But if we hope for what we do not yet see
Romans8:26 In the same way
Romans8:27 And He who searches our hearts knows the mind of the Spirit
Romans8:28 And we know that God works all things together for the good of those who love Him
Romans8:29 For those God foreknew
Romans8:30 And those He predestined
Romans8:31 What then shall we say in response to these things? If God is for us
Romans8:32 He who did not spare His own Son but gave Him up for us all
Romans8:33 Who will bring any charge against God’s elect? It is God who justifies.
Romans8:34 Who is there to condemn us? For Christ Jesus
Romans8:35 Who shall separate us from the love of Christ? Shall trouble or distress or persecution or famine or nakedness or danger or sword?
Romans8:36 As it is written: “For Your sake we face death all day long; we are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.”
Romans8:37 No
Romans8:38 For I am convinced that neither death nor life
Romans8:39 neither height nor depth

Romans9:2 I have deep sorrow and unceasing anguish in my heart.
Romans9:3 For I could wish that I myself were cursed and cut off from Christ for the sake of my brothers
Romans9:4 the people of Israel. Theirs is the adoption as sons; theirs the divine glory and the covenants; theirs the giving of the law

Romans9:6 It is not as though God’s word has failed. For not all who are descended from Israel are Israel.

Romans9:22 What if God

Romans9:24 including us

Romans12:1 Therefore I urge you
Romans12:2 Do not be conformed to this world
Romans12:3 For by the grace given me I say to every one of you: Do not think of yourself more highly than you ought
Romans12:4 Just as each of us has one body with many members
Romans12:5 so in Christ we who are many are one body
Romans12:6 We have different gifts according to the grace given us. If one’s gift is prophecy
Romans12:7 if it is serving
Romans12:8 if it is encouraging
Romans12:9 Love must be sincere. Detest what is evil; cling to what is good.
Romans12:10 Be devoted to one another in brotherly love. Outdo yourselves in honoring one another.
Romans12:11 Do not let your zeal subside; keep your spiritual fervor
Romans12:12 Be joyful in hope
Romans12:13 Share with the saints who are in need. Practice hospitality.
Romans12:14 Bless those who persecute you. Bless and do not curse.
Romans12:15 Rejoice with those who rejoice; weep with those who weep.
Romans12:16 Live in harmony with one another. Do not be proud
Romans12:17 Do not repay anyone evil for evil. Carefully consider what is right in the eyes of everybody.
Romans12:18 If it is possible on your part
Romans12:19 Do not avenge yourselves
Romans12:20 On the contrary
Romans12:21 Do not be overcome by evil

Romans13:2 Consequently
Romans13:3 For rulers are not a terror to good conduct
Romans13:4 For he is God’s servant for your good. But if you do wrong
Romans13:5 Therefore it is necessary to submit to authority
Romans13:6 This is also why you pay taxes. For the authorities are God’s servants
Romans13:7 Pay everyone what you owe him: taxes to whom taxes are due
Romans13:8 Be indebted to no one
Romans13:9 The commandments “Do not commit adultery
Romans13:10 Love does no wrong to its neighbor. Therefore love is the fulfillment of the law.
Romans13:11 And do this

Romans13:13 Let us behave decently
Romans13:14 Instead
Romans14:1 Accept him whose faith is weak
Romans14:2 For one person has faith to eat all things
Romans14:3 The one who eats everything must not belittle the one who does not
Romans14:4 Who are you to judge someone else’s servant? To his own master he stands or falls. And he will stand
Romans14:5 One person regards a certain day above the others
Romans14:6 He who observes a special day does so to the Lord; he who eats does so to the Lord
Romans14:7 For none of us lives to himself alone
Romans14:8 If we live
Romans14:9 For this reason Christ died and returned to life
Romans14:10 Why
Romans14:11 It is written: “As surely as I live
Romans14:12 So then
Romans14:13 Therefore let us stop judging one another. Instead
Romans14:14 I am convinced and fully persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean in itself. But if anyone regards something as unclean
Romans14:15 If your brother is distressed by what you eat
Romans14:16 Do not allow what you consider good
Romans14:17 For the kingdom of God is not a matter of eating and drinking
Romans14:18 For whoever serves Christ in this way is pleasing to God and approved by men.
Romans14:19 So then
Romans14:20 Do not destroy the work of God for the sake of food. All food is clean
Romans14:21 It is better not to eat meat or drink wine or to do anything to cause your brother to stumble.
Romans14:22 Keep your belief about such matters between yourself and God. Blessed is the one who does not condemn himself by what he approves.
Romans14:23 But the one who has doubts is condemned if he eats

Romans15:3 For even Christ did not please Himself
Romans15:4 For everything that was written in the past was written for our instruction

Romans15:6 so that with one mind and one voice you may glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Romans15:15 However

Romans15:21 Rather

1Corinthians1:1 Paul
1Corinthians1:2 To the church of God in Corinth
1Corinthians1:3 Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
1Corinthians1:4 I always thank my God for you because of the grace He has given you in Christ Jesus.
1Corinthians1:5 For in Him you have been enriched in every way
1Corinthians1:6 because our testimony about Christ was confirmed in you.
1Corinthians1:7 Therefore you do not lack any spiritual gift as you eagerly await the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ.
1Corinthians1:8 He will sustain you to the end
1Corinthians1:9 God
1Corinthians1:10 I appeal to you
1Corinthians1:11 My brothers
1Corinthians1:12 What I mean is this: Individuals among you are saying
1Corinthians1:13 Is Christ divided? Was Paul crucified for you? Were you baptized into the name of Paul?
1Corinthians1:14 I thank God that I did not baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius
1Corinthians1:15 so no one can say that you were baptized into my name.
1Corinthians1:16 Yes
1Corinthians1:17 For Christ did not send me to baptize
1Corinthians1:18 For the message of the cross is foolishness to those who are perishing
1Corinthians1:19 For it is written: “I will destroy the wisdom of the wise; the intelligence of the intelligent I will frustrate.”
1Corinthians1:20 Where is the wise man? Where is the scribe? Where is the philosopher of this age? Has not God made foolish the wisdom of the world?
1Corinthians1:21 For since in the wisdom of God the world through its wisdom did not know Him
1Corinthians1:22 Jews demand signs and Greeks search for wisdom
1Corinthians1:23 but we preach Christ crucified
1Corinthians1:24 but to those who are called
1Corinthians1:25 For the foolishness of God is wiser than man’s wisdom
1Corinthians1:26 Brothers
1Corinthians1:27 But God chose the foolish things of the world to shame the wise; God chose the weak things of the world to shame the strong.
1Corinthians1:28 He chose the lowly and despised things of the world
1Corinthians1:29 so that no one may boast in His presence.
1Corinthians1:30 It is because of Him that you are in Christ Jesus
1Corinthians1:31 Therefore

1Corinthians2:2 For I resolved to know nothing while I was with you except Jesus Christ and Him crucified.

1Corinthians2:4 My message and my preaching were not with persuasive words of wisdom
1Corinthians2:5 so that your faith would not rest on men’s wisdom
1Corinthians2:6 Among the mature
1Corinthians2:7 No
1Corinthians2:8 None of the rulers of this age understood it. For if they had
1Corinthians2:9 Rather
1Corinthians2:10 But God has revealed it to us by the Spirit. The Spirit searches all things
1Corinthians2:11 For who among men knows the thoughts of man except his own spirit within him? So too
1Corinthians2:12 We have not received the spirit of the world
1Corinthians2:13 And this is what we speak
1Corinthians2:14 The natural man does not accept the things that come from the Spirit of God. For they are foolishness to him
1Corinthians2:15 The spiritual man judges all things
1Corinthians2:16 “For who has known the mind of the Lord

1Corinthians3:2 I gave you milk
1Corinthians3:3 for you are still worldly. For since there is jealousy and dissension among you
1Corinthians3:4 For when one of you says

1Corinthians3:7 So neither he who plants nor he who waters is anything
1Corinthians3:8 He who plants and he who waters are one in purpose
1Corinthians3:9 For we are God’s fellow workers; you are God’s field
1Corinthians3:10 By the grace God has given me
1Corinthians3:11 For no one can lay a foundation other than the one already laid
1Corinthians3:12 If anyone builds on this foundation using gold
1Corinthians3:13 his workmanship will be evident
1Corinthians3:14 If what he has built survives
1Corinthians3:15 If it is burned up

1Corinthians3:19 For the wisdom of this world is foolishness in God’s sight. As it is written: “He catches the wise in their craftiness.”

1Corinthians4:1 So then
1Corinthians4:2 Now it is required of stewards that they be found faithful.
1Corinthians4:3 I care very little
1Corinthians4:4 My conscience is clear
1Corinthians4:5 Therefore judge nothing before the appointed time; wait until the Lord comes. He will bring to light what is hidden in darkness and will expose the motives of men’s hearts. At that time each will receive his praise from God.
1Corinthians4:6 Brothers

1Corinthians8:1 Now about food sacrificed to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up
1Corinthians8:2 The one who thinks he knows something does not yet know as he ought to know.
1Corinthians8:3 But the one who loves God is known by God.
1Corinthians8:4 So about eating food sacrificed to idols: We know that an idol is nothing at all in the world
1Corinthians8:5 For even if there are so-called gods
1Corinthians8:6 yet for us there is but one God
1Corinthians8:7 But not everyone has this knowledge. Some people are still so accustomed to idols that they eat such food as if it were sacrificed to an idol. And since their conscience is weak
1Corinthians8:8 But food does not bring us closer to God: We are no worse if we do not eat
1Corinthians8:9 Be careful

1Corinthians9:19 Though I am free of obligation to anyone

1Corinthians15:38 But God gives it a body as He has designed
1Corinthians15:39 Not all flesh is the same: Men have one kind of flesh
1Corinthians15:40 There are also heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. But the splendor of the heavenly bodies is of one degree
1Corinthians15:41 The sun has one degree of splendor

1Corinthians15:43 It is sown in dishonor; it is raised in glory. It is sown in weakness; it is raised in power.
1Corinthians15:44 It is sown a natural body; it is raised a spiritual body. If there is a natural body

1Corinthians15:47 The first man was of the dust of the earth

1Corinthians15:51 Listen
1Corinthians15:52 in an instant

1Corinthians15:54 When the perishable has been clothed with the imperishable and the mortal with immortality

Ephesians1:2 Grace and peace to you from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.

Ephesians1:9 And He has made known to us the mystery of His will according to His good pleasure
Ephesians1:10 as a plan for the fullness of time
Ephesians1:11 In Him we were also chosen as God’s own
Ephesians1:12 in order that we
Ephesians1:13 And in Him
Ephesians1:14 who is the pledge of our inheritance until the redemption of those who are God’s possession
Ephesians1:15 For this reason
Ephesians1:16 I have not stopped giving thanks for you
Ephesians1:17 that the God of our Lord Jesus Christ
Ephesians1:18 I ask that the eyes of your heart may be enlightened
Ephesians1:19 and the surpassing greatness of His power to us who believe. These are in accordance with the working of His mighty strength
Ephesians1:20 which He exerted in Christ when He raised Him from the dead and seated Him at His right hand in the heavenly realms
Ephesians1:21 far above all rule and authority
Ephesians1:22 And God put everything under His feet and made Him head over everything for the church
Ephesians1:23 which is His body
Ephesians2:1 And you were dead in your trespasses and sins
Ephesians2:2 in which you used to walk when you conformed to the ways of this world and of the ruler of the power of the air
Ephesians2:3 All of us also lived among them at one time
Ephesians2:4 But because of His great love for us
Ephesians2:5 made us alive with Christ even when we were dead in our trespasses. It is by grace you have been saved!
Ephesians2:6 And God raised us up with Christ and seated us with Him in the heavenly realms in Christ Jesus
Ephesians2:7 in order that in the coming ages He might display the surpassing riches of His grace
Ephesians2:8 For it is by grace you have been saved through faith
Ephesians2:9 not by works
Ephesians2:10 For we are God’s workmanship
Ephesians2:11 Therefore remember that formerly you who are Gentiles in the flesh and called uncircumcised by the so-called circumcision (that done in the body by human hands)—
Ephesians2:12 remember that at that time you were separate from Christ
Ephesians2:13 But now in Christ Jesus you who once were far away have been brought near through the blood of Christ.
Ephesians2:14 For He Himself is our peace
Ephesians2:15 by abolishing in His flesh the law of commandments and decrees. He did this to create in Himself one new man out of the two

Hebrews8:11 No longer will each one teach his neighbor or his brother
Hebrews8:12 For I will forgive their iniquities and will remember their sins no more.”
Hebrews8:13 By speaking of a new covenant
Hebrews9:1 Now the first covenant had regulations for worship and also an earthly sanctuary.
Hebrews9:2 A tabernacle was prepared. In its first room were the lampstand
Hebrews9:3 Behind the second curtain was a room called the Most Holy Place
Hebrews9:4 containing the golden altar of incense and the gold-covered ark of the covenant. Inside the ark were the gold jar of manna
Hebrews9:5 Above the ark were the cherubim of glory
Hebrews9:6 When everything had been prepared in this way
Hebrews9:7 But only the high priest entered the second room
Hebrews9:8 By this arrangement the Holy Spirit was showing that the way into the Most Holy Place had not yet been disclosed as long as the first tabernacle was still standing.
Hebrews9:9 It is an illustration for the present time
Hebrews9:10 They consist only in food and drink and special washings—external regulations imposed until the time of reform.
Hebrews9:11 But when Christ came as high priest of the good things that have come
Hebrews9:12 He did not enter by the blood of goats and calves
Hebrews9:13 For if the blood of goats and bulls and the ashes of a heifer sprinkled on those who are ceremonially unclean sanctify them so that their bodies are clean
Hebrews9:14 how much more will the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 Therefore Christ is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 In the case of a will
Hebrews9:17 because a will does not take effect until the one who made it has died; it cannot be executed while he is still alive.
Hebrews9:18 That is why even the first covenant was not put into effect without blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when Moses had proclaimed every commandment of the law to all the people
Hebrews9:20 saying
Hebrews9:21 In the same way
Hebrews9:22 According to the law
Hebrews9:23 So it was necessary for the copies of the heavenly things to be purified with these sacrifices
Hebrews9:24 For Christ did not enter a man-made copy of the true sanctuary
Hebrews9:25 Nor did He enter heaven to offer Himself again and again
Hebrews9:26 Otherwise
Hebrews9:27 Just as man is appointed to die once
Hebrews9:28 so also Christ was offered once to bear the sins of many; and He will appear a second time

Hebrews10:2 If it could
Hebrews10:3 Instead

Hebrews10:13 Since that time

Hebrews10:30 For we know Him who said
Hebrews10:31 It is a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.
Hebrews10:32 Remember the early days that you were in the light. In those days
Hebrews10:33 Sometimes you were publicly exposed to ridicule and persecution; at other times you were partners with those who were so treated.
Hebrews10:34 You sympathized with those in prison and joyfully accepted the confiscation of your property
Hebrews10:35 So do not throw away your confidence; it holds a great reward.
Hebrews10:36 You need to persevere
Hebrews10:37 For

Hebrews11:1 Now faith is the assurance of what we hope for and the certainty of what we do not see.
Hebrews11:2 This is why the ancients were commended.
Hebrews11:3 By faith we understand that the universe was formed at God’s command
Hebrews11:4 By faith Abel offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. By faith he was commended as righteous when God gave approval to his gifts. And by faith he still speaks
Hebrews11:5 By faith Enoch was taken up so that he did not see death: “He could not be found
Hebrews11:6 And without faith it is impossible to please God
Hebrews11:7 By faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:9 By faith he dwelt in the promised land as a stranger in a foreign country. He lived in tents
Hebrews11:10 For he was looking forward to the city with foundations
Hebrews11:11 By faith Sarah
Hebrews11:12 And so from one man
Hebrews11:13 All these people died in faith
Hebrews11:14 Now those who say such things show that they are seeking a country of their own.

Hebrews11:21 By faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 By faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 By faith Moses’ parents hid him for three months after his birth
Hebrews11:24 By faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 He chose to suffer oppression with God’s people rather than to experience the fleeting enjoyment of sin.
Hebrews11:26 He valued disgrace for Christ above the treasures of Egypt
Hebrews11:27 By faith Moses left Egypt
Hebrews11:28 By faith he kept the Passover and the sprinkling of blood
Hebrews11:29 By faith the people passed through the Red Sea as on dry land; but when the Egyptians tried to follow
Hebrews11:30 By faith the walls of Jericho fell
Hebrews11:31 By faith the prostitute Rahab

Hebrews11:34 quenched the raging fire
Hebrews11:35 Women received back their dead

Hebrews11:38 The world was not worthy of them. They wandered in deserts and mountains
Hebrews11:39 These were all commended for their faith
Hebrews11:40 God had planned something better for us
